> **‚òÖ Batch 9 (`nuke`) covers the Cold-War / modern HALF (1948‚Üí~2005)** ‚Äî the
> single largest corpus, and the direct predecessor of the batch-3 `modern`
> (2004‚Üí2020) thread (read them as one 1948‚Üí2020 campaign; the seam is the 2004
> election). Five headlines: (1) **the #92 era-band model now holds across a THIRD
> start year (1948)** ‚Äî confirmed across **1772 / 1800 / 1948** ‚Äî and is **refined
> into TWO LEVELS**: point-banked **Historical Eras** (here "Nuclear Age" 1948‚Äì2000
> ‚Üí "Era of Terror" 2000‚Äì~2012) vs **decade/census boundaries WITHIN an era** (1972/
> 1980/1990 = bulk EV reallocation + wholesale state-bias re-lean + content rotation).
> **The earlier-extractor "Era of Neocons" band was a MISLABEL ‚Äî it is a FACTION
> REBRAND + a decade content-rotation, NOT a point-banked band** (logged so it is
> not re-introduced; #92). (2) **The "Cold War" is NOT a system** ‚Äî it is the
> generic naval‚Üíland war engine RELABELED + event-flavor; **no nuclear/MAD/NATO/
> space-race subsystem, no army/navy/air branches, wars never resolve, and the USSR
> NEVER FALLS** (the dissolution chain stalls at a ~5% gate; still a named ally in
> 2005). (3) **The realignment is mechanically-but-GRADUALLY enforced** (4 cooperating
> levers, "1964‚Üíearly-1990s"), NOT a single scripted flip ‚Äî and BLUE/RED meaning
> **flips DURING the game** (Dixiecrats start INSIDE Blue). (4) **The ideology scale
> is a CIRCLE** (LW-Pop‚ÜîRW-Pop adjacent at 25%) ‚Äî independently CORROBORATED from a
> SECOND thread (POST 9842; was #99 from `rep1800`). (5) This is the **fullest modern-
> systems capture in the KB** (the primary‚Üíbrokered-convention‚Üígeneral machine, the
> diplomacy subsystem, first impeachment/resignation/unelected-succession, legislated-
> variable SCOTUS, mutable cabinet, Era-of-Terror content). **‚òÖ And the strongest
> corpus statement of design intent: the digital APP is built for 1-human-vs-9-CPU**
> (multiplayer "goes off the rails"), even though `nuke` itself is ~7‚Äì10-human MP ‚Äî
> validating the roadmap's solo-first sequencing (#114). The thread breaks at 2005;
> the **seam to the successor `modern` (2004‚Üí2020) is the next likely batch.**

## ‚òÖ North-star: the app must subsume the human GM

**AMPU's core engineering mandate is a zero-human-GM game.** Every playtest and
discussion thread in this corpus is run by a human Game Master (usually
MrPotatoTed / vcczar) who, turn after turn, performs work *by hand* that the
shipped build does not yet do. **Every GM hand-adjudication recorded in these
digests is, by definition, a feature the shipped game must perform
automatically and deterministically** (seeded RNG, `src/rng.ts` ‚Äî never a human
die-roll or a raw `Math.random`). The gap log below is, read correctly, the
catalogue of exactly that work.

The GM is doing **two distinct jobs**, and the game must own **both**:

**‚ëÝ GM as referee / calculator** ‚Äî the rules math and machinery the GM computes
by hand each turn. This is the half that is *least* visible in a casual read of
the corpus, and the one the batch-27 `redbutton` platform finding surfaced
(a 492-40 landslide caused by the GM mis-applying a `+1` as `+3`; the platform
"engine" does not exist in code ‚Äî it is GM arithmetic). It must move into the
deterministic engine:

| GM computes by hand | Must become engine | Gaps |
|---|---|---|
| Election/platform scoring, the swing die, historical-bias terms | The election scorer owns the math; UI shows only player-visible numbers ("fog of war") | #184, DH-72, #18, #14, QW3, **QW19‚ÄìQW21** |
| Primary ‚Üí brokered-convention: delegate weights, balloting, walkouts, kingmaker VP | A convention state-machine | #185, #183, #19, #20 |
| Scenario boot: year-correct incumbents, seeded delegates, back-dated careers, retro-fatigue | A deterministic per-year `BootSheet` (incl. mid-cycle/late-era shape) | #186, #187, #86, #115, #173 |
| Cabinet meter rolls / lingering effects | A lingering-roll engine | #179 |
| Treaty / decision-event / diplomacy adjudication, retry budgets | Era decision-event spine + treaty‚Üíterritory + bounded retries | #177, #178, #107, #162 |
| End-condition tracking (incl. the "big red button" nuclear loss) | Meter-floor game-end model | #88, #188 |
| Explaining the rules so players don't need a GM to interpret them | In-app rules surface | DH-69, QW21 |

> **‚òÖ Charter metric (batch 33, `excelautomate` ‚Äî the build-side burden budget):**
> the spreadsheet-era GM stated an explicit, dated **GM-burden budget ‚Äî *">30 min/
> phase ‚áí must automate"*** (POST 59: *"if any phase is still taking me more than 30
> minutes to process, that's a phase I'll focus on automating further"*), with the
> aspirational end-state that once the math is automated *"updating [the History tab]
> will become the only time-consuming part of this game"* (POST 10). The same thread
> is the cleanest itemized inventory of the ‚ëÝ referee/calculator load and its lived
> automation-PRIORITY order: **(1) seat tallies ‚Üí (2) per-politician trait/eligibility
> lookup ‚Üí (3) per-office election scoring ‚Üí (4) legislative-package point scoring**.
> Tellingly, the two tasks the GM *couldn't* automate in Excel ‚Äî **term-limit history-
> counting** and **per-candidate die rolls** ‚Äî are exactly the two the app handles
> trivially (office tenure in `snap`; seeded `rng.ts`): the hardest manual GM tasks
> are the engine's easiest. This is the concrete, quantified statement of the ¬ß‚ëÝ
> mandate ‚Äî corroborates **#114 / DH-36** from the build side (`excelautomate` POST 1,
> 10, 52, 59).

**‚ë° GM as opponent** ‚Äî the GM also *plays* every non-human faction (in solo
runs, all 9). This half the docs already headline hard:

| GM does by hand | Must become engine | Gaps |
|---|---|---|
| Per-faction CPU decisions: candidate selection, voting, meter management, cabinet/legislative heuristics | The **E9 handler suite + K5 `CpuController`** (today `grep runCpuFaction\|perFactionSim src/` = ZERO) | #114, E9, K5, CPU-AI spec cluster #70‚Äì#78 |
| (cost of NOT having it) | ‚Äî | **DH-36** ‚Äî 4 documented GM-burnout playtest deaths |

**The single most-corroborated cross-batch lesson** in the corpus is that the
manual CPU-faction simulation + opaque rules are what *kill* playtests (burnout
on the build side, confusion on the player side), and that the only runs to reach
a natural endpoint did so because the **designer personally absorbed the GM load**
(batch-25 `principle1772`: a 1-human-vs-9-CPU game completed *only* because Ted
hand-simulated all 9 CPU factions to ~1800). That is precisely the work ‚ëÝ + ‚ë°
above must remove from the human.

> **Going-forward convention (digests + gap log):** when a digest records the GM
> doing something by hand, tag it **`GM‚áíApp`** and ensure it has (or gets) a
> gap-log row. A `GM‚áíApp` item is never "designer flavor" to leave to a human ‚Äî
> it is an automation requirement. The roadmap's **"GM-replacement" lens**
> (`roadmap.md`) groups these into a buildable program; the technical-guide
> (`technical-guide.md` ¬ß9) sequences them.

## What this game is

AMPU is a turn-based American political strategy game that runs in the browser
(React + TypeScript + Vite; state persists in IndexedDB). The player runs a
**faction** inside one of two parties (BLUE / RED) and steers it across a
historical timeline: draft politicians, develop them, win elections, staff the
government, pass legislation, fight wars, and resolve era-defining decision
events. There is no single win screen baked in for both scenarios; play is a
campaign across a sequence of phases per turn, with terminal endings on certain
era events (`GameState.gameEnded`, `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd`, types.ts:1476,
1635).

As shipped, AMPU is **single-player**: the human owns exactly one faction
(`Faction.isPlayer`, types.ts:1303; `playerFactionId`, types.ts:1566) and the
engine drives every other faction as CPU. The turn loop is a fixed **phase
sequence** of ~36 phases grouped into 10 categories (`PHASE_SEQUENCE`,
phases.ts:3-47), advanced two years at a time (`nextPhaseInfo` adds 2 per turn
rollover, phases.ts:161). Most systems are **pure functions over a single
`FullGameSnapshot`** (types.ts:1811) in `src/engine/`; the UI is a registry of
~35 pages (registry.ts) with no router.

The engine is deterministic by design (seeded RNG, `src/rng.ts`) and the phase
taxonomy is numbered ("2.1.1 Politician Draft", "2.9.4 Presidential Election",
‚Ä¶) ‚Äî the same taxonomy the forum game uses, so this build is recognizably an
implementation of that game.

The player's goal is open-ended faction-building: accumulate Political Value
(PV) across your politicians, control offices (Presidency, Congress, governors,
cabinet, courts), shape national meters and interest-group standing, and survive
/ steer the era's crises. **PV drives elections** (`src/pv.ts`; CLAUDE.md).

## Eras & scenarios

The codebase has **four era values** ‚Äî `independence`, `federalism`,
`nationalism`, `modern` (`Era`, types.ts:1337) ‚Äî but only **two shipped
scenarios**, and only **three eras are reachable in play**. The forum runs a
**continuous multi-era campaign** (a 1772 or 1800 start runs straight through
independence ‚Üí federalism ‚Üí nationalism ‚Üí gilded ‚Üí ‚Ä¶), with **points reset at
each era boundary** (federalism digest post 518). Eras are not just flavor: each
is a balance dimension *and* carries its own faction roster, nickname table,
draft-ideology profile, era-event spine, bill catalog, card pool, and SCOTUS
case set.

| Scenario | Start | Era(s) reachable | Defining issues | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1772 ‚Äî Founding** | 1772, era `independence` (scenario1772.ts:97) | `independence` ‚Üí `federalism` on Constitution ratification (constitutionalConvention.ts:198) | Independence vs. loyalty, the Revolutionary War, confederation vs. federal union, writing the Constitution | **Built** |
| **1788 ‚Äî Early Republic** | 1788, era `federalism` | `federalism` (1788‚Äì1800) ‚Üí `nationalism` at ~1800 | **Hamilton vs. Jefferson**: assume Rev-War debt, Bank of the US, US Mint, tariff (set to 10%), bimetallic currency, Militia Act; Compromise of 1790; Whiskey/Fries rebellions; **party-formation as era events** (~1792); French-Revolution wars (War of 1795, Barbary); per-state shift to popular-vote electors; state-by-state slavery abolition vs. Cotton Gin; Louisiana Purchase | **Designed, not built** ‚Äî now richly documented (federalism digest is the spec) |
| **1800 ‚Üí Nationalism (the `rep1800` arc, 1800‚Üí1868)** | continuous campaign, mid-government boot (`BLUE = Dem-Republicans`, `RED = Federalists‚ÜíNational Republicans` ‚Äî **party frame inverted** vs later eras) | Forum-internal sub-bands of `nationalism`: **Republicanism (1800‚Äì20) ‚Üí Democracy (1820‚Äì40) ‚Üí Manifest Destiny (1840‚Äì56) ‚Üí Nationalism (1856‚Äì68)**; eras advance on **game-state, not year** (#92) | **Republicanism:** British impressment vs France, 12th-Amendment unlock, the 2nd Bank + tariff. **Democracy (Jacksonian):** universal white-male suffrage, the **Bank War**, internal improvements, tariff, slavery. **Manifest Destiny:** Pacific expansion, Texas/Mexican-Cession, slavery-cannot-expand, SC secession threat, Catholic-immigration nativism. **Nationalism:** alt-Civil-War (Pres defects to CSA, UK intervention, guerrilla phase) ‚Üí Reconstruction | **Designed, not built** ‚Äî the `rep1800` digest is the spec; **fills the previously-uncovered 1800‚Äì1856 gap**. Predecessor of the `gilded` (1868) arc; the 1800 campaign is now documented end-to-end |
| **1856 ‚Äî Antebellum** | 1856, era `nationalism` (scenario1856.ts:177) | **Shipped:** `nationalism` only (spine ends at the Trent Affair, 1861). **Designed (`hd`):** the same start runs **continuously 1856‚Üí1904+** through Nationalism ‚Üí Gilded (1868) ‚Üí Progressivism (1892) | Slavery, popular sovereignty / free soil, sectional crisis, **secession & Civil War ‚Üí Reconstruction** ‚Äî then forward into the tariff/currency/civil-service/imperialism Gilded issues and the Progressive issue-space | **Partially built** ‚Äî the antebellum *start* ships, but the **Civil-War combat engine, Reconstruction subsystem, secession-trait gating, sectional-balance crisis, and Canada/era-continuation** are designed-not-built (`hd` digest is the spec; gap rows #56‚Äì#69) |
| **1800 ‚Üí Gilded Age (1868‚Äì1892)** | continuous campaign | Reaches a `gilded`-style era (likely the `modern` enum, or its own value) | **Tariff** (national integer set by legislation); **currency** (Gold-Standard vs Bimetallism/Free Silver); **civil-service reform / anti-corruption** ("Honest Gov't" crisis); **imperialism** (island naval bases); Native-relations bills; Chinese / Jewish immigration bans; women's suffrage (state-by-state); Jim Crow / Poll Tax / Prohibition policy switches | **Designed, not built** ‚Äî confirmed by the gilded digest |
| **1948 ‚Üí Modern (the `nuke`+`modern` arc ‚Äî one campaign family 1948‚Üí2020)** | a **1948 mid-20th-c. boot** (the `nuke` thread runs 1948‚Üí~2005; `modern` continues 2004‚Üí2020) | The full **`modern`** era, split into the two-level era model: point-banked Historical Eras **"Nuclear Age" (1948‚Äì2000) ‚Üí "Era of Terror" (2000‚Äì~2012) ‚Üí Populism (2012‚Äì2024)**, with per-decade census boundaries (1972/1980/1990) inside each | **Most mechanically mature era.** Modern issue menu (tariff power to the President, income-tax brackets, healthcare, immigration, climate, Wall Street, gun control, surveillance/drone/detention); the **named meter bank + enthusiasm/Party-Pref engine**; **full primary‚Üíbrokered-convention‚Üígeneral** subsystem; **named-Justice SCOTUS docket** (legislated-variable size, court-packing, compelled votes); modern legislative depth (Crisis Bills, packaging, Executive-Branch-Interference, investigations, mutable cabinet); 53-state alt roster (DC/Cuba/PR) under a Wyoming-Rule apportionment; **the realignment** (Dixiecrats inside Blue ‚Üí modern polarity by ~1980); diplomacy (8 named nations); the **generic war engine relabeled** as the Cold War | **Designed, not built** ‚Äî `nuke` backfills the **1948‚Üí2004 origin** (the fullest modern capture); `modern` is the 2004‚Üí2020 continuation |
| **"The next era" (post-1892, pre-modern)** | implied | unknown | Feminists, socialists, communists, prohibitionists, eugenicists, labor activists ‚Äî narrated as forthcoming in the Gilded-Age era brief | **Designed, scope unclear** |

The forward arc is therefore **nationalism ‚Üí gilded ‚Üí progressive ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí
modern**: the `nationalism` Civil-War scenario is built, but the gilded/
progressive/modern eras at the end of the timeline are only `modern`-enum tuning
rows with no scenario. **federalism** sits *between* the two built scenarios and
is well-documented (`fed`); the **modern** end-state is now the best-documented
unbuilt era (`modern` digest ‚Äî a 2276-post campaign).

**Alt-history note.** The forum campaigns are **alternate timelines**, not
re-enactments. The `modern` campaign began at **1948** and 60 in-game years of
player decisions produced a divergent present: a **53-state** union (DC, **Cuba**,
**Puerto Rico** are full states), ahistorical presidents (Cuomo, Kirk, a 3-term
Cochran), and ahistorical SCOTUS rulings (Janus/Atkins/Dobbs all decided
differently, flagged "ahistorical"). The game runs on **its own clock**, with
**fictional era names** ("Era of Terror" ‚Ä¶‚Äì2012, "Era of Populism" 2012‚Äì2024) and
events firing ~10 years behind real tech. Real history supplies *texture and the
menu of plausible issues*; outcomes, officeholders, and the enacted-law record are
the campaign's own. (Per historical-context.md ¬ß10, the **modern era is the one
window where BLUE=Democrat=left / RED=Republican=right with no polarity caveat**,
and the shipped flat interest-group pool ‚Äî Big Tech, Big Oil & Gas, Globalists,
LW/RW Media, anachronistic in 1856/1868 ‚Äî **fits here**.) `modern` 426, 1080,
2067; historical-context ¬ß10.

- **1772** is a long "expansion-style" inaugural draft: the entire politician
  pool is drafted snake-order (scenario1772.ts:86, 107-117); historical founders
  enter via the CSV-driven inaugural draft, not the seed roster. It starts with
  **no government** ‚Äî no president, no cabinet, no governors, no courts ‚Äî which
  unlock through era events (Declaration ‚Üí governors; Constitution ‚Üí presidency,
  courts, cabinet). Era-event spine (eraEvents1772.ts): Intolerable Acts,
  Lexington & Concord, Continental Army, Alliance with France, Declaration of
  Independence, Articles of Confederation, Treaty of Paris, Annapolis ‚Üí
  Constitutional Convention, plus westward events (Vermont, Fort Stanwix).
- **1856** starts **mid-government**: Buchanan is president, the Constitution is
  ratified, governors and courts exist (scenario1856.ts:179-193). It begins past
  the draft phase (`phaseId: '2.1.2'`, scenario1856.ts:153). Era-event spine
  (eraEvents1856.ts): Bleeding Kansas, Dred Scott, Panic of 1857, Southern
  Secession Threat, Secession Winter (1860‚Äì61), Trent Affair.
- **Era is also a balance dimension**, not just flavor: many rule tables are keyed
  by `Era` ‚Äî mortality multipliers (`MORTALITY_RULES.eraConfig`, types.ts:507),
  faction-leadership churn (`LEADERSHIP_RULES.eraConfig`, types.ts:460), anytime-
  event rates (`ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES.eraConfig`, types.ts:1076), and several
  trait election/governance effects are era-scaled. `modern` rows exist
  throughout but no shipped scenario reaches that era.

## Core entities & systems (index)

One line each; see `game-mechanics.md` for the actual rules/math.

- **Politician** (types.ts:1251) ‚Äî the unit. Has `skills` (6 keys, 0‚Äì5),
  `command`, `traits[]`, `expertise[]`, `ideology`, faction/party, age, office,
  career track, PV cache, loyalty. **Drafted, never hand-placed** in 1772.
- **Skills** (types.ts:24) ‚Äî `admin, legislative, judicial, military, governing,
  backroom`, integers 0‚Äì5.
- **Command** ‚Äî a separate combat/leadership stat (not in `Skills`), gates
  kingmaking and military command.
- **Traits** (types.ts:62) ‚Äî ~60 positive/negative tags (Charismatic, Integrity,
  Corrupt, Iron Fist, ‚Ä¶) added over time (PR4b/PR6 batches), with symmetric
  conflict pairs (`TRAIT_CONFLICTS`, types.ts:658) and election/governance
  effects (`TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS`, `TRAIT_GOVERNANCE_EFFECTS`).
- **Expertise** (types.ts:182) ‚Äî a third axis, 19 policy domains (Economics,
  Foreign Affairs, Military, Labor, ‚Ä¶), granted by office/career/lobby.
- **Ideology** (types.ts:5) ‚Äî 7-point scale `LW Populist ‚Ä¶ RW Populist`; drifts
  and can be deliberately shifted.
- **Career tracks** (types.ts:43) ‚Äî 7 tracks; politicians accrue skills/traits/
  expertise over up to 20 years on a track (phase 2.1.2).
- **Faction** (types.ts:1293) ‚Äî what the player runs. Belongs to a party, has a
  `personality` (LW/Center/RW) and three card decks: `ideologyCards`,
  `lobbyCards`, `interestCards`. Factions drift alignment over time (phase 2.1.8).
- **Party** (types.ts:1306) ‚Äî two: BLUE and RED. Meaning is **era-specific**
  (see glossary).
- **State** (types.ts:1318) ‚Äî 13 colonies (states1772.ts) or 31 states
  (states1856.ts); has region, electoral votes, `bias`, `industries`, senators/
  reps/governor; 1772 colonies carry `ccDelegateSlots`/`colonySize`. The `region`
  enum already includes `Caribbean`/`Latin America`/`Pacific`/`Atlantic` ‚Äî partial
  readiness for the modern 53-state alt roster (DC/Cuba/PR as states; gap-log #55).
- **Offices** (`OfficeType`, types.ts:1111) ‚Äî President, VP, 7 cabinet seats
  (era-gated by year, `cabinetSeatsForYear`, types.ts:1196), General-in-Chief /
  Admiral, Chief/Associate Justice, Senator, Representative, Governor, Speaker,
  Pro Tem, Committee Chair, Faction/Party Leader, CC President, Ambassador.
- **Elections** (`calcStateVote`; `ElectionContext`, types.ts:697) ‚Äî 6 contexts:
  presGeneral, presPrimary, house, senatePre17 (both eras use pre-17th-Amendment
  legislature-elected senate), governor, internalParty. PV-driven.
- **Legislation** (types.ts:1506) ‚Äî factions propose bills routed to one of four
  committees (Domestic/Foreign/Economic/Justice), committee review, then House +
  Senate floor votes (phases 2.6.1‚Äì2.6.3).
- **National meters** (`NationalMeters`, types.ts:1399) ‚Äî revenue, economic,
  military, domestic, honest, quality, planet; tick in the Lingering phase (2.5.1)
  and via events/legislation. The forum's modern bank adds **Party Preference**,
  **per-power relation meters** (8 powers), and **per-ideology enthusiasm** to this
  set, with crisis/cascade rules (gap-log #50).
- **Interest groups / enthusiasm / party preference** ‚Äî `InterestGroupScores`,
  `Enthusiasm` (ideology√óparty ‚àí5..+5), `partyPreference`: the electoral mood
  substrate. The forum drives these via a formalized **4-part enthusiasm-reshuffle
  + faction-Score** engine (gap-log #51).
- **Era events** (`EraEvent`, types.ts:1466) ‚Äî interactive scripted decisions
  (phase 2.4.3) with a `decider` (president/congress/cabinet/cc-president/auto)
  and effect-bearing responses; some unlock systems or end the game. 1772 also
  has a data-driven **precondition graph** (`Predicate`, types.ts:1487;
  `ERA_GRAPH_RULES`).
- **Anytime events** (phase 2.4.2) ‚Äî non-scripted historical/scandal events
  (anytimeEvents.ts, anytimeNationalEvents.ts), era-rate-scaled.
- **Era systems (1772 only, in the build):** Continental Congress
  (`ContinentalCongress`, types.ts:1347; built via phase 2.9.6), Revolutionary
  War (`RevolutionaryWar`, types.ts:1371; battles in 2.7.2), Constitutional
  Convention (`ConstitutionalConvention`, types.ts:1801; sets
  `ConstitutionalArticles`). **Design intent:** war is a *generic* cross-era
  system (War of 1795, Barbary, 1812) ‚Äî see gap-log #45 ‚Äî and Constitutional
  change continues post-founding via durable **Amendments** (gap-log #39).
- **Player meta-systems (per-turn passes, phases 2.1.3‚Äì2.1.8):** Flip-Flopper
  cleanup, Relocations, Ideology Shifts, Faction Conversions (poaching/signing),
  Kingmakers & Prot√©g√©s, Faction Personalities/Alignment drift. Each has its own
  rule const block and event feed in types.ts.
- **Cabinet & loyalty (PR5/PR6)** ‚Äî cabinet appointments scored per seat
  (`CABINET_SEAT_SCORING`); 1856 cabinet members carry `loyalty` that decays in
  the Secession Winter event and can trigger defection.
- **Draft dataset** ‚Äî bundled "standard draft classes" are **generated** by Node
  scripts (`scripts/`), ~18.5k politicians runtime-loaded from
  `public/standard-draft-classes.json`; users can import custom draftees
  (`ImportedDraftee`, types.ts:1780). Do not hand-edit generated files.

## Glossary

- **PV (Political Value)** ‚Äî a politician's weighted worth, office-weighted;
  the currency that drives elections (`computePV`/`refreshPv`, src/pv.ts).
- **BLUE / RED** ‚Äî the two parties; **meaning is era-specific.** 1772: BLUE =
  Patriots / Anti-Federalists, RED = Federalists (factions1772.ts:27-28). 1856:
  BLUE = Democratic Party, RED = Republican Party (factions1856.ts:19-22). Do not
  assume a fixed left/right reading across eras.
- **Faction personality** ‚Äî `LW | Center | RW`, the faction's coarse lean;
  distinct from a politician's 7-point `ideology`.
- **Era** ‚Äî engine bucket (`independence/federalism/nationalism/modern`), a
  superset of scenario; used for balance scaling, not just narrative.
- **Half-term / turn** ‚Äî the loop advances 2 years per full pass of the phase
  sequence; `HalfTermSummary` (types.ts:1762) records per-turn deltas.
- **senatePre17** ‚Äî election context: both shipped scenarios predate the 17th
  Amendment, so senators are chosen by state legislatures, never popular vote
  (types.ts:696-703).
- **electorsByLegislature** *(early-republic, `rep1800`)* ‚Äî a proposed per-state
  flag (sibling of `senatePre17`): pre-12th-Amendment, many states choose
  presidential electors by **state legislature**, so the EV winner is decided by
  **who holds Gov/Sen/Rep** (majority party; Gov breaks ties), **not** by PV+dice.
  Phases out per-state by amendment/event; the "Nationwide Popular-Vote Surge" era
  event flips all-but-SC. Distinct from #44's `electionMethod`. `rep1800` ¬ß2-A.
- **Content-band era** *(architecture, `rep1800`)* ‚Äî the design's actual notion of
  an "era": a fixed **band of available bills/events/draftees + a state-bias
  table**, entered/exited by **game-state + territory-ownership** triggers, NOT by
  calendar year. The clock runs ~30 yrs behind content (LA Purchase fires 1834).
  Contradicts the shipped `year % 4`/`year % 2` predicates. See gap-log #92.
- **Internal era names** *(era-sensitive, `rep1800`)* ‚Äî the forum's eras are
  **finer-grained** than the 4-value `Era` enum: Republicanism (1800‚Äì20) ‚Üí
  Democracy (1820‚Äì40) ‚Üí Manifest Destiny (1840‚Äì56) ‚Üí **Nationalism** (1856‚Äì68).
  The first three are **sub-bands of the shipped `nationalism` enum**;
  "Nationalism" internally = antebellum/Civil-War = the shipped `nationalism` value.
- **Cohens-v-Virginia precedent** *(early-republic, `rep1800`)* ‚Äî an ahistorical
  scripted SCOTUS ruling that **federal law does NOT supersede existing state
  law**, making slavery a **state issue**: a slave state's slavery can be ended
  **only by a constitutional Amendment** (or a per-state Gov action). The engine
  substrate for the whole sectional design; there is **no mechanism to reverse an
  ahistorical ruling**. `rep1800` ¬ß2-B.
- **Command** ‚Äî combat/leadership stat outside the 0‚Äì5 skill set; gates military
  command and kingmaker eligibility (`KINGMAKER_RULES.commandGateByScenario`).
- **Kingmaker / Prot√©g√©** ‚Äî a high-Command mentor anoints a young prot√©g√© who
  graduates to higher office after ~20 years (`KINGMAKER_RULES`, types.ts:295).
- **Lingering phase (2.5.1)** ‚Äî per-turn drift of national meters based on
  cabinet/conditions.
- **Sub-floor stats** ‚Äî failed candidates / era figures in the dataset get
  deliberately weak electoral stats so they rarely win (CLAUDE.md dataset rules).
- **Lobby / Ideology / Interest cards** ‚Äî the three faction card decks that
  define a faction's coalition and shift over time (types.ts:1298-1300).
- **Southern Unionist (trait) / Secessionists (pool)** *(1856-arc, `hd`)* ‚Äî on
  secession, pols of seceded/border states go inactive into a "Secessionists"
  pool **unless** they hold the `Southern Unionist` trait; they return on the
  state's readmission. The draft dataset carries the trait on Southern pols.
- **Reconstruction +2-Red bias** *(1856-arc, `hd`)* ‚Äî a time-boxed (8-yr,
  sunsetting) per-state partisan bias toward the incumbent party, granted by the
  Ironclad-Oath readmission plan; distinct from the permanent `State.bias`.
- **Acting President** *(1856-arc, `hd`)* ‚Äî when the elected line fails (e.g. a
  foreign-born Speaker is ineligible), the Senate Pro Tempore acts; a **0-Command
  acting president is inert** (no exec actions, can't compel SCOTUS, can't be
  elected). The succession order is itself legislatable.
- **Primary Group (1‚Äì5)** *(Primary Era, `hd`)* ‚Äî states opt into presidential
  primaries (via a Gov action) and are bucketed into 5 sequentially-voting groups;
  once any state opts in, the Primary Era begins (primaries precede conventions).
- **War Score** *(`hd`)* ‚Äî per-theater Civil-War tally (+1 win / +3 Difficult land
  / ‚àí1 loss); **+10 auto-wins the theater**, else a carry-roll continues the war
  next half-term.

## Built vs. Designed ‚Äî gap log

The heart of this doc; the roadmap is built from this. Rows are grouped by area.
Each is tagged `ingested` (forum-cited via a digest under
`docs/game/playtest-digests/`) or `codebase` (derived from the AMPU source
tree). Digest shorthands: **`gilded`** = the f4c7c2c4 1868 Gilded-Age digest;
**`fed`** = the f55d3e21 1788 federalism digest; **`1772s`** = the 85f8e6b4
1772 solo "aesthetic" digest; **`modern`** = the 3a9ac985 1948-campaign **2004‚Üí2020 successor** digest;
**`hd`** (= `house-divided`) = the 77db6e6f **1856-native** 1856‚Üí1904 digest
(cited `hd I-n` for its Part-I unique-system sections, else `hd POST n`);
**`hd1`** = the c015a0cb **"A House Divided" PART 1** digest (batch 16; the
**SEPARATE, EARLIER 1856-start run** distinct from `hd`/Part-2 ‚Äî alt-history cast
**Chase president / Lincoln VP**, secession 1860-61 via a **bungled John-Brown's-
Raid decision**, **CSA president Herschel V. Johnson / Davis only War Secretary**,
Union wins 1864, ends the Era of Nationalism at 1868 then **restarts with vcczar's
revised Reconstruction rules**; the **first Civil-War‚ÜíReconstruction run played by
HUMANS on both sides of the Reconstruction tug-of-war**; cites `POST n`);
**`drums`** = the e1776bbd all-CPU 1841‚Üí1924 digest; **`pop`** = the c50d9da7
**fresh-modern-boot** Era-of-Populism 2012‚Üí2017 digest (the **FIRST** 2012-start
run); **`pop2012b`** = the 409a7c18 **SECOND, DISTINCT 2012-start** "Era of
Populism ‚Äî AMPU Playtest, 2012 Start Date" digest (batch 23; GM **Rodja**, same
seed/factions as `pop` but a SEPARATE run ‚Äî GOP field Condi Rice/Fred Thompson/
Rick Perry/**Ron Paul wins**/Palin, ran **2012 ‚Üí the 2014-midterm cycle** then
**GA-burnout-died**; @MrPotatoTed plays as a PLAYER on Blue-5/Obama, his point-of-
order corrections designer-authoritative; load-bearing value = **heavy 2nd-source
CORROBORATION** of the modern cluster + every `pop` delta + 2 NEW committee-/
legislation-data gaps #174/#175; cites `POST n` + `chN`); **`rep1800`** = the
6aa7309a **1800‚Üí1868** "Era of Republicanism" digest (the early-republic gap-fill;
cites `¬ßA/¬ßB/¬ßC` section-digest refs + `POST n`); **`new1772`** = the afc6cbd7
**first-multiplayer 1772** "AMPU New 1772 playtest" digest (1772‚Üí1796 founding,
abandoned at GM burnout; cites `¬ßn` digest-section refs + `POST n`); **`tea1772`**
= the ad0f2875 **solo all-CPU 1772‚Üí1874** "Stamping out America's love for tea"
digest (stalls mid-Gilded; cites `¬ßn` + `POST n`); **`nuke`** = the be4e0f70
**Cold-War/modern-half 1948‚Üí~2005** "The Nuclear Age 1948‚Äì2004" digest (the
**largest corpus**; the **PREDECESSOR of `modern`** ‚Äî read together as one 1948‚Üí2020
campaign; cites `¬ßn` digest-section refs + `POST n`); **`dem1820`** = the cc37d770
**1820-start** "1820 ‚Äî The Era of Democracy" digest (a NEW 1820 scenario start,
Monroe's 2nd term ‚Üí Jacksonian; GA-burnout-killed at ~1822-23 / never reaches 1824;
the **3rd-start corroboration of #92**; cites `POST n`); **`arkzag`** = the 152c2881
**continuation of that SAME 1820 save** "Ark and Zags ‚Äî The Era of Democracy" digest
(run by @Zagnut + @Arkansas Progressive; **ran the FULL arc 1822‚Üí1840** ‚Äî fictional
D-R presidents Benton‚ÜíCheves‚ÜíEnoch-Lincoln‚ÜíDudley, a party system that never realigns;
the source for the **late-game** systems batch 10 never reached: multi-cycle
presidential elections, the Bank-War‚ÜíIndependent-Treasury economic arc, Force-Bill/
tariff fights, amendment machinery, presidential assassination+succession; cites
`POST n`); **`smallbugs`** = the cf82a7d3 "A Thread for small low priority changes

bugs + designer rulings; cites `POST n`); **`tedchange`** = the a0f0bf04
"Discussion: Ted's Change Log of Doom" **discussion** digest (the AUTHORITATIVE

`POST n`; the most current statement of rule wording); **`oopscpu`** = the
699113d6 **all-CPU 1788 CPU-stress-test** "Oops, All CPUs" digest (Ted-run
1788‚Üí~1798; the **deliberate CPU-ruleset interrogation** ‚Äî its load-bearing
signal is CPU-decision-making holes + Ted's live rulings; **designer-authoritative**,
same Ted > GA hierarchy as `tedchange`; cites `POST n`); **`terror2000`** = the
3843d2da **first-native 2000-start** "Era of Terror (2000)" digest (**Ted-run**,
CPU-heavy, ~2000‚Üí~2010; designer-authoritative, Ted > GA; modern polarity

**(corroborated across N eras)** has been independently observed in multiple
threads ‚Äî the strongest signal that it is a real, load-bearing rule rather than
a one-off ruling.

> **DEFECTS vs. GAPS.** This table is *missing features* (designed-but-unbuilt).
> Outright **bugs in shipped behavior** live in the separate "Confirmed shipped
> bugs" subsection below ‚Äî the roadmap should treat those as fixes, not
> features.

### Platform / cross-cutting

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Multiplayer** | Single-player: one human faction (`isPlayer`), all others CPU (scenario1772.ts:64, scenario1856.ts:121) | **Multiplayer** ‚Äî 8‚Äì10 human drafters across 2 parties (5 Blue + 5 Red), with handles; CPU factions only fill empty seats; **factions are transferable between humans mid-campaign** and CPU is a true per-faction fallback (`modern`: a new player took a CPU faction and won the presidency days later; `pop`: `Vee01` took R5 mid-thread, won the 2016 GOP nomination). (Both solo threads, `fed` + `1772s`, confirm the *same game* is also played solo-with-AI ‚Äî so solo and multiplayer are two modes of one engine) | Multi-faction human control / turn arbitration; per-player handle + identity; async/seated turns; GM-style narration layer; CPU only as fallback; faction-handover support. (**7+ era confirmed** ‚Äî very high confidence). **‚òÖ BUT the strongest design-intent statement (`nuke`) says the digital APP is 1-human-vs-9-CPU** (multiplayer "goes off the rails") even though the forum game is team MP ‚Äî so the build is a **solo adaptation of a multiplayer game**; see #114. **`nuke` 1948 boot:** ~7‚Äì10 humans, **5 RED + 5 BLUE**, CPUs fill VACANT factions only (suffix `CPU`), humans rotate in/out. **`dem1820` 1820 boot:** 10 humans, **5 BLUE + 5 RED** (the cap holds an 8th era); 1772s 1, 11; modern 1, 7, 424, 444; **hd 1, 38, 153, 456, 875**; **pop 1, 17, 24, 303, 312, 320; 1856-native + fresh-modern**; **nuke / POST 1, 16-17, 33, 483, 6966-6970; see #114**; **dem1820 POST 1, 412-414, 591, 601**; Ideology is likewise era-relative (Wilson/TR "Lib" = Progressive-era reform liberalism ‚âÝ FDR New-Deal "Lib" ‚âÝ modern "Lib") ‚Äî anchor every read to `draftYear`. This corroborates #4's per-(faction,era) era-relative drafting from the roster side and supplies the reference data any per-era ideology/team assignment must reproduce. Worked example: **Bernie Moreno** carries a dated **2021 ‚Üí RW-Populist** ideology shift (his MAGA turn); the roster companion's **Reagan** (FDR-Democrat ‚Üí GOP 1962) is the archetype the flip-date fields exist to capture. | 232 | **‚òÖ STARTING-year draft needs a TIGHTER ideology restriction (2/faction) or manual seeding than a mid-game/rookie draft (3) ‚Äî NEW (`primary2020`; distinct angle on #4/#171)** | The build has no draft-ideology restriction at all (#4 documents the early-era restriction as designed-only; #171 makes it an era-keyed toggle); neither captures the STARTING-draft-vs-mid-game-draft distinction | **A draft-balance finding from the May-2022 2022-start CPU draft (`primary2020` ¬ß5, POST 96, 99-104).** With 3 ideologies/faction at a COLD START the CPU produced incoherent factions: "Joe Biden is in charge of Bernie Sanders and AOC," only Seth Moulton could lead Harris/Warren, and Trump landed in a faction he wasn't even allowed to lead (couldn't be Party Leader; GM hand-traded him for Santorum). Fix: trim the STARTING draft to 2 ideologies/faction (an explicit ten-faction ideology ladder, POST 96/99). V (POST 100): 3 is fine for a ROOKIE (mid-game) draft but NOT a STARTING-year draft ‚Äî "there's no established baseline to temper the factions"; for 1960 they MANUALLY balanced starting pols. So the draft-ideology restriction is tighter at a cold boot (2 or manual seeding) than mid-game (3). Corroborates the CPU-draft holes (#114/DH-36): non-served pols leaked Charisma/Leadership (should be rare, the sub-floor rule); no CPU rule to prioritize sitting office-holders ("passed the sitting President"); too many pols with 2-3 command. Distinct from #171 (era-keyed on/off) ‚Äî this is start-of-game-vs-mid-game TIGHTNESS. Pairs with #4 (draft profile) + #171 (era-keyed toggle) + #115/#86 (boot) + #114/DH-36 (CPU draft) | Make the boot-time starting draft use a TIGHTER ideology restriction (2/faction) or manual seeding vs the mid-game rookie draft (3); fold into the scenario-boot pipeline (#115). Pairs with #4/#171/#115/#86 | ingested (**primary2020 ¬ß5 / POST 95, 96, 99-104; corroborates #114/DH-36 (non-served-pol leakage, no sitting-officeholder priority)**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b61) AUTHORITATIVE CPU-RULES CHANGELOG `b83f7fd0` ("CPU Rule Updates", @MrPotatoTed) SHARPENS the CPU off-ideology DRAFT gamble (a DIFFERENT axis than this row's cold-start ideology-COUNT restriction ‚Äî this is the CPU's per-pick ideology-MATCH gamble). NEW RULE (POST 1, verbatim): if the best-priority pol on the board is OFF the CPU's draft ideologies, the CPU takes them anyway only **25% of the time AND only if that off-ideology pol's PV is ‚â•25 points higher** than the CPU's best in-ideology available pol; else it drafts the in-ideology pol (its certain pick). The "25+ PV" threshold is the change (old rule grabbed an off-ideology pol even at equal/+1 PV, "high risk of blowing the pick"). ‚òÖ SHIPPED = 0% of this: `pickBestForFaction` (`phaseRunners.ts:33-53`) is a DETERMINISTIC score-max ‚Äî every pool pol scored `pvCache + (personalityIdeoMatch?25:0) + (eligIdeos.includes?50:0)`, take the MAX (`recordDraftPick`, `:55`); it never ROLLS a 25% chance and never checks a 25-PV gap; the +50 strict-ideology bonus applies **ONLY in the 1772 inaugural/expansion draft** (`isExpansion1772`, `:38-43,49`) so post-1772 drafts have NO ideology gate at all ("off-ideology" isn't even modeled). The gamble is a wiring/replacement over the existing seeded `chance` + PV cache, not new state. `b83f7fd0` POST 1, 5**) |
| 92 | **‚òÖ Eras-as-content-bands gated by game-state (NOT calendar year) ‚Äî MULTI-SAVE CONFIRMED across 1772/1800/1820/1948; TWO-LEVEL model** | `Era` is a 4-value enum (`independence/federalism/nationalism/modern`, types.ts:1337); era predicates are pure calendar ‚Äî `isDraftYear`/`isPresidentialYear` = `year % 4`, `isElectionYear` = `year % 2` (phases.ts:49‚Äì58); `shouldRunPhase` keys off the year | **THE headline architectural delta ‚Äî now the most-corroborated finding in the KB.** An "era" is a **fixed band of available bills/events/draftees + a state-bias table**, advanced by half-term at a **game-state boundary**, NOT by calendar year ‚Äî and **content is gated by TERRITORY OWNERSHIP**: bills/events/draftees for un-owned land are *invalid* (Indian Removal gated behind owning LA; Louisiana-born pols undraftable until LA owned). `rep1800`'s clock runs ~30 yrs behind its content (LA Purchase fires calendar 1834). **‚òÖ MULTI-SAVE CONFIRMED (batch 8):** the **1772-start `tea1772`** save and the **1800-start `rep1800`** save ‚Äî two fully-independent campaigns begun **28 in-game years apart** ‚Äî emit the **identical named era-bands at the same in-game dates** (Era of Federalists ~1800 ‚Üí Age of Republicanism ~1820 ‚Üí Era of Democracy ~1840 ‚Üí Era of Manifest Destiny ~1856 ‚Üí Era of Nationalism ~1868), each scored at its boundary. Transitions are game-state-gated, not annual (the CW band opens on an era event + DomStab/secession, not a date; Reconstruction ends piecemeal by legislation). The bands are **start-scenario-independent** (a 1772 start still closes Nationalism ~1868 on post-Reconstruction game-state). The forum's internal early eras (**Republicanism / Democracy / Manifest-Destiny**) are **sub-bands of the shipped `nationalism` enum**. **‚òÖ THIRD START CONFIRMED (batch 9, `nuke`):** the same engine runs in the MODERN half from a **1948 start**. **‚òÖ FOURTH START CONFIRMED (batch 10, `dem1820`):** a **1820 start** prints the identical per-faction draft-ideology bands ‚Äî "**Era of Democracy (1820-1840)**" preceding "**Manifest Destiny (1840-1856)**" (the early sub-bands of `nationalism`) ‚Äî so the model now holds across **1772 / 1800 / 1820 / 1948** starts. **‚òÖ TWO-LEVEL refinement (`nuke` settles a partial-digest conflict):** there are **two distinct mechanics**, an earlier extractor conflated them: **(a) point-banked Historical Eras** = the big content-bands (the original #92 mechanic): here **"Nuclear Age" = ONE era 1948‚Äì2000 ‚Üí "Era of Terror" 2000‚Äì~2012**, entered at a **2000 point-banking boundary** with explicit "now that we are in the Era of Terror‚Ä¶" rule deltas; the *transition trigger* is the calendar year hitting 2000, but the *content/scoring/rules* are era-gated. **(b) decade/census boundaries WITHIN an era** (1972/1980/1990) = a SEPARATE schedule-fired mechanic doing THREE things at once: bulk **EV reallocation** state-by-state, **wholesale state-bias re-lean** (Blue5..Tossup..Red5), and **content rotation** (draft pool / lobby cards / era-activated industries). **‚òÖ CORRECTION ‚Äî logged so it is not re-introduced: "Era of Neocons" / "Corporate Republicans" is a FACTION REBRAND (~1980‚Äì82) + a decade content-rotation label, NOT a point-banked band** (the shared sheet's tabs loosely name it, conflating decade-rotation with "era"; the digest is authoritative). So the design needs **finer-grained era bands than the 4-value enum**, a **two-level model (point-banked Historical Eras + per-decade census/content boundaries within each)**, AND a **game-state/territory-ownership era-advance condition** in place of `year % 4` | **Top-priority architecture row.** Decide: era bands gated by game-state + territory ownership (the design) vs the shipped `year % 4` predicates; sub-band the early eras under `nationalism` (Republicanism/Democracy/Manifest-Destiny) or add enum values; add a **territory-ownership content filter** on bills/events/draftees (pairs with #33/#43/#60 territory gating + BUG-1 era-event scheduling). | 247 | **‚òÖ EVENT-DRIVEN state party-lean realignment ‚Äî a state's base lean must shift when the CAUSAL game-state EVENT fires, NOT when the calendar reaches a hardcoded year/era tab; #92's TWIN on the state-lean/realignment axis ‚Äî NEW (`statelean`, `GM‚áíApp`)** | **Shipped `state.bias` is a single STATIC scalar** (`states1856.ts`, header "positive = Red lean, negative = Blue lean"; e.g. SC ‚àí2.4, VT 1.6) with **no decade/era bias schedule, no per-year party-modifier table** at all. The only in-game-dynamic nudges are (a) the ~30%/term governor drift `(governing‚àí1)*0.05` (`phaseRunners.ts:3389`) and (b) a region-keyed anytime-event `stateBias` effect **clamped to ¬±1, un-time-boxed** (`phaseRunners.ts:2608`, codebase-verified). `bias` also feeds pol ideology-drift (`:903-906`) + a ¬±3 on-ballot election term (`:3697` `baseLean`) ‚Äî so it IS the state-lean lever | **The forum's Excel census doc pins each state's party-modifier to a HARDCODED YEAR / "historical-era tab," which mis-fires when the timeline diverges. Canonical exhibit (POST 2/10/15): in the 1928 playtest the market crash never fired in-game (it's a ~50% chance event), so the meters were GOOD going into the 1930 midterms ‚Äî yet the year-keyed party modifiers still moved most states heavily BLUE "as if the Depression happened," so Democrats unrealistically SWEPT 1930 ("almost a red supermajority ‚Üí blue taking control"). The base lean had "nothing to justify moving to its decade set-points." Fix (broadly endorsed): tie each era's bias shift to a specific scripted EVENT, not to the year ‚Äî exactly the #92 thesis projected onto state-lean/realignment. Nuance: SOME shifts are genuinely gradual/decade-keyed (POST 5/15) ‚Üí design is PER-SHIFT (event-keyed vs gradual-decade-keyed). Keep DISTINCT from meters to avoid double-counting (POST 6/7/15 ‚Äî meters already drive national incumbent/ideology swings; the +1928 blue dominance was partly meters + the midterm ‚àí1-to-reds, which are NOT the bug). Design unresolved between bias-SETS-per-decade (POST 6, SELF-REJECTED POST 25 as arbitrary/un-modelable) and an industry-keyed time-boxed event modifier (POST 11/16/19/20, PREFERRED ‚Äî one base-lean set; an event applies e.g. "+1 blue for 10 yrs to all agriculture-leading states," leveraging the underutilized `industries` map; modifiers ON TOP of a decade/start-date base, POST 20). PROOF-OF-CONCEPT already in the census doc: Reconstruction differentiates bias "Reconstruction strong" vs "Hayes ended it" and "has generally worked well" (POST 32/33). **‚òÖ SUB-FINDING (couples to #92's politician-party constraint): landmark counterfactual events must re-key BOTH regional realignment AND the incoming-politician PARTY ASSIGNMENT** ‚Äî if Taft (Red Pres) ends Jim Crow in 1953, the South does NOT flip red in '64/'72 AND MLK (a 1953 Republican) STAYS Republican rather than party-switching to D (POST 30); the draft/party-assignment pipeline must be reactive to game events, not pinned to real-history dates (this is also why MrPotatoTed's "remove historical bias entirely" option was countered, POST 18/26 ‚Äî it desyncs hard-coded pol parties). Product rationale (POST 31): era-locked biases negate the game's counter-factual aspects (the explicit contrast with *The New Campaign Trail*'s rigidity). Pairs with #92 (era-as-content-band), #221 (scripted-event registry), #108 (realignment levers), #205 (Vic3 dynamic IG‚Üíparty realignment, parking-lot ‚Äî this is the near-term scoped version), #20 (governor actions also move `bias`) | **Build it EVENT-KEYED FROM THE START** (per #92) ‚Äî do NOT replicate the spreadsheet's year/era-tab pinning that caused the 1928 bug. (a) Add an era/decade base-lean schedule for `state.bias` PLUS an event-keyed realignment layer: a scripted-event effect that, on firing, applies a TIME-BOXED (e.g. 10-yr) modifier to a selected set of states (selected by region OR by `industries`), stacking on the base; (b) lift the shipped region `stateBias` effect (`:2608`) beyond its ¬±1 clamp / add expiry to make it a realignment, not a nudge; (c) make the draft/party-assignment pipeline read landmark-event flags so incoming pols' parties react to the played timeline; (d) keep the realignment input DISTINCT from the meter inputs (no double-count). | 108 | **‚òÖ The realignment is enforced mechanically but GRADUALLY (4 cooperating levers, NOT a single scripted flip); BLUE/RED meaning flips DURING the game** | Seed factions are static per scenario; BLUE/RED are fixed per scenario (factions1856.ts:19-22); no engine pressure resorts ideology over time | **The dominant strategic story of a 1948 campaign, and a resolution of an upstream "forced vs gradual" ambiguity.** The designer frames the ideological resorting as a **decade-by-decade gradual process** ("began in 1964, concluded in the early 1990s"), enforced by **four cooperating levers**: (1) **era-locked draft-pool ideology scarcity** ‚Äî the per-era rookie roster starves Cons/Trad/RW-Pop slots so factions drift Moderate ("Ted Cruz will be drafted Red regardless"); (2) **enthusiasm-gated one-directional disgruntled-pol party-flips** (conversion step 2.1.6) ‚Äî a pol auto-flips (25%) when its faction's enthusiasm hits **max the opposite way**; **high/max-enthusiasm factions are SHIELDED**; direction is one-way toward modern polarity; (3) **pinned party-preference** ‚Äî the GOP held at **Blue +3** for the realigning band so the sort has a stable gradient; (4) **per-decade CENSUS state-bias doc + a "Revision to Mean" centering damper** every Lingering phase. **‚òÖ BLUE/RED meaning FLIPS over the course of one game:** **Dixiecrats START INSIDE the BLUE party** (the segregationist far-right wing); a **transient mid-sort artifact ~1968‚Äì72** (Dems = party of the Center, GOP = both left AND right ‚Äî treat as transient, NOT a stable alternate alignment); **FULLY MODERN by ~1980** (Blue=left, Red=right). **Caveat:** "realignment done" applies to party LABELS; ideology-bloc loyalty stays only PARTLY modern (Moderates remain the swing bloc into the 2000s) | Implement the 4-lever realignment as an emergent engine process (NOT a scripted one-time flip): per-era draft-pool ideology scarcity (#4), enthusiasm-gated one-directional disgruntled party-flips with high-enthusiasm shielding (#76), a pinnable per-band party-preference baseline, and a per-decade census state-bias doc + Revision-to-Mean damper (#34/#50). Support BLUE/RED *meaning* changing within a single campaign. | 114 | **‚òÖ Design intent: the APP is SOLO (1-human-vs-9-CPU); the forum game is team MP ‚Äî the build is a solo adaptation; ‚òÖ the MANUAL CPU-faction burden is the load-bearing cost (2 1772 runs DIED of it)** ‚Äî NEW | Build IS already single-player (one `isPlayer` faction, 9 CPU) ‚Äî this row VALIDATES the shipped shape against design intent (most other rows are MP-vs-solo deltas) | **The strongest corpus statement of the digital app's intended mode.** The designer states the computer game is built for **1 human vs 9 CPU** because human multiplayer "goes off the rails"; the points system is for CPUs + enthusiasm, not humans (low points = better draft order, so humans could tank it). A full modern turn (2022 midterm: elections ‚Üí age rolls ‚Üí gov-stat gains ‚Üí conversions ‚Üí kingmakers ‚Üí leadership votes ‚Üí cabinet confirmation ‚Üí ambassadors ‚Üí events) ran CLEANLY without stalling or looping [posts 129-380] = direct charter evidence that hands-off modern resolution WORKS and produces plausible outcomes (net R+1 Senate 51-49, House R+33 246-189, judged realistic; CPU loses gracefully). Pairs with #110 (the ~25-subphase loop) + #208 (CPU legislative logic) + E9/K5**; **‚òÖ (b43) the THESIS for the browser port stated outright by the designer + corroborated by manual-sim friction: `ddd179cb` (playtester survey) MrPotatoTed Q10 ‚Äî "I'd make it into a computer game so I didn't have to CPU all the time"; "Processing Lingering" named the most time-consuming/boring GA chore (matthewyoung123 + jnewt, Q8). `06fbb2e5` (Lars-Ark 1772) shows the bookkeeping load live ‚Äî mis-run proposer order (CPU-random instead of descending-Legis, POST 33/36), bad eligibility picks (POST 64), a stalled CPU 2.1.5 relocate phase (POST 20, transient) ‚Äî exactly the CPU-AI handlers the app must own. ‚òÖ Q9 CROWN JEWELS (record once here ‚Äî the port must NOT automate these away): playtesters' near-unanimous strengths = the **legislative/Congress phase (proposal‚Üíexecutive action) + primaries + elections** (the most legible/trusted resolution); preserve their depth/legibility. `ddd179cb` Q8/Q9/Q10 (POST 2, 9, 11, 13, 14, 20, 21, 22); `06fbb2e5` POST 20, 33, 36, 64**; **‚òÖ (b58) `eb573c91` (1928 DUO) is one more witness that by-hand CPU-faction operation is the load-bearing cost: the duo started with the other 8 seats on CPU (POST 1) but the CPU burden was felt and REJECTED mid-thread (Zagnut POST 9: "much more fun if you dropped the CPU rules and just run it normally as human players"), and the table switched to a "Mixed Doubles" human-cluster split (#1) to avoid CPU-ing ‚Äî the same CPU-rules-must-be-automatable pressure as #296 and the GA-burnout finding, confirming the solo app must own the CPU-faction AI (#70-#78). `eb573c91` POST 1, 9, 14-16**) + codebase (scenario1772.ts:64, scenario1856.ts:121 ‚Äî single-player today) |

This pins the demographic-tagging schema + tiering structure + lowest-score routing the build's #115 must implement. ampuUpdates ¬ß3 / POST 4, 14, 39, 45, 46, 62**; **‚òÖ CORROBORATED + SHARPENED CPU autodraft HEURISTIC (batch 31, `playtestdraft`): two concrete, citable draft-automation specs the build's boot/#115 + the CPU-draft cluster (#114/DH-36) need ‚Äî (a) PREFERENCE-LIST autodraft = a player privately submits names OR "preferred skills, ideologies, etc." and the GM "use[s] the list as if you were automating the draft" (the automation-of-PICKING core, in use throughout: POST 1, 19, 39, 48-50, 62, 79, 92, 105, 124, 140, 150, 158, 208); (b) ‚òÖ CPU autodraft = roll a D10 ‚Üí take the Nth-highest-PV-ranked available pol of that party ("if you roll an 8, give me the 8th highest ranking politician in that party," POST 132; in use POST 14/18/29/53/76/108/147) ‚Äî i.e. PV-rank + a small random offset, the within-party CPU draft heuristic. NB this is the WITHIN-PARTY top-of-pool variant; the rulebook ¬ßB POST 3 CPU rule is "picks one of the TOP 10 available, prefers one matching its ideology card else random among the top 10" ‚Äî reconcile the two (D10-offset vs top-10-by-ideology-card) as the CPU draft spec. Distinct from the #115 GENERATE-a-candidate backfill ‚Äî this is automation of PICKING, not CREATING. playtestdraft POST 1, 14, 132; rulebook ¬ßB POST 3**; **‚òÖ batch 32 ‚Äî NEW SUB-ROW (folds HERE, NOT a new top-level gap): a CURATED "WHAT-IF" draft-pool TOGGLE at game setup, distinct from this row's PROCEDURAL backfill. `georgemartha` POST 1/6/14/20: Lars added "the most realistic of the what-if special draft pols‚Ä¶ a bunch of women, Toussaint Louverture, and a few more" ‚Äî real people who never served the ACTUAL US (women barred by suffrage; Louverture is Haitian) added to the DRAFTABLE dataset for alt-history flavor (Toussaint drafted to the American Whigs, persists as a real draftee). The closest shipped analog is `ERA_FIGURES` (CLAUDE.md: founding-era notables who never served Congress, additive-only, sub-floor stats) ‚Äî this thread argues that pool should be a **setup-time opt-in toggle that EXPLICITLY includes women + non-US figures**, not just male non-Congressmen. Ties #238 (the gender-eligibility model that makes drafted women PLAYABLE). So: a "what-if draft pool" boot toggle, separate from the procedural #115 backfill, layered on the existing `ERA_FIGURES` mechanism. georgemartha (0500fa72) POST 1, 6, 14, 20; pairs with #24 (draft/faction pool) + #238 (gender) + the `ERA_FIGURES` mechanism per CLAUDE.md**; **‚òÖ (b41) TWO threads touch the name-DB + what-if pool. `964b8857` (Future SC Cases) is a NEW CONSUMER of this row's human-name DB: the procedural SC-case GENERATOR (NEW #270) composes `<human-name> v. <state>` cases from the SAME #115 name DB + a small state-name DB ‚Äî the name DB now feeds candidate-gen AND case-gen. `4e518e05` (Historical Relocation) corroborates that alt-state / what-if start-state is mutable pol data: Boris Johnson is tagged a "what if politician" (NYC-born variant, POST 8/9) and de Valera floated as an NYC-born what-if (POST 7/10) ‚Äî minor reinforcement of the what-if pool, no new requirement. 964b8857 (see #270); 4e518e05 POST 6, 7, 8, 9, 10**; **‚òÖ (b42) `d474f718` (What-If Politicians) is the MAY-2022 source of the what-if POOL content this row's toggle draws from: vcczar's canonical ~24-name list (JFK Jr, Joseph P Kennedy Jr, Napoleon, Marx, Lafayette, Emperor Norton, Churchill, Nathan Hale, Quentin/Tad/Willie/Philip-Hamilton-line, MLK, Malcolm X, ‚Ä¶) + community RADICAL suggestions (eveofanticreation: Jim Jones, Vito Marcantonio, Ted Bundy, Cotton-Ed Smith, Rankin ‚Äî "three, possibly four already in the game," all scandal-prone) + SPARSE ahistorical-state pols (~20-25 Canadians, 5-10 Brazilians ‚Äî volunteer-fill-later, low priority). Route to `scripts/seedDataset.mjs` `ROWS`/`ERA_FIGURES` with sub-floor stats (the alt-history extension of `ERA_FIGURES`; dataset side on #240). ‚òÖ OPEN ‚Äî the SPECULATIVE-DRAFT RULES are explicitly undefined: vcczar "I need to make draft rules for this" (how a draftable-but-maybe-never-playable pol is graded/presented, pick-cost, whether the CPU drafts one). The novel ENTRY mechanic (scripted-event playability) is broken out as NEW #272. d474f718 POST 1, 9, 10-14**; **‚òÖ (b56) `b15af728` (Jun 2025) CORROBORATES this boot-procedure gap from the start-PHASE angle: "the rules never state how to start a post-1772 game or which phases get skipped because people are already in office" (POST 1) ‚Äî the same missing setup spec, now scoped to *which phase the turn enters* (option A=2.4 / B=2.1 / C=election; team leans B). The full start-phase detail (the A/B/C decision, the shipped-1856-hybrid, the 2.4‚Üíno-Faction-Leader-turn-1 gap, and the start-date-sheet seat errors) lives on the boot-shape row #86. `b15af728` POST 1**) + codebase (scenario1772.ts, scenario1856.ts ‚Äî hand-authored, no generic boot) |
| 186 | **‚òÖ Late-era / mid-cycle scenario boot must be DETERMINISTIC (correct year-incumbents+offices, auto-elected Party Leader, retro-applied fatigue, seeded delegate weights+calendar, back-dated career tracks) ‚Äî the 1960 boot was entirely improvised** ‚Äî NEW (`redbutton`; extends #115/#86/#187) | Two hand-authored early scenarios only; no generic boot; #115 captures the boot-procedure gap from an 1820 start, #86 the boot-data shape | The 1960 MP is the **first MODERN-era boot in the corpus and the cleanest late-start failure case.** The 1960 scenario was authored for the **1960-62 governance turn (JFK president)** but players forced an **Elections-Phase start one turn earlier** ‚Äî exposing every hole a late/mid-cycle boot must fill deterministically: **(a)** offices/incumbents were set for *post*-election 1961, so a pre-election start had mass-wrong jobs + a Senate seeded **50-50** vs real **66-D Jan-1960** ‚Üí a full mid-phase re-do of rep/sen/gov picks (POST 543-560); **(b)** no Blue **Party Leader** existed (game requires the Leadership trait) ‚Üí GM hand-picked LBJ (POST 73); **(c)** the **party-leader on-election rolls** (+Command / lose-Obscure / +3-primaries / 3-term party-pref penalty) were **skipped** for the seeded leader (POST 931-938); **(d)** the **party-fatigue penalty** never applied (see NEW #187); **(e)** governor terms-served unknowable ‚Üí blanket "assume 2 two-year terms / ex-govs 8 yrs" (POST 64); **(f)** primary delegate weights + primary calendar had to be hand-assigned (POST 73, 79-80); **(g)** DC EVs invalid until 1961 (POST 84-87); **(h)** mid-cycle start = **NO Command-earning runway** ‚Üí much of the roster is frozen out of high office, players asked to **back-date the last 2-4 draft classes into career tracks** (POST 522, 528 ‚Äî the DH-25 fix from a modern angle). **‚òÖ Designer verdict on era-changing a started game:** Ted ‚Äî **eras are "the stoniest" thing in the game**, can't be retrofitted at the press of a button (every bill/event/bonus is manual + research-bound), so a clean per-year boot table is the only path (POST 528) | Author a deterministic late/mid-era boot that, for any chosen start year: (a) sets correct incumbents+offices+chamber balances for that exact year (with a verifier), (b) auto-elects a valid Party Leader + runs the on-election rolls, (c) retro-applies party-fatigue (#187), (d) seeds delegate weights + the primary calendar, (e) back-dates recent draft classes into career tracks (#86/DH-25) so the modern roster isn't frozen, (f) handles year-keyed EV validity (DC 1961). Consolidates the boot cluster #86/#115/#187 from the modern era | ingested (**redbutton ¬ß1 / POST 64, 73, 84-87, 522, 528, 543-560, 645, 931-938**; **‚òÖ batch 29 ‚Äî `wilsons1916` is the START-AT-ERA-BEGINNING TEST that answers #186's mid-cycle question, and BOTH new threads converge on the same boot lesson: METHODOLOGY CONVERGENCE ‚Äî start at an era's BEGINNING, not its edges. `wilsons1916` opened with the explicit decision that "starting at the BEGINNING of an era, rather than the middle, was better‚Ä¶ due to the issues in 1960" (P1, the deliberate fix to this row's 1960 failure) ‚Üí it booted cleanly into a normal post-election 2.1 sequence and ran a full term + the 1918 midterm (the catastrophic mid-cycle improvisation was AVOIDED), AND the 1918 election-scorer map was designer-praised as plausible ("Maybe we finally got the formula right?‚Ä¶ given the +3 and the era biases," P448 ‚Äî a POSITIVE datapoint on the post-redbutton scorer nerf, #184). BUT starting at the beginning did NOT eliminate boot defects ‚Äî it exposed a NARROWER class: the **1916 BootSheet is "bare"** ‚Äî "the start era for 1916 is pretty bare," with **NO starting generals/admirals** (the SecWar had to appoint the entire officer corps from scratch, while "the other start eras all have starting generals," P149-157 ‚Üí see DH-75 military-appointment + #209 cohort backfill) and **start-date credit/debt are admitted "filler numbers"** (vcczar zeroes US-debt-to-UK/France by fiat, P340-345). `welcome2future` confirms the INVERSE edge ‚Äî a 2022 start at the TAIL of the Populism band hit thin scripted-event density ("only one scripted event capable of occurring right now ‚Äî that's what I get for starting a game at the tail end of an era," POST 378). So the era-boundary lesson is two-sided: BOTH a mid-cycle/late-era start AND a tail-of-band start are thin-content friction zones, and even a clean era-OPENING start needs a fully-populated per-year BootSheet (officers + real economic-meter seeds, not filler). Pairs with #173 (era-boundary-aligned starts)**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ batch 35 ‚Äî `redbutton1960` (3c3c46f0, the RESTART of the `redbutton`/8bc0231c 1960 run) is THE CLEANEST LIVE PROOF that #186 is not cosmetic ‚Äî it gates whether the meter engine works at all: the original 1960 run was STOPPED because the cabinet used STARTER-LEVEL stats ("especially Admin‚Ä¶ all at starter levels and it tanked all the meters since we didn't have any Cabinet members above 3 (and very few at 3)," POST 1). The fix was a manual whole-cabinet RE-STAT to mid/peak (now one 5-Admin, four 4's, rest 3's "no more 2's!!"; ambassadors a 5 [Kissinger] + four 4's; a Key Advisor 2‚Üí4) and a re-derivation of the meters before the era was playable again (POST 1-2). This is the #179 lingering-engine failure reproduced at WHOLE-CABINET scale ‚Äî a deterministic late/mid-era boot MUST seed cabinet/ambassador/advisor officers at era-appropriate mid/peak strength, not draftee starter levels, or the #179 engine drags every meter into crisis at turn 1. Strongest single datapoint that "correct stats for the year" is load-bearing (mirrors `wilsons1916`'s "1916 BootSheet is bare / filler economic numbers"). The per-era cabinet-strength seed is still an OPEN Q ‚Äî the mid/peak reset was eyeballed by the GM. redbutton1960 POST 1, 2**; **‚òÖ (b48) `3b46b086` (1960-playtest question, the ROOT-CAUSE thread UPSTREAM of 8bc0231c + 3c3c46f0) is the cleanest statement of WHY this boot must seed correct stats AND supplies the data model behind it: the boot "used the stats Cal started the playtest with ‚Ä¶ midway and peak stats were NOT added" so "everyone is starting with rookie stats" (POST 5-7); the concrete back-fill rule keyed to draft-class AGE = **‚â•16 career-yrs ‚Üí MAXED/peak, 8-15 ‚Üí MIDWAY, <8 ‚Üí rookie** (Vols21 POST 9), applied ONLY to pre-game draft classes (in-game drafts develop via the shipped career track, POST 7-8). The underlying 3-tier stat data model (per-politician `peakYear` + rookie/midway/historic-peak tiers) that the shipped `Politician` (single `skills` block) CANNOT represent is broken out as NEW **#289** ‚Äî this row remains the boot PROCEDURE that consumes it. Playtesters voted mid-course-correction over restart 8-4 (POST 15-18) yet a full restart (3c3c46f0) happened anyway ‚Üí evidence the boot must be correct the first time. `3b46b086` POST 5-9, 15-18**; **‚òÖ (b56) `b15af728` (Jun 2025) adds the START-PHASE-ENTRY axis + a DATA-QUALITY bar to this deterministic-boot row. (1) the post-1772 boot must decide which phase turn-1 ENTERS (A=2.4 cabinet-confirmed / B=2.1 post-election-pre-cabinet / C=election; team leans B, POST 1-9) ‚Äî the deterministic boot's phase axis (full detail on #86). (2) ‚òÖ the per-era start-date sheet the BootSheet would canonicalize is KNOWN to contain incumbent/seat ERRORS ‚Äî e.g. Senate seats marked "vacant" that don't actually vacate until after the midterms, plus "other little inconsistencies" found while building an 1820 template (POST 1) ‚Äî so any data-driven BootSheet built from that sheet inherits them: a seat/incumbent-correctness pass is a precondition. The shipped 1856 boot sidesteps it by assigning senators algorithmically (`scenario1856.ts:61-89`). `b15af728` POST 1**) |
| 187 | **Party-fatigue penalty (6-yr party-leader decay forcing ~8-yr presidential rotation) is undocumented in game-context + was silently dropped by the mid-cycle 1960 start** ‚Äî NEW (`redbutton`) | Not in the build; not previously logged in game-context | A **deliberate balance lever** Ted surfaced: once one person has been **Party Leader for 6 years**, a fatigue penalty kicks in (strengthening the longer they hold it) **specifically to make parties trade the presidency every ~8 years** as they do historically. (Distinct from the per-term-after-3rd ‚àí1 party-preference in #15/the party-leader rolls.) In the 1960 MP it would have hit RED ~1958 (8 yrs of Eisenhower-era GOP) but **1958 wasn't played, so the penalty never applied** ‚Äî a factor in RED's unrealistic down-ballot landslide (Ted: "We probably should have incorporated it anyway, but didn't think of it in the moment," POST 645). This is both a **mechanic to build** and a **boot requirement** (#186 must retro-apply it at a mid-cycle start) | Implement the 6-yr+ party-leader fatigue decay (a growing penalty designed to rotate the presidency ~every 8 yrs); ensure the scenario-boot retro-applies it based on the seeded leader's tenure-to-date. ### Legislative (committees, bills, crises, budget)

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | **Committee block-and-replace + chair-blocks-one + repeating-bill auto-block** | Bills proposed ‚Üí committee ‚Üí floor (phases 2.6.1‚Äì2.6.3). No block-and-replace | Committee chair can **block and/or replace** a bill from their own committee's domain (Block alone kills it); some bills can't be blocked. Chair eligibility requires prior committee service (`modern`: **Leadership OR Legis ‚â• 2**). Bills marked with a star/`##` are block-eligible. **`pop` confirms a clean chair-blocks-ONE rule** (POST 487-490): chair may BLOCK exactly **one proposal** from the slate **before** the vote, then the committee votes on the rest. Also: **repeating-bill committee auto-block** ‚Äî if the same legislation is proposed twice in the same cycle, the Chairman auto-blocks the duplicate (`pop` POST 712-714) | Add committee-chair "block-and-replace" action with same-committee replacement check + chair-eligibility gate + **chair-blocks-exactly-one-pre-vote** + duplicate-bill auto-block. (**6+ era confirmed**; pervasive ‚Äî `hd` sharpens the rule: a chair may replace only a bill whose **proposer has LESS Legislative AND lacks Efficient**; `pop` confirms in fresh-modern boot) | ingested (gilded 160-163; fed 160, 209, 305, 380, 563, 723; modern 46, 285, 1312, 1870; **hd 195-203, 469, 5123**; **pop 487-490, 712-714**) |
| 9 | **Bill packaging / bundling** | Each bill votes separately | Chair can package multiple committee bills into one floor vote that passes/fails as one; **packages can't be split-voted** (`modern` 1341). Federalism nuance: a chair **won't bundle** a net-negative bill *unless* it's a statehood bill; CPU bundles ~75%. Used as a weapon (`modern`: pair a poison-pill with a desired bill so both die). **`pop` reconfirms** in a fresh-modern boot: after committee votes, chair **packages passing bills together OR leaves them separate** (POST 502-507) ‚Äî load-bearing weaponization, **4 unrelated GOP bills jammed into Bill #3** to amortize sign/veto cost (POST 507, 542) | Add packaging + vote aggregation; bundling-eligibility rule; no-split-vote constraint. (**4-era confirmed**) | ingested (gilded 176-179, 188, 194; fed 160, 213, 564-565; 1772s 18, 46, 57; modern 60, 297, 1327, 1341, 2004; **pop 502-507, 542**) |
| 10 | **Filibuster + cloture + Iron-Fist-SML auto-54-vote** | No filibuster | A senate floor leader / eligible senator can filibuster a bill or package; filibustered bills die *or* return next term. Federalism: a **standing rule toggled ON by a law** ("Institute Filibuster", 1792); trait-gated (a Puritan senator). `modern` adds: gate is **Puritan + Legislative > 1** (**not a roll** ‚Äî you qualify or not); **Disharmonious filibusters twice**; SML calls **Cloture**, which needs **~67%** (ties sustain the filibuster). **`drums` sharpens to "deterministic per-Senator" (no roll)**: specific named pols (Yulee, Morgan, Dorr, Cockrell, Simmons, Sweet) filibuster automatically per trait; **CPUs filibuster crisis legis they ideologically oppose to extend the crisis to election day** (POST 7081); **Iron-Fist CPU Maj Leader auto-cloture** for majority items (POST 5920). **`pop` confirms named-Senator filibuster + 60-vote cloture in fresh modern boot** (POST 519, 521): Rand Paul, Tom Coburn, Mike Lee, Ilhan Omar are can-filibuster; Omar filibustered Bill 2 (Stand Your Ground + Federal Prisons); needed 60 for cloture, got 58 ‚Üí bill killed. **`pop` sharpens Iron-Fist-SML floor effect to "auto-54-votes"** (POST 523): McConnell (5 legis + Iron Fist) passed everything else unilaterally ‚Äî Iron-Fist SML guarantees ~54 yes votes (sharpens ¬ß25.9 / #77). Iron-Fist players ALSO publish conditional vote-rules (POST 1111): *"McConnell will vote NAY to all Libs, Progs, and LW Pops with less than 3 Admin, AYE to everyone else"* ‚Äî IF controllers issue data-driven vote policies | Add `Filibuster` action (one per faction w/ eligible senator); unlocked-by-law gate; `Cloture` counter-action w/ supermajority threshold; filibustered bills persist; **deterministic per-Senator dispatch + CPU-Maj-Leader-IF auto-cloture + Iron-Fist-SML auto-54-vote floor effect + IF-controller conditional vote-rule data structure**. (**6+ era cpu-confirmed** ‚Äî `hd`: Disharmonious filibusters twice; filibustered bills carry to next session and must re-pass BOTH chambers; no Cloture until the Cloture bill passes, ‚Öî. **Open: package re-filibuster** ‚Äî Tyler "rules actually don't say" POST 3275) | ingested (gilded 189-194; fed 159, 566, 725, 730; modern 72-91, 1355, 1598, 2015; **hd 222, 242, 498-501, 3500-3502, 5167, 6205, 8166-8167**; **drums 48, 140, 2716, 2871, 3115, 3273, 3275, 4610, 4751, 5103-5105, 5494, 5496, 5920, 5921, 6554, 7081**; **pop 519, 521, 523, 1111**) |
| 11 | **Crisis Bills + national-crisis state** | `Crisis Admin/Gov` traits exist (types.ts:85-86) but no crisis-bill tag and no national-crisis state | Bills tagged `*Crisis Bill*` *resolve* an active national crisis and **bypass the spending cap**; the nation enters/exits named crises by meter movement. Crisis taxonomy is **era-dependent** (fed: Budget/Economy/DomStab/HonestGov; gilded adds Anti-Naturalization/-Native/-Chinese). `modern` adds **collective accountability**: if a chamber lets *most* crisis bills die, the chamber's controlling party loses Party Pref (or it blames the President, unless a Charismatic save) | Add `Crisis` model + `bill.resolvesCrisis` link + crisis-bypasses-budget rule + crisis-bill collective-accountability penalty; meter-derived crisis entry/exit. (corroborated across 4 eras) | ingested (gilded 160, 164, 167, 176; fed 39, 159, 166, 372, 477, 480; modern 32, 46, 943, 1597, 2015; **arkzag ‚Äî named CRISIS states fire from meter thresholds across the full arc (EconStab "CRISIS" multi-half-term = a Panic-of-1837 analog, ch17 POST 402, ch19 POST 2005; HonestGov CRISIS, ch17 POST 424) and meter-crater Anytime-Evos fire below thresholds ("Economic Collapse" EconStab 3, "Govt Overthrown" DomStab 7, ch27); the marquee CRISIS bills are "Establish Bank of the US" (filibustered/returns, ch11/ch20) and "Create a non-partisan independent treasury" which REPLACES the Bank under Pres Dudley (ch33 POST 106). ‚òÖ crisis-bill-FAILURE scoring rule: ‚àí100pts is WAIVED if the bill conflicts with the faction's ideology ‚Üí instead +1 enthusiasm (ch24 POST 1993-1994)**; **nixon1972 ‚Äî a 1972-start opens IN a named DOMESTIC-STABILITY CRISIS (anti-Vietnam-War protests, POST 1) tracked as a DomStab meter that EXITS the crisis by meter movement ("Dom Stab 6 (+1) no longer in crisis," POST 350) ‚Äî corroborates crisis = meter-derived named state in the modern era**; **‚òÖ to-do ¬ßLegislative POST 812 ‚Äî DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE: a Pres with the **Crisis Manager** trait controlling BOTH chambers lets the relevant crisis committee make **DOUBLE proposals** [models FDR/Lincoln legislative floods] ‚Äî a crisis-bill packaging lever [#42/#144]. fixes ¬ßH POST 8 ‚Äî the "majority-of-crisis-bills-pass ‚Üí no blame" rule [Ark, OPEN ‚Äî Ted+Will agreed to change]. completions ¬ßLegislation POST 29, 30, 34, 144 ‚Äî DESIGNER-DONE/UNVERIFIED: many modern Legis Props added [student-debt-forgiveness Pres Actions 10k/50k/all; 3rd "Rebuild Infrastructure" = the Biden IIJA marked passed-2021; 4 "Curb Inflation" bills modeled on the 2022 IRA; ~11 "Adam-Ruins-Everything"-style bills] ‚Äî corroborates the post-2020 modern band [#169]**) |
| 12 | **Bill scoring uses *all* faction cards; failed bills also score; sign triggers ideology + lobby + meter simultaneously** | Mixed; needs audit | Legislation scores *every* ideology/lobby/interest card on each faction (opposite cards net toward 0), not just the leader's `ideology`; 1772s adds the **staged tabulation method**. `modern` confirms **failed bills also score** (reverse effects + meter moves) and that scoring also awards **¬±1 to named pols' reelection**. **`pop` sharpens to "single-event simultaneous resolution"** (POST 539, 542): each signed bill triggers **points to ideologies (¬±50/100/150/250/350) + meter ticks (Planet Health, Economic Stability) + lobby points ("+100 for High Tech", "+50 for Human Rights") in ONE resolution event**, not sequential phases | Verify scorer iterates all three card decks + scores failed bills + applies per-pol reelection deltas + **all three score categories (ideology + lobby + meter) fire simultaneously per signed bill**. Still 0% shipped. d4cd2ee6 POST 1, 2; beb258f9 POST 1, 3, 6, 9, 10**; **‚òÖ (b46) `9f2ab25f` (Environment) = ANOTHER genre instance + the canonical genre-OVERLAP case. The genre carries the framework's signature shapes: a federal‚Üîstate authority `*-Default`-flavored axis ("States handle matters concerning environment and conservation" vs the federal acts), opposed Pres-Action poles (Energy-over-Environment ‚Üî Environment-over-Energy), and the regulate-sector-X family (transportation/airlines/auto/construction/mining for clean air). ‚òÖ vcczar's OWN overlap flag (POST 1): *"some things that should be here might be under 'Regulations.' No matter"* ‚Äî the canonical Environment‚ÜîRegulations multi-genre case (the regulate-sector-X axis lives in BOTH), do NOT double-count the regulate-industry rows. ‚òÖ OPEN genre-boundary Q (carry): is **Energy** its own genre distinct from Environment or one combined genre? ‚Äî the entire fossil-fuel/EV/pipeline/drilling/grid/nuclear-thorium-hydrogen cluster straddles an Energy genre + Regulations; the opposed Pres-Action pair implies Energy‚ÜîEnvironment are two POLES of one axis, but the content lists treat them together. 9f2ab25f POST 1, 2, 5**; **‚òÖ (b47) FOUR MORE genres extend the corpus (the framework remains OPEN-ENDED, NOT closed): `d62ea397` (Infrastructure, UNTAGGED 3-partition; L‚âà28 / P=1 / G=4), `1bf19872` (Taxation, UNTAGGED; L‚âà37 / P=2 / G=3 ‚Äî strongly STATEFUL: nearly every row mutates a persistent fiscal regime [a tax exists / its rate / brackets / loopholes] that later rows modify, the canonical program-state case), `5d7c29ee` (Slavery, UNTAGGED; L‚âà24 / P=3 / G=2 ‚Äî the MOST era- AND subsystem-COUPLED genre yet: its "state" is not a national meter but a **per-state slavery FLAG feeding the secession subsystem**, see NEW #288), and ‚òÖ `de64de1a` (Currency) ‚Äî the **prereq-STRUCTURED exemplar**: authored organized BY PREREQUISITE with each item carrying an action-type (L/P) + era/ideology tag, so it is the **model for the catalog SHAPE** a generic policy system must support (`{actionType, era-tag, precondition: Predicate, effects}`). Still 0% shipped: `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has no `subtype`/`policyGenre`/`prereq` field; the whole library is 8 generic `BILL_TEMPLATES` (`phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429`). Need a data-driven `{actionType, era-tag, precondition, effects}` catalog ‚Äî Currency is its reference shape. d62ea397 POST 1; 1bf19872 POST 1; 5d7c29ee POST 1; de64de1a POST 1**; **‚òÖ (b48) `391df03b` (Credit/Debt, vcczar) = the 2nd prereq-STRUCTURED subtype confirming the catalog SHAPE ‚Äî same `L/P` action-type + era/ideology suffix (Ind/Fed/Prog) + prereq tree as Currency, with a `*-Default` baseline ("No National Credit/Debt", L-Ind-Default), a 2-deep policy CHAIN (Funding-Act ‚Üí Emergency-Foreign-Loan), and both `P` items Federalist-era executive debt moves (Hamiltonian finance was executive-driven, like Currency's L/P-tracks-real-enactment). ‚òÖ ADDS a **THIRD action type ‚Äî Constitutional AMENDMENT** (Balanced Budget Amendment, POST 10) + a **state Gov-action version** of the same (extends the L/P split ‚Üí actionType ‚àà {Congress-bill, President-action, Amendment, Governor-action}). Designer RULINGS (genre boundary): Lend-Lease routed to the **Diplomacy** genre, NOT Credit/Debt (POST 8); filial/personal-debt = an early **repealable** law only, never with real US support (POST 7). Novel low-priority effect/lever classes: sell-territory-for-debt-reduction (couples to expansionism #4e76e6c3), an eligibility-toggling law ("unbalanced budget ‚Üí no Congressional reelection", POST 11), a debt-ceiling EVENT (reached ‚Üí expand/keep/reduce, POST 10). Still 0% shipped (`Legislation` has no `subtype`/prereq field; the catalog itself is unbuilt). Currency + Credit/Debt = the two worked exemplars. 391df03b POST 1, 7, 8, 10**; **‚òÖ (b49) TWO more genres extend the count + confirm TWO authoring styles coexist. `5261c8ff` (Bailouts) = the **3rd prereq-tree exemplar** (after Currency/Credit-Debt) in the IDENTICAL tagged shape (default node ‚Üí no-prereq nodes ‚Üí prereq-grouped nodes, each tagged action-type + era/ideology) ‚Äî three independent threads in the same shape strongly imply a uniform `PolicyNode { id, actionType, era-or-ideology tag, prereq: Predicate, effects }` model is the target; vcczar intends to scale it to a **per-industry bailout bill** for every game industry (POST 5, against the `PolicyArea` enum `types.ts:183-189`) + accepts disaster-area-declaration‚Üífederal-rebuild-funds as bailout content (POST 4). `faf2d8b3` (Drugs) = a genre in the OTHER (flat 3-partition, UNTAGGED) style ‚Äî vcczar EXPLICITLY drops eras + prereqs "to save time" (POST 1) ‚Äî so the corpus now has BOTH a tagged-prereq-tree authoring style AND a flat-3-partition style, and the schema-tagging pass is still deferred for Drugs. ‚òÖ Drugs surfaces NEW sub-models the framework lacks: a **substance-class enum (‚â•3 buckets** ‚Äî marijuana / hard drugs / **psychedelics-as-separate**, POST 2; + tobacco/alcohol/e-cigarettes/virtual-drugs), a **decriminalize TIER** (a distinct middle pole between legalize and criminalize, POSTs 8-9), **county/local-option + dry-by-default states** (KS/MS/TN dry-by-default, AL local-option, POST 4 ‚Äî a SUB-STATE granularity the build has no model for), and a **policy‚Üícourt-case TRIGGER** (religious-ritual-exemption Gov-Action ‚Üí free-exercise challenge, POST 3 ‚Äî ties the courts/SCOTUS-case system). 5261c8ff POST 1, 4, 5;
