2nd-Am‚Üîban-ownership), a state-scope mirror (Gov-Actions arm-teachers / restrictive-laws mirror the federal rows), and program-state gates (shield-in-force; weapon-tech-tier prereqs). ‚òÖ DESIGN-ASSERTED-SHIPPED, build-UNVERIFIED discrepancy logged: vcczar asserts "leave gun laws to the states" ALREADY EXISTS in the game (POST 6, "The game has that") ‚Äî but the build has NO gun-policy devolution lever; the only "states-rights" construct in `src/` is the `StatesRights` **ideology card** on factions (`types.ts:323`, `factions1772.ts:16`, `factions1856.ts:5-6`), a faction ideology TAG, not a gun-specific (or any policy-genre) devolution toggle. vcczar refers to the master design list / federalism framing, NOT shipped Guns content ‚Äî confirming the whole stateful policy-genre framework (incl. its devolution `*-Default` baselines) is 0% shipped. `c2bea165` (National Security) likewise realizes the genre with a "Leave National Security to the states" devolution pole and a fund-up‚Üîfund-down modifier lever, 0% shipped. 067233f3 POST 1, 6; NB tariff/economic meters gate legislation (no tariff change until 1836, POST 928)**; **dem1820 ‚Äî a GA published a concrete enthusiasm-processing rule (POST 575, 618): non-enthusiasm meters MOVE the per-ideology enthusiasm boxes, which then apply to EVERY state UNLESS a state penalizes that ideology, with a hard ¬±3 cap on ideology+party-pref (state-specific bonuses uncapped); ‚òÖ this is a GA-vs-designer model FORK ‚Äî Ted's "every state" diverges from V's "ideology-leaning states only" and Matt's "primaries only" (POST 569); the build must pick. Published "State of the Meters" table POST 618**; **terror2000 ‚Äî ‚òÖ FORK RESOLVED to V's CANONICAL 2-LAYER model (Ted, the DESIGNER, reversed his OWN interpretation to V's 2022 intent, POST 913-926): (a) the top/non-enthusiasm meters apply a FLAT UNIVERSAL per-ideology modifier to ALL candidates of that ideology, BOTH parties, in primary AND general (concrete modern table: Rev-Budget‚ÜíLib +1/Trad ‚àí1; Honest-Gov‚ÜíCorrupt-incumbent +1, Integrity-vs-Controversial-challenger +1, LW/RW-Pop ‚àí2, Prog ‚àí1; QoL‚ÜíCons/Trad +1, LW-Pop/Prog ‚àí1; Planet‚ÜíRW-Pop +1, Prog ‚àí1); (b) per-ideology ENTHUSIASM is a SEPARATE per-party (per-state-bias) bonus layered on top ‚Äî NOT the meters moving enthusiasm. This matches the `dem1820` "every-state-unless-penalized" reading for the universal layer and pins enthusiasm as the second per-party layer. Build this 2-layer map**; **ideo1928 ‚Äî the 2-layer model exercised LIVE at the PRESIDENTIAL level in the 1932 Hoover-v-Roosevelt general: the Depression's universal econ-crisis penalty (‚àí1/‚àí3 incumbent) + cabinet-region-snub penalties + Party-Pref-maxed-Blue enthusiasm layer all STACK against Hoover [POST 1224, ch17]; "State of the Meters"-style per-cycle enthusiasm/party-pref publishing [POST 481]; ‚òÖ GA balance flag ‚Äî the era's static party-modifier bonuses are "SUPER STRONG‚Ä¶ maybe too large," decoupled from the actual economy [ch18 POST 8] (see #160/DH-67)**; **‚òÖ modernday CORROBORATES the 2-LAYER model from CURRENT RULES ‚Äî the new-era "State of the Meters" published VERBATIM (POST 2380): (a) a UNIVERSAL per-ideology modifier from the top meters applied to all candidates [Domestic-Stability bad ‚Üí incumbent-party ‚àí1; Honest-Gov ‚Üí Controversial-incumbent +1 / Integrity-vs-Controversial-challenger +1 / LW-Pop+RW-Pop ‚àí2 enth / Prog ‚àí1; QoL ‚Üí Lib +1 enth; Planet ‚Üí LW-Pop/Prog/Lib ‚àí2 enth] + (b) a SEPARATE per-ideology Enthusiasm √ó Blue/Red layer [Progs Blue+3, Libs Blue+3, Mods Red+1, Cons/Trads/RW-Pop Red+2]; the candidate-level modifier stack [#103] also exercised in the 2020 primary scorecard (Incumbent-President +2 / Runner-Up-2016 +1 / Incumbent-in-Economic-Recession ‚àí1 / Previous-Scandal ‚àí1, POST 855-863). Matches the terror2000-settled 2-layer map; build this**; **pop2012b ‚Äî 2nd 2012-start corroboration of the 2-layer map: the "PRE-MIDTERMS STATE OF METERS" (POST 840) prints both layers verbatim ‚Äî (a) a UNIVERSAL per-ideology modifier from top meters [Rev-Budget ‚Üí Mod +1; QoL ‚Üí Mod/Cons +1, LW-Pop ‚àí1; Planet ‚Üí Cons +1, Prog ‚àí1; EconStab + DomStab ‚Üí Dem-party ‚àí1] PLUS (b) a SEPARATE per-ideology Enthusiasm √ó Blue/Red layer [LW-Pop Neutral, Progs Dem +1, Libs/Mods Dem +3, Cons/Trads/RW-Pop Red +3]; matches the terror2000/modernday model**; **‚òÖ batch-28 ELECTION-DIE reconciliation ‚Äî the per-state win roll oscillated D6‚ÜíD3‚ÜíD6 in the reference game: to-do leaned **D3** (POSTs 892, 901-913, 920; "D100 makes traits meaningless, only in-play states get randomness"), then `completions` REVERTED it **D+3‚ÜíD+6** (POSTs 123-125, 142, 146, 153 ‚Äî "d3 breaks fluidity / no upsets; state biases were retuned to assume d6") and `fixes` POST 276 confirms "update all d3 rolls to d6 (state biases go to ¬±5)". `completions` tags this **CONFIRMED-SHIPPED** in our build (our `calcStateVote` resolves on a 0‚Äì6 biased scale, `phaseRunners.ts:727`, consistent with the d6 era) ‚Äî **tech-lead to verify the cited `file:line` before treating the die-size as settled**; note `redbutton`'s designer-blessed nerf direction (#184) re-floats D6‚ÜíD3, so the die-size is a live tension between the 2022 "d6 final" and the 1960-run "d3 to tame landslides." Also originating here: the **era state-bias blanket +2/+3** compensation (to-do POSTs 919-922) and the **¬±3 party-pref-swing cap** (`fixes` ¬ßA POST 407-408 = DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE ORIGIN of #80; max state lean ¬±5) ‚Äî see #80/DH-42**) |

Designed-slot / unbuilt-body. Cross-ref #228 (spoiler VOTE-DRAIN ‚Äî the effect half), #18/#51/#124 (meter‚Üíenthusiasm substrate), #4 (neutral-ideology faction origin), DH-55 (the win-and-absorb permanence rule this same POST resolves). `5b162cf5` POST 2**; **‚òÖ (b64) `1b2e9ff9` ("Something I noticed with the Meters") finds a FALSE-POSITIVE in the b62 gate above + SHARPENS the spawn condition: the two-meter-NEUTRAL gate fires a third party from a middle-ideology bloc REGARDLESS of WHY it is neutral ‚Äî so a bloc that BOTH parties actively COURTED can land dead-center and wrongly BOLT (a courted group has no reason to defect; middle-from-consensus is indistinguishable from middle-from-neglect). FIX (theFreezerFlame POST 3 ‚Üí relaxation POST 4): **AND the neutral/middle trigger with a "dislikes BOTH platforms" test** ‚Äî an ideology goes third-party only if it also **scores BELOW the average faction** (a relative gate; courted-and-happy blocs sit at/above average and won't run, genuinely-neglected blocs sit below and can). So #48's gate = party-pref NEUTRAL **AND** ideology-enthusiasm NEUTRAL **AND** below-average-satisfaction. ‚òÖ BUILD-STATE nuance (load-bearing): the per-party approval data this test needs ALREADY EXISTS ‚Äî `game.enthusiasm` is already 2-D per (ideology, party) `{BLUE,RED}` each ¬±5 (`types.ts:1415-1418`); only `game.partyPreference` is 1-D (¬±5, `types.ts:1570`); `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:~3696-3709`) still has NO third-party/spoiler term (cross-ref #228). OPEN: is "below the average faction" averaged over ALL factions or same-ideology-adjacent, and read off `enthusiasm[ideology][party]` or a combined score? (design call). **‚òÖ (b64) DEFERRED-TO-AMPU-2 note:** POST 1's bigger proposal ‚Äî RETIRE the 1-D `partyPreference` for a full **2-D per-(faction,party) SATISFACTION model** (separate Red/Blue approval per faction; third party only when BOTH ‚âà ‚àí3; both ‚âà +3 ‚Üí bloc splits evenly, no third party; stronger third party the more-negative both; and election advantage = the BLUE‚àíRED DIFFERENCE, replacing the 1-D lean as the vote-shift driver) ‚Äî is **EXPLICITLY DEFERRED TO AMPU 2** by the designer (MrPotatoTed POST 2): it hasn't bitten in practice (needs a rare trifecta ‚Äî both parties courting the same ideology + it landing dead-center + overall popularity also centered) and a rework "would necessitate readjusting **every single legislative proposal, event, and significant sections of the rules**," which vcczar "just isn't going to do at this stage." NOT a near-term build ‚Äî record alongside the AMPU-2 parking-lot (#205/#234) and the meter/vote-score cluster (#292 headline / #18 / #51 / #124 + DH-55); the `enthusiasm` 2-D `{BLUE,RED}` structure is the extend-from hook if AMPU 2 builds it. `1b2e9ff9` POST 1, 2, 3, 4**) |

So the CPU spec PRESUMES a working endorsement‚Üímomentum channel ‚Äî exactly the channel the build lacks (`grep momentum|endorse` in `src/` = none). | 230 | **‚òÖ Competitive-map / swing-state set ‚Äî cap which states can flip so the jitter can't swing safe states ‚Äî NEW (`primary2020`; election scoring)** | `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) scores EVERY state identically and lets the `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` jitter flip anything; no swing-state set / safe-state floor | **A NEW election-scoring constraint surfaced in the May-2022 stress-test (`primary2020` ¬ß3, POST 28, 40-50).** The 2020-map re-rolls repeatedly produced implausible flips ‚Äî Dem wins OK/KS/AK; "Florida votes to the right of Mississippi"; "Wyoming and Kentucky highly competitive"; CA/NV margins swapped. Root cause (POST 28): the state-bias MARGIN BONUS only fires above a sub-point CUTOFF (‚â•6, debated ‚â•4), so safe states never LOCK and tiny rolls flip them. Proposed fix (ShortKing + others, POST 40-49): CAP the competitive map ‚Äî only party-pref ‚â§1 states (the 0-lean PA/WI + the ¬±1 list ME/MI/NH/NV / AZ/FL/GA/MT/NC/OH) are true BATTLEGROUNDS; everything else draws its margin from a BOUNDED random range so MS/CA can't become swing states. V deferred ("might wait until we can do elections on CPU‚Ä¶ run 100+ tests a day," POST 61). Pairs with #184/DH-72 (the flat-margin-amplifier overpower) + #18 (swing-die / meter‚Üíelection). **Open: wanted feature, or shelved as "wait for CPU elections"? ‚Äî designer confirm before roadmapping** | Add a swing-state set / safe-state floor to the election scorer: only party-pref ‚â§1 states are flip-eligible battlegrounds; safe states draw a bounded margin (so the jitter can't flip them); reconcile the sub-point margin-cutoff so safe states lock. Pairs with #184/DH-72/#18 | ingested (**primary2020 ¬ß3 / POST 28, 40-50, 61; codebase-verified calcStateVote scores all states identically (phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723)**) |
| 231 | **‚òÖ Modern Moderate over-performance is METER-DRIVEN and reproducible (Buttigieg sweeps every 2020-primary run) ‚Äî balance datum ‚Äî NEW (`primary2020`, designer-confirmed cause)** | The shipped meter‚Üíelection map (#18) + per-ideology enthusiasm (#51) exist but are unbuilt; this is a balance OUTCOME of how the modern-era meters value Moderates | **A named, REPRODUCIBLE primary-balance break the designers diagnosed as systemic, not rolls (`primary2020` ¬ß2-¬ß3).** Running the 2020 Dem primary 4+ times, BUTTIGIEG wins every run (600 ‚Üí 989 ‚Üí 700 delegates) while the real front-runners (Biden/Sanders) are "mired toward the bottom" (POST 4, 7, 8, 11). Designer answer (Ted, POST 12): SYSTEMIC ‚Äî "in the modern era Liberals, Progressives and LW-populists have MALUSES due to meter issues. Biden is a Lib. The only Mods are Buttigieg and Bloomberg" ‚Äî the ideology-enthusiasm METER STATE, not candidate quality, hands Moderates the primary. It carries into the GENERAL too (POST 66-67): Moderates get +1 in the 2020 general (after ‚àí1 in the primary), are rarely any state's hated ideology, preferred in many R-leaning states ‚Üí Buttigieg/Klobuchar land at ~49.7% vs Trump (a coin-flip). So the primary-penalty + general-bonus is an INTENTIONAL knob that reads as overpowered in the modern era. This is a meter-driven datum feeding #18 (meter‚Üíelection) and #214 (which abilities/ideologies are over-weighted) ‚Äî NOT a new system, a balance regression case to fix when #18/#51 land | Treat as a balance regression case when implementing #18/#51/#214: the modern-era ideology-enthusiasm maluses on Lib/Prog/LW-Pop + the Moderate primary-penalty/general-bonus knob make Moderates sweep modern primaries reproducibly ‚Äî re-tune so candidate quality, not meter state, decides. Pairs with #18/#51/#214 | ingested (**primary2020 ¬ß2-¬ß3 / POST 4, 7, 8, 11, 12, 66-67**) |

(corroborated across 3 eras) | ingested (gilded 201-203; fed 46, 89, 170, 201, 392, 496, 575; modern 100-104, 729, 1390, 2043; **‚òÖ completions ¬ßExec POST 43, 74 ‚Äî NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD: the 2022 ref game added **10 "Do Not Enforce ‚Ä¶" Pres Actions** [min-wage floor, slavery ban, desegregation, public-schools ban, evolution-teaching, LGBT protections, marijuana, hard drugs, gay-marriage protections, poll-tax] that let a state CONTRADICT federal law [not Amendments/Planks], paired with the **state-contradicts-federal rule**: a contradictory Gov Action scores **double points, half effect**. Absent from `src/` [no "do-not-enforce" Pres Actions]; this is the federal-vs-state nullification axis [#20/#21/#23] still owed. Add the Do-Not-Enforce action class + the double-points/half-effect contradiction rule**; **‚òÖ (b43) `ddd179cb` (playtester survey, Q10) ‚Äî a balance-tuning ask on the Exec-Actions library: "Executive Actions are two-fold too difficult to implement (or aren't worth the points) and the blunders for implementation are too easy to come by" (jnewt) ‚Üí lower the difficulty/blunder rate OR raise the payoff (tuning, not a new mechanic; ties the impl-blunder model #22/#126). `ddd179cb` Q10 POST 20**; **‚òÖ (b64) `02e2dd6d` (Spoils System scope) names a specific missing exec-order AND surfaces a MISSING EFFECT-CLASS this library needs: the **Spoils System executive order** (Jackson-era; grep `spoils`/`Spoils` in `src/` = **0**) installs a **STANDING effect with a fixed DURATION ‚Äî a same-party HARD-LOCK on cabinet/minister appointments for the NEXT HALF-TERM** (only the President's own party may be seated for the duration; the cabinet-composition half of the lock is owned on #25). This is a THIRD persistence mode the shipped green/yellow/white model (this row) does NOT cover: green=stays-until-changed, yellow=auto-off-on-admin/party-change, white=inactive are all admin-SCOPED toggles ‚Äî none is a **fixed half-term TIMER** that auto-expires on a turn count. So the Exec-Actions library needs (a) the Spoils order as content and (b) a `durationHalfTerms`/expiry-countdown effect-class (a bounded standing effect), which the 4-action stub (`phaseRunners.ts:3632-3646`, 50%-fire pick-1-of-4 one-tick nudges) cannot express. NOTE the SCOPE ruling (POST 2) bounds the order: it reaches cabinet+ministers ONLY ‚Äî NOT the military (officers non-political, #49) and NOT the Supreme Court (no President-removal of a Justice, #270/#273). `02e2dd6d` POST 1, 2**) |

(corroborated across 2 eras) | ingested (fed 41, 119, 131, 177, 354, 392, 454, 547; modern 587-605, 1499-1513, 1903, 2172; **pop2012b ‚Äî 2nd 2012-start corroboration: 28-seat 2nd-term cabinet, requirements printed verbatim (‚â•2 admin / ‚â§16 yrs total as Sec-cabinet-ambassador / relevant expertise / fired-or-quit = permanent appointment ban / keep ‚â§5 incumbents, POST 507); military auto-confirmed; botched implementation fired Sec-State Power for incompetency (POST 558); ‚òÖ designer ruling ‚Äî Key Advisor is an INFORMAL office, double-holdable with a real seat (POST 591-592); ‚òÖ MrPotatoTed (designer) ‚Äî the rulebook's per-office accept/decline percentages are CPU-ONLY; human players freely accept/decline any nomination except VP (POST 820-821); the no-double-dipping-offices rule traces to the 1960 test (POST 817)**; **‚òÖ SHARPENED-AUTHORITATIVE (batch 31, `apptdeepdive` ‚Äî the Sept-2022 ORIGIN of the cabinet/sub-cabinet/military/ambassador tenure + scoring rules; merged here rather than re-created): ‚óÜ TENURE = 16 years total as Secretary / cabinet-level / ambassador (CUMULATIVE), 4-term max whichever first; MILITARY service does NOT count; CIA/DNI & FBI EXEMPT (no term limit); Era of Independence has no terms (committee chairs may replace every 2 yrs) ‚Äî this thread BROADENED the 16-yr cap from Secretaries-only to everything-but-military and made it cumulative (POST 39, 40, 144, 146). ‚óÜ AMBASSADOR tenure/rotation: 16-yr limit, rotate only ONCE PER FULL TERM (fixes the observed 4-5‚òÖ-ambassadors-endlessly-musical-chairing bug in a cabinet-less 1772 game, POST 4-5); +1 admin chance on INITIAL appointment (POST 3); prereq = Foreign-Affairs / Business / Trade (POST 43-47); ISOLATIONIST/Nationalist ambassador ‚Üí roll to HARM relations with the host (Joe-Kennedy/UK-WWII precedent; the relations penalty attaches to an ambassador who personally has Foreign-Affairs in the Isolationist faction, distinct from the faction's lobby-enthusiasm boost, POST 51-56). ‚óÜ EXPERTISE SCORING (NEW MODEL replacing "bold expertise"): each role lists accepted expertises, nominee needs ‚â•1, and EACH EXPERTISE BEYOND THE FIRST = +50 pts to the President (POST 39-40). ‚óÜ SENIOR military confirmation: Senior Generals/Admirals MUST be confirmed by Congress next Legislative Session (Foreign/Military Committee ‚Üí full chamber, mandatory "business"); a voted-down senior officer behaves like a defeated SCOTUS nominee (new appointment auto-confirmed); branch-lock (Gen‚âÝAdm), no-demotion, JCS-from-Gen/Adm + Military-Leader (POST 21-24, 36-38) ‚Äî corroborates #49. ‚óÜ FIRING PRECEDENT (#25): set via a "Set Precedence for Firing Cabinet Members" Exec Action (late-Adams), AUTO-SET for post-1800 starts; ‚â§5-retention cap (CIA/FBI/DNI don't count); moving an incumbent doesn't count vs the 5 but requires re-confirmation (POST 19, 22, 40). Codebase HAS `ABILITY_EARN_RULES.cabinetConfirmAdmin` (types.ts:570-595, the coded face of the retained-officer admin-gain rolls) but LACKS the 16-yr/ambassador-rotation/+50-per-expertise/firing-precedent state ‚Äî all designed-but-unbuilt. apptdeepdive ¬ßA/¬ßB/¬ßD POST 3, 4-5, 19, 21-24, 36-47, 51-56, 144, 146**; **‚òÖ batch 36 ‚Äî `judiciaryidea` (fd4c3c4e) adds a JUDICIAL-appointment reveal/career-track datum that pairs with the SC cluster (NEW #251): the Judicial Career Track is one of three ways a justice's hidden Judicial Philosophy gets revealed (alongside Focus-Court appointment + direct SC appointment), and Cal proposes RENAMING "State Judicial Career Track" ‚Üí "Judicial Career Track" (POST 1) ‚Äî an author-time naming/scope decision adjacent to the appointment pipeline this row covers. judiciaryidea POST 1**; **‚òÖ (b64) `02e2dd6d` (Spoils System scope) adds a TEMPORARY-TIGHTENING cabinet-composition rule this row's opp-party-cap framework must support: the **Spoils System exec order** (triggered on #23) imposes a **HARD same-party LOCK on cabinet/minister seats for one HALF-TERM** ‚Äî "only Dems in cabinet or as ministers." ‚òÖ This is the INVERSE of the shipped default: `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) already leans same-party but by ALLOWANCE-not-lock ‚Äî per seat it rolls `CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_RATE = 0.1` (a 10% chance to ADMIT an opposite-party appointee, `types.ts:1233`) + a `CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_PENALTY = -3` score hit (`types.ts:1234`). The Spoils order must temporarily **force `crossPartyAllowed = false` (drop the 10% window) for the half-term**, then auto-restore ‚Äî i.e. layer a bounded overriding party-lock ON TOP of the standing opp-party cap this row already owns. No duration/standing-effect plumbing exists to hold that state (the half-term timer is the #23 effect-class). "Ministers" ‚âà cabinet seats (`game.cabinet` `types.ts:1580`; no separate minister entity ‚Äî confirm at build whether any non-cabinet appointee also counts). Scope: the lock does NOT reach military (#49) or SCOTUS (#270/#273). `02e2dd6d` POST 1, 2**) |
| 229 | **‚òÖ CPU appointee NOMINATION-ACCEPTANCE odds matrix (who says yes by candidate type) + "humans freely accept/decline except VP" ‚Äî NEW (`apptdeepdive`, designer-authoritative; `GM‚áíApp`)** | No acceptance/decline roll on the appointee side of the appointment phase; #25 notes the human/CPU split (pop2012b POST 820-821) but not the per-candidate-type CPU odds table as a build requirement | **The appointee-SIDE of the appointment phase ‚Äî the full per-candidate-type CPU acceptance matrix (`apptdeepdive` ¬ßE POST 40, ACCEPTED FINAL), with the human rule confirmed: humans freely accept/decline ANY nomination EXCEPT VP (a sitting VP cannot accept any nomination, POST 40/250).** CPU odds: ‚óÜ Senate/House officers, SC justices = 10% top-cabinet (State/Treasury/AG/War-Def), 25% for Sec-State, NEVER accept lower-cabinet/cabinet-level/ambassador. ‚óÜ Senators (non-officer), Governors = 90% top / 75% lower / 50% cabinet-level / 10% ambassador (UN-Amb is cabinet-level). ‚óÜ Representatives = ALWAYS (unless a House officer). ‚óÜ Generals/Admirals/military = ALWAYS unless Disharmonious (50%). ‚óÜ Career-track pols = cabinet 100% / cabinet-level 75% / ambassador-or-military 50%; if confirmed, PULLED off career track with milestone benefits earned so far. ‚óÜ Incumbent movement = top-cabinet only retain/move to another top post; lower-cabinet retain/move-up 100% / cabinet-level 50% / ambassador 25%; cabinet-level & ambassadors accept all. ‚óÜ Sitting VP = CANNOT accept any nomination. Also the trait gates (Incompetent can't accept any cabinet post; Easily-Overwhelmed declines 50% ‚Äî both lifted only if no one else; fired/quit pol never appointable again, POST 40). Distinct from #25 (firing-precedent/tenure) and the CPU FILL logic (#73, the picker side) ‚Äî this is the ACCEPT/DECLINE side | Add a per-candidate-type CPU acceptance/decline roll on nominations (the ¬ßE matrix) + the human rule (free accept/decline except VP) + the Incompetent/Easily-Overwhelmed/fired-quit trait gates; on a career-track pol accepting, pull them off-track with milestone benefits. `GM‚áíApp`. Pairs with #25 (tenure/firing) + #73 (CPU fill/picker) + #112 (¬±1-ideology accept gate) | ingested (**apptdeepdive ¬ßE/¬ßB POST 40, 250; corroborates the human/CPU split at pop2012b POST 820-821 (#25)**; **‚òÖ (b44) APPOINTMENTS-2.3 MAINTENANCE PASS ‚Äî `5d225e8a` (topic 5647, Aug 2024; CLOSES the b43 "Appointments-2.3 owed" debt ‚Äî b43's `754a0a4c` was a byte-identical mis-export, so the real 2.3 content was never captured until now). This thread is a ~2-yr-later AUDIT of the `apptdeepdive`/b9028796 ruleset, not a redefinition. KEY FINDINGS: (a) **CPU over-rejection of nominees = FIXED & playtest-confirmed** ‚Äî the longstanding "CPU votes against too many nominees" bug (ahistorical; confirmations were rarely controversial) was fixed by ebrk's CPU-rule adjustment and confirmed by Tyler's CPU-vs-CPU playtests (POST 2-4, 6). **NOT a new build gap ‚Äî resolved in the manual ruleset; and the SHIPPED engine has NO confirmation vote at all (see shipped-reality below), so this fix lives only in the manual rules.** (b) **SHIPPED-REALITY (code-verified):** `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158`) is a thin DETERMINISTIC CPU auto-fill ‚Äî it scores eligible pols (`scoreCabinetCandidate` + `CABINET_SEAT_SCORING`) and seats the top one, log says "confirmed as‚Ä¶" ‚Äî with **NO confirmation vote, NO appointee accept/decline roll, NO lobby‚Üíenthusiasm.** The cross-party gate as SHIPPED is **NOT the designed ¬±1-ideology gate** (#112d): it is a flat **10% per-seat chance to relax the same-party filter** (`CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_RATE`) with a **‚àí3 score penalty** (`CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_PENALTY`) ‚Äî no ideology-distance check, no Top-Four cap, no presidential-trait gate. BOTH 2.3.1 (Cabinet) and 2.3.2 (Military) phases are **SKIPPED entirely in the independence era** (`phases.ts:89-90`). So this entire ¬ßE acceptance matrix + the ¬±1 gate are designed-but-unbuilt; the shipped path is far thinner. (c) **CAREER-TRACK REMOVAL GUARD (anti-snipe) ‚Äî NEW PROPOSED, leaning accept:** the existing spec ("career-track pols accept cabinet 100%/cabinet-level 75%/amb-or-military 50%, then are pulled off-track w/ rounded-down milestone benefits ‚Äî applies to humans AND CPU", already in this row's matrix) is flagged as a SINGLE-PLAYER EXPLOIT: because such pols ALWAYS accept, a human can "snipe" rival-faction career-trackers to stunt CPU growth (player power creep; in the 1840 game the worst offender is the human, POST 8, 14). Proposed guard (ebrk POST 12, Tyler concurs): **only allow nominating a career-track pol if NO ONE with ‚â•2 Admin is available/eligible** for that post. UNRESOLVED ‚Äî remove the early-removal entirely vs keep-with-guard. (d) **CPU cabinet auto-resign ‚Äî SPEC (committed to rules doc, POST 7), design-only/not shipped:** in the era between "Continental Congress dissolves" and "President sets firing precedent", a CPU cabinet member **auto-resigns if the relevant skill (usually Admin) drops below 2** ‚Äî EXCEPT a member serving FOR LIFE under an **opposite-party** President never resigns regardless. `5d225e8a` POST 2-14**) |

| 112 | **‚òÖ Modern institutional layer: first impeachment + presidential resignation + unelected succession + VP-vacancy confirmation; ¬±1-ideology cabinet eligibility; 60‚Üí70% confirmation; the "Iron Fist" force-vote** ‚Äî NEW | `vicePresidentId` exists (types.ts:1568) but no impeachment, no succession line, no VP-vacancy fill, no ideology-distance appointment gate, no force-vote | The institutional machinery a long modern campaign actually exercises (first live use in the corpus): **(a) impeachment** ‚Äî must come from a legislative proposal ‚Üí House Judiciary approves the article ‚Üí full House ‚Üí Senate trial; **any officeholder facing impeachment may resign at any stage**; **resignation-while-impeached penalties** (permanent ‚àí3 future elections, barred from any appointment, ‚àí500 pts, loses Integrity). | 326 | **‚òÖ SC-NOMINEE AGE-MINIMUM / JUDICIAL-RATING SEATING GATE ‚Äî a hard age floor + a Judicial-keyed eligibility table on who may be seated as a justice; the shipped auto-replace requires ONLY `judicial>=2` with NO age floor (a 20-yr-old is seatable) ‚Äî NEW (b56, `70a67176` Exec/SC deep-dive; cross-ref #66/#52/#270)** | **0% built ‚Äî no age floor on new justices.** The reactive auto-replace filter is `!currentOffice && !deathYear && !retiredYear && partyId===president.partyId && skills.judicial >= 2`, sorted by highest `judicial` (`phaseRunners.ts:3661`) ‚Üí seated and logged "confirmed" with NO vote; `age >= 75` only TRIGGERS retirement (`:3655`), never gates an appointee. So a 20-yr-old, J2, extreme nominee is seatable today (reproduces the live ‚òÖ7 bug ‚Äî a voted-down "extreme, under-30, still-in-law-school, J1" nominee whose auto-confirmed replacement was ALSO under-30/J1, just extreme the other way) | **`70a67176` (vcczar 2.8 deep-dive, Sep 2022, ‚òÖ8) ‚Äî add a seating gate so law-students/undergrads can't be appointed. TWO proposed forms: (a) a flat **age-minimum of 30** + a **huge penalty under 40 (or 35)**; OR (b, the more elegant self-balancing form) tie the floor to the nominee's Judicial rating: **J5‚Üí30, J4‚Üí35, J3‚Üí40** (appointable WITHOUT penalty) and **J2 or J1‚Üí45+**. The live ‚òÖ7 bug is the motivation: the current auto-pick selects a SECOND equally-bad candidate (equally extreme + under-age) ‚Üí the compromise-candidate requirement (#66, ‚òÖ6) and this age/Judicial gate together fix it. NOTE this is the SEATING-eligibility gate, distinct from the auto-confirm-vs-always-vote VOTE fork (which lives on #66)** | Add a per-nominee age floor + a Judicial-rating-keyed eligibility table (J5‚Üí30 ‚Ä¶ J2/J1‚Üí45+) to the justice-appointment path; reject (or heavily penalize) under-age/low-Judicial nominees so the auto-replace (and any future nomination flow) cannot seat a law-student. OPEN (human): flat age-min+penalty vs the Judicial-keyed table (the table is self-balancing ‚Äî confirm which the designer wants). ### Governors, states, statehood

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |

| 20 | **Governor's actions library** | Phase 2.5.1/2.5.2 exists but no `governor.takeAction` library | Each governor takes 1‚ÄìN actions/half-term against a fixed library, rolled vs threshold (~d100 vs 20¬∑`Gov`; **Efficient ‚Üí +1 action**). Era-flavored sets: gilded (Gerrymander, Women's Suffrage, Jim Crow‚Ä¶), fed (Native-lands, assimilation, criticize Pres‚Ä¶), 1772 (improve industry, set term length). `modern` adds primary-control (**Set Primaries WTA**, **State Primary Placement Group N**, **Split Electoral Votes**), voter-suppression (Purge Rolls / Strict ID / Decrease Polling Stations / Literacy Test ‚Äî **all blocked while Honest-Gov't maxed**), culture (Ban Evolution, Arm Teachers), and **success can grant +1 Command**. **Some gov actions are ideology-gated** (a non-Mod fails an industry action). `drums` adds **fixed faction-archetype CPU mapping** (Theocrat‚ÜíProhibition/Abortion/Ban Evolution; Mining-state‚ÜíImprove Mining; Liberal/Prog‚ÜíStrengthen Labor Unions; Reformist‚ÜíAllow State Primaries; Big-Ag‚ÜíMajor Irrigation) and the **small/large-state action-impact multiplier** (RI at 2.5% half of 5%; large states double ‚Äî POSTS 6676-6680) | New governor-action enum + handlers + per-action prereqs (incl. ideology gate + Honest-Gov't gate + small/large-state √ó0.5/√ó2 multiplier) + state-status checks + Efficient extra-action + faction-archetype CPU mapping + **OC-7 help-allies ‚Üí gov-term-config sub-rule** (`oopscpu` Ted ruling). **‚òÖ Batch-28 v1-endorsed ADDITIONS to this enum (Vic3 B2/B3, Ted-endorsed, vcczar actively building ‚Äî `vic3` POST 6, 7, 15):** (a) **meter-boosting "Advocate" Gov Actions** ‚Äî *Improve Infrastructure, Advocate Nationalism, Advocate Social Mobility, Advocate Environmentalism (‚ÜíPlanet/climate meter), Advocate Military Service (‚ÜíMilPrep), Advocate Traditional Family Values, Advocate Equity for Blacks, Advocate Political Reform, Advocate Isolationism, Advocate US Exceptionalism*, **each era-keyed with a start era** (#92); Ted's load-bearing point: these are **REPEATABLE meter-boost actions** (current meter-actions are non-repeatable ‚Äî a state goes "spent‚Ä¶forever" once popped); (b) **migration Gov Actions** ‚Äî *Encourage Domestic Migration* (raise a state's population **at the expense of a random bordering state** ‚Äî zero-sum, prefiguring the later "EV-gain-taken-from-another-state" rule) and *Encourage Immigration* (raise population; **"Nationalists will hate it"** ‚Üí Nationalist-faction enthusiasm friction). These touch #35/#92 (population/EV) too. **The `completions` GERRYMANDER rework also lands as a Gov Action here** (shifts house-deviation bias +1 to the Gov's party except +5 the other way; once per 10 yrs/state; tied to a census) ‚Äî NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD (no gerrymander action in `src/`, `completions` POST 145), and the `completions` "double-success-chance column for certain Gov types" so Deep/Upper South quickly pass Jim-Crow-type laws (DESIGNER-DONE/UNVERIFIED, POST 140). (**6-era cpu-confirmed** ‚Äî `oopscpu` adds the **help-allies gov-term-config sub-rule** [Ted "command decision": when the help-allies CPU gov rule fires, a same-party-leaning state lengthens terms / removes term-limits, an opposite-leaning state adds limits / shortens ‚Äî POST 264, 275, 941] and confirms the CPU gov action set runs by faction archetype with success rolls; ‚Äî `hd` adds **skill-match doubles success, 5-Gov autopass, success‚Üí10% +1 Command [except autopass actions], national-meter-impact roll, Gov incumbency decay after 8/12 yrs**, and the **"Activate State Primaries"** action [#63]; `drums` adds the static fixed-menu archetype rule, the small/large multiplier, **and the no-industry-stack-awareness gap ‚Üí no-op silently when capped ‚Äî see DH-19**) | ingested (gilded 134-150; fed 33, 79, 144, 205, 298, 375, 482, 558, 713; 1772s 25, 46, 56, 90; modern 17-28, 1962, 2234-2245; **hd 125, 889, 2936-2945, 3094, 3134, 6119-6153, 6997**; **drums 30, 372-373, 574, 580, 584, 588, 721, 914-917, 1456, 2516, 2520, 3081-3083, 5894, 6159, 6289, 6519, 6671, 6676-6680, 6888, 7062, 7424, 7532**; **vic3 B2/B3 POST 6, 7, 15 ‚Äî Ted-endorsed v1 Gov-Action additions (Advocate-* meter-boosts + migration/immigration), era-keyed; completions POST 140, 145 ‚Äî gerrymander rework NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD + Jim-Crow double-success column DESIGNER-DONE/UNVERIFIED**; **‚òÖ BATCH-33 SHARPEN (`legisexecgov` 518fb253 POST 31) ‚Äî ‚òÖ all Governor Actions are currently **FLAT / STATE-AGNOSTIC** ("all gov actions, as they currently are, are the SAME for every state") ‚Äî there is NO per-state unique-action table; governors were the lowest-priority entity (originally only ratified amendments, then accreted "token things to do"). **Per-state unique gov actions are an EXPLICITLY DEFERRED upgrade** ("if I have nothing on the to-do list"). This is why **railway-ownership bills, state-based fugitive-slave acts, and per-state Native-American suffrage are NOT-yet-authored** (POST 31 ‚Äî they can't apply to all states under the flat constraint). Accepted gov actions confirming the state-level lever model: legalize casinos/lottery/prostitution with historic activation dates (POST 8); attract-immigration-to-grab-a-House-seat (POST 5); statues-to-Statuary-Hall (POST 37); vacant-seat fill rules (POST 2); abolish/reintroduce public executions (POST 15). `ampuelections` adds the election-lever gov actions (Allow State Primaries, set primaries WTA, set primary delegates, split EVs, State-Legislature-Selects-President, Jim-Crow disenfranchisement ‚Äî confirming primaries + the per-state legislature-chooses-electors model are gov-action TOGGLES, not always-on). So #20 stays the state-scoped-action row; the new datum is the **flat-vs-per-state axis** (today flat; per-state is the deferred upgrade). legisexecgov POST 2, 5, 8, 15, 31, 37; ampuelections POST 1**; **‚òÖ BATCH-36 ‚Äî `space` (5da48f61) supplies a CONCRETE second worked example of a Gov action that CANNOT be flat (alongside `completions`' Jim-Crow double-success column): the **geography-gated NASA launch-site Gov action** ‚Äî "Propose to be official NASA launch-site state (currently FL), requires leadership + science/tech interest for the governor," with the explicit per-state-odds mechanic *"You can make it EASIER for deep-south state govs to have a successful action, and HARDER for it to happen everywhere else"* (POST 2-4) ‚Äî i.e. a **per-state success-probability MODIFIER column keyed to geography** (equator/Deep-South favored; FL/French-Guiana/Vandenberg precedents). Plus two NEW per-state Gov-action sub-mechanics worth noting for #20: a **one-winner RACE across states** ("first successful gov action gets the funding," e.g. Mission Control/Houston, POST 9) and a **state-adjacency AGGREGATION rule** (on NASA defunding, govs spin up state space agencies; enough ADJACENT states with agencies can MERGE into a regional one, POST 11 ‚Äî a novel adjacency mechanic with no analog elsewhere in the KB). then expand the existing "20% prot√©g√©-w/-kingmaker / 20% charisma-leadership-likable-manipulative-integrity" CPU rules to ANYONE with a guaranteed **+1 election bonus** ("a guaranteed +1 beats the maybe-+1 traits") ‚Äî ‚òÖ BLOCKED: NO per-politician `electionBonus` FIELD exists (`applyTraitElectionBonus`/`electionEffects.ts` is the unrelated TRAIT-bonus path; `0ff5e582`):** the table/spreadsheet rule humans use to drive a CPU governor is **25% ideology-card / 25% crisis / 50% "help party overall"** (POST 1) ‚Äî its named flaws: many eras have NO ideology card (the 25% dead-ends), "help party overall" is too undefined to be an action, and traits don't matter. The proposed replacement is a **multi-bucket %-weighted selector** (POST 1/2/5): %improve-state-industry (+2nd roll for WHICH if >1) / %ideology+lobby+crisis (the survivable part of today's rule, one bucket) / %tinker-election-laws (gerrymander, term limits ‚Äî gated on qualifications, e.g. ‚â•1 same-party Senator) / %special-passion (Civil-Rights/Theocrat ‚Üí roll to let the passion guide the action), with two cross-cutting overrides selecting WITHIN buckets: **trait-gated** (`Integrity` ‚Üí 50% institute/tighten term limits; `Controversial` OR `Manipulative` ‚Üí 50% remove term limits) and **expertise-as-guide** (`Justice`‚Üípolice/prisons, `Business`/`Economics`‚Üíimprove a state industry, preferring an industry matching another expertise tag e.g. Ag). ‚òÖ SHIPPED reality is none of this: the ENTIRE CPU governor turn is a **flat 30% governing-skill `bias` nudge** (`runPhase_2_5_2_Governors` `phaseRunners.ts:3382-3392`: `chance(0.3)` ‚Üí nudge `s.bias` by `(gov.skills.governing‚àí1)*0.05`, signed by party, clamped ¬±5) that reads ONLY `gov.skills.governing` + `gov.partyId` ‚Äî no traits, no `gov.expertise`, no cards, no crisis, no industry. Integrity/Controversial/Manipulative `types.ts:64/73/103`; the 19-tag `Expertise` axis `types.ts:182-192`; seeded `chance`/`pick`/`pickWeighted` `phaseRunners.ts:9`) but the gov phase wires NONE of them ‚Äî so the overhaul is wiring existing pieces into a new selector, not building from zero. The classification is the INPUT to the influential-Rep delegation count (#308: Big=3 / Medium=2 / Small=1 elected Reps) and (likely) to AI/UI targeting. ‚òÖ DISTINCT from #293 (the DIFFICULTY model = faction ideology √ó era ‚Äî NOT a state-size concept; do NOT conflate). Feeds #308 (influential-Rep count) + AI/UI targeting. Pairs with #34 (the recompute that supplies EVs) / #308 (the consumer) / #305 (population input). The EV-derived output of the census recompute #34; the input to the influential-Rep model #308. OPEN: 10%-boundary tie-breaking**) |
| 308 | **‚òÖ INFLUENTIAL-REP DELEGATION-VOTING-POWER model ‚Äî abstract each state's House delegation to 1/2/3 elected "influential Reps" carrying the delegation's FULL weighted vote (weight = (EV‚àí2)√∑count, leftover‚Üíhighest-`legis` Rep) + ahistorical-seat BIAS rules ‚Äî NEW (b53, `5e5735ec` Census ruleset 3.0.24)** | **0% shipped (the formula is recognized in a COMMENT only).** `scenario1856.ts:93` (`const reps = Math.max(1, s.electoralVotes - 2); // EVs = reps + 2 senators`) uses the SAME EV‚àí2 arithmetic the ruleset specifies for total Reps ‚Äî but it seeds **one politician PER SEAT** (e.g. ~33 Reps for a 35-EV NY), the OPPOSITE of the design's abstraction. NO `influentialRep`/delegation-weight concept (`grep influentialRep` = 0), no per-Rep vote weight, no leftover-tiebreak, no recompute on later censuses, no bias-typed seats | **`5e5735ec` POST 1 (rules 3.0.24): the game does NOT model all 435 House members ‚Äî it ABSTRACTS each state's delegation to **1/2/3 elected "influential Reps"** by size class (#307): **Big‚Üí3, Medium‚Üí2, Small‚Üí1**. These Reps face election + represent the delegation. ‚òÖ VOTING-POWER FORMULA: a state's **total US Reps = EV ‚àí 2** (the 2 Senators); divide among the influential Reps (**√∑3 Big, √∑2 Medium, √ó1 Small**); round so the sum is correct; **leftover votes go to the delegation's highest-`legis` Rep (random if tied)** ‚Äî so influential Reps carry UNEQUAL vote weights. Worked example: CA 55 EV ‚Üí 53 US Reps ‚Üí 3 influential Reps ‚Üí 53/3 = 17.7 each ‚Üí give each 17 (= 51), the 2 leftover votes go to the highest-legis Rep. ‚òÖ AHISTORICAL-SEAT BIAS RULES (sub-rule): historical eras decide if a delegation's seats deviate from the **state bias**. ADDING an ahistorical seat ‚Üí **25% state-bias / 25% tossup / 25% Red+1 / 25% Blue+1**; SUBTRACTING a seat ‚Üí remove one AT RANDOM but **one seat must retain the state bias** (a deviant seat goes BEFORE the bias seat ‚Äî e.g. between AZ's "Red+1" bias seat and a "Blue+2" deviant, the deviant is removed). DISTINCT from #34/#307 (this is the House-representation subsystem the apportionment OUTPUT feeds, not the recompute or the size classification)** | Replace the one-Rep-per-seat seeding with an influential-Rep abstraction: 1/2/3 elected Reps per state by `sizeClass` (#307), each holding a vote WEIGHT = (EV‚àí2)√∑count with leftover‚Üíhighest-legis Rep; recompute on each census (#34); add bias-TYPED seats (state-bias / tossup / Red+N / Blue+N) + the ahistorical add (25/25/25/25) and subtract (protect-the-bias-seat) rules. Pairs with #34 / #307 / #21 (State Bias) / #62 (the related US-Rep voting-power-% model) ‚Äî RECONCILE with #62 which already logs a "delegation-%" voting model NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD. OPEN: rounding rule when EV‚àí2 doesn't divide evenly across a tie of highest-legis Reps; how "historical eras decide" the deviation is data-encoded | ingested (**`5e5735ec` (US Census ruleset "rules 3.0.24", Apr 2022) POST 1; codebase-verified the EV‚àí2 formula appears only as a SEEDING COMMENT (`scenario1856.ts:93`, one Rep per seat ‚Äî the opposite of the abstraction), no `influentialRep`/delegation-weight/leftover-tiebreak/bias-typed-seat concept in `src/`. The House-representation subsystem consuming the census output (#34‚Üí#307‚Üíthis). RECONCILE with #62 (delegation-% voting model). ‚òÖ (b53 tech-lead) NOT a standalone epic ‚Äî FOLDS into the already-tracked House cluster #62/#191/¬ß29.5: the flat 3/2/1 count is the superseded Apr-2022 ORIGIN of the `housepoll` 1-10 EV-derived ladder (¬ß29.5), the (EV‚àí2)√∑count vote-power = ¬ß29.5's floor formula, the ahistorical-seat bias = the #191 deviant-bias engine; only genuine new delta = the era-gated House cap + output-consumer wiring (see technical-guide debt #208). NEW SUB-DESIGN ‚Äî **naval as a DIFFICULTY MODIFIER, not a standalone win-condition**: fight ‚â•1 naval + ‚â•1 ground per phase, naval FIRST; a naval WIN gives the next land general +1 Mil (75%) for that battle only, a naval LOSS ‚àí1 Mil ‚Äî so naval persists and FEEDS land difficulty rather than "ending forever" (the design fix for the shipped DH-79 bug). **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b50) `59d805be` ("The Hand of Providence," all-CPU 1772 run) carries an AUTHORITATIVE designer ruling on the War-End Multiplier this row records + corroborates DH-81. ‚òÖ DESIGNER RULING (POST 49-50, @ebrk85, RECENT 2025): **the Revolution's war-end multiplier was changed from 1.0 ‚Üí 0.5** because in testing "the Revolution was ending in one turn (usually as a loss) way too often for what was historically a longer war ‚Äî the MASTER DOC was updated." So chance-to-end = `(WarScore)√ó0.5√ó10 = %` (the author recomputed `(3+1‚àí2)√ó0.5=1 ‚Üí 10%`). ‚öÝ CODE-VERIFIED (b50) the shipped `revolutionaryWar.ts` has **NO war-end MULTIPLIER at all** ‚Äî the war ends on a FIXED win/loss-COUNT threshold (`war.currentGroundWins >= war.groundWinsNeeded` / `currentGroundLosses >= groundLossesRemaining`, `:254-263`), not a `WarScore√ómultiplier‚Üí%` end-CHANCE roll. So the build BOTH lags the master-doc 0.5 ruling AND uses a different end-MODEL entirely (count-threshold vs the WS√ómult %-roll #45 generalizes). This reinforces DH-81 (the per-battle-vs-per-term war-end-roll cadence bug) ‚Äî the generalization must adopt the per-term WS√ó0.5(Revolution)/√ó1(normal) end-chance, not the shipped count cap. ‚òÖ ADDITIVE-TERMS DIVERGENCE confirmed in code: shipped battle target = `planning + general.mil*10 + (frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0)` with difficulty `difficult ‚àí20 / moderate 0 / easy +15` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212-216`) ‚Äî a FIXED +25 French-alliance add and NO benchmark term; the forum instead itemizes a **Benchmarks +15 (for 3/4)** term + a naval bonus (the core planning+officer√ó10‚àídifficulty MATCHES, only the additive bonuses diverge). ‚òÖ NAVAL‚ÜíLAND bonus (POST 43-44, @Arkansas-Progressive designer-confirmed): a prior naval WIN should grant a **75%-chance bonus to the NEXT land battle** (possibly only non-domestic landings) ‚Äî CODE-VERIFIED the engine records `navalWins` (`:202`) but NO land-battle read of it exists (confirms DH-79's "naval win does nothing downstream"; the naval‚Üíland 75% bonus is the design fix). ‚òÖ Confirms the French-alliance UNLOSEABLE floor in code: `:259` ends the war as a LOSS only `&& !war.frenchAlliance` and `applyFrenchAlliance` sets `Defeat is no longer possible` (`:274`) ‚Äî matches the POST 67 "Rev War cannot end in US defeat" event. War-Score accrual (win +1..+3 / lose ‚àí2..‚àí3, POST 40/59/73), senior-general/admiral planning drive, multi-roll Treaty-of-Paris (3-roll Admin check, only QoL+1 here, POST 75), and senior-general succession on Washington's resign‚ÜíGreene (POST 89) all corroborated. `59d805be` POST 40, 43-44, 49-50, 59, 67, 73, 75, 89; codebase-verified NO war-end multiplier (count-threshold end at `revolutionaryWar.ts:254-263`), fixed +25 French add + no benchmark term (`:212-216`), `navalWins` recorded (`:202`) but unread by land battles, French-alliance unloseable floor (`:259, :274`)**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b58) AUTHORITATIVE DESIGNER CHANGELOG ‚Äî the Military-Preparedness‚Üíbattle-bonus formula was REPLACED (`97aa3f6f` "State of the Rules" POST 1, Anthony's coding, "reflected on the Meters master sheet"): the meter no longer rolls a probability distribution over +15/+10/+5/immediate-defeat ‚Äî each of the **9 meter tiers now gives a FLAT additive battle bonus: ‚àí10 / ‚àí5 / 0 / +5 / +5 / +10 / +10 / +15 / +20** (low‚Üíhigh; tiers 4&5 both +5, 6&7 both +10; the **"immediate defeat" branch is REMOVED**). ‚òÖ SHIPPED reads NEITHER table: the `military` NationalMeter exists + the UI labels it "Military Preparedness" (`types.ts:1402`, `components/Meter.tsx:6`) but combat in `revolutionaryWar.ts` computes win purely from commander+SecWar skill + French-alliance with **NO preparedness term** (naval `:187-191` `win=d100()<=target+30`; ground `:212-218` `win=d100()<=baseTarget+diffMod`; grep of `revolutionaryWar.ts` for `meters.military` = 0) ‚Äî so the preparedness‚Üícombat coupling is ENTIRELY unbuilt, and when built it must land in the NEW FLAT form, added to `target` (= +bonus to chance-of-success) at `:191`/`:218`. (Note: the b50 finding above is about the war-END multiplier; this b58 entry is the separate per-BATTLE preparedness bonus. Generalizes to #56/#85.) `97aa3f6f` POST 1** |
| 106 | **‚òÖ The "Cold War" is NOT a system ‚Äî it is the generic war engine RELABELED + event-flavor (no nuclear/MAD/NATO/space subsystem; USSR NEVER falls)** ‚Äî NEW | No Cold-War / containment / nuclear subsystem; `revolutionaryWar.ts` is the only war code (1772-scoped) | **The single biggest modern-half structural finding.** Despite the "Nuclear Age" title there is **NO purpose-built Cold-War subsystem** ‚Äî only the **same naval‚Üíland d100 battle engine (#45) relabeled** with era-appropriate war names (US‚ÄìSpain/Franco, the "Korean War" run as the Vietnam-quagmire stand-in to its ~17th‚Äì19th year, GOP-opened Cuba/Cambodia/Somalia/Rwanda, Gulf War, Early War on Terror) + scripted **event-flavor**. **NO nuclear / arms-race / MAD / space-race subsystem** (nukes = a Soviet-Nuclear-Test event + one "2√ó points for nuke/mil bills next session" multiplier; NASA = a bill + a prerequisite flag; NATO/d√©tente/SALT/Berlin/Watergate are one-off A/B point-swing events, not systems ‚Äî Watergate even fires **no impeachment trigger**). The war engine has **no army/navy/air branch separation** (army generals command navies; "naval" pols die in infantry attacks) and **wars never resolve on their own** (end-roll odds so low the Korean War ran ~two decades; **white peace rarely possible** ‚Äî most wars have no Treaty B/C authored). **‚òÖ The end of the Cold War is NOT modeled ‚Äî the USSR NEVER FALLS:** the clock crosses 1982‚Üí2005 with no fall-of-USSR / German reunification / peace dividend; the USSR is a **live named ally + diplomatic actor in 2004‚Äì05** (a sitting Ambassador to the Soviet Union; the Soviets launch Mir as a current event; "Russo-Georgian War" treats Russia as "the USSR, a nuclear power"). The dissolution event-chain exists but is gated (Solidarity ‚Üí JPII ‚Üí Glasnost ‚Üí SDI ‚Üí **Gorbachev ~5% gate ‚Äî never cleared** ‚Üí 1989 20% ‚Üí Dissolution 10%) and **never fired** ("we keep missing the 5% chance"). Cold-War-end triggers are also buggy (only Dissolution/Soviet-Alliance explicitly end Cold-War status; an LW-Pop early alliance would prevent the Cold War triggering at all) | This is **negative scope** for the roadmap: do NOT build a bespoke Cold-War/nuclear/MAD/NATO/space subsystem ‚Äî model the Cold War as (a) the generic war engine (#45) + (b) scripted events/event-flavor + (c) the diplomacy meters (#107). BUT the **war engine needs branches + a real resolution model + white-peace rules** (DH-47), and the **USSR-collapse / Cold-War-end trigger chain must be re-tuned so it can actually fire** (DH-45). Pairs with #45/#107 + DH-45/DH-46/DH-47 | ingested (**nuke ¬ß4 / POST 494-500, 1101-1102, 1234, 2599-2609, 6266-6272, 6354-6358, 6674-6676, 7097-7099, 9745, 9796, 11055, 11058-11060, 11398, 11466, 11706, 11750-11752**; **terror2000 CORROBORATES the relabeled-engine modern-native (War on Terror + War in Afghanistan run the SAME d100 naval‚Üíland engine, no MAD/nuke subsystem, Air-Force/Marine generals are naming-only POST 50-56) AND refines "wars never resolve on their own": here the War on Terror DID RESOLVE ‚Äî in DEFEAT (POST 639, see #152) ‚Äî and Afghanistan ran to Phase II; so wars CAN end, incl. in loss; Iraq War never fired (timeline diverged)**; **nixon1972 ‚Äî a 1972-start pins the Watergate gate: "75% chance to fire with a president who has CONTROVERSIAL" / "Can't fire, controversial is required" (POST 16, 18) ‚Äî confirms Watergate = a Controversial-gated ~75% EVENT (no impeachment trigger), exactly as this row states; never fired live (thread stalled in turn 1)**; **‚òÖ wilsons1916 (batch 29) ‚Äî WAR ENTRY IS EVENT-GATED AND OPTIONAL/SKIPPABLE: the US NEVER entered WWI the entire 1916‚Üí1920 run (Wilson kept the no-war promise; the war-entry event simply never fired), and "Because we've not entered WWI, no military phase" (P230) ‚Üí the ENTIRE military/combat subsystem stays DORMANT unless the war-entry decision is taken. Congress created a Selective Service (P320, "handy for that World War thing") and grew the army/navy WITHOUT a war ever running. Contrast `solo1916`, where the Unrestricted-Submarine-Warfare "Enter the World War" A/B decision WAS presented ‚Äî so the same era can branch into or out of the war. Confirms wars are optional event-gated triggers (#106's "Cold War as events" generalized): the war engine doesn't run on a schedule; it runs only when its entry event is accepted. Pairs with #45/#107/#152**; **‚òÖ (b40) the `military` genre (145db158 POST 2-3) reinforces this row's "no army/navy/air branch separation" finding from the CONTENT side: the RNG tech-testing Gov-Actions (#221) increment a **per-branch fighting-power** (army/navy/air strength) the single shipped `military` scalar cannot separate ‚Äî i.e. the design wants the branch-separation #106 found missing, with per-branch power as a tracked quantity. military POST 2, 3**) |
| 107 | **‚òÖ Diplomacy subsystem ‚Äî 8 per-nation relation meters + ambassador action menu + d√©tente/summit exec actions + a Diplomacy phase (the real, working Cold-War part)** ‚Äî NEW | A Diplomacy phase exists (#26) but no per-nation relation ladder, ambassador roster, or summit/d√©tente actions | **The working modern foreign-affairs system** (vs the non-system #106). **8 per-nation relation meters** (UK, France, Spain, Germany, Russia/"Soviet Union", China, Japan, Israel) on a **9-point ladder** (Hostile < Enemy < Neutral < Friendly < Allies). Each nation has a **standing Ambassador** (cabinet-level appointment; eligibility = Admin 2+ & Business/Foreign-Affairs/Trade; can't send to a Hostile-or-worse / at-war nation; **Naive-Strategist trait = ‚àí1 to every ambassador roll**). In the **Diplomacy phase (2.7.1)** the Sec of State *suggests* one action per ambassador; the President approves/overrides; each ambassador sent once/phase. **Ambassador actions:** Increase Relations (¬±1), Increase Trade Relations (‚â•neutral ‚Üí +rev/budget), Extend Credit / **Take a Loan** (‚â•neutral; +rev/budget; tracked on a Loans/National-Debt credit-debt scale, **gated by the Rev-Budget level** to stop a debt-manipulation exploit), Provoke (‚â§neutral; ‚àí1 relation + 1‚Äì2% war chance, needs Congress until the Pres has full tariff power). **D√©tente/summit EXEC actions:** "First President to Visit the USSR", "D√©tente Policy w/ USSR" (+1 Russia), US‚ÄìUSSR Summit (25%/25% improve/worsen, +to 35% with Foreign-Affairs/Sec-State 4‚Äì5). **Cold War status hard-caps Russia/China relations ‚â§ Neutral while active.** **EraEvo "wars" (Six-Day, Yom Kippur, Coup in Haiti) are pure relation-meter ¬±1 flavor**, distinct from engine wars (#45/#106) | Add a **diplomacy module**: 8 per-nation relation meters (era-dependent roster, #25b) + a standing-ambassador appointment slot per nation + the ambassador action menu (Increase/Trade/Credit/Loan/Provoke, rolled by Admin, with the Naive-Strategist + send-once + can't-send-to-hostile gates) + a credit/national-debt scale gated by Rev-Budget + d√©tente/summit exec actions + Cold-War relation hard-cap. **BUT the meters need downward pressure** (DH-46 ‚Äî currently "allied with everyone"). Pairs with #25b/#26/#27/#106 + DH-46 | ingested (**nuke ¬ßDiplomacy / POST 270, 343, 475, 1089-1094, 1397, 1868, 1895, 2057-2062, 2117, 2133, 8990-8993, 9792-9998, 10841, 10914, 11058-11060**; **ideo1928 ‚Äî corroborates the diplomacy subsystem from a 1928 angle with a 7-NATION era roster (UK/France/Spain/Germany/Russia-Soviet/China/Japan ‚Äî ‚òÖ NO Israel yet, confirming the era-dependent roster, #25b) + the ambassador action menu Increase-Relations/Trade-Relations/Extend-Credit/Take-a-Loan per-nation ¬±1 [POST 403-404] + interwar decision-events (Purchase-Iceland, Lausanne-war-debt, Japan-Manchuria-embargo, Russian-trade-tension) driving relation meters [POST 261-267, 809, 862]; see NEW #162**; **‚òÖ (b39) `b8aecb83` (AMPU Diplomacy authoring thread, Apr 2022) is the STRONGEST case yet that the per-nation diplomacy STATE model must GROW from the shipped flat relation scalar (`GameState.diplomacy: Record<string,number>`, `types.ts:1574`, clamped ‚àí5..+5) into per-nation **{relations, recognized?, aidLevel, trade/embargo status}** + **per-nation `wars`** (today war is a GLOBAL `wars: string[]`, `types.ts:1575`, not per-nation). The authored content keys off per-country game-STATE not the calendar: `At War with this country` gates embargoes; `Relations worse than neutral` gates boycotts/sanctions; the Recognize‚ÜîDon't-Recognize set (9 nations + breakaway states) implies a per-nation recognized bit; the Aid/Increase/Decrease/Withdraw/End sets imply a per-nation foreign-aid LEVEL distinct from the relation number. | 243 | **‚òÖ War-death rate must SCALE to roster size + an ERA-GATED death-reduction (cap deaths / tune % / drop % once DoD-CJCS exists) ‚Äî NEW (`kiaofficers`)** | The only combat-death code is `revolutionaryWar.ts` `applyCasualties` (`:67-121`): difficulty-tiered death/wound dice pick victims from pols with `military >= 1 && !Frail` and call `recordDeath(snap, id, 'battle')`; **there is NO death-% scalar, NO roster-vs-deaths governor, and NO era-conditional reduction** ‚Äî and no combat-death loop exists for any war beyond RevWar | **The war out-kills the historical KIA roster.** vcczar (`kiaofficers` POST 1, 2, 8, 12): the battle-death loop kills more military pols than the dataset historically has dying in those wars ("the deaths are exceeding the number of in-game politicians that died in those respective war"), draining the KIA-eligible pool ‚Äî "it's not like historically we've had boatloads of flag officers die in combat‚Ä¶ for the entire history of the country." Two committed levers: **(a) SLIGHTLY lower the death %** (a RevWar half-term was producing Civil-War-level officer fatalities; reversible/tunable, magnitude unquantified) and a **post-early-release MEASUREMENT LOOP** ‚Äî measure avg generals/admirals/career-track pols killed per playtest vs the # of historically-killed statesmen in the dataset ‚Üí if UNDER, raise the %; if still OVER, add more military pols (POST 12); **(b) an ERA-GATED reduction** ‚Äî death %s drop further once the **Chairman of the Joint Chiefs / DoD** exists (post-DoD flag officers command from the rear, rarely die at the front; modern politically-ambitious KIAs are also scarcer). The thread's whole Rule-B redistricting exception (waive ideology rules for one election when a state loses a seat) presumes this reapportionment that the build lacks. `b7acabd2` POST 17, 18; code-verified `electoralVotes` fixed for the run**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b52) `074b6d6f` (AMPU Census, Sep 2022) is this row's MOST CONCRETE, ALREADY-VALIDATED spec ‚Äî the decennial census/reapportionment subsystem RUN AND TESTED in vcczar's spreadsheet (1850 census executed inside the 1840 playtest ‚Üí **EV Total 300 / House Seats 236** with mostly-historical per-state deltas, POST 4). New code-grade mechanism detail: (a) **probabilistic per-state EV-CHANGE triggers at 10%/25% odds** (POST 4 ‚Äî ~8-9 fired, 2 succeeded "statistically average") ‚Äî the explicit roll mechanism this row didn't previously specify; (b) the **435 House cap is ERA-GATED (post-1929 only)** ‚Äî the 1850 test produced 236 (PRE-cap); the House is uncapped/recomputed before 1929, so reapportionment must know the era's House-size rule (POST 7, 9); (c) a decade can **run the census but SKIP reapportionment** (1920: Congress couldn't agree ‚Üí NO seat changes 1920‚Üí1930 despite booming population, POST 5-10) ‚Äî a no-change census MODE; (d) FL projected 6-EV-min / 12.5%-of-8 post-1860 (POST 12). Code status STILL 0% shipped (verified): no `population`/`houseSeats` field on `State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`), no `435`/House-size constant, no census/apportionment phase in `phases.ts`, `electoralVotes` written only at statehood (`constitutionalConvention.ts:210`; `expansionStates.ts:178` hardcodes 4) and read only by elections ‚Äî never recomputed by population. The missing PRIMITIVE under this row = a per-state POPULATION model (NEW #305); the AZ/NM ‚àí2 event modifier = a per-state EV/apportionment EVENT modifier (also NEW #305). `074b6d6f` POST 1, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 12**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b53) `5e5735ec` (AMPU US Census, Apr 2022) is this row's MOST CONCRETE WRITTEN ALGORITHM ‚Äî the canonical census ruleset "rules 3.0.24" (POST 1, vcczar), the SPEC that PRECEDES + underlies the b52 `074b6d6f` spreadsheet RUN. (A) ‚òÖ THE FULL ORDERED 5-STEP EV RECOMPUTE, applied per-state IN ORDER: **(1)** add the **HISTORICAL** census +/- to each state's current EVs; **(2)** apply **SCRIPTED-EVENT** EV adjustments (**"Indian Removal" = the primary growth-affecting scripted event** ‚Äî the source of the AZ/NM ‚àí2-type modifier on #305); **(3)** apply **INDUSTRY-SHIFT** EV adjustments as probabilistic rolls (the exact %s sharpen #294, below); **(4)** apply **LEGIS-PROP** EV adjustments (the CC slavery question can shift a state's EV); **(5)** SUM all EVs per state with a **‚â•3 EV FLOOR**. (B) Activation/cadence: the census is **automatically in play once the US CONSTITUTION is adopted** (Constitution-triggered, NOT era-gated ‚Äî before adoption there is NO census) and alters the map at the **decennial, presidential-term-anchored** cycles **1790-92 / 1800-1802 / 1810-12 ‚Ä¶** (the change lands at the election under the new census) ‚Äî sharpens the b28 `completions` "auto-active on Constitution adoption, effect at the census-half-term-start" reconciliation. (C) ‚òÖ THE HOUSE-CAP RECONCILIATION ALGORITHM, run AFTER the 5-step sum: **if no cap exists ‚Üí SKIP entirely** (pre-1929 eras, matches the b52 era-gated 435 finding); **if a cap exists**, compare your computed EV total to **`House Cap + (number of states √ó 2)`** (the √ó2 = each state's 2 Senators), **EXCLUDING the EVs of DC, the Moon, and Mars**, then reconcile by **randomly adding/subtracting EVs** under TWO constraints ‚Äî **no state loses/gains 2√ó as many EVs as any other state** in the process AND **no state may fall below 3 EVs** ‚Äî with the POST-5 QA caveat that reconciliation must NOT silently "undo" the step-1-4 deltas (the author-flagged edge case). Code status STILL 0% shipped (re-verified b53): no census/apportionment phase (`phases.ts:3-47`), no House-cap constant, EV static (`expansionStates.ts:178`). The state-size classification + influential-Rep delegation-voting model + ahistorical-seat bias rules from this SAME ruleset are the apportionment-engine cluster broken out as NEW #307-#309 (they consume this recompute's final EVs). `5e5735ec` POST 1, 5**; **‚òÖ (b55) RECONSTRUCTION coupling ‚Äî the readmission census must count CW casualties + freed slaves ‚Äî `secession-and-civil-war` (6b680261, ¬ß6 / POST 4): readmitted states keep their **pre-secession House-seat count UNTIL the next census**, and that census **should count CW casualties** (more the longer the war ran) AND, for a Southern Confederacy, **freed slaves IF given the vote** ‚Äî i.e. the post-war census recompute gains two war-specific population inputs (war-duration‚Üícasualty deduction + a freed-slave enfranchisement‚Üípopulation add) feeding this row's per-state EV/seat recompute. Couples the (still-unbuilt) census subsystem to the Reconstruction readmission flow (#156/#284) and the per-state population model (#305). `secession-and-civil-war` ¬ß6 / POST 4** |
| 39 | **Constitutional Amendments as durable, separately-ratified state ‚Äî MUTATE CORE RULES** | `constitutionalConvention.ts` covers the *initial* Constitution; later amendments not surveyed | Amendments pass Congress then go **to the states for ratification**. They durably change rules: term-length, popular-vote-everywhere, VP-vacancy fill, court size, suffrage. **Ratification mechanism + threshold vary by era** (fed/gilded by legislatures, often unanimous / "2/3 of states"; **`modern` by GOVERNORS, 40 of 53**; **`rep1800` by Govs at 2/3, lowered from 3/4 by an earlier amendment, taking effect the next half-term, with Puritan/Integrity Gov overrides**), and amendments need a **2/3-House supermajority** (382/572). A **grandfather clause** can exempt the sitting officeholder. **‚òÖ `rep1800` makes the rule-mutation concrete ‚Äî amendments rewrite the constitutional shape MID-GAME**: the **"Sexenio" experiment** ratified 3 amendments in 1834 that turned the Presidency into a Mexico-style **single 6-year term** (16th = Universal White Male Suffrage; 17th = One-Term Limit; 18th = Six-Year Term), with a 25th-style succession rule (a VP elevated past the half-way point can't run); terms later reverted by amendment. **No grandfather clause modeled** (the real 22nd exempts the incumbent; GM: "my rules" ‚Äî design hole). NB: a two-term limit **doesn't exist until ratified** | New `amendments[]` state + cross-state ratification vote (era-keyed AND in-game-tunable ratifier + threshold, #64) + 2/3-House gate + **effect-binding that mutates core rules** (term length, one-term limit, suffrage, tariff cadence, 6-yr "Sexenio" term) + grandfather support + amendment bill type. (**4-era confirmed**; refines #16 amendments-toggle-capabilities, #64 ratifier-threshold) | ingested (gilded 159, 164, 173, 188, 276-277; fed 38, 76, 201, 214, 297, 306, 373, 468, 746; modern 15-29, 938, 1278, 1597; **rep1800 ¬ßB 5352, 5484-5595, 6095, 6134, 6411, 6431; ¬ßA 2957-2959**); **gild1868 (b14) confirms the amendment lifecycle (pass both Houses 2/3 ‚Üí states ratify in Governor‚Äôs Action Phase via a recurring "Ratify Amendment" action, ~2 turns) AND flags the 1-vs-2-turn AMBIGUITY (POST 437-445); 15th Amendment effect = "+2 incumbent next election, AfAm men can hold office" POST 434; 2-Term-Limit + Lifetime-President amendments both FAIL, POST 572, 6275-6309** |
| 100 | **SCOTUS can OVERTURN a ratified Amendment (review/repeal loop) + threshold-itself-amendable** ‚Äî NEW | Amendments treated as durable (#39); no judicial-review-of-amendment path; no mutable amendment threshold | **‚òÖ A ratified constitutional Amendment is NOT final ‚Äî SCOTUS can strike it.** Via a **Governor-requested judicial review**, the Court can **overturn a ratified Amendment** (`tea1772`: the timeline's 13th "White-Male Suffrage" Amendment was overturned **4‚Äì3**, reverting it to a mere governor-action that Congress may re-pass; a Taylor-McDowell law was similarly overturned). So the amendment lifecycle is **propose ‚Üí ratify ‚Üí (Gov-requested) judicial review ‚Üí possible repeal ‚Üí re-pass** ‚Äî a loop, not a one-way ratchet. Separately, the **amendment threshold is itself amendable** (Convention set 3/4; the "Morris 2/3 Ratification Amendment" lowered it 3/4 ‚Üí 2/3 in play) ‚Äî corroborates #64. Distinct from #52's SCOTUS-strikes-a-single-LAW and #98's amendment-only-abolition (`Cohens`): this is the Court voiding an **Amendment** itself | Add a **judicial-review-of-amendment** action (Gov-requested) to #52's SCOTUS module that can repeal a ratified amendment (‚Üí revert to gov-action / re-passable by Congress); make #39/#64's ratification threshold a mutable in-game field. Pairs with #39/#52/#64 | ingested (**tea1772 ¬ß3 / POST 48, 50, 57, 675**) |

Over a 2-yr half-term the shipped allowance is ~10 attempts/faction vs the ruling's 4 successes. FIX = a per-faction SUCCESS counter scoped to the HALF-TERM, max 4, alt-state EXCLUDED. `97aa3f6f` POST 4**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b61) THE DETAILED SELECTION SPEC + A CONTESTED-CAP RECONCILIATION ‚Äî AUTHORITATIVE CPU-RULES CHANGELOG `b83f7fd0` (Ted) REPLACES the old relocation rule with a 19-PRIORITY LIST (POST 4, extended POST 6). OLD (provenance): 20% chance to move ONE random eligible pol/phase (eligible = not US-Senator/Rep/Governor). NEW: **75% chance to attempt up to FIVE pols/phase** ‚Äî roll the 75% per attempt, stop on first fail or after 5; for each SUCCESS walk the priority list from the top and pick the first priority with an eligible pol+destination (ties ‚Üí random). **The 19 priorities are a KINGMAKER-PAIRING-MANUFACTURING engine:** 1-9 move a rookie into a KINGMAKER'S state to form a KM-prot√©g√© pairing (an "auto success") ‚Äî 1-3 overpop‚Üí(underpop/regular/any) alt where it creates a pairing, 4-6 regular‚Üí‚Ä¶pairing, 7-9 any‚Üí‚Ä¶pairing; 10-15 population-based moves of likeable / not-unlikeable / unlikeable+Teflon pols (overpop‚Üíunderpop, same-region-first); 16-19 (community-added) overpop‚Üíunderpop-alt / ‚Üíany-alt / any‚Üíunderpop-alt / any‚Üíany-alt **regardless of pairing** (Ted flags #19 as risky but keeps it for historical realism). **HARD RULE: an unlikeable pol WITHOUT Teflon is NEVER moved** (only 14/15 admit unlikeable, and only with Teflon). ‚òÖ SHIPPED = NEITHER old NOR new: `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations` (`phaseRunners.ts:623-679`) rolls a per-pol gate `cpuGate.withAltState=0.3` (‚Üímove to altState) else `withoutAltState=0.1` (‚Üíthin state by same-region‚Üífewest-residents‚Üíid), then band odds (`types.ts:242`); **no 19-priority list, no likeable/Teflon filter, no kingmaker-pairing targeting** (relocation never reads `protegeId`/`Kingmaker`). The shipped 0.3-with-alt gate is closest to priorities 16-19 (move-to-alt regardless of pairing) but ungated by population/pairing. **‚òÖ‚òÖ CONTESTED CAP ‚Äî THIS b61 CHANGELOG vs THE b58 CHANGELOG CONFLICT (route to a DESIGNER DECISION):** this b83f7fd0 rule specifies **75% √ó up-to-5 ATTEMPTS per PHASE (per turn)** ‚Äî which AGREES with shipped on unit (attempts, per-turn) and nearly on count (5) ‚Äî but **b58's `97aa3f6f` POST 4 ruled 4 MOVEMENTS (successes), per HALF-TERM, alt-state EXCLUDED**. The two AUTHORITATIVE designer changelogs disagree on BOTH the value (5 vs 4) AND the unit/scope (attempts/turn/alt-included vs successes/half-term/alt-excluded); shipped = 5 attempts/turn/alt-included. b83f7fd0 is the DETAILED SELECTION spec (the 19-priority list); the CAP value+unit is UNRESOLVED across shipped-5-attempts-turn / b58-4-successes-half-term / b61-5-attempts-turn ‚Äî a B# designer-reconciliation candidate for the roadmap. Both changelogs live under this row. COUPLES to #128/#129 (priorities 1-9 exist to feed the same-state-gated pairing infra `protegeCandidates` `phaseRunners.ts:1282`; no relocation code targets a kingmaker's state today ‚Äî the coupling is the requirement). `b83f7fd0` POST 4, 6**; **‚òÖ (`pol-additions-2024`, source `4b5d4ca7`) ‚Äî the ‚òÖ 182-column intended DATA MODEL supplies the CONCRETE SCHEMA of the historical-relocation-date DATA LAYER this row flags (the `4e518e05` layer): **three `Alternate State 1/2/3` columns + three `When Relocated to AS 1/2/3` timing columns** (`vstyle_clean.csv` cols 163-167) ‚Äî i.e. up to 3 scheduled relocations per pol, each with a dated move year, vs the shipped SINGLE `altState?` (`Politician types.ts:1256`). Real worked examples the model encodes: **Matthew Lyon** VT‚ÜíKY (AS=KY, relocated 1801 ‚Äî he literally represented two states' delegations); **Laphonza Butler** MD‚ÜíCA (relocated 2021, 2023); **Ira Allen** VT‚ÜíPA (relocated 1803). This is the multi-alt + timing form the shipped model can't express ‚Äî pairs the relocation SCHEDULE with the #302 party-switch schedule and the #4/#171 ideology-shift schedule (all authored as per-pol dated events in the same model). See digest `playtest-digests/pol-additions-2024.md`**) + codebase (types.ts:247) |
| 109 | **Era CONTENT is scripted and IGNORES player history (distinct from era BANDS being game-state-gated)** ‚Äî NEW | Era events fire from the 1772 precondition graph / 1856 spine; no concept of "scripted content fires regardless of divergence" | **Keep two things separate (the build must encode BOTH):** era **BANDS** advance on game-state/territory (#92), but era **EVENTS/CONTENT** are **scripted and fire on a schedule that ignores in-game history.** Events land out of historical order vs the in-game clock (Hungarian Revolution, James Meredith, Woodstock, Stagflation, Roe, Watergate, AIDS, Stonewall fire in decoupled windows); the **SCOTUS docket fires on its era schedule regardless of divergence** (Roe in a 1966-equivalent cycle; Lawrence/Romer run "early" because the playtest is so left). **Clearest statement:** a player who ended Jim Crow EARLY (GOP-Taft, by legislation + federal troops) **cannot pre-empt the Civil-Rights-Movement era content** ‚Äî downstream events depend on it, so the GM **house-rules a ~5%/phase trigger roll** to fire it anyway. Civil Rights is the canonical example of "era content distributed across generic mechanics" ‚Äî spread across **scripted events + ordinary ideology/lobby-scored bills + a schedule-fired SCOTUS docket** (flagged "this is the historical outcome"), never a bespoke subsystem. Counter-historical play is **allowed** but does not rewrite the scripted deck. (Some events ARE gated on a prior choice ‚Äî Nixon Doctrine gated OUT because the US never entered Vietnam ‚Äî so the model is "mostly schedule-fired, with selective game-state preconditions") | Model era content as a **schedule-fired deck independent of player divergence** (NOT gated on game-state like the bands), with: optional per-event preconditions (a few events keyed to a prior choice), a **~5%/phase catch-up trigger** when a player's history would otherwise skip required content, and a "historical outcome" flag on scripted-outcome events (esp. the SCOTUS docket, #52). Distinguish sharply from #92 (bands ARE game-state-gated). (5-source confirmed)**; **‚òÖ (b39) `f735601c` (AMPU Rules Update, Aug 2021) is the DESIGN-ORIGIN of "earn Command in-game" that this row's doubled-gain-% later formalizes: answering Ted's "too few ways to gain command," vcczar ADDED two new Command-acquisition paths ‚Äî **(1) Keynote Speaker at the Convention** = a small chance of +1 Command available to ANYONE, even a 0-Command pol (POST 17, the first documented bootstrap-from-zero source that isn't a scripted event); **(2) a tiny % chance of Command via certain career tracks** (POST 20, explicitly "won't help Benedict Arnold" ‚Äî not all tracks grant it). (The Washington-Rule-can-FAIL nuance from this same thread is at #104.) `e86d8661` POST 1**; **‚òÖ (b50) `58da1636` FAQ supplies the AUTHORITATIVE ~22-source COMMAND-EARNING enumeration this row's doubled-gain-% multiplies (POST 195-200, the table behind "earn Command in-game"): **faction leader 20%; party leader 75% first election / 25% after; winning the Pres election 50%; re-elected VP 25%; Sec-of-State 10% first confirm; Continental-Congress president 20%; committee chair 10%; military leadership 20% in a major war; governing/Senate longevity; prot√©g√© of a command-kingmaker; events & crisis legislation 10%; keynote/filibuster small chances** (~22 ways total). ‚òÖ Command can also be LOST: **losing a medium/easy battle can take command away** (POST 199, ties #143 command-decay + the RevWar battle-loss model #45). FAQ POST 100-101 confirms the principle: most skills/traits seed from real-life achievement, but **command, leadership (and ~controversial) must be EARNED in-game** unless the start year is past when they earned them IRL. This is the concrete spec for the under-built command-gain channels the √ó2 multiplier applies to. `58da1636` POST 100-101, 195-200**) |

So the build coincidentally matches the poll's HOUSE answer (leave empty) ‚Äî but for EVERY seat, without the special-election framing, and implements NONE of the CT pull / generator / 50-50-retire / House-Senate split. `cb91e00e` POST 2, 3, 4, 5, 6**) |
| 155 | **‚òÖ Civil-War engine is TOO EASY for the Union (balance) ‚Äî NEW (`hd1`)** | No war model (#56 captures the engine); `revolutionaryWar.ts` 1772-scoped | **Both human players AND the GA flag the war system as far too generous to the Union** (`hd1` POST 1000-1004, after "2 civil wars where the Union is winning easily"): **(a)** the end-war **multiplier is too high** (1.0 ‚Üí players demanded **0.5**, **adopted live** POST 1314); **(b)** war scoring should be **per-theater** (adopted live); **(c)** **Officer-Mil dominates the formula** ‚Äî "a 5-Mil leading officer gives a larger bonus than all other factors combined," so a board of 4-5 Mil officers makes any war a near-lock; **(d)** there is **no real enemy-strength term** ‚Äî "repelling a British invasion is only slightly easier than a native tribe, and is *easier* than the Civil War"; **(e)** a Difficult-battle loss (‚àí1) is **too small** ‚Äî **battle SIZE should matter** (Bull Run small vs Gettysburg/Wilderness large). **COUNTER-CONSTRAINT:** don't over-harden ‚Äî the **1772 Revolutionary War is a game-over on a loss**, so a harder engine risks ending games before they begin (POST 1004) | Add an **enemy-strength term** + **battle-size weighting** to the war success formula (#56/#45); tune the war-end multiplier (reconcile 1.0-vs-0.5) + per-theater scoring; **cap Officer-Mil's share** of the formula; keep the 1772 RevWar floor playable. COMPOUNDED by NEW DH-81 (the per-battle game-over) AND by MilPrep stuck at 2 all war (#176). 3rd/4th-source proof (joins `ted1772`/`grass1772`/`rookie1772`) that the 1772 floor MUST stay playable under any #155/#56 hardening; the doubled-officer Planning cap edge = NEW DH-82. `06fbb2e5` POST 73-74, 81-92, 112-118, 123**) |
| 156 | **‚òÖ Revised Reconstruction ruleset (vcczar) ‚Äî 4 plan types on BOTH branches + a "plan-adopted" PREREQUISITE that breaks the deadlock ‚Äî NEW (`hd1`, AUTHORITATIVE)** | None (extends #57) | **vcczar's authoritative rewrite at the `hd1` restart** (POST 2693-2694), the design payoff of the whole Part-1 run: **FOUR readmission plan types ‚Äî No-reconstruction-plan / 10% plan / Ironclad (Wade-Davis) / Military-district (= historical Congressional Reconstruction) ‚Äî available to BOTH the President AND Congress.** **‚òÖ The deadlock-breaking PREREQUISITE:** ALL Southern-state readmissions require **"a reconstruction plan adopted by Congress OR by the President"** ‚Äî so the President can **unilaterally adopt** one (no more null drift). **States need INDIVIDUAL readmission only under the Ironclad or Military-district plan** (No-plan / 10% = states just come back). **Pardon types** added to both branches. **15th-Amendment effect** = **+2 state bias in all Deep-South states / +1 in all other former-seceded states toward the incumbent party while Reconstruction is active** + "African-American men can now hold office" (sunsets when Reconstruction ends ‚Äî formalizes #57's Solid-South bias). 13th/14th adjusted; plan/pardon ideology-impact tables re-tuned. **CSA-victory branch ruling** (POST 2692): seceded pols removed, Unionists move to the nearest loyal state, events drive eventual reintegration | Implement the **4-plan Reconstruction model** with the **"plan adopted by Congress OR President" prerequisite** as the deadlock-breaker (President may adopt unilaterally); individual readmission only under Ironclad/Military-district; the +2 Deep-South / +1 other-seceded incumbent-party bias keyed to active Reconstruction; pardon tiers on both branches; CSA-victory remove-and-reintegrate branch. **This is the canonical Reconstruction design and the direct fix for DH-29.** Pairs with #57/#58/DH-29 | ingested (**hd1 POST 2680-2687, 2692-2694**; **‚òÖ (b47) the SOUTHERN-LED Reconstruction BRANCH (South-wins inverse) sharpened ‚Äî `9c383f22` POST 3: if the North loses / South wins, Reconstruction flips to "probably some of the most racist stuff imaginable, with factions having the 'Civil Rights' card being expelled from government." So Reconstruction is **outcome-BRANCHED**: the North-wins tug-of-war (this row + #57/#284) has a mirror South-wins track that (a) swaps legislation to its most-racist form and (b) **EXPELS `CivilRights`-card factions from government** ‚Äî keying on a REAL shipped faction tag (`CivilRights` / `SlaveryRights` / `StatesRights` / `Abolition` / `Antislavery`, `eraEvents1856.ts:325-361`), but the faction-EXPULSION mechanic itself is 0% shipped. (2) ‚òÖ MILITARY-DISTRICT apparatus: on war end (**phase 2.7**) formerly-seceded states go IMMEDIATELY into **5 military districts** under Union Generals (don't wait for the Congressional phase), auto-proposed in Congress for "affirmation"; the **President appoints Governors (2-yr / half-term terms, in phase 2.8 Executive)** ‚Äî Gov needs ‚â•1 Gov skill + in-state residence; **ALL real-life pols (Blue & Red) must be appointed before any are created**; no term limits; the state gets **no Congressional representation** until Congress readmits it, and the President **loses** Gov-appointment power there as states return via Congress. (3) ‚òÖ Readmission also requires **14th-Amendment ratification**. (4) The named 3 plans = **Immediate/pre-war-bias** / **10% Ironclad-Oath (Andrew Johnson's; no party-pref penalty)** / **"Strict Majority Loyalty w. Ironclad Oath"** (states voted INDIVIDUALLY; each readmitted state shifts **+2 toward the incumbent** AND its Gov/Congress party-pref shifts **+2 in the OPPOSITE direction of its historical-majority** for **8 YEARS / 4 HALF-TERMS** ‚Äî e.g. TN Blue+2‚Üíneutral; CT Red+1‚ÜíBlue+1) ‚Äî reconciles with this row's 4-plan list (`hd1`'s No-plan/10%/Ironclad/Military-district). Build re-verified (b55): still NO Reconstruction phase (`phases.ts` has 2.7/2.8 but no military-district/martial-law/Gov-under-occupation/readmit step), no `readmission`/`reconstruction`/`militaryDistrict` token anywhere in `src/`.
