| 158 | **‚òÖ CPU leans toward PEACE / game-over decisions ‚Äî a SOLO-game-ender (esp. the 1772 RevWar) ‚Äî NEW (`ted1772`)** | No CPU war/surrender/game-over vote logic; `revolutionaryWar.ts` 1772-scoped | **The marquee mostly-CPU finding, and a direct balance threat to #155.** When a war/era event offers a game-ending peace (rejoin Britain / accept terms), **CPUs vote FOR it by their point-math** ‚Äî they don't weight "keep the game alive." In `ted1772` two game-over peace events (Carlisle Peace Commission, Conciliatory Resolution) were defeated ONLY by the 2/3 Articles-of-Confederation threshold (Carlisle got a 5-4 *majority* for peace = 55.5% < 66%) [POST 461, 637]. Ted flagged it explicitly: *"most players will be single player, and if the CPU constantly leans towards peace, there is going to be a lot of frustration"* and patched it LIVE with a new rule ‚Äî **"CPU factions oppose automatic game-over decisions 75% of the time, independent of all other considerations"** [POST 638] ‚Äî plus a proposed points fix (*"give points to most ideologies to be opposed to peace and 90% of the time that's the way the CPU will swing,"* POST 459-460). Same theme-blind point-math root as #74/DH-21/OC-3 (Red CPU voted NAY on the Declaration "bc it damages the other party more if it fails," POST 143, 167) | Implement a **CPU bias against game-ending/surrender decisions** (the flat 75%-oppose rule OR the points-based anti-peace bias ‚Äî human call which); ensure the 1772 RevWar's peace/game-over paths can't be CPU-passed in a solo game. **Bears directly on #155** (the RevWar-winnable floor) + #114 (solo-app is the target mode). "The first playthrough in testing history to actually reach a scripted ending" (Ted). | 176 | **‚òÖ Founding MilPrep meter is hard-capped at 2 for the WHOLE Era of Independence ‚Üí the auto-forced war bills are dead rolls + a permanent military crisis ‚Äî NEW (`grass1772` + `rookie1772`)** | `revolutionaryWar.ts` 1772-scoped; the MilPrep meter ladder + its per-tier legislative prerequisites are not founding-audited (`Meter` UI is a generic shell; no founding meter-prereq gating in `src/`) | **A genuinely-new founding rules/balance gap, surfaced INDEPENDENTLY by both batch-24 1772 runs.** Military Preparedness starts at 2 and **cannot rise above ~2 for the entire Era of Independence** because the meter's tier prerequisites are mis-ordered: **MilPrep 3-4 require the Militia Act (a federalism-era bill, ~1792)**, while the **auto-forced 1774 war bills (Continental Army / Continental Navy)** are wired to the HIGHER tiers (5-6). So **every MilPrep roll from the forced opening war legislation is wasted, every game** ‚Äî "until the Constitution exists, AFTER the war is over, military preparedness can never increase, rendering all war legislation completely pointless‚Ä¶ It's a glaring issue" (`grass1772` Cal POST 88-90; Ted confirms the cap, POST 86/88). The rookie hit the same wall blind ("so much for the 'Continental Army' of minutemen!", `rookie1772` POST 32-33). Result: a **permanent Military-Preparedness CRISIS** for the whole founding era + a recurring ‚àí1 election drag. Cal's house-rule fix (used live, flagged for V): reverse the MilPrep 1-4 prerequisite ordering so Continental Army ‚Üí MilPrep 3 and Continental Army+Navy ‚Üí MilPrep 4 (treat Continental Army/Navy as Standing Army/Navy), capping the founding era at ~4-5 so the forced war bills actually help. **Bears on #155/#56 (the forced founding war legislation must be able to move the war-relevant meter) + #67 (meter caps/prerequisites).** | Audit the founding-era MilPrep ladder prerequisites: the auto-forced Continental Army/Navy bills must be able to raise MilPrep (cap ~4-5 pre-federalism), else the 1772 RevWar's forced bills are dead rolls and the era sits in permanent military crisis. Surface meter prerequisites/caps in-UI (a new player can't see them ‚Äî DH-69). Human call: keep the founding military crisis as intended, or adopt the reverse-prereq fix. | 160 | **‚òÖ The Great Depression is a SCRIPTED ERA EVENT with VARIABLE timing that carries a BUNDLE of meter shocks + an EconStab CASCADE ‚Äî and the era's static party-modifiers are decoupled from it (balance) ‚Äî NEW (`ideo1928`)** | No EconStab/economic-stability meter, no Great-Depression event, no `progressive` era in shipped code (`src/types.ts` `Era` = `independence/federalism/nationalism/modern`; `Meter.tsx` is a generic UI shell; 0 hits for `econStab`/`depression` in `src/`) | **The headline 1928 finding + the concrete face of the EconStab meter (#116, interwar angle).** The crash is **NOT auto-fired on the real Oct-1929 schedule** ‚Äî the in-game economy stayed "great" into Jan 1930 (`ideo1928` POST 567) and players debated keeping prior-era modifiers **"until the great depression event hits, if it ever does"** (POST 545); it finally fired in the 1930-1932 turn (POST 862). The **"Wall Street Crash and Great Depression" event is a META-EVENT** carrying a bundle: **EconStab ‚àí4 (‚ÜíRecession & Crisis), Rev/Budget ‚àí2 (‚Üíoverspending/Crisis), QoL ‚àí1, Mil Prep ‚àí1, Party-Pref ‚àí3** + a binary presidential choice (let-markets-resolve / congressional-bailout). The event text encodes the **interconnected-global-economy** ("The London Stock Exchange crash has led to our own crash on Wall Street"). **EconStab CASCADE:** at Recession, **2 random industries drop ‚àí1 nationwide in EVERY state** + a Dom-Stab-drop chance; industry-leadership flips ‚Üí **EV changes** (CO+1/OK‚àí1 here, POST 866-887); EconStab also **gates Dom-Stab/QoL increases** while in crisis (POST 723). **‚òÖ BALANCE DELTA (GA post-mortem, POST ch18-8): "These era bonuses are SUPER STRONG‚Ä¶ the 'Depression' wasn't nearly as bad this time around. For **who becomes acting President**: the dead Pres's party picks a new party leader ‚Üí the **US House votes 1-vote-per-state between the two party leaders** (ineligible PL ‚Üí highest-PV eligible faction leader; cascade if a party can't field one; CPU votes party-line except Integrity‚Üíincumbent-party + Can-Be-Independent‚Üíclosest-ideology; tied states abstain; total state-tie ‚Üí SCOTUS / game-over). **DomStab penalty SCALED by outcome**: incumbent-party-PL = 0; incumbent faction leader = ‚àí1; challenging PL = ‚àí2; challenging faction leader = ‚àí3. A **coup branch** fires if a player has Controversial + (LW/RW-Pop or Military Leader) ‚Üí the 3.0.2 coup rules. The simpler interim default (vcczar, POST 849-850) is **PPT-as-acting-President until a succession bill passes**; a PPT/Speaker who inherits **resigns from Congress** and is treated like a VP-who-inherited for retirement rolls. Distinct from #61 (the normal line) ‚Äî this is the **whole-line-empty fallback**. Pairs with #61 (succession/acting-president scope) + #88 (coup) + #112 (institutional layer). **Open edges:** ineligible-PPT (foreign-born/young) handling; special-election-vs-House-choice-vs-game-over default | Spec the no-successor crisis: emergency-Congress succession-law vote (auto-signed/un-vetoable), House 1-vote-per-state acting-President election with the eligibility cascade + CPU vote rules, scaled DomStab penalty, the coup branch, and the all-positions-vacant fill order (President‚ÜíVP‚Üígovernors‚Üí‚Ä¶). The thread also names the *WHY* behind era-keying: Hestia's POST-6 coalition-vs-ideology question (*"do our factions set what is a 'faction' simply by having a coalition of people, or do the ideologies set the factions?"*) + vcczar's concession that a hard ideology gate *"isn't realistic [because] that's generally what happens in history"* (coalitions hold ideologically-mismatched members) ‚Äî i.e. restriction should be LOOSER in the modern present where coalitions are messier (exactly this row's modern-OFF state) and TIGHTER in early/realigning eras. `567b03c8` POST 1, 6**; **‚òÖ (b52) `7d91c4c7` CORROBORATES the toggle's design space from the rollout side: vcczar proposes the matrix restriction roll out starting at **1788 (Era of Federalism)** ("This will likely require adjusting the factions," POST 1) ‚Äî the early-era ON edge ‚Äî and the matrix SHIFTING per era is exactly the "era-keyed" half of this row; plus the two optional MODES that bracket the restricted default (parties-only; explicitly-unrestricted ‚Äî see NEW #302/#263). POST 9 = "allow all these options as options" (hard-set / soft-priority / unrestricted as switchable modes). `7d91c4c7` POST 1, 9**; **‚òÖ (b56) `0af55af3` (Party-shift inevitability, Dec 2023) CORROBORATES this toggle as a user-facing SETTINGS switch (atop the era-keyed automatic flip): "you can turn off draft restrictions like this in the settings, if you so choose" (theFreezerFlame, POST 6), and POST 7 frames the polarity ‚Äî **default = restrictions ON ‚áí the historical party shift happens "as it did historically"; OFF ‚áí ahistorical play (Republicans-stay-liberal, draft AOC) "certainly possible."** Both forms coexist (era-keyed auto-flip OFF in the modern present, #171/`modernday`; AND a manual global override anywhere via settings, #263/#306). Re-confirmed 0% built: grep `draftRestriction`/`restrictDraft`/`unrestrictedDraft` over `src/` = ZERO; the only restriction shipping is the hardcoded `isExpansion1772` 1772-inaugural branch (`phaseRunners.ts:38`), neither era-keyed-general nor toggleable. `0af55af3` POST 6, 7**) |

NB this always-vote Top-Four DIFFERS from the cross-party-CAP Top-Four (State/War-Def/AG/Treasury, POST 40) ‚Äî the two "Top-Four" sets are not identical (AG is in the cross-party cap, NOT the always-vote set) ‚Äî flag for the build. ‚óÜ PARTY-WIDE BLOCK (`rulebook` POST 16): the SML needs Manipulative or Leadership + a stated reason ‚Äî Controversial / Too-Partisan (LW/RW Activist) / Inexperienced (admin ‚â§2) / Wrong-Personality (Disharmonious or Unlikable) / Just-for-Spite (Iron-Fist only); a harmonious SML may only block on inexperience; block cap = the opposing leader's Legislative Power; a blocked officer ‚Üí Pres picks a replacement that ALLEVIATES the stated concern (Controversial‚Üíneeds Integrity; Too-Partisan‚ÜíLib/Mod/Con; Inexperienced‚Üíadmin ‚â•3), AUTO-CONFIRMED. ‚óÜ FILIBUSTER + CLOTURE (`rulebook` POST 16): a committee-relevant Senator with Puritan can filibuster one cabinet officer (officer withdraws) unless bypassed; INVOKE CLOTURE = SML w/ Manipulation + higher Legislative than the filibusterer forces a vote (this is the cloture half DH-76 found missing). ‚óÜ PRESIDENT'S TOOLS (`rulebook` POST 16): higher Legislative than the SML ‚Üí compel confirmation; Iron-Fist (same-party SML) ‚Üí compel; Legislative ‚â•1 ‚Üí convert up to (Legislative#) moderate/pliable/own-ideology Senators (5-6 roll; 4-6 vs pliable). ‚óÜ ADMIN-1 PENALTY (`apptdeepdive` POST 82): a nominee with only 1 in the relevant skill ‚Üí a senator who'd vote AYE has a 50/50 flip to NAY (the explicit DH-23 root-cause mechanic). ‚óÜ TRAIT‚ÜíVOTE = 75% (`apptdeepdive` POST 93): trait impact on a confirmation vote changed from 100% certainty to 75% (some still 100%/50-50) ‚Äî humans can no longer predict every vote. ‚óÜ OFFICE-VACANCY CASCADES (`rulebook` POST 16): confirming a sitting Governor ‚Üí placeholder same-state Lt-Gov; confirming a sitting Senator ‚Üí Governor replaces or leaves vacant. The shipped `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (phaseRunners.ts:2158) auto-picks the top-scored candidate and logs "confirmed" with NO vote/block/filibuster/cloture/nuclear-option ‚Äî this entire subsystem is the canonical spec for #172 + DH-76 + the promise system #181. rulebook ¬ßM2/¬ßZ POST 16, 32, 34, 35; apptdeepdive ¬ßG POST 57, 82, 87, 88, 92, 93, 100, 102**; **‚òÖ (b38) `fixes` (a852d451 POST 8) supplies a CABINET-CRISIS CONFIRMATION SAFEGUARD that is the design intent closing the shipped `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158`) auto-confirm-WITHOUT-vote loophole noted in this row: appointing a low-Administration (Admin-1/Admin-2) officer to a Lingering-Phase crisis ‚Üí escalating point loss (‚àí100 first ‚Üí ‚àí1000/‚àí500/‚àí1000 subsequent), party-pref & Ideo-Enthusiasm drops, a chance to add Controversial to BOTH the President and the appointee, **NOT Iron-Fist auto-confirmable** (carve-out folded on #77), and **Integrity/Harmonious senators get an ADDITIVE oppose-chance** (+10%‚Üí+100% by tier ‚Äî maps onto the apptdeepdive confirmation-inflation fix). This is the DESIGNED anti-exploit that the whole #172 confirmation subsystem replaces the auto-confirm with; note the TENSION with DH-23 (the shipped accidental over-rejection) ‚Äî the safeguard makes low-Admin crisis appointees intentionally harder to confirm. (3) **Acquitted-bonus / convicted-malus NEXT-election modifier** (POST 4): a per-pol one-shot election modifier seeded by a prior arrest‚Üíacquit/convict roll ‚Äî does NOT exist (no acquit/convict resolution; trait bands like Controversial `presGeneral` ‚àíMEDIUM `types.ts:831` are static, not event-seeded per-pol) ‚Üí folds to the election-scoring cluster #292/#189 (b62). The enthusiasm/party-pref/domestic-stability EFFECT half is already expressible on `EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) ‚Äî a missing TEMPLATE, not a type. ‚òÖ The proposer's #1 choice ("do it however we do Debs's arrest") is a DEAD REFERENCE: no `Debs` politician or event exists at HEAD (grep=0; Debs is a Progressive-era figure, shipped eras are 1772/1856) ‚Äî any arrest mechanic is net-new. This whole feature is MODERN-flavored; moot for the shipped eras. `5500e143` POST 2, 3, 4**) |

### Leadership pipeline

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 28 | **Congressional Leadership pipeline (2.2.1)** | Phase exists | Speaker, House/Senate Maj+Min Leaders + Whips + **Pres Pro Tem** (`modern`: auto-assigned by seniority); **majority leadership of the dominant party is uncontestable** (the minority side can be challenged by Mod factions); challenges resolved by **weighted/ranked vote**; **committee-chair eligibility requires prior committee service** (`modern`: **Leadership OR Legis ‚â• 2**); each role grants ¬±points + traits. `modern` adds a **CPU committee auto-fill** rule (keep current, even out new, Ranking‚ÜíChair). `drums` confirms the **on-win-bundle is asymmetric**: Speaker on win loses Obscure + gains Leadership/Legis+1/Kingmaker; Speaker re-elect Legis+1+Magician; **PPT gains nothing** | Pipeline runner + RCV/weighted vote + committee-eligibility check + role-effect table + Pro-Tem-by-seniority + CPU auto-fill + asymmetric on-win bundle. ### Modern macro-state, enthusiasm engine & SCOTUS (NEW ‚Äî mostly modern-era)

The prior threads saw meters/enthusiasm/SCOTUS in pieces; the `modern` thread
supplies the concrete, mature specifications below ‚Äî the spine of the modern
election engine.

| 50 | **The named meter bank (concrete set) + crisis/cascade rules** | `NationalMeters` exists (types.ts:1399): revenue, economic, military, domestic, honest, quality, planet | The concrete modern bank, each on a **banded-text ladder** nudged ¬±1 by phases/cabinet/"Lingering"/"Revision to Mean"/"Volatility"/legislation: **Revenue-Budget, Economic Stability, Military Preparedness, Domestic Stability, Honest Gov't, Quality of Life, Planet's Health, Party Preference** + **per-ideology enthusiasm** + the **8 per-power relation meters**. Dropping a meter into a bad tier **begins a named Crisis** with knock-ons; recovery ends it; meters **cascade** (one level caps/forces another); top-of-meter has hard rules (**Honest Gov't maxed deactivates all Political Machines + Gerrymandering**). **`pop` confirms the bank in a fresh-modern-boot:** rev/budget, Economic Stability, Honest Gov, Quality of Life, Domestic Stability, Military Prep, Planet Health, Party Pref, per-ideology enthusiasm, per-power relations all tick in Lingering | Verify the shipped meter set matches; add banded-text ladders + crisis entry/exit by tier + cascade rules + top-of-ladder effects. (**5+ era confirmed** ‚Äî modern is the canonical spec; **`hd` corroborates across a 2nd era and extends it ‚Äî see #67 for the full ~16-meter bank** + the 9-part Lingering resolution + policy-gated caps; **`pop` confirms in a fresh boot**) | ingested (modern 12, 114, 618, 721, 1238, 1537, 1962, 2230; **hd I-12**; **pop 463-464, 525, 539, 542, 544, 567, 681, 1124**; **pop2012b ‚Äî 2nd 2012-start corroboration: 8-meter Lingering bank ticks (Rev/Budget ‚Üí "Rev/Budget Crisis", Planet's Health ‚Üí "Crisis") + per-ideology enthusiasm, with a war-began ‚Üí +1 Party Pref ("boost in patriotism") interaction; NB Planet's Health hit "Crisis" but NO apocalypse clock fired ‚Äî confirms #88's trigger is a specific bottom-tier, not generic "Crisis" ‚Äî POST 632**) |

+400 SCOTUS confirm; ¬±100-300/bill); the "PRE-MIDTERMS STATE OF METERS" prints the 2-layer map verbatim, see #18 ‚Äî POST 285, 584, 632, 840, ch11**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ CARD-ASSIGNMENT ALGORITHM ‚Äî CANONICAL (batch 31, `rulebook` ¬ßI POST 10, the most data-dense post; the authoritative source spec for the #50/#51 card LAYER under the enthusiasm engine): each faction holds Ideology + Interest + Lobby cards re-evaluated EVERY 2 YEARS, scoring +/- points when relevant laws/events occur, driven by the STRENGTHS of the faction's own politicians. ‚óÜ Ideology cards (6: Progressive/Liberal/Moderate/Conservative/Traditionalist/Populist [= most LW+RW Populists combined]) ‚Üí the faction with the MOST pols of that ideology (PLURALITY wins, e.g. 40 Lib/39 Mod/38 Con ‚áí Liberal); ties ‚Üí cards SHARED; a faction may hold >1; EVERY faction MUST have ‚â•1 ideology card ‚Äî if none, it gets its party's Historical-Era ideology (era-specific). ‚óÜ Interest cards (8: Nationalist/Expansionist/RW-Activist/LW-Activist/Reformist/Civil-Rights/Pacifist/Theocrat) ‚Üí most-pols-with-that-interest; COMPATIBILITY BANS: Civil-Rights ‚úó Con/Trad cardholder; Expansionist ‚úó Progressive; Nationalist ‚úó Lib/Mod; Pacifist ‚úó Con/Mod; LW-Activist ONLY Lib/Prog; RW-Activist ONLY Con/Trad; Reformist ‚úó Moderate; Theocrat ‚úó Progressive. ‚óÜ Lobby cards (25+, each keyed to an EXPERTISE) ‚Üí most-matching-expertise-pols; full ban table (Wall Street[Econ] ‚úó Prog/Pop/Reformist; Big Oil&Gas ‚úó Prog/Lib/LW-Activist; LW-Media MUST have Lib/Prog; RW-Media MUST have Con/Trad; Military-Industrial ‚úó Prog/Pacifist; Big Ag/Transportation join anyone). ‚óÜ 5-POLITICIAN FLOOR: interest AND lobby cards each require the faction to have ‚â•5 pols with the required interest/expertise; if no faction qualifies, NO ONE gets that card. ‚óÜ CASCADE: when a faction is INELIGIBLE for a card, it cascades to the next-most-relevant ELIGIBLE faction. This plurality + compatibility/ban tables + 5-pol-floor + cascade is the canonical card-assignment engine the shipped meters/enthusiasm partial implementation lacks; it is the LAYER under #50/#51 and the FL ideology-binding (which enthusiasm meter the faction scores on). Corroborates the draft‚Üícard pipeline #24 (cards derive from the most-represented ideologies/interests among drafted pols, cross-party-relative). rulebook ¬ßI POST 10; corroborated by playtestdraft POST 218 (faction cards derive from draft, "depends on the Red Party Draft")**) |

(1) `scotusfuture` (aa227625) adds a per-case IMPORTANCE layer on top of the docket+decide-mechanism this row describes ‚Üí see NEW #249 (the "Landmark Tier" model + the AMPU-2 context-dependent-significance opinion-meters), and supplies the ~45-case CONTENT-FROZEN Era-of-Future SCOTUS docket (case name + constitutional question; era = thread-level key) = the data #25/E25 needs to seed the Future docket file (robot/AI personhood, consciousness-upload, space/sea-colony jurisdiction, crypto, corporate-sovereignty, US-self-dissolution; names need normalization), POST 1/7/10. TWO things sharpen #52 directly: (1) ‚òÖ the player-vs-CPU debate is FULLY ARGUED here ‚Äî ConservativeElector2 (POST 10, 12) attacks today's player-controlled court as ahistorical (justices are openly faction-commanded while real justices have NO accountability; the "Puritans must vote their ideology" rule isn't even followed; produces "Ginsburg votes down gay marriage / Scalia champions big government") with a concrete **Roe v Wade ABUSE-VECTOR (1960 game, POST 12): only the Healthcare-lobby holder (aye) + Theocrat-card holder (nay) have ANY point incentive, so 7 of 9 justices are indifferent and via wheeling-and-dealing you can have Scalia legalize abortion for a party-leadership vote at ZERO point cost** ‚Üí CE2 wants independent-by-default courts; vcczar (POST 3, 18) wants to KEEP the player SC (CPU only as an option, "I wanna play the SC even though it's p basic rn"); CE2's interim bridge (POST 14) = independent voting by an ideology/personal-interest % (not faction interest) so Scalia almost always rejects Roe but can still cast liberal-outcome 4th-Amdt votes ‚Äî the bridge from the coin-flip to the full Philosophy model. This is the SAME player-vs-CPU fork `pop` POST 479-480 / `dem1820` POST 420-443 / `oopscpu` leave unsettled; the design is genuinely unresolved. (2) ‚òÖ the SHIPPED-in-FORUM-AMPU-1 drift partial (POST 15/18): a confirmed justice has a **10% immediate ¬±1 ideology shift ON CONFIRMATION**, further shifts only per 10 yrs served, **+5% leftward** (Souter) ‚Äî a tighter, confirmation-anchored variant of this row's 10-yr drift (drums POST 7533) that is ALSO absent from the browser build; if ported, reconcile so the two drift rules don't double-fire (see #251). judiciaryidea POST 1, 2, 3, 6, 10-15, 18**; **‚òÖ (b40) `crimes` (e456b6b3 POST 3) supplies the clearest worked case of **a SCOTUS outcome writing a persistent GAME-STATE precedent FLAG that gates a whole downstream CONTENT tier** ‚Äî the obscenity doctrine **Hicklin (1879) ‚Üí Roth (1957) ‚Üí Miller (1973)**, each a SCOTUS-case scripted-event that SETS a precedent flag, with content gated on it: "If no obscenity law OR Hicklin exists ‚Üí left to the states"; "If Miller test exists ‚Üí" unlocks a distinct Legis/Gov-Action set + a Prostitution graded-severity sub-cluster. This is **precedent-as-content-gate** (the court-precedent-exists predicate, ties #258) + a **chained SUPERSESSION** S-event sequence (each test replaces the prior) ‚Äî neither expressible by the shipped coin-flip court (`phaseRunners.ts:3397`, 50%/turn on 4 hardcoded strings). Flag for consolidation whether "precedent-flag-as-availability-gate" lives under #258 or enriches this SCOTUS cluster (#249/#218/#251). NB: the batch-40 digests miscite this as "#25" ‚Äî #25 is cabinet firing-precedent/tenure, NOT the SCOTUS docket; the docket home is #52. crimes POST 3**; **‚òÖ (b41) `a863421c` (Courts) is the AUTHORING SOURCE for the full court-STRUCTURE this row references but never enumerated: (a) the complete court-SIZE LADDER ‚Äî Set No. of Justices to **5/6/7/9/10/13** by ordinary Legis-Prop (per-value era-band-flavored), with **21 & 31 EXPANSION-GATED** (21 needs US-controls-Philippines+Brazil+Mexico+Canada; 31 needs MAX-expansion ‚Äî a new gate axis tying the court to the expansion system #268, see #258); (b) **court-PACKING** as its own Legis-Prop + a **per-Pres CAP** ("Limit Court Packing to only 6 Extra Justices Per Pres") ‚Äî a self-limiting governor on packing; (c) **tenure rules** ‚Äî 40-yr & 50-yr minimum age, 70-yr & 75-yr mandatory retirement, and the **8-Year Term-Limit Amendment** (life tenure ‚Üí fixed terms; the hinge the elected-judiciary #271 composes with). Also ‚òÖ **Judicial Review as a togglable on/off INSTITUTION** (Establish-‚ÜîAbolish-Judicial-Review amendments gated on opposite states) ‚Üí the rulings‚Üílaw-deactivation hook this row owns would itself be GATED on a Judicial-Review-active flag (see #258). The shipped coin-flip (`phaseRunners.ts:~3397`) models none of it; the SC-case CONTENT system (schema + generator) is broken out as NEW #270. `964b8857`/#270 is the sister case-content thread. a863421c POST 1; 964b8857 (see #270)**; **‚òÖ (b42) `07fa6116` (Future SC Cases brainstorm, Mar-Apr 2022 ‚Äî the EARLIER ORIGIN of the docket, see #270) corroborates that the docket spans STRUCTURAL / constitutional QUESTIONS, not just rights cases: former-Pres-eligible-as-VP-after-two-terms (line of succession), Pres self-pardon + blanket pardons, Congress delegating powers to the executive, **secession by MUTUAL CONSENT** (the Texas-v-White "if all other states agreed" carve-out, POSTs 12-16), Confederate-soldiers-as-US-Veterans (states-in-rebellion vs legal-secession, antagonizes the South, POSTs 16-19), carbon/wealth tax + federalism, plus the sci-fi personhood core (AI/robot/clone/downloaded-consciousness) and space/sea jurisdiction (airspace‚Üíspace boundary, Space-"Jones-Act", sea-colony EEZ) and an Antarctica-treaty case that could overturn Medell√≠n-v-Texas. These are the upstream seed of the authored docket #270 froze. ‚òÖ NB the digest aliases this docket-data as "#25/E25"; the docket HOME is #52 (#25 is cabinet firing-precedent/tenure ‚Äî not the court). 07fa6116 POST 2-24** |
| 249 | **‚òÖ SCOTUS case-importance "Landmark Tier" model (a movement-SPAWNING tier) + context-dependent significance via drifting national OPINION METERS (AMPU-2) ‚Äî NEW (`scotusfuture`; extends #25/E25/#52, ties #6/#354)** | **0% shipped.** The shipped court (`phaseRunners.ts:3397`) is a 50%/turn coin-flip that picks a `title` from **4 hardcoded generic strings**, rules conservative/liberal by raw justice-ideology count, and nudges `partyPreference` by ¬±0.1 ‚Äî **NO docket, NO `ScotusCase` type, NO `scotusCases*.ts`, NO per-case identity, NO importance tier, NO opinion meters** (codebase-verified; same finding as #52/#218's docket gap) | **Two NEW design layers on top of the docket (#25/E25) and the decide-mechanism (#52).** **(1) LANDMARK TIER:** SCOTUS cases carry an importance/impact TIER ‚Äî vcczar names the top tier "Landmark Tier" (as big a deal as Roe/Brown/Plessy/Dred Scott, POST 1/11). Players' sharper working definition (POST 9): a Landmark case is **a ruling that can CREATE or fuel a political MOVEMENT by itself** ("like Roe ‚Üí the Pro-Life movement") ‚Äî so a Landmark ruling is an event that can SPAWN a faction/movement, not just nudge a meter, coupling the tier directly to the social-movement system (#6/#354). Needs at minimum a 2-tier per-case `tier` field (Landmark vs "less influential tier," POST 9). Tier is FUZZY/context-sensitive (POST 9 puts several cases "in this category, but I could also see them going into a less influential tier") ‚Üí which sets up (2). Consensus #1 Landmark = *Advanced Robot Inc v California* (robot personhood; flagged by every scorer). **(2) CONTEXT-DEPENDENT SIGNIFICANCE (AMPU-2, POST 8 ‚Äî the poster's own framing):** a case's importance should NOT be a fixed tier ‚Äî it depends on the playthrough. Proposed: national OPINION METERS (Religious Affiliation, Fundamentalism, Luddism, "+ others") that DRIFT from SUSTAINED policy over time (length-of-time a policy is in place, not a one-shot ‚Äî same "policy-genre over time" dynamic as #237); case significance = a FUNCTION of those meters at decision time, with **ruling-AGAINST-the-population's-drift = high significance** (worked example: *Roe Jr v Wade Jr* is explosive if the court rules pro-abortion in a high-religion/high-fundamentalism country, or anti-abortion in a low-religion/low one; robot-marriage cases keyed to Luddism + Religious-Affiliation + Fundamentalism). This **reframes the static tier (layer 1) as a v1 approximation of a v2 dynamic, meter-driven significance score.** Both layers PRESUME a per-case OUTCOME, so they couple to #52's unsettled decide-mechanism. New national-meter axis (sibling of the shipped `NationalMeters` / #237 policy-genre meters / #18/#51 enthusiasm). Adjacent to #218 (Rule-of-Four = which cases are HEARD; this is which heard cases MATTER). Pairs with #25/E25 (the docket data ‚Äî see Sources for the ~45-case Era-of-Future docket this thread froze), #52, #6/#354, #237, #18/#51 | (a) Add a per-case `tier` field (‚â•2 tiers; Landmark = movement-spawn-capable) to the docket record (#25/E25); wire a Landmark ruling into the movement-spawn system (#6/#354). (b) AMPU-2 aspirational: add drifting national opinion meters (Religious Affiliation / Fundamentalism / Luddism / ‚Ä¶) fed by sustained policy, and compute case significance = f(meter state, ruling direction-vs-drift) ‚Äî treating the static tier as the v1 fallback. The build's `resolveIdeologyShift` (phase 2.1.5) is the GENERAL faction-conversion engine over PERSONAL ideology ‚Äî NOT a SCOTUS-specific drift, and it does NOT fire on confirmation (codebase-verified) | **‚òÖ This Apr-2022 thread (Cal's proposal) is the DESIGN ORIGIN of the SCOTUS cluster ‚Äî it predates `scotusfuture`/#249 by ~6 weeks and seeds #52, #218, #249, #25/E25.** TWO big systems, both EXPLICITLY punted to AMPU-2/a DLC by vcczar (#2,#4) + CE2 (#6): **(A) JUDICIAL PHILOSOPHY** ‚Äî a justice's behavior is a SEPARATE axis from Personal Ideology, composed of **(A1) Judicial Ideology** on a Conservative/Swing/Liberal scale (per-personal-ideology BASE % table: RW-Pop 90/10/0 ¬∑ Trad 80/20/0 ¬∑ Cons 70/30/0 ¬∑ Mod 15/70/15 ¬∑ Lib 0/30/70 ¬∑ Prog 0/20/80 ¬∑ LW-Pop 0/10/90; trait mods **LW-Activist +25 Lib ¬∑ RW-Activist +25 Cons ¬∑ Pliable +50 Swing ¬∑ Lackey +50 toward active Kingmaker's direction ¬∑ Puritan ‚àí50 Swing**; mods can exceed 100% ‚Üí CPU re-normalize, an open UX Q) + **(A2) Judicial Doctrine** = one/two per-case aye/nay ¬±% modifiers on TAGGED cases, independent of ideology (the "Scalia joins the liberals on 4th-Amdt grounds" effect; real taxonomy floated: Textualism/Intentionalism/Purposivism, POST 7-9). **HIDDEN until revealed** via one of three paths (POST 1): graduating the **Judicial Career Track** (proposes renaming "State Judicial Career Track" ‚Üí "Judicial Career Track"), appointment to a **Focus Court**, or **direct SC appointment** (a gamble ‚Äî unscrutinized; the only reliable way to seat justices under 45). **(B) FOCUS COURTS** ‚Äî 4 regional single-judge appellate courts at start (New England / Mid-Atlantic / Upper South / Deep South), President-appointed to retirement, confirmed like a justice but with RELAXED opposition; jurisdiction routing (**Original** ‚Üí straight to SC; **Appellate** ‚Üí usually a Focus Court, chance to reach SC directly); Focus Courts decide INDEPENDENTLY of the player (the Focus Judge's Judicial Philosophy sets odds); a Focus Judge can gain Jurisprudence / Judicial Ability. **Rule-of-Four cert (= #218):** after a Focus Court rules, SC grants cert iff ‚â•4 of 9 justices want it (denial rare-but-possible, driven by who-appointed-whom + traits + Philosophy); if granted SC **affirms or reverses**. **‚òÖ Scoring rules:** points/meter effects **do NOT change on affirm vs reverse** (only aye-vs-nay matters; reversing has zero point effect); **cert DENIED** ‚Üí effects computed as if the Focus Judge's ruling were the SC's, EXCEPT SC justices get no points and the **Focus Judge gets the case-law points**; **deny cert on a LANDMARK case ‚Üí the SC LOSES points** (ties #249's Landmark tier); a Focus Judge whose ruling deducts the most from the appointing-President's faction (while in office) triggers a dice-roll for that faction's enthusiasm to drop; Manipulative/Iron-Fist/Jurisprudence party leaders may interfere with a granted-cert case. (Cal IGNORES state-supreme-court jurisdiction, out of scope.) **‚òÖ The ONLY thing SHIPPED here is in FORUM-ERA AMPU 1, NOT this browser build** (POST 15/18, "partially redone"): a confirmed SC justice has a **10% chance to immediately shift ideology ¬±1** on confirmation, **further shifts only per 10 yrs served**, **+5% more likely leftward** (Souter archetype) ‚Äî a small NEAR-TERM candidate (it's a tighter, confirmation-anchored variant of #52's 10-yr drift) but currently absent from the build. **‚òÖ Also fully argues the #52 debate** (see #52's annotation): CE2's independent-default-court critique with a concrete Roe abuse-vector vs vcczar's keep-the-player-SC. ### Antebellum / Civil-War / Reconstruction systems (`hd` + `rep1800`)

The `house-divided` thread is the **first 1856-native procedural record** of the
antebellum ‚Üí Civil-War ‚Üí Reconstruction ‚Üí Progressive-finale machinery. **`rep1800`
now corroborates these rows from a SECOND, DISTINCT campaign with DIVERGENT
outcomes** (a different Civil War: fires ~1846 off DomStab=1 not a secession
convention; the President defects to lead the CSA; UK-intervention theater; a
guerrilla 4th stage; National Republicans not Whigs) ‚Äî strong independent
confirmation that the engine is one ruleset, plus new variant content. The shipped
`scenario1856.ts` / `eraEvents1856.ts` spine **ends at the Trent Affair (1861)** and
has none of this ‚Äî the war these campaigns play is host-improvised content.

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 56 | **Civil War two-theater combat engine + tiered war framework** | No Civil War model; `eraEvents1856.ts` ends at Trent Affair (1861); `revolutionaryWar.ts` is 1772-scoped. **`hd1` build-check: 1856 ships a secession‚Üí`startWar` trigger + a generic `warScore`/`Battle[]` shell + a basic War Dashboard, but NO two-theater engine / named-battle casualties / war-resolution package** | A multi-term subsystem **separate from the turn loop**: **two theaters (East/West), both fought; 3 naval wins gate ground combat**; per-battle `success% = base ‚àí difficulty + planning(SecWar+SrGeneral / SecNavy+SrAdmiral) + officer(Mil√ó10, +10 Decisive) + meters(+15) + benchmarks(+15)` ‚Üí d100; **per-theater War Score** (+1 easy/naval, +3 Difficult land, ‚àí1 loss; **+10 auto-wins**; else end-of-phase roll vs WarScore√ómult to carry to next half-term); **named historical battles kill named military-track pols** (both sides); officers gain Leadership/Military-Leader/+Mil/Decisive/Celebrity; **`Naval experience` ‚âÝ a `Naval` trait** (gates Admiral). War-active globals (incumbent ‚àí2 elections at crisis DomStab; opp-VP penalty ‚àí1). **Union win**: Treaty of Appomattox, +250 to 6 lobbies, **President gains a PERMANENT +1 in ALL elections**, seceded states ‚Üí Reconstruction, 25% guerrilla-war roll. **War-hero down-ballot +1 in every state if a major war ended <20 yrs prior.** Same engine runs **tiered Major / Minor / Operation** wars (√ó-multipliers + reward packages differ): China Naval War, Red Cloud's War, Spain ‚Üí Puerto Rico; allies assist. **`drums` independently confirms the formula end-to-end** across maps 5/8/10/11 + Eastern + Western theatres + Utah + WWI + Mexico (all theatres + tiers) and adds: **WS‚â•+11 auto-win** (POST 5111, 6708); **naval-N-then-ground gating is per-war** (Mexico=3, WWI=2); **post-war defeat = |WS|√ó2√ó10%**; **officer KIA on natural 1** auto-replaced unanimously; **catastrophic 100/100**; **Treaty A-D tiers + Basic vs Special routing by Admin** (POST 6928); **WS-momentum bonus +1/-2 turn-vs-turn**. **‚òÖ `rep1800` adds CIVIL-WAR VARIANTS from a distinct campaign:** the CW can **trigger EARLY off DomStab=1** (an era-event "Southern States form the CSA" ‚Äî NOT a secession convention; this trigger uniquely fails to spawn the convention gov-action, a likely missing event field); the **sitting President can DEFECT to lead the CSA** ("Oaths to Two Masters" achievement); the engine models **Hartford-Convention + Northern-Secession** CW variants too; a **UK-Intervention theater** can spawn (3rd theater) when "UK Considering Recognition of CSA" blunders ‚Üí UK Hostile; and an improvised **guerrilla 4th stage** runs when the CSA rolls not-to-concede (GM had no chart rule). The Confederacy runs **its own internal president & elections** during the war | Build a generic multi-theater `War` model with the success-chance formula, naval‚Üíland gating (per-war N), per-theater WarScore + auto-win + carry-roll + post-war-defeat formula + KIA-on-nat-1, named-battle casualties on the military track, war-end reward package + permanent presidential election bonus, war-hero <20yr bonus, a Major/Minor/Operation tier with per-tier multipliers, **+ Treaty A-D tier + Basic-vs-Special routing**, **+ the CW VARIANT set: early-trigger-off-DomStab, President-defects-to-CSA, Hartford/Northern-secession variants, UK-intervention theater, guerrilla 4th stage, internal CSA government**. **‚òÖ CW branch points are VARIABLES, not scripted ‚Äî now confirmed across THREE independent outcomes:** `hd` (President defects to the CSA), `rep1800` (UK intervenes), and **`tea1772` (NEITHER ‚Äî a Republican prosecutes the war from the Union side, no UK intervention)** ‚Äî strong evidence the major branch points (defection, foreign intervention) are decision/roll-driven, not a single scripted path. (B) ‚òÖ POLITICIAN-SECESSION = separate Southern & Northern **stay-with-the-Union %** tables ‚Äî SOUTHERN stay%: Lib/Prog/LW-Pop **90%**, Black **99%**, Nationalist **50%**, Mod-UpperSouth **10%**, Mod-Southwest **10%**, Cons-UpperSouth **5%**, Mod-DeepSouth **3%**; NORTHERN stay%: LW-Pop/Prog **10%**, Black **1%**, Lib **25%**, Mod **50%**, Nationalist **75%**, Trad **75%**, RW-Pop **90%**. ‚òÖ NATIONAL-FIGURE MODIFIERS to the secede chance: Gov/US-Sen/US-Rep of a seceding state **+5% more likely**; President/VP/cabinet/cabinet-level **‚àí10%**; SC Justices **‚àí25%**; Generals & Admirals **‚àí10%**. (D) ‚òÖ LOYALTY TRAITS ‚Äî **Union Loyalist** (EARNED by a pol who defies a seceding home state): never secedes; **+1 party-pref to a presidential ticket** during CW/Reconstruction; **‚àí1 in home-state elections AFTER Reconstruction**; keeps the seceded home state as residence; can serve in US military/cabinet/as Ambassador (but seceded states send no Reps/Senators/Justices). **Southern Unionist** referenced as the same archetype. ‚òÖ **Provincial = 100% secession-OR-loyalty by REGION, regardless of ideology** (theFreezerFlame's accepted addition, POST 9-10) ‚Äî OVERRIDES the % tables. | 63 | **Primary Era ‚Äî state-opt-in primaries ‚Üí Presidential-Primary Groups 1‚Äì5** | Phase 2.9.1 presPrimary context exists; `modern` (#47) specifies a primary loop but `hd` adds the **emergent state-opt-in calendar** | A **Governor action "Activate State Primaries"** turns a state into a primary state (**WTA / plurality / proportional**) and assigns it to a **Primary Group 1‚Äì5** (random roll); once any state has them, the game enters the **Primary Era** (primaries precede the convention), spreading by bill/Gov-action until all states have them. Logged so a future roadmap pass does NOT re-add it. summer2021 POST 1, 8, 10, 15, 19, 143**; **‚òÖ (b37) `benchmarkupd` (217d1977) ‚Äî the benchmark scoring IS this win/score model, now CONTENT-COMPLETE: see NEW #253 for the ~260-predicate Red/Blue per-era benchmark dataset (10/era √ó 13 eras √ó 2 parties) evaluated at era-end with active-or-issued semantics. vcczar CONFIRMS the OLD benchmark point system is RETAINED ("only slightly amended," POST 3) ‚Äî the per-era benchmark list SUPPLEMENTS, does not replace, this banking pipeline; weights still TBD. Comparative ("vs previous era") benchmarks require a per-era SNAPSHOT archive of key economic levers (tariff, income-tax bracket) ‚Äî folded into #253. benchmarkupd POST 1, 3**; **‚òÖ (b42) `f012e5cc` (AMPU Benchmarks, Dec-2021 origin) SETTLES the "End of Term" scoring numbers this row references: the era-end benchmark award = **+1,000 / ‚àí1,000 PER FACTION** (raised live from 250; POST 3, 5, 8, 9), living in the rulebook "End of Term" section, with the 4-case rule ‚Äî **one party accomplishes ‚Üí that party gains / the other loses; both ‚Üí nobody scores; neither ‚Üí only the party-leader factions lose.** ‚òÖ CONFIRMS **5 FACTIONS PER PARTY** (= 10 total): vcczar corrected his own "4 factions" and Ted's "8 factions each" math (POST 4, 6) ‚Äî so +1,000/faction √ó 5 = a ~10,000-point party-gap swing (POST 8-9). Scale calibration for weight-tuning: most laws ‚âà "a couple hundred points in either direction, or less"; the biggest single play seen ‚âà ~2,250 (jvikings1 as SecDef + Military-Industrial-Complex faction self-proposing "President Calls Up Militia" = 4√ó usual + faction gain) ‚Äî so the benchmark is DELIBERATELY the largest single point swing in the game. f012e5cc POST 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9**) |
| 102 | **DUAL era scoring ‚Äî per-era winner + cumulative "winner of the game so far" (keyed to B#/R# slots)** ‚Äî NEW | No era-to-era scoring; no cumulative leaderboard; `HalfTermSummary` is per-turn only (types.ts:1762) | At **each** era boundary the engine declares **TWO** results, tracked independently: (1) a **per-era winning faction** ("winner of this era / round"), and (2) a running **cumulative "winner of the game so far"** spanning all bands. `tea1772` shows the two diverging: **POST 153** declares per-era winner = R2 but cumulative leader = B4 (also POST 91: round B5 / cumulative B4; POST 130: era B4 / cumulative B4). Scores are **keyed to the fixed faction slots B1‚ÄìB5 / R1‚ÄìR5**. This pairs with #68's per-era point-banking (the bank feeds the cumulative score) and suggests the **cumulative bank ‚Üí end-of-game total is the cross-era win condition** (open #68/long-arc Q). The exact point formula is not visible in `tea1772` (only the boundary announcements) ‚Äî `rep1800` POST 6201 is the closest known formula | Track **two** running scores at each era boundary: a per-era winner (resets each band) + a cumulative leader (banks across bands, keyed to faction slots) ‚Äî surface both as a scoreboard; wire the cumulative bank to #68's point-banking + the candidate end-of-game win condition. (**2-save confirmed** ‚Äî same dual-scoring shape in `tea1772` 1772-start + `rep1800` 1800-start; extends #68) | ingested (**tea1772 ¬ß2 / POST 21, 62, 91, 130, 153; extends #68; cf. rep1800 POST 6201**; **terror2000 ‚Äî when the game ENDED on the Autocratic Coup (#88), a CUMULATIVE-score winner was declared across the whole run (`@matthewyoung123` R4, "dominating from a scoring standpoint" ‚Äî POST 839), even though the faction holding the presidency at collapse was a different one (Ridge's) ‚Äî confirms the cumulative-leader-as-end-of-game-win-condition independent of who holds office**; **‚òÖ batch 30 `summer2021` ‚Äî see the #68 HYGIENE note: the "winning-party lowest-faction" score penalty (which would have fed this dual-scoring pipeline) was ADOPTED then REVERSED (POST 1/8 ‚Üí POST 10, "nosedive" effect) ‚Äî DO NOT REINTRODUCE without the dead-last-whole-game guard. summer2021 POST 1, 8, 10**; **‚òÖ (b37) `benchmarkupd` (217d1977) ‚Äî the per-era benchmark dataset (NEW #253) is the per-party CONTENT that feeds this per-era + cumulative scoring; benchmarks are scored AT each era boundary (active-or-issued), so they slot directly into both the per-era winner and the cumulative bank. benchmarkupd POST 1, 3**; **‚òÖ (b42) `f012e5cc` confirms the per-FACTION granularity this dual-score keys to: the era-end benchmark award is +1,000 / ‚àí1,000 PER FACTION across **5 factions/party** (vcczar corrected the math, POST 4, 6, 8) ‚Äî the party-relative gain/loss feeds both the per-era winner and the cumulative bank. f012e5cc POST 1, 3, 4, 6, 8**) |

| 99 | **Ideology scale is a CIRCLE (LWPop‚ÜîRWPop adjacency) ‚Äî THREE-THREAD CONFIRMED + Ted-RULED** | `Ideology` is a linear 7-point type `LW Populist ‚Ä¶ RW Populist` (types.ts:5); shifts/conversions assume a line | A **mid-era rule change wraps the 7-point ideology scale into a RING**: **LW Populist ‚Üî RW Populist shifts are allowed at 25% base** (the two ends become adjacent), and **conversions extend to adjacent ideologies** (same-party other-faction pols of same OR adjacent ideology ‚Äî was same-only earlier). This directly contradicts a linear enum: the design treats Prog‚ÜîLib‚ÜîMod‚ÜîCons‚ÜîTrad‚ÜîRWPop‚ÜîLWPop‚ÜîProg as a closed loop for both **ideology shifts** (the 2.1.5 shift pass) and **conversions** (#76 conversion adjacency). **‚òÖ INDEPENDENTLY CORROBORATED (batch 9):** the `nuke` thread reports the SAME rule (POST 9842). **‚òÖ TED-RULED (batch 12b, `tedchange`):** vcczar declared the rule directly ‚Äî *"all ideologies are now a CIRCLE, rather than a line"* (POST 24); shift rate set at **25% base** with **auto-Two-Faced on cross-circle success** (POST 28-29, 51). Three independent threads + Ted's official ruling ‚Äî the rule is locked. | Treat the ideology scale as **circular** (LWPop adjacent to RWPop) wherever adjacency is used: ideology-shift steps, conversion target eligibility, faction-card adjacency (#24), VP/platform "adjacent ideology" checks. (Refines the 7-point ideology model + #76 conversion adjacency. **3-thread confirmed + Ted-RULED**) | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ßB 5717, 5730; nuke ¬ß6 / POST 9842; tedchange POST 18-31, 51 ‚Äî Ted's CANONICAL declaration + 25%-rate + Two-Faced penalty**) + codebase (types.ts:5) |

### CPU AI specifications (NEW ‚Äî `drums`-derived, **`oopscpu`-corroborated**; resolves DH-8 heuristics)

The `drums` all-CPU playtest is the **first explicit forum record of CPU
heuristics**. The shipped game is single-player; **every faction except the
player is CPU**, so these rows are first-class build requirements. They
**RESOLVE the bulk of DH-8** (CPU AI under-spec); the REMAINING DH-8 gaps live
in DH-19..DH-22 below. **‚òÖ Batch 13 (`oopscpu`) is the second all-CPU run and
the first DELIBERATE CPU-ruleset interrogation** (Ted-run, explicitly "to find
where the rules are insufficient" ‚Äî POST 1, 5). It **corroborates #70/#72/#73/
#74/#75/#76 from a founding-era angle**, adds **NEW CPU sub-gaps OC-1..OC-8**
below, and pins several **Ted rulings** (the post-election Command-loss rule, the
pre-12A CPU nomination trio, the help-allies gov-term sub-rule). **Coverage
caveat: the convention CPU AI (#71) is UNTESTED by `oopscpu`** because the 1788
pre-12A era runs no conventions ‚Äî leadership IRV (#70) is the only convention
proxy here. **Shipped reality:** the engine has CPU code for the meta-passes
(draft pick / career track / relocation / ideology shift / conversion ‚Äî see
`phaseRunners.ts` `RELOCATION_ODDS.cpuGate`, `IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS.cpu`) but
**none of the #70‚Äì#78 handler suite** ‚Äî those remain designed-only.

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 70 | **CPU leadership / Speaker / PPT IRV bloc-vote + 3-inconclusive-ballot collective endorse** | Leadership phase exists; no CPU bloc-vote model | Authoritative IRV tie-break ladder confirmed across **4+ maps** (Speaker, PPT, Sen Maj/Min Leader/Whip, House Maj/Min Leader/Whip elections): (1) ballot 1, each faction casts as a UNIT for own/closest candidate; (2) **closest ideology**; (3) **highest PV**; (4) **highest Legislative skill**; (5) random same-party as last resort. **After 3 inconclusive ballots, CPUs collectively endorse a winner** (confirmed POSTS 5272, 5841, 6099). Designer patches: **continuous IRV elimination** (no ballot-3 random collapse, POST 3419) + **randomize only on FIRST elimination** (POST 3419). **No reciprocity / vote-trading exists** (POST 4875 ‚Äî designer comment "the CPUs don't understand reciprocity"). Closest-ideology can flip leadership to the minority party (POST 3419, Magoffin/Cons-Dem 1880). Asymmetric on-win bundle (#28) | Implement the 5-step IRV ladder + bloc-vote unit per faction + continuous elimination + first-round-only randomization + 3-inconclusive-ballot collective endorse + asymmetric on-win bundle. Human factions do NOT auto-collapse ‚Äî must manually endorse. **No-reciprocity is architectural** ‚Äî DH-20 below. (**4+ maps in `drums` + corroborated by `gilded`/`fed`/`hd` party-leader/internal-vote variants + `oopscpu` confirms the WHOLE ladder across multiple founding-era Speaker/PPT/Party-Leader cycles**) | ingested (gilded 50, 60-61; fed 3; hd 4646-4726, 4872-4877; **drums 604, 679-680, 847-851, 1010, 1158, 2657, 2992-2994, 3170-3173, 3419, 3596-3597, 3885, 3980, 4175-4177, 4322-4326, 4513, 4667-4671, 4872-4877, 4875, 5014, 5272, 5841, 6099, 6448, 6623, 6747, 6828, 7349, 7500**; **oopscpu ‚Äî IRV ladder + faction bloc-vote + PL-backed-candidate tiebreak confirmed across cycles (Speaker POST 42; PPT POST 115 "unbreakable tie ‚Üí Party Leader's backed candidate wins"; Party-Leader POST 56-57; post-Washington-death Red PL + PPT IRV POST 329, 332); ‚òÖ marquee CPU bug OC-2 ‚Äî closest-ideology lets a CPU vote for a PPT OUTSIDE its party, so ALL Senate chairs went BLUE while Red held 73% of the chamber, then the ‚â•60%-chamber chair-proposer rule let those Blue chairs propose anyway ‚Äî Ted: "Adding this weirdness to the stuff to correct when I rework CPU rules" (POST 151)**; **ted1772 ‚Äî 5th-thread corroboration from a 1772‚Üífederalism boot: committee-chair appointment + the 1788 first Speaker/PPT elections + Senate-Maj-Leader-recommends-5-confirmable-cabinet-names ladder [POST 138]; FL-replacement each cycle w/ Incompetent-blocked [POST 620] and ‚òÖ dead-FL IMMEDIATE-replacement [CHANGED LIVE from empty-until-next-phase, POST 426-429 ‚Üí 624]; controversial nominee 60% confirm + ‚òÖ a 5-5 committee TIE = FAIL [no tiebreaker, killed Arnold's admiralty, POST 514] + already-controversial-and-defeated = lifetime cabinet ban + permanent ‚àí1 elections [Jay POST 439, Arnold POST 515]**; **pop2012b ‚Äî modern-era corroboration of the leadership pipeline: Party-Leader election via kingmaker-weighted faction bloc vote with endorsement rolls (McCarthy 14 vs Bannon 9, winner gets +1 Command + "+3 in any primary while party leader"); first-elected-president auto-becomes faction + party leader; Speaker/Sen-Maj-Leader appoint 12 majority members + chairs, Min-Leader appoints 10 + ranking members (chairs/ranking need 2 legis) ‚Äî POST 332, 390, 503-506**; **grass1772 ‚Äî 5th-thread corroboration that the CPU handler suite is so incomplete the GM AUTHORED HIS OWN rules: CPU military-officer replacement [highest-Mil / favor-own-party / 50-25-25 same-party tiebreak + a separate no-major-war 5-way 20% rule, POST 132], CPU cabinet-retention [retain ‚â§5 unless a same-party higher-Admin exists; Passive-Pres 50% retains anyway; Micromanager 25% replaces anyway, POST 273], CPU-president-resigns-on-ailment unless ‚â•3 Command/Leadership/Charisma/Iron-Fist [POST 120]; Speaker IRV + endorsement defections exercised [POST 235, 269]; the exact #70-#79 handler holes from a 5th 1772 angle**) |
| 71 | **CPU convention per-ballot inter-ballot menu + compromise picker + dark horse** | Convention phase exists (#13); CPU AI absent | Per-ballot momentum + 10-action interballot menu (each candidate picks ONE per ballot; 1d6 success): No action / Stifle Competition (frontrunner, ‚â§~90 floor) / Appeal to Credibility / Integrity / Charisma (matching trait ‚Üí +1 Mo) / Whip Party (PL + Leadership) / Presidential Promise (target rolls vs 50; 25 for VP; **Puritan auto-declines**) / Kingmaker Interference (-1 Mo to target) / Drop-out+endorse (+1 Mo) / Influence Smoke-Filled Rooms (Kingmaker-gated) / Rouse Convention with Lies (Manipulative) / Request Ballot Shift / Call Rules Change (‚â•ballot 5 ‚Üí **weighted-kingmaker vote**). Speech roll 1d6 (1=-1 Mo / 5-6=+1; Orator upgrades start at 4). **Frontrunner -1 Mo for not winning ballot 1** (permanent -1 in future primaries if convention never won, POST 4820). **Compromise candidate at ballot 10** (Tyler invented POST 3719): each calling faction picks ONE from another faction in its ideology cluster ‚Äî **rigid highest‚Üílowest faction-points picker order; last picker gets leftover pool with NO cross-faction coordination** (POSTS 7229-7244); compromise candidates absorb originals' delegates after one ballot. **Dark horse at ballot 25** (POST 3719): Party Leader picks from lowest-scoring faction; auto-nominated. **Pineapple Primary** (POST 2562, 5515-5520): random pres candidate shot at convention; d100 ‚â§50 = killed (Grant 1872, Tweed 1876, Cornelius Bliss 1884, Yates 1900 ‚Üí first 3rd-party-elected President). **Kingmakers refuse to endorse** Lowbrow / Easily-Overwhelmed / Unlikable. CPU running-mate logic does NOT penalize ticket experience holes (Taft/Hughes both Justices, POST 7249). **NO auto-drop-out at 2-3 stuck ballots** ‚áí 10-13 ballot deadlocks (Whig 1852, 1856 went 11; Whig conv 13 ballots in ¬ßB) | Implement: per-ballot momentum + the 10-action menu + the 1d6 success roll + Stifle floor + weighted-kingmaker rules-change vote + ballot-10 compromise picker (highest‚Üílowest order + leftover-pool constraint) + ballot-25 dark horse + Pineapple Primary roll + kingmaker-refusal-to-endorse list + **auto-drop-out at 2-3 ballots of 0 Momentum (per player proposal, DH-17 below ‚Äî currently NOT implemented)**. (**Multi-cycle confirmed in `drums`; `hd` confirms convention richness across a 5th era**) | ingested (hd 4646-4726, 5594-5713; **drums 426, 429-431, 769-786, 781, 795, 1152-1168, 1162, 1164, 1841, 1949, 2210, 2216, 2244, 2552-2566, 2562, 2888, 3294-3318, 3690, 3719, 3737-3766, 4063-4117, 4419-4441, 4779-4822, 4820, 5116-5118, 5515-5520, 7214, 7229-7244, 7249, 7257, 7263**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b55) THE CANONICAL CPU-CONVENTION SPEC (rewrite of the prior agreed rules-doc) ‚Äî `cpu-rules-for-presidential-conventions` (d1058b12, AP, Jan 2026, POST 1; framing/deltas POST 4): the authoritative gates-over-RNG CPU-convention algorithm, several pieces NEW vs the `drums` capture above. (A) ‚òÖ PRESIDENTIAL-PROMISE REWARD = a **d6** (explicitly REPLACES the old 6√ó d100, POST 4) with per-reward CPU ACCEPT odds: **1=VP (accept 50%)** / **2=Senior cabinet** [SecState 40 / Treasury 20 / War-Defense 20 / AG 20] **(accept SecState 40%, Treas¬∑War¬∑AG 33% each)** / **3=Minor cabinet** (random non-senior seat, accept 25%) / **4=Cabinet-level-non-secretary OR ambassador** (random; ambassador accept 20%) / **5=SCOTUS** (accept 20%) / **6=Party-plank choice** (accept 20%). Offered only by a CPU major **1st in delegates**, 25% of the time, to a non-faction candidate with <half the CPU's delegates (50% the offer goes to the candidate / 50% to its faction; ineligible-candidate ‚Üí passes to the faction; **Puritans never accept/make a presidential promise for a drop-out**). (B) ‚òÖ PLACE-BASED candidate behavior (fills the idle-middle-candidate hole OrangeP47 raised, POST 6): **1st** = run the promise logic (A) + threshold-LOWER (C); **2nd/3rd/4th** = 33% drop out & endorse the most-momentum candidate (tie‚Üímatching-ideology‚Üírandom) / 25% run the promise logic as offerer; **LAST** = 50% offer (VP 50% / faction-eligible cabinet 50%) to a candidate with >half its delegates / 25% drop with NO promise ‚Äî **rising to 50% if last for the 3rd consecutive ballot**. (C) ‚òÖ THRESHOLD-MANIPULATION + BALLOT-SHIFT: 1st-in-delegates tries to **LOWER** the nomination threshold 25% (if possible); last tries to **RAISE** it 25%; a 1st-place faction calls a **ballot shift** if the leading major has >50% of delegates; a last-place faction calls one if the 2nd-place candidate has <half the front-runner's. All offers target **same-ideology first ‚Üí closest ideology ‚Üí random**. (D) ‚òÖ MINOR-candidate drop logic by faction place (1st: drop if major leads momentum else stay; 2nd-4th: drop if major leads momentum, else 25% drop+endorse momentum-leader of faction-leader's ideology; last: 50% drop-no-endorse / 50% drop+endorse). (E) ‚òÖ COMPROMISE/DARK-HORSE thresholds: compromise call at **10 ballots @25%** (pick same/adjacent-ideology from an allied faction: 50% current office-holder / 40% former / 10% anyone); Dark Horse after **5 compromise ballots @25%**, **AUTOMATIC at 25 ballots** (party leader picks an of-age ‚â•1-command pol from the LOWEST-score faction; ALL CPUs support). (F) ‚òÖ KEYNOTE selection: from the lowest-score same-party faction 50% / next 25% / next 25%; a random office-holder with ‚â•1 command 75% / non-office-holder ‚â•1 command 25%; **orators prioritized (pick one 75%)**; nominators = orator 60% / else 40%, **never Incoherent, never anyone running**. (G) Standing CPU behaviors: always attempt appeals / smoke-filled-room / whip-the-party / kingmaker interference. ‚òÖ‚òÖ BINDING DESIGN RULE (do NOT re-litigate): the multi-ballot SLOW-ROLL is an INTENTIONAL, settled compromise ‚Äî AP: "intentional to slow-roll the conventions to maintain the multi-ballot effect‚Ä¶ probably the single biggest argument (in terms of game mechanics) we've had on the forum" (POST 7, 10); CPUs are deliberately limited (no full optimization) for the same reason ‚Üí a hard constraint on any convention build. The whole CPU layer is 0% shipped (2.9.2 is a log stub `engine.ts:69`; no delegate/ballot/momentum model ‚Äî see #185 for the build-state). VP-selection lives on #72; platform-stance on #181; drop-out-and-endorse-momentum on #183. `cpu-rules-for-presidential-conventions` POST 1, 4, 6, 7, 10**; **‚òÖ (b58) `fb8070f3` is the HUMAN-RULEBOOK source under this CPU spec ‚Äî the full inter-ballot trait-APPEAL table with exact gates + d6 thresholds: Appeal-to-Integrity (self Integrity vs opponent Controversial, 5-6‚Üí+1 Mo), Appeal-to-Credibility (economy/war/domestic-crisis + matching expertise, 5-6), Reliable-Party-Nominee (`Predictable`, in 1st/2nd, 5-6), Will-of-the-People (RW/LW-Pop + Nationalism/Civil-Rights/Activist/Theocrat, 5-6), **Lies-and-Propaganda** (`Controversial`+`Propagandist`, 4-6‚Üí+1 Mo BUT 1-2 ‚Üí ‚àí1 party-preference on the National meter), Whip-the-Party (PL candidate, d6 6 / 5-6 w/ Leadership), Influence-Smoke-Filled-Rooms (active prot√©g√© of an active Kingmaker, 5-6), Kingmaker-Interference (non-candidate faction leader with Kingmaker, 5-6 ‚Üí +1 own/allied or ‚àí1 allied), Force-a-Rules-Change / Request-a-Ballot-Shift / Drop-out(+endorse). Matches the b55 CPU spec's 10-action menu + the Presidential-Promise reward odds + compromise@10 / dark-horse@25; 0% shipped. `fb8070f3` POST 1**) |
| 72 | **CPU candidate selection: 75/25 rule + no-VP-retention + no-primary-incumbent** | Open-seat selection not specified | **Major candidates: 75% party leader / 25% random Gov-Sen-VP-FmrVP-FmrPres with ‚â•1 Command** (POST 143, authoritative GM ruling). Fallback = any random ‚â•1 Command. **Minor candidates: CPU always runs one** (random ‚â•1 Command). **CPU does NOT primary an incumbent** unless faction ideology-enthusiasm is "extremely unhappy" (POST 1777). Auto-major: incumbent / faction leader / Celebrity-trait pol (POST 2210). **Open-seat selection** = (1) state's preferred-ideology filter; (2) random among matches; (3) **Kingmaker-faction members get primary bonus** that usually beats un-bonused stars. **VP selection: NO retention logic** (POST 167, authoritative bug ‚Äî designer-acknowledged: "CPU picked from the faction with the lowest points that had a candidate not from the President's region. So it was basically random"). VP-impact rubric is the 8-element rubric (#15). **VP can be off career-track for *appointment* but NOT for *election*** (POSTS 2227-2234 ‚Äî Hancock rejected). **Open Q:** does the 75/25 rule apply to faction-leader picks? Tyler asks explicitly (POST 143) | Implement: 75/25 split for major-candidate selection; minor-always-run rule; no-primary-incumbent gate (faction ideology-enthusiasm "extremely unhappy" threshold); 2-step open-seat filter + Kingmaker primary bonus; **VP retention logic (was absent in `drums` #72/POST 167; ‚òÖ Ted ADDED it in `oopscpu`)**; VP-off-career-track rule (appointment-only). (NEW ‚Äî `drums` is the canonical source; **`oopscpu` corroborates + ADDS the pre-12A CPU nomination trio**) | ingested (drums 143, 167, 1176, 1777, 1784, 2210, 2227-2234; **oopscpu ‚Äî ‚òÖ Ted RULED LIVE the pre-12A CPU nomination rules (POST 192): (a) an incumbent President ALWAYS re-nominates the incumbent VP if eligible [the VP-retention rule `drums` lacked]; (b) CPU nominates an alternate when no one else has [defeats the throwaway-vote tie exploit in the two-votes-per-state no-ticket era]; (c) CPU prioritizes its OWN faction's candidates; (POST 197) two candidates from the same state can't both win that state's EVs. Scoped to pre-12A election rules (POST 193-194). Also confirms no-primary-incumbent: only Blue Mods/Cons + Red LW-Pops were challenged by same-party factions; no 8-yr-served-gov challenges (POST 294)**; **ted1772 corroborates the pre-12A nomination from a 1772 boot: with NO party leaders yet, the faction-with-most-kingmakers picks the nominee, ties random [POST 753-755]; CPU CANNOT add a nominee unless it is the party leader, but a CPU pol nominated BY A HUMAN always accepts [POST 1661-1664]; pre-12A "ideally exactly 3 candidates ‚Äî Pres, VP, and a 1-state spoiler so the VP doesn't pass the Pres in EV" [POST 1349]; ‚òÖ pre-12A VP rule corrected mid-thread ‚Äî only the President needs >50% EV, the VP is the most-EV runner-up, no Senate contingent unless 2nd place TIES [POST 1683, 1714-1716] (sharpens DH-62)**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b55) the CANONICAL CPU VP-SELECTION algorithm (the post-12A modern flow the prior doc lacked detail on; FILLS the "no VP retention logic" hole #72 flagged from `drums`) ‚Äî `cpu-rules-for-presidential-conventions` (d1058b12, AP, Jan 2026, ¬ß1.4 / POST 1): **(1) keep the incumbent VP 90%** (or vote for the VP if the convention chose the nominee) ‚Äî VOIDED if the VP has **Incompetent or Controversial** or the player declines; **(2) else source the VP: 50%** eligible pol from the **lowest-scoring faction + a DIFFERENT region** / **25%** any faction with an eligible OFFICE-HOLDING pol + different region / **25%** any faction with an eligible pol (any office); **(3) trait preference: 33%** pick a VP with any of **harmonious / likable / provincial / cosmopolitan**, on tie/none ‚Üí random VP that is **not unlikable or disharmonious**; **(4) hard constraints: NEVER same state as the nominee**; the CPU **ALWAYS keeps a presidential promise** (a promised VP overrides all the above). Convention VP-vote: support own faction; if both options are the CPU's faction, pick the one with the **most of harmonious/likable/cosmopolitan/provincial** (tie/none ‚Üí random). Also corroborates the major-candidate selection this row owns: faction **leader 75%** / a **celebrity with ‚â•1 command 10%** / no-major 15% (‚Üí minor 50%, or 75% if no incumbent from their party; **‚â•1 faction per party MUST run**). 0% shipped ‚Äî `vicePresidentId` is never SET by the engine (see #185 build-state). Cross-ref #71 (the convention CPU spec), #15 (VP-impact rubric), #185 (the unbuilt system). `cpu-rules-for-presidential-conventions` ¬ß1.1, ¬ß1.4 / POST 1**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b58) `fb8070f3` supplies the ELIGIBILITY rules this row selects within AND surfaces the load-bearing 12th-Amendment P+VP TICKET-FORMATION hole (folded here, not a separate row). ELIGIBILITY: each faction nominates ‚â§2 ‚Äî one **MAJOR** (faction leader OR a `Celebrity`, ‚â•1 Command) + one **MINOR/favorite-son** (any pol, ‚â•1 Command); convention order = out-of-White-House party first; dark-horse = lowest-score faction + Presidency-eligible (35/native-born). ‚òÖ TICKET-FORMATION (the headline shipped-vs-designed delta): the 12A makes electors vote SEPARATELY for Pres + VP, so a convention forms a deliberate P+VP ticket with ticket-balancing meter effects ‚Äî but **`vicePresidentId` is NEVER SET by any election path** (code-verified whole-codebase: only nulled on init `scenario1772.ts:76` / on VP-vacate `phaseRunners.ts:2450`, seeded once `scenario1856.ts:156`, READ for display only `CabinetPage.tsx:20`/`Dashboard.tsx:14`; `runPhase_2_9_4_PresidentialGeneral` `phaseRunners.ts:3752-3814` runs a head-to-head EV race with NO running mate and resets the cabinet to null). So there is no ticket formation, no VP-selection, no ticket-composition meter effect ‚Äî the 12A ticket is entirely unmodeled, the precise hole this row's VP-selection logic plugs into. Pairs with #185 (the system) / #93 (12A state machine). `fb8070f3` POST 1**) |

| 74 | **CPU legislation voting heuristic (the 3-step canonical rule)** | Vote tally exists; no heuristic model | Matt's clearest articulation (POST 2161, observation-corroborated 6+ times): **(1) Bill helps opposition Pres's meters/election ‚Üí NAY; (2) gives points to my ideology/lobbies ‚Üí AYE; (3) NAY default**. Proposer = each faction's **highest-Legis pol** (POST 2524). **Efficient ‚Üí +1 proposal** (POSTS 2842, 3625). **Free Executive proposals** (POSTS 2523, 3086): SecTreas & SecWar each get 1 free bill that ANY congressional proposer can pick up. **Crisis bills:** CPU prioritizes when meter in crisis; **failed crisis bill still carries penalty** (POSTS 2525, 3122, 3129). **Disallowed proposals:** faction CANNOT propose a bill that COSTS points to one of its own cards (POSTS 2530-2531, 2851 ‚Äî mid-stream GM rule). **Bills must be checked against proposer's own cards** (POST 933 ‚Äî currently slips through). **Validity checker must look at active amendments** (POST 2006). **CPU optimizes meter math not theme** (POST 1683: proposed "Grant States Power to Secede" because it had a 25% Dom-Stab boost). **CPU has NO meter-guarding on stacked event picks** ‚Äî DH-21 below | Implement: 3-step heuristic + Legis-by-faction proposer pool + Efficient +1 + Free-Executive carry-pool + Crisis-bill priority + bill-cards-against-proposer-cards check + active-amendment validity checker + meter-math optimization. (NEW ‚Äî `drums` is the canonical source; **`oopscpu` corroborates + exposes OC-3 crisis-vote-against-self**) | ingested (drums 933, 1683, 2006, 2161, 2523, 2524-2537, 2530-2531, 2710, 2832-2879, 2842, 2851, 3086, 3122, 3132, 3508-3527, 3625, 3924-3940, 4267-4280, 4396-4416; **terror2000 corroborates the proposer-eligibility rules natively: committee CHAIRS + House/Senate leadership CANNOT propose unless their party holds ‚â•66% of the chamber ‚Äî but RANKING members CAN (vcczar OK'd it, POST 630, 634, 642); free-Executive carry-pool + chair-replaces-one-bolded-bill confirmed POST 671-677**; **oopscpu ‚Äî 3-step heuristic + the no-points-gained ‚Üí 50/50-default confirmed (Marine Corps bill died because nobody gained points, POST 157); duplicate-bill resolution by POINT ORDER (of 3 tariff bills, Osgood's beat Ames's + Read's, POST 282); Free-Executive carry-pool confirmed (SecWar/SecTreas crisis proposals picked up by congressional proposers, POST 154, 278); ‚òÖ OC-3 ‚Äî CPU crisis-support rule is TOO GENEROUS: all CPU factions (incl. Southern) rolled to support the Abolish-Slavery CRISIS bill on crisis-resolution grounds ‚Üí slavery abolished by 1792 with no secession, "the committee rules don't mention how CPUs vote on crisis if it hurts them, and 3.0.30 is more lenient on crisis support for the majority than the opposition" (POST 165-180); also theme-blindness (signed Fugitive-Slave-Act then took Don't-Enforce because the action helped an ally by point-math, POST 95-96 ‚Äî same class as DH-21)**; **ted1772 ‚Äî Ted's CLEANEST articulation of the heuristic (POST 221-222): Step 1 crisis bill ‚Üí same-party-as-Pres 75% / minority 25% auto-AYE; Step 2 net-points-for-my-faction ‚Üí AYE/NAY; Step 3 net-for-my-TEAM ‚Üí AYE/NAY; Step 4 net-for-OPPONENT-team ‚Üí hurts-them AYE / helps-them NAY; else coin-flip [lives in "3.1 Misc ‚Äî How the CPU makes decisions"]; many founding bills were NEUTRAL across all 4 steps ‚Üí lots of coin-flips; ‚òÖ founding-specific marquee: Red CPU voted NAY on Lee's Resolution + the Declaration "bc it damages the other party more if it fails" [POST 143, 167] ‚Äî pure meter-math theme-blindness; see NEW #158 for the CPU-peace-lean game-ender**; **‚òÖ (b43) `06fbb2e5` (Lars-Ark 1772) CONFIRM + a content hole: (1) proposer = each faction's highest-Legis pol ‚Äî mis-run as CPU-random by the GM (POST 33/36, "I ran the numbers of who got to propose and did CPU rather than do it in descending legis order, whoops") + bad eligibility picks (proposers without ‚â•1 Legis, POST 64) ‚Üí the app must OWN proposer selection (ties #114/DH-36); (2) the CPU AYE-only-if-it-helps-me heuristic stalled the **Declaration of Independence 3√ó** (POST 47 "stalls 3-5", 67 "dies 7-3", 76 "CPU fails to support the declaration") ‚Äî it passed only on the 3rd legislative session (POST 106-107). Ark's diagnosis (designer-flagged as good feedback, POST 77/80): the Declaration "either needs to be foundational (so that we trigger the 75% chance of support) or it needs to appeal to more than just nationalists that like and pacifists that oppose." ‚òÖ CANDIDATE requirement (sub-req here): mark independence-chain / foundational bills as ~75%-support / can't-perma-stall so the war-trigger isn't gated on a random draft (ties #155's foundational-bill ~75%-support floor). `06fbb2e5` POST 33, 36, 47, 64, 67, 76, 77, 80, 106-107**; **‚òÖ (b51) `8a0a14a1` (the structural-flexibility wish-list thread) supplies the SHARPEST single statement of the designed CPU-vote objective + its tension with history, and SHARPENS the shipped-vs-designed delta with file:line evidence: (1) DESIGNED ‚Äî Largo833 POST 11: *"effect on the faction score plays the largest part in how a CPU votes"* ‚Üí the core of the CPU vote model is **faction-score optimization** (a CPU supports/opposes a measure primarily by its projected effect on the controlling faction's SCORE, the operative scoring signal the port must encode) ‚Äî consistent with the Ted heuristic's Step-2/3 "net-points-for-my-faction/team" here but stated as the PRIMARY factor, not a step. SHIPPED: the only legislative vote in the build is the CC-era `voteCC` (`continentalCongress.ts:199-226`), which decides aye/nay by a FLAT affiliation probability keyed on relationship to the bill's SPONSOR (same-faction 0.92 / same-party 0.60 / else 0.20, `:211-215`) + `cardVoteBias` over the bill's cards ‚Äî **no computation of the bill's projected effect on the voting faction's score** (identity-proxy, not a score-weighted vote). Delta = replace the identity-proxy with the score-weighted vote this row already specs. (2) DESIGNED ‚Äî ebrk85 POST 8: *"the CPU is programmed to want historical choices‚Ä¶ players have to band together to get anything ahistorical through a Convention"*; this is in TENSION with (1) (pure faction-score optimization can OVERRIDE the historical bias ‚Äî a long 9-CPU game plausibly never frees the slaves / never enfranchises women, Largo833 POST 11) ‚Üí the historical bias is a default/tie-break/weight ON TOP of the faction-score objective, blend UNSPECIFIED (open Q). SHIPPED: a deterministic pro-historical default EXISTS but ONLY for the 1772 era-event graph (`eraGraph.ts:88-109`, "the spine/historical response id 'a' is the default"); the **Convention CPU has ZERO historical weighting** (`autoFillCPUVotes`/`preferredOption` `constitutionalConvention.ts:81-123` = party identity + `chance()` coin-flips). Delta = define the historical-bias √ó faction-score composition + extend the score-led model to Conventions/legislation. (3) BALANCE NOTE (not a system): early eras (1-2) CPU bias is intentionally HEAVY (mirrors real path-dependence); the designed ahistorical-play lever is coordinated control of the Blue Party / 2-3 center-Blue factions before 1840 (pman POST 13, "really something we won't be able to test until Beta"). `8a0a14a1` POST 8, 11, 13**; **‚òÖ (b54) `410ed3c8` (CPU Issues) is the META "we need ONE set of rules" thread for the whole CPU spec ‚Äî Bushwa777 POST 1: *"I keep finding issues with the CPU rules and the rules that you have all told me. We need to have one set of rules."* It demonstrates manual-CPU-spec-vs-shipped-engine DRIFT (the canonical-rules-reconciliation concern this row + #20 carry) using the CPU ideology-MOVEMENT rule as its exhibit. ‚òÖ KEEP DISTINCT: the CPU ideology-SHIFT/movement rule is a SEPARATE system from this row's legislation-VOTING heuristic ‚Äî it lives on NEW #314, cross-linked here, NOT folded into #74. `410ed3c8` POST 1**) |

| 76 | **CPU conversion AI: deterministic %-table + Pliable + adjacency gating + failure-strip-Pliable** | Faction conversion phase exists; CPU model absent | Two-layer model: **(1) Disgruntled auto-flip** (no roll): pols whose ideology is maxed +3 for the OPPOSITE party flip at half-term to the receiving party's faction holding the matching ideology card (POST 1419 ‚Äî "most aligns" = active cards, not raw label). **(2) Active poach** (rolls + gates) with rates scaling **10%‚Üí58% by adjacency + leader-bonuses**: Party Leader cross-party 10% base +5% same-ideology ‚Üí ~15% (Pres+Manipulative observed 43%); Mod PL 5% base; LW Pop PL (Weaver) 15% / 25% same-ideology; Faction Leader inter-party 10-20% base up to 48% kingmaker-buffed; Master-Kingmaker tier 43%; Roosevelt as Lib Whig PL **48% / 58% same-ideology**; Iron Fist gets **3 attempts**. **Restrictions/immunities:** Harmonious/Passive/Predictable FLs cannot convert at all; Manipulative blocks FL converting + cannot target Manipulative; cannot target own party, Abolitionist/Radical, Pres/VP/Cabinet/Speaker/Leader/Whip/FactionLeader, Harmonious, Trad Dems (era-specific), Pliable own-party, factions at +3 enthusiasm; **targets must be Pliable AND same-or-adjacent ideology** (POSTS 4310, 4313-4315). **Failure strips Pliable on target** (POST 3162); roller may flip Pliable‚ÜíIntegrity (POST 3873); survivor often gains Integrity (POST 5002). **Multi-faction collision bug** (POSTS 79, 215) ‚Äî all CPU factions greedily target the same opponent. **Kingmaker-flip grabs prot√©g√©s** (DH-5 reconfirmed) | Implement the conversion %-table + Pliable + adjacency gating + restriction list + failure-strip + collision tie-break (currently absent). (NEW ‚Äî `drums` + corroborated by `hd`/prior threads at coarser grain; **`oopscpu` confirms FL-replacement runs each cycle by faction with the obscure/passive gates [e.g. KevinCPU drops Passive Huntington, POST 122-123; new FL picked on death, POST 326] ‚Äî but did NOT exercise the negative-trait cascade**) | ingested (drums 226-227, 337, 354, 519, 1649, 2481-2484, 2622, 2653, 2786, 2766, 3610-3617, 5033, 6836-6845; **oopscpu ‚Äî FL replacement each cycle; Passive leaders dropped (POST 122, 261); FL-on-death picks (POST 326)**) |
| 143 | **‚òÖ Post-election Command-loss rule ‚Äî "shit or get off the pot" (Ted RULED, tested live)** ‚Äî NEW | No post-election Command decay; Command is sticky once gained | **‚òÖ Ted's marquee `oopscpu` proposal, RULED + TESTED LIVE.** Floated in POST 1 ("a post-election % chance you lose Command if you didn't run for President/VP"), then applied in POST 224: **"Politicians who don't run for President have a 40% chance of losing ‚àí1 Command after the Presidential election. Purpose: stop Command from piling up on pols who never contest the presidency, so the candidate pool stays meaningful (pairs with #136 "Presidents should come from somewhere" + the CPU candidate-selection rule #72). **RULED at 40% / ‚àí1 Command per presidential cycle, applies to anyone who did NOT run for Pres/VP.** | Add a per-presidential-cycle 40% ‚àí1-Command roll on every politician who did NOT run for President or VP. Pin the rate. Pairs with #72/#136. | ingested (**oopscpu POST 1, 224**; **terror2000 ‚Äî the same "use-it-or-lose-it Command" principle appears as an EVENT variant: the "Ambitious Judge" Anytime Evo grants a Justice +1 Command but "must run for President in the upcoming election or he'll lose the command point" (POST 430 ch16) ‚Äî a Justice-specific one-cycle Command-decay, consistent with the #143 framing**; **‚òÖ fixes ¬ßE POST 210-212 ‚Äî DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE ORIGIN of the whole Command-decay model [strong 2nd source]: the multi-page "command never decays ‚Üí has-beens [Dewey/MacArthur/Gore/Dukakis] run decades late" debate resolved into **Ted's synthesized proposal, V ACCEPTED** ["fine with this, nothing more extreme"]: post-1772 boots get no command >10 yrs after a real pres run; a **census-decade 60% lose-1-Command roll**; + a TOGGLE "no command at rookie draft, must earn it" paired with **DOUBLED command-gain %** [the origin of #153]. V REJECTED the per-pol date-stamp variant + "strongly opposed to no one born with command" as a default [POST 67] ‚Äî so #153's zero-rookie-Command is a TOGGLE, not the default in the 2022 design. This `fixes` ruling is the joint origin of #130/#143/#153/#36/#40**; **‚òÖ batch 30 `summer2021` adds the Era-of-Independence Faction-Leader WAIVERS (a founding-gating set distinct from the Command-decay rule above): the prior-offices requirement for Faction Leader does NOT apply in the Era of Independence, and the "obscure" requirement is REMOVED (POST 1); a faction with no ideology card ‚Üí its leader must have a personal ideology that is a DOMINANT ideology for the Historical Era, and any card-less faction auto-gains the era's dominant ideology after cards are dealt (`GM‚áíApp`, POST 1); CPU faction-leader-selection AI was ADDED (POST 1, 51). summer2021 POST 1, 51**) |

| 208 | **‚òÖ CPU LEGISLATIVE/CONFIRMATION logic is UNSPECIFIED ‚Äî filibuster-override, majority/minority-leader vote rule, and cross-party cabinet caps were all GM-improvised at modern scale ‚Äî NEW (`welcome2future`, `GM‚áíApp`)** | The #70-#78 cluster + OC-1..OC-8 cover CPU candidate-selection / draft / crisis-vote / vacancy-fill, but **none specify how a CPU runs the LEGISLATIVE + CONFIRMATION machinery**: how a CPU Majority Leader decides to override (or not) a filibuster, how the maj/min leaders themselves VOTE, or the cross-party cabinet limits | **The clearest mass-CPU legislative-behavior sample in the corpus (8 of 10 factions on CPU rules at modern scale) ‚Äî and it exposes that large swaths of CPU legislative behavior are undefined and were invented on the fly.** `welcome2future` ran a full modern turn hands-off without stalling, but the GM had to improvise: **(a)** *"there is NO rule for whether a CPU Majority Leader overrides a filibuster"* ‚Üí improvised "won't override own party unless it targets his faction" (posts 331, 343 ‚Äî CPU senators with the right profile DID filibuster nominees: Mike Lee killed Sullivan+Locke, Cruz killed Solis, filibustered pols gained `Controversial`); **(b)** no rule for **how maj/min leaders themselves vote** ‚Üí invented "support own party + within-2-ideology-clicks; iron-fist ‚Üí within-1" (post 322); **(c)** **cross-party cabinet caps** felt "too easy" ("weird that it's so Republican") ‚Üí GM rewrote them mid-thread (‚â§25% accept w/o integrity; ‚â§1 cross-party in top-4; ‚â§3 total; ‚â§1 ideology step away, posts 307-349). All three are `GM‚áíApp` automation requirements at the heart of the "subsume the GM" charter.
