Pairs with #52/#172/#25/DH-76 + the #70-#78 cluster | ingested (**welcome2future posts 307-349, 322, 331, 343; the hands-off modern run is ALSO the most complete modern CPU-turn spec in the corpus ‚Äî see the #114/#110 annotations**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ batch 35 EXTENDS ‚Äî `redbutton1960` (3c3c46f0) hands us TWO GM-improvised CPU subsystems verbatim (`GM‚áíApp`; POST 11 "Can't wait for the actual game so the computer can take over a lot of the tedious processes"): (1) ‚òÖ CPU moderate-faction SWING-VOTE model ‚Äî *"the moderate faction starts at 50-50 but the bill gains or loses support based on (1) which party proposed the bill, (2) if their party leader supports it or not, and (3) the actual make-up of the faction"* ‚Äî applied ONLY to MODERATE factions because partisan factions' votes are obvious ("easy when the bill helps or hurts your faction"); a base-rate-plus-modifiers form COMPLEMENTARY to `welcome2future`'s leader-level own-vote rule. The build's CPU legislative engine should subsume BOTH (a faction-level swing model AND a leader-level vote rule). (2) ‚òÖ Presidential PRESSURE (Command-driven) ‚Äî see #182: roll (Command) d6 ‚Üí a 5-6 rerolls ‚Üí result √ó10 = % of a faction swayed. ‚öÝ RECONCILE: this Presidential-pressure variant uses COMMAND (this thread) vs the `rulebook` ¬ßY/¬ßM2 "President may convert up to (Legislative#) senators on a 5-6" which uses LEGISLATIVE ‚Äî two designer-improvised forms; tech-lead to pick the canonical stat. Also DH-53/legislation-scoring corroborated here: the Farm-Subsidy bill = +100 pts to every ag-state Gov/Sen/Rep (a geographic bill‚Üípoints table). redbutton1960 POST 11, 17-18**) |

### NEW mechanics surfaced by `pop` (fresh-modern-boot deltas)

The `pop` thread is the **first record of how a modern-era scenario BOOTS from
cold** (vs. `modern`'s 60-yr continuation from a 1948 campaign). It surfaces a
small cluster of HIGH-signal mechanics around the **scenario-boot model** + the
**Era-of-Populism mechanical surface** that the modern-era flat card pool
configures rather than introduces. These are net-new rows; the bulk of `pop`
**corroborates** ¬ß22 systems already in the gap log (see #1, #8, #9, #10, #12,
#47, #50, #51, #52, #67 strengthened above).

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 86 | **Scenario-boot model (mid-government, pre-built sheet, era-tuned faction decks)** ‚Äî NEW | Two shipped scenarios: 1772 (cold draft) + 1856 (mid-government at phase 2.1.2). No formalization of the boot-shape pattern | A **single canonical boot shape** for mid-government scenarios (**1788 designed / 1856 shipped / 1800 in `rep1800` / 2012 fresh-modern in `pop`** ‚Äî now 4 instances), constructed from a **single pre-built sheet**: (a) **two parties, 5 named factions each** (B1-B5, R1-R5; the per-party-5-faction cap holds **7+ eras**); (b) **archetype politicians + era-tuned ideology + interest + lobby decks per faction at boot** (R1 deck = "Trumpism" RW Pop + Trad + Nationalist + Protectionist + RW Media + Isolationists; B1 deck = "Bernie-populism" LW Pop + Reformist + Welfare + Public Housing ‚Äî **the era identity is EMERGENT from the seed decks, not a new code path**); (c) **sitting government pre-loaded** (`pop` Obama+Biden+7-Dept cabinet+50-state Congress+9 SCOTUS; **`rep1800` Jefferson+Burr+full cabinet+6-member SCOTUS+Speaker/PPT+Congress, some seats VACANT to be appointment-filled in setup**); (d) **EXPLICITLY ABSENT at boot:** no faction/party leaders (selected after the meta-passes ‚Äî **so phase 2.1.6 Conversions is SKIPPED turn 1**, confirmed by `rep1800`), no career-track pols by default, **no inherited PV/Kingmaker/legacy** ‚Äî BUT **`rep1800` adds that a mid-government start STILL RUNS A ROOKIE DRAFT** at boot (the new 25-yr-old class; pols already holding "start" offices keep them + can't be career-tracked) **and a GM-hand-seeded career track** (7 pols/faction, 1/track) **patching the later-start "inherit a generation of 1-skill pols" hole** ‚Äî an explicit BUILD ASK; (e) **EV map keyed to year** (`pop` 2012 = 50+DC, NOT 53 ‚Äî `modern`'s 53 is 60 yrs of annexation) | A **scenario-boot pipeline** producing a `FullGameSnapshot` from a per-era boot sheet: pre-built faction roster + era-tuned deck assignment + sitting-government seeding (incl. vacant seats) + **a boot-time rookie draft for mid-government starts** + **auto-seed N pols/faction onto career tracks for later starts** (the explicit build ask ‚Äî pairs with DH-25) + EV map keyed to year + "no leaders / skip-2.1.6-turn-1 / no legacy at boot" baseline. Instantiate per-era (1788, **1800**, **1820**, 1856, 2012 minimum). (**5-instance confirmed**; **`dem1820` adds a 5th 1820 instance + the boot-PROCEDURE gap #115** ‚Äî the inaugural career-track seed pulls the **last 3 draft classes** [1820/1816/1812/1808, POST 28], new states init era-keyed industry not 0 [POST 532], and an **undocumented "strip Command from ‚â§40 boot pols" house rule** [POST 82] shows the boot procedure itself is unspecified ‚Äî see #115) | ingested (pop 1, 12, 17, 30, 38, 45, 50, 54, 205, 264, 359, 419, 422, 426, 475; all-era-shape vs fed 1, 14; scenario1856.ts:153, 179-193; scenario1772.ts:97; **rep1800 ¬ßA 6, 15-19, 24, 50, 55, 80, 82, 90-96, 105, 123, 159**; **dem1820 POST 1, 2, 28, 32, 82, 159, 532; see #115**; **ted1772 ‚Äî the COLD-1772-boot inverse of #115's "strip Command at boot": instead of stripping, Ted's house rule is **zero rookie Command + √ó2 Command-gain** [#153] so Presidents emerge in-game; turn-1 SKIPS most phases [no congress/president/leaders, POST 19, 42]; inaugural draft distributed pre-game [POST 4]; meta-pass order = career-track‚Üíflipflopper‚Üírelocate‚Üíshift‚Üíconvert‚Üíkingmaker‚Üícards‚Üíleaders [POST 4, 42]; the founding boot does NOT seed era-active wars [the NW Indian War "3 chances" + War-of-1812-in-1782 branch had to be improvised, see #158] ‚Äî corroborates the boot-procedure gap from a 4th 1772 start**; **pop2012b ‚Äî 2nd independent 2012-start instance of the SAME pre-built sheet (same 10 named factions/decks as pop), confirming: pre-loaded sitting government [Obama+Biden+9 SCOTUS+50-state Congress], NO faction/party leaders at boot ‚Üí Major-candidate eligibility falls back to the faction-leader criteria with an explicit fallback ladder [omit leadership/lackey ‚Üí omit interest/lobby ‚Üí void career-track/obscure], 50+DC map; the boot starts at the election ("usual AMPU way") while the app starts at phase 1 [Rodja, POST 1, 16, 20-22, 374]**; **‚òÖ batch 30 `ampuUpdates` is the DESIGNER CHARTER STATEMENT of start-anywhere boot (sharpens #86/#186/#187): "any presidential term 1789-2021 (+1774) is a valid start" ‚Äî at a chosen start incumbents are pre-seated, the CABINET is PRE-APPOINTED, the president is treated as "just elected," and the draft surfaces the offices each pol already holds (POST 102-105); ~1,000-1,250 new pols were authored to cover all start dates ‚Üí ~9,000+ total (POST 100, 109-111); a TIME-VARYING-RATINGS need is flagged (a pol should present then-current, not peak, stats at a later start year ‚Äî POST 175, an open boot-data hole). This is the canonical designer formulation of the start-anywhere boot requirement #86/#186 implement. ampuUpdates POST 100, 102-105, 109-111, 175**; **‚òÖ (b56) `b15af728` ("When should a new game actually BEGIN?", MrPotatoTed, Jun 2025) adds the START-PHASE-ENTRY axis to this boot keystone ‚Äî *which phase a non-1772 turn-1 enters and which turn-1 phases skip because incumbents are already seated* (the rules NEVER specify this). The poll's 3 options: **A** = start post-election WITH cabinet pre-confirmed (1856 = Buchanan + cabinet already seated ‚Äî ahistorically early, Buchanan was sworn in 1857); **B** (front-runner) = begin at phase **2.1**, post-election but BEFORE cabinet nomination; **C** = begin at the election (play it out ‚Äî could end with Buchanan losing). Team **leans B** for coding simplicity + accessibility (POST 3, 7, 8, 9). ‚òÖ SHIPPED-vs-design: ‚òÖ‚òÖ OVERRIDE the thread's stale "only 1772 ships" (POST 6, Jun 2025) ‚Äî the build offers BOTH 1772 and 1856, with **1856 the DEFAULT New-Game scenario** (`NewGameScreen.tsx:25` `useState<ScenarioId>('1856')`; `startNewGame(factionId, scenarioId?)` `GameContext.tsx`). Each scenario HARDCODES its entry phase (1772‚Üí`2.1.1` inaugural draft; **1856‚Üí`2.1.2`** Career Tracks, `scenario1856.ts:153` "start past draft for first turn (rookie crop already included)") ‚Äî NO generic/configurable start-phase boot, NO A/B/C toggle (#263). ‚òÖ The shipped 1856 boot is a HYBRID matching NEITHER A nor B: option-A-LIKE in seating incumbents (Buchanan + partial cabinet + SCOTUS + governors + full Senate/House, `scenario1856.ts:124-193`, the ahistorical pre-1857 start the thread critiques) BUT enters at **2.1.2** ‚Äî upstream of the 2.2.x leadership phases (NOT option-A's 2.4-start) AND skips the turn-1 draft (NOT option-B's 2.1.1) ‚Äî its own third thing; needs an explicit reconcile. ‚òÖ NEW option-A-conditional RULES GAP (POST 7): a true option-A boot ‚áí first turn starts at **2.4**, which by current rules SKIPS phase 2.2.3 Faction Leaders ‚Üí **no Faction Leader for any faction on turn 1.** Not live today (1856 boots upstream at 2.1.2, so FLs ARE selected turn-1), but a live hole the BootSheet must pre-seed (pre-assign turn-1 FLs) or the phase sequence special-case IF any era ever adopts a 2.4-start. ‚òÖ Start-date-sheet ERROR-class (data-quality bar for #186): vcczar's per-era start-date sheet has incumbent/seat errors (Senate seats marked "vacant" that don't vacate until after the midterms, POST 1) ‚Äî the shipped 1856 boot sidesteps them by assigning senators algorithmically (`scenario1856.ts:61-89`), but any data-driven BootSheet built from the sheet would inherit them. `b15af728` POST 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9**) |

Implicit: the same mechanism likely applies to era-defining issues of other eras (slavery in Nationalism, tariff in Gilded, etc.) | Add an `era.doubleScoringIssues: IssueTag[]` table that doubles ideology/lobby/meter awards on signed bills tagged with those issues; per-era authored | ingested (pop 699; sharpens #12; **tea1772 corroborates an era-sensitive issue overlay from a 2nd angle ‚Äî a per-era Popular/Unpopular issue list (one global list + era flags) breaks bill/platform ties when ideology is silent; ¬ß3 / POST 137. See #103**) |
| 88 | **Meter-driven game-over: APOCALYPSE 10-yr clock (`pop`) + the enumerated coup/collapse end-conditions (`rep1800`)** ‚Äî NEW | `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` exists (types.ts:1635) but is event-driven, not meter-driven; no coup path | Two flavors of **meter-driven endgame** (distinct from event-driven game ends). **(A) `pop` APOCALYPSE clock:** a meter into a specific bottom-tier **starts a 10-game-year countdown to mandatory game-end** (climate, GM Rodja: *"END OF THE WORLD IN 10 YEARS"*); Planet Health is the modern-era clock-trigger meter. **(B) `rep1800` enumerates the meter-driven END-CONDITION SET** (¬ßC POST 7274): **Autocratic Coup** (HonestGov=1, scaled by an "Era of Democracy" meter, 0% until 1868), **Standard Coup** (DomStab‚â§2 AND EconStab‚â§2 ‚Üí 10%), **Attempted Coup** (EconStab‚â§2 AND MilPrep‚â§3 AND DomStab‚â§2 ‚Üí 10%), **Economic Collapse** (20% if EconStab=1), **Enemy Takes Defenseless US** (MilPrep=1 AND a foreign power < Neutral). Game can END on a per-event-phase roll (~20%) when meters sit at the floor (multiple near-losses); plus a **Coup-at-low-meters AnytimeEvo (3.0.2)** where the opposition PL chooses Move-On / Condemn. **‚òÖ Balance note: coups are effectively DEAD mid/late game** ‚Äî MilPrep + foreign relations are **trivially maxed** with a decent cabinet ("a coup is damned near impossible"). So the MilPrep coup gate may not be worth wiring as-is | Add a **meter-derived endgame model**: (A) per-meter bottom-tier threshold + countdown clock (Planet Health 10-yr) + scheduled game-end; (B) the **enumerated per-event-phase end-condition roll set** (Autocratic/Standard/Attempted Coup, Economic Collapse, Enemy-Takes-US) + the Coup AnytimeEvo. **Open balance Q: is the coup path worth implementing given MilPrep trivially maxes?** Pairs with #50/#67 meter bank. **NEW endgame mechanism ‚Äî roadmap item.** **‚òÖ `terror2000` PARTIALLY RESOLVES the balance Q ‚Äî the "Autocratic Coup Ends America" end-condition FIRED LIVE and ENDED the game (POST 827, 829), the FIRST game-ending event EVER in an AMPU playtest (Ted-confirmed).** It is gated on **Honest-Gov at its lowest level** (which it had been since late-Bush, briefly recovered under Oprah then crashed) and fires at **20%/event-phase** "after just barely missing it in multiple previous phases." So the **HonestGov-gated Autocratic Coup is the live/worth-building path** (the MilPrep/EconStab coup gates remain the trivially-maxed ones); the endgame manifested as the dystopian "Rockefeather coup" narrative (President Ridge deposed; Harris/Ventura/Schoomaker resistance). **Build this end-condition first.** | ingested (pop 542, 548; **rep1800 ¬ßC 7274, 7275, 7836-7838, 9317, 9322, 9593, 9853**; **terror2000 POST 827, 829, 788-824 (ch44) ‚Äî first-ever game-ending event, HonestGov-floor Autocratic Coup at 20%/phase**; **‚òÖ redbutton (b27) ADDS a THIRD nominal end-condition ‚Äî a LITERAL NUCLEAR-WAR loss ("the big red button") framed as the campaign's premise ["no one wins if that big red button gets hit," POST 1] ‚Äî but it is UNDOCUMENTED as a mechanic and NEVER FIRED: the run paused at ~1962-63 before any Cuban-Missile-Crisis analog, so how it triggers (meter floor? scripted escalation? war-engine?) is entirely unknown. Confirms #88's pattern (dramatic end-conditions exist as intent, not built) + #106 (no nuclear/MAD subsystem; the only Cold-War "nuke" content is a sign/don't-sign Test Ban Treaty event, POST 1677-1688). See NEW #188**; **‚òÖ (b40) Largo833's "meter-at-floor = no deterrent" systemic flaw (broken out as NEW #266) directly TOUCHES this clock: once DomStab / party-pref floor, further penalties are toothless (the root cause of the ignore-losing-treaty exploit). Floated fix (b) is a **direct modifier on THIS countdown** ‚Äî a floored meter that would drop has a % chance to SHORTEN the loss-window (DomStab=1 ‚Üí 50% chance 8yr‚Üí6yr), implying the multi-year loss-countdown #88 owns can be accelerated by floor-penalties; fix (a) is spill-to-other-scores instead. See #266 for the full systemic gap + the open one-gap-vs-#88-sub-row call. foreign-affairs/treaty POST 1, 16**) |

The Climate Crisis bill *"Create Department of Environment and Climate"* (Wasserman Schultz) passes in 2015-17, and the 2017 cabinet now includes **Sec. of Environment & Climate (Sally Jewell)** (`pop` POST 699 ‚Üí 1100). | 101 | **Mutable cabinet ‚Äî offices CREATED *and* ABOLISHED by ordinary law (FOUNDING‚ÜíMODERN confirmed)** | `cabinetSeatsForYear` (types.ts:1196) is **year-keyed and HARDCODED** ‚Äî the comment block schedules seats by fixed year ranges (SecNavy @1798, PMG @1829, Interior @1849); no in-game office creation OR abolition. SCOTUS/courts ship pre-existing in 1856 | **‚òÖ The defining shape of the founding era: foundational institutions do NOT exist at game start ‚Äî they are CREATED by in-game bills, years after the Constitution, each opening new appointable offices.** `new1772` stands up the whole federal apparatus by legislation: **Supreme Court via TWO bills** ("Establish Federal Judiciary" + a separate "Set the Number of SCOTUS Justices to 6" ‚Äî NOT auto-packaged; if the court passes but the number bill fails the **game cannot advance**; era cap 6); **Bank of the US ‚Üí a new "President of the Bank of USA" cabinet-level seat**; **Dept of the Navy ‚Üí a new SecNavy seat**; **Attorney General office created by bill** (AG un-appointable until it passes); plus US Military/Naval Academies, Library of Congress, District of Columbia, US Mint, US Marine Corps. **This is a FOUNDING-era confirmation that the modern "offices created by law" pattern (#66 Fed-Chair/CoS/CNO/FBI in `hd`; #89 Dept-of-Environment in `pop`) is the design's GENERAL mechanism across ALL eras** ‚Äî the shipped year-keyed hardcode is the wrong model end-to-end, not just for late eras. **‚òÖ MODERN confirmation + the ABOLISH side (batch 9, `nuke`):** in the 1948‚Üí2005 run the cabinet is **fully mutable both ways** ‚Äî departments are CREATED by ordinary law (OMB, HEW, HUD, Dept of Transportation, NSC + Office of National Security Advisor, Job Corps, **Dept of Energy** (HR.2), **Dept of Homeland Security** (Era of Terror)), **SPLIT** by law (**HEW ‚Üí HHS + Education**, H.R.3), and **ABOLISHED** by law (**Postmaster General office abolished** by the postal-independence bill). Cabinet appointment slots track the live set. So founding‚Üímodern both confirm: the year-keyed `cabinetSeatsForYear` hardcode must become a runtime-mutable office registry | Generalize `cabinetSeatsForYear` into a fully **dynamic, bill-/era-event-driven office list across all eras** that supports CREATE / SPLIT / **ABOLISH** (not just a modern add-on): SCOTUS-establishment + a separate set-Justice-count bill (with the "court exists but count unset ‚Üí cannot advance" guard), Bank ‚Üí unremovable Bank-President seat, Dept ‚Üí Secretary seat, AG-by-bill, department-split ‚Üí two seats, office-abolition ‚Üí seat removed, etc. Unifies #66 (progressive layer) + #89 (modern) + #95 (Bank-President office) under one founding+modern-confirmed pattern | ingested (**new1772 ¬ß4 / POST 2251, 2421, 2444-2446, 3053, 3096, 3140-3164, 3271; nuke (mutable cabinet) / POST 379, 405, 436, 501, 1047, 1952, 6730, 6799, 6946, 10910, 11779; extends #66/#89/#95**; **‚òÖ principle1772 ‚Äî 7th-source FOUNDING‚ÜíFEDERALISM confirmation from the DESIGNER's run: the 1788 cabinet does NOT exist ["cabinets have to be created by law‚Ä¶ can't be created until after the Revolutionary War ends," POST 880] ‚Üí President Whipple governs alone with only a Key Advisor [the one office not created by law]; the federal apparatus is then stood up bill-by-bill [Dept of Treasury‚ÜíSec seat, Establish-Federal-Judiciary + a SEPARATE Set-SCOTUS-count-to-6 bill, Office of AG, US Mint (requires Bank), Dept of State, Dept of War, Bank of the US, US Dollar (requires Mint), Library of Congress, ch14-16/ch26]; the Bank can be repealed then RE-established by bill [V: "there was a 2nd bank of the US," POST 1820]; the late-1790s Federalist cabinet is fully populated [Sec State Peters, Sec Treasury Stoddert, Sec War Key, AG Sullivan, Key Advisor HAMILTON, POST 1809]; ‚òÖ corroborates DH-40 ‚Äî same "Judicial Act" (Constitution) vs "Judiciary Act" (legislation) NAMING-MISMATCH bug, POST 962-964**) + codebase (types.ts:1196 hardcoded-by-year) |

Adds no mechanic/trigger detail (the answering player: "I don't know all the scenarios off the top of my head"), re-affirming #188's finding that the nuclear loss is premise-only and its trigger conditions are not even common player knowledge. The game ships NO modern scenario (1772/1856 only; `Era='...|modern'` enumerated `types.ts:1337` but no modern `scenario` file), so this content genre is 0% reachable; grep `nuclear\|apocalypse\|doomsday` in `src/` = 0. Corroboration only, no new gap. `a19001a5` POST 5-7**) |

### Early-republic systems (NEW ‚Äî `rep1800`-only; the 1800‚Äì1856 gap-fill)

The `rep1800` thread is the **first procedural record of the 1800‚Äì1856 early
republic** (Jeffersonian ‚Üí Era-of-Good-Feelings ‚Üí Jacksonian ‚Üí Manifest-Destiny).
The rows below have no shipped representation and were not surfaced by the prior
seven threads. The headline architecture finding lives in #92 (cross-cutting);
these are the era-content + subsystem deltas. Several **refine** existing rows
(noted) rather than duplicate ‚Äî dedup is hard here.

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 93 | **12th-Amendment before/after state machine (conventions + VP-on-ticket are unlocked, not always-on)** | No 12th-Amendment toggle; conventions (phase 2.9.2) + VP exist unconditionally; no `electorsByLegislature` flag | A genuine **era-specific state machine** absent from all post-12A threads. **PRE-12A:** **no conventions at all**; many states pick presidential electors **by legislature** ‚Äî for those the EV winner = **who holds Gov/Sen/Rep** (majority party; Gov breaks ties), **NOT** PV+dice (a per-state `electorsByLegislature` flag, sibling of `senatePre17`; in 1804 CT/GA/MA/NJ/NY/SC/VT were legislature-decided). **Passing the in-game "President Elections by Party Tickets Amendment" (= the 12A)** is the single toggle that **unlocks conventions + the separate VP-on-the-ticket** rule (and gates "Separate VP Election" + "Send VP to Shore Up Support", confirming #91). The **"Nationwide Popular-Vote Surge" era event** later flips **all states except SC** off legislature-electors ‚Äî the end-state of the machine. Also the **first fully-recorded FIRST convention** (resolves `gilded` open-Q #10) | Add the 12A as a **toggleable amendment state** that gates conventions + VP-on-ticket; add a per-state `electorsByLegislature` flag flipped off by amendment/the popular-vote-surge event; pre-12A EC = state-control tally not PV+dice. Pairs with #39/#44/#91 + the convention subsystem #13 | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß2-A, ¬ßA 222, 264, 276, 502, 638, 691, 708, 2468; ¬ßB 6650**; **new1772 reaches the SAME machine organically from a 1772 start ‚Äî pre-12A ALL candidates run for President and the top 2 become Pres/VP (no conventions); passing the "President Elections by Party Ticket Amendment" turns on conventions + running mates for 1796; pre-12A EVs split between legislature-decided states (vote by Kingmakers/Gov) and a popular-vote subset; ¬ß2h+¬ß5 / POST 1981-1987, 2296, 2665, 3460**; **arkzag ‚Äî the holdouts persist into the 1820s-40s: in the 1828 presidential election DE + SC still choose electors by LEGISLATURE ("Delaware/South Carolina: Legislature selects [candidate]", ch15 POST 1253-1257) while every other state runs the d6 popular vote**; **‚òÖ completions ¬ßElections POST 59, 156 ‚Äî DESIGNER-DONE/UNVERIFIED: the 2022 ref game updated/added the **pre-12th-Amendment general-election rules** [no true party ticket; the "50%‚Üí100% trait" rule does NOT apply pre-12th]; to-do ¬ßPlatform POSTs 4, 118, 773-774 ‚Äî the major-vs-minor candidate rule [auto-major = incumbent Pres / faction leaders / Celebrity; vcczar DECLINES adding ex-Pres/ex-VP/ex-noms, citing historical non-major precedents] [#47/#104]**) |
| 94 | **Slavery = persistent state flag tied to Plantation + Cohens-v-Virginia amendment-only abolition** | `SLAVE_STATES_1856` const + `State` exists; **no `State.policies` flag** (per #21); no scripted-ruling rule-modifier on legislation | **Slavery ON/OFF is a persistent state-level policy flag tied to the Plantation industry** (extends #21's Jim-Crow/poll-tax pattern *backward* ‚Äî the gap-era flag is `slavery`/Plantation): a successful abolition bill turns OFF Plantation nationwide + permanently deactivates all slavery legislation; abolition "counts" only when NO state has it active. **‚òÖ The ahistorical `Cohens v. Virginia` SCOTUS precedent holds federal law does NOT supersede existing state law** ‚Üí slavery is a **state issue**, abolishable **only by a constitutional Amendment** (or per-state Gov action); existing slave states keep it, all new states/territories enter free, spread halts. **This is the engine substrate for the whole sectional design** (feeds #59's free/slave-balance crisis) ‚Äî and there is **no mechanism to reverse an ahistorical ruling** (cross-thread AMPU-2 need) | Add `slavery`/Plantation to #21's `State.policies` set with the nationwide-off-on-abolition behavior; model the **scripted-SCOTUS-precedent-as-persistent-legislation-rule-modifier** (`Cohens` blocks legislative abolition until an Amendment) + a ruling-reversal-via-amendment path. Pairs with #21/#52/#59 | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß2-B, ¬ßA 2161, 2180‚Äì2182, 2675; ¬ßB 3363, 3366, 4329, 5567, 5820**) |
| 95 | **Second Bank of the US: recharter clock + Bank War exec action + unremovable Bank-President office** | No Bank model; cabinet seats are year-keyed (`cabinetSeatsForYear`, types.ts:1196), fixed | The Bank is a **toggleable, cabinet-seat-granting institution with a 20-yr recharter clock** (created as a Crisis Bill; **recreates the President-of-US-Bank office, which is UNREMOVABLE while the Bank exists**; must be rechartered within 20 yrs or it lapses). Jefferson's exec action **"Weaken US Bank by Removing Deposits ‚Üí State Banks"** deactivates it unless the next President reverses it = **the Bank War in miniature**; by the era boundary the Bank had **lapsed** (next Pres didn't reverse), and recharter bills then fail in committee. (`hd`/¬ßC confirm the same Bank-creates-an-office + Rotation-in-Office-exempt pattern across eras) | Add a **Bank institution model**: toggle + 20-yr recharter clock + auto-lapse-on-expiry + the President-of-US-Bank office (created-by-bill, unremovable-while-active, exempt from cabinet rotation) + the deposits-removal deactivation exec action. Pairs with #66/#89 (bill/exec-action-created offices) | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß1/¬ß2-D, ¬ßA 954, 2123, 2161, 2350, 3175; ¬ßC 8362**; **‚òÖ fixes ¬ßJ POST 339, 393 ‚Äî DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE: V RULED the **"President of the US Bank (Economics)"** office goes to the **Wall-Street-Lobby** faction, **+100 pts**, **Controversial-gated** ‚Äî the office-assignment detail the Bank model needs [#95/#89/#101]**) |
| 96 | **"Call for New Constitutional Convention" ‚Äî 20-state DomStab-crisis subsystem** | No constitutional-convention-recall path (the 1772 `constitutionalConvention.ts` builds the *initial* Constitution only) | A **Governor action available during a DomStab crisis** that calls for a new Constitutional Convention: needs **20 states to call**, and the call **persists until 20 are reached OR DomStab recovers above level 1**. A separate **"New Constitutional Convention Amendment"** bill needs 20 states (2/3) to ratify (failed 16/20 here when Puritan govs voted against their players). A distinct antebellum crisis-escalation subsystem (the legal pathway the secession debate routes through) | Add a 20-state convention-call gov action gated on DomStab-crisis + a persistent call counter (clears at 20 or on DomStab recovery) + the convention-amendment ratification path. Pairs with the secession/DomStab-game-ender machinery (#58 / #98) | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß3, ¬ßB 6356, 6360, 6419**) |
| 97 | **Civil-War VARIANTS (this campaign's alt-CW diverges from House Divided)** | No Civil War model at all (#56 is the `hd`/`drums` spec; `eraEvents1856.ts` ends at the Trent Affair) | `rep1800` is a **second, divergent Civil War** ‚Äî independent corroboration of #56's engine *plus* new variant branches the build must support: (a) **early trigger off DomStab=1** via the "Southern States form the CSA" era event (NOT a secession-convention ‚Äî GM flags this as the one CW trigger missing the convention-spawn field, a likely bug); (b) **the sitting President can defect and become President of the CSA** ("Oaths to Two Masters" achievement); (c) the engine models **Hartford-Convention + Northern-Secession** CW variants, not just Southern; (d) a **UK-Intervention third theater** (from a blundered "UK Recognition of CSA" event ‚Üí UK Hostile ‚Üí "Enemy Takes Defenseless US" risk); (e) a **guerrilla 4th stage** with no chart rule (GM improvised); (f) the **CSA runs its own internal president & elections**; (g) Appomattox: Party Pref +4, Pres permanent **+1 in future elections**, blunder does NOT undo the treaty | Generalize #56's Civil-War engine to support **multiple secession variants** (Southern/Hartford/Northern), a **DomStab=1 auto-secession trigger**, a **President-defects-to-rebel-state** path + the achievement, a **foreign-intervention theater**, a **guerrilla 4th stage** (the missing chart rule), and a **rebel-government-runs-its-own-elections** model. Refines/extends #56 + #58 | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß3-1, ¬ßC 6884, 6905, 6909‚Äì6914, 6934, 6943, 7253‚Äì7256, 7404, 7418, 8542‚Äì8550, 8654, 8661**) |
| 98 | **Coup-at-low-meters + the enumerated meters-driven game-over set (effectively dead late game)** | `GameState.gameEnded` + `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` exist (event-driven); no meter-driven game-over set, no Coup path | **Meters-driven game-over is REAL and enumerated** (¬ßC POST 7274): on each general-event phase, with meters at the worst tier the game can END on a roll ‚Äî **Autocratic Coup** (HonestGov=1, scaled by an "Era of Democracy" meter, 0% until 1868), **Standard Coup** (DomStab‚â§2 AND EconStab‚â§2 ‚Üí 10%), **Attempted Coup** (EconStab‚â§2 AND MilPrep‚â§3 AND DomStab‚â§2 ‚Üí 10%), **Economic Collapse** (20% if EconStab=1), **Enemy Takes Defenseless US** (MilPrep=1 AND a foreign power < Neutral). The **Coup AnytimeEvo (3.0.2)** lets the opposition PL choose Move-On / Condemn. **‚òÖ Balance note: coups are effectively DEAD mid/late game** ‚Äî "our military is at max prep so a coup is damned near impossible" (MilPrep + foreign relations trivially maxed with a decent cabinet ‚Äî links #73/#77 and the DH-4 "extend credit" loop). Companion to #88's APOCALYPSE meter-driven endgame: this is the **early-era enumerated game-over set** | Add a **meter-derived game-over set** (the 5 enumerated triggers with their tier-gated %s) + the Coup AnytimeEvo (PL Move-On/Condemn) + an **era-scaling factor** (Autocratic-Coup 0% until late). Rebalance the trivially-maxed MilPrep/foreign-relations gate so coups aren't dead (pairs with #88/#73/#77, DH-4) | ingested (**rep1800 ¬ß3, ¬ßC 7274, 7275, 7836‚Äì7838, 9317, 9322, 9593, 9853**) |

The 1772 solo thread exists specifically to prototype the **look and feel** of
the eventual computerized AMPU. Its thesis: *a political sim wearing
fantasy-sports / trading-card clothes with an old-school almanac vibe.* None of
this is a deliberate visual language in the shipped UI (functional pages only).

| # | Area | In the build today | In the documented design | Delta / requirement | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Politician portrait / art pipeline** | `Politician` (types.ts) has no image/portrait field; no card view | Hand-authored art for marquee figures + **CK2-style procedural portraits** for the ~18.5k long tail. **Hard stance: no AI-generated imagery or text in the shipped product** (AI used only as a proof-of-concept stand-in). **`modern` reinforces:** the dataset runs out in deep-modern ‚Üí procedurally-generated pols (#43) need procedural portraits too, so the portrait system must cover *generated* pols, not just the real dataset | Add a portrait field + a procedural-portrait system covering generated pols; commit to no-AI-in-product art direction | ingested (1772s 13, 14, 51, 860; modern 456, 1771) |
| A2 | **Canonical ideology‚Üícolor palette (cross-cutting)** | `components/Meter.tsx` colors meters; no documented 7-ideology palette reused everywhere | A canonical ideology‚Üícolor mapping (Prog/Lib/Mod/Cons/Trad/RW-Pop legend) used consistently across roster, congress, maps, score sheets. Color-coding is core to both *reading* and *playing* (it makes Committees tractable) | Define + apply one ideology‚Üícolor palette app-wide | ingested (1772s 12, 25, 70, 71) |
| A3 | **"Sport-card" politician infobox + styled persistent scoreboard** | `Roster.tsx` is tabular; scoring has `HalfTermSummary` (types.ts:1762) but no marquee leaderboard view | Per-politician character "sport card" (portrait/traits/stats/PV/office) + an always-on styled scoreboard rendered each turn | Add a card component + a persistent scoreboard view | ingested (1772s 13, 14, 15, 22, 53) |
| A4 | **Legible battle-card with additive-odds breakdown** | Rev-War dashboard exists (`RevWarDashboard.tsx`); whether it surfaces the additive odds breakdown is unverified | A battle card showing the itemized to-hit math (difficulty + planning + commander + meters + benchmarks ‚Üí % victory) | Verify/add the additive-odds breakdown in the war UI (pairs with #45) | ingested (1772s 22, 48, 60) |
| A5 | **Era/colony-specific office titles** | Generic "Governor" label | Period-correct executive titles ("President of the Supreme Executive Council of Pennsylvania", "1st President of Delaware", ‚Ä¶) per colony and era | Per-(office, era, state) title strings | ingested (1772s 25) |
| A6 | **Honorific-memory + "remembered for‚Ä¶" legacy lines** | Not in build | Persist a politician's prior-office honorific ("Senator X") for use in narration, and award durable legacy/epitaph lines from era-event participation | Add honorific-memory + legacy-line fields driven by office history + era-event participation | ingested (1772s 16, 17, 18) |
| A7 | **Election-result maps + popular-vote atlas + era-correct flags** | No election maps; no era iconography | Election-result maps + era-correct flags as set-pieces. `modern` shows the bar concretely: players hand-build **yapms.com / Election-Atlas maps** and the GM hand-produces a **per-state popular-vote % table + national totals** every presidential election ‚Äî the build should generate this 53-state map + popular-vote atlas automatically | Map renderer + per-state popular-vote atlas + era-iconography assets | ingested (1772s 23, 24, 40; modern 131, 438, 1082, 1749) |
| A8 | **Narration density / voice** | `EraEvent` text fields exist (types.ts); generated-log voice unverified | The build's generated log should aim for the forum's in-character narration density (verbatim historical flavor + editorializing asides) | Verify `log.ts` output voice against the forum bar | ingested (1772s 9, 18, 37, 56) |
| A9 | **Persist + auto-fill House candidate slates & committee rosters** ‚Äî NEW | Election + committee assignment exist; no persistence of slates across cycles | At modern scale (House **~572-601** under the Wyoming Rule, Senate 106) the **manual** House-election and committee-staffing phases are the dominant tedium ‚Äî the forum's RepElections tab is **wiped every cycle**, players keep companion files, and **a human quit over it**. **Hard requirement:** persist a faction's House candidate slate + committee rosters across cycles and **auto-fill/carry-forward** by default | Persist `House` candidate slates + committee rosters in the snapshot; default to carry-forward + bulk auto-fill; this is a *scaling-wall* requirement, not cosmetic | ingested (modern 115, 185, 419-421, 1281) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|

The `modern` rows are no longer hypothetical ‚Äî there's a 2276-post spec | codebase + ingested (gilded 1; modern 1, 769, 1106); **‚òÖ gild1868 (b14) is the strongest case for a dedicated gilded era ‚Äî a native 1868‚Üí1886 full-arc spec; see NEW #147-#150; gild1868 1, 70, 462-494, 696, 816, 6240**; **‚òÖ ideo1928 adds the strongest case for a dedicated `progressive`/interwar era ‚Äî a native 1928‚Üí1936 interwar arc (Hoover‚ÜíFDR, the crash/New-Deal/EconStab content) that currently has NO era value (runs on `modern` tuning); the long timeline now wants at minimum gilded + progressive + modern splits; see NEW #160/#161**; **‚òÖ completions ¬ßEras POST 121-136 ‚Äî DESIGNER-DONE/UNVERIFIED: the 2022 ref game **SPLIT "Era of the Future" into TWO** (Near Future / Distant Future), dividing Legis Props / Gov Actions / Pres Actions / Scripted Events / SC cases / state biases ["Distant Future quite chaotic"] ‚Äî corroborates the era-band model #92 and the near-future band [#161/#169]; NONE of these future bands exist in our 2-scenario (1772/1856) build. completions POST 128-131 ‚Äî **NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD: rising-sea-level events** [Distant-Future only, needs low Planet's Health ‚Üí deplete HALF the EVs of affected coastal states, redistributed to interior states at next census; SW can become uninhabitable] ‚Äî a future-era EV mechanic absent from `src/`. Pairs with #34/#109/#92** |

(Confirms A9 House-slate persistence as a scaling wall ‚Äî the 1822 midterm took ~10 forum pages / 8 real days) | ingested (modern 426, 438, 462, 495, 1785, 2240; **dem1820 POST 643, 665-696, 704**; **‚òÖ BATCH-33 SHARPEN (`housepoll` aed35c6e) ‚Äî replace the flat "Large=3/Med=2/Small=1" focus-rep description with the **EV-derived 1-10 focus-rep ladder** (US Reps = EVs‚àí2 ‚Üí ladder ‚â§5‚Üí1 ‚Ä¶ ‚â•46‚Üí10; Small=1 / Large=4+; see #219 for the full ladder + the per-focus-rep VOTE-WEIGHT split with remainder‚Üíhighest-Legislative). Shipped reality is still the flat per-state `representativeIds: string[]` (types.ts:1327) with **NO EV‚Üíladder sizing, NO per-seat vote-weight split, and NO census recompute** ‚Äî that tri+absence is the #55 DELTA. The old flat 3/2/1 was actually BACKWARDS in some eras (1788 Federalists got under-counted; a single NY rep could hold ~20 of 30 state votes = unbalanced super-rep, and players skipped NY/CA pols as unwinnable ‚Äî the concrete failure the ladder fixes). vcczar BUILT the ladder into every start date by hand (POSTs 27-41); open Q (tech-lead): should the engine AUTO-derive focus-rep counts from EV at each census, or keep the static authored per-start-date rosters? housepoll POST 1, 6, 19, 23, 27-41**; **‚òÖ batch 35 ‚Äî `electiondisc` (5a5d988f) corroborates seat-locked incumbency AND articulates the UNLOCK refinement: a hard seat-lock that RELEASES only when the state's rep count changes (reapportionment) so the displaced pol gets a chance to move (Rezi POST 3); reapportionment edge ‚Äî GA shrinking to 1 Rep frees the GA-2 incumbent to contest GA-1 (POST 5). See NEW #250 for the full seat-competition fork + the CPU-keeps-incumbents-in-seat rule. electiondisc POST 1, 2, 3, 5**; **‚òÖ (b39) `8f12ac05` (US House Votes, May 2022) corroborates the focus-Rep abstraction KEPT and the era-keyed sizing: named-rep count (1/2/3) is **population-RANK + ERA keyed** ‚Äî "top 3 states have 3 reps, then later top 5, eventually top 10 in the modern era" (Ted POST 9); the count of states allowed 3 reps GROWS by era (this thread caps at 3, the pre-housepoll-expansion statement). ‚òÖ vcczar confirms he has **a per-era per-state reps-size progression DOCUMENT** ("a whole document that shows the progression of the US Reps sizes for each state for each era," POST 10) ‚Äî authored DATA the build needs (the same artifact housepoll later hand-built). vcczar WON'T add ~20k more Reps to support a 4-5 cap (POST 14, the authoring-cost wall). 8f12ac05 POST 9, 10, 14**; **‚òÖ (b40) `expansionism` (4e76e6c3 POST 1, 5) corroborates this roster row: "Create District of Columbia" + annex Cuba etc. are roster data the modern 53-state apportionment partly anticipates (#55 already records DC/Cuba/PR as full states); but **state-FISSION** (split one state into two) is unmodeled (admit is add-only) and the West/East-Florida statehood-vs-fold alt-history boundary branches (POST 5) aren't in the fixed scenario rosters ‚Äî both broken out as NEW #269. expansionism POST 1, 5**; **‚òÖ (b63) `4c020c50` ("Are there any Native American states that can be admitted?", theFreezerFlame) adds the first-named **NATIVE-NATION STATES** as intended roster content ‚Äî a distinct pre-1900 addition to this roster row (Indian-Territory / Revolution-era, NOT the modern annexed Cuba/PR/DC set): **Sequoyah** ("most notable" ‚Äî petitioned for statehood IRL ~1905 Sequoyah Convention; the flagship, sized like a real state), **Haudenosaunee** (Iroquois/Six Nations ‚Äî could be OFFERED statehood DURING the American Revolution, i.e. a 1772-era admission branch on the `admitState` pipeline, POST 3), **Din√©tah** (Navajo) and **Lakota** ‚Äî the last two floated as an **"always-3-EV" fixed small-state class** (never grows with census, POST 3). Two sizing classes: Sequoyah/Haudenosaunee as full/normal states with real admission events; Din√©tah/Lakota as fixed-EV=3. ‚òÖ SHIPPED: grep `Sequoyah\|Lakota\|Haudenosaunee\|Din[e√©]tah\|Navajo` in `src/` = ZERO state entries ‚Äî the roster has ONLY `oklahoma` (`expansionStates.ts:97`), which stands in for the whole Indian-Territory region (direct GM confirm POST 4: "Sequoyah is not in the list of states, only Oklahoma"). No always-3-EV class exists: every expansion state is stamped flat `electoralVotes:4, bias:0` (`expansionStates.ts:178-179`) with no per-state EV, no fixed-EV=3, no census-driven EV growth (`admitState` just copies the seed) ‚Äî corroborates the b58 flat-EV=4 finding + feeds this row's census/EV-recompute delta. The Haudenosaunee-during-Revolution path would ride the 1772 territories/admission pipeline (`engine/territories.ts admitState`); no Native-admission branch exists. ‚òÖ CROSS-REF #337 (Indian Sovereignty) ‚Äî granting a region sovereignty is the plausible ALTERNATIVE to admitting these as states (grant sovereignty OR admit; POST 66 open Q asks if Oklahoma gets split into / replaced by Sequoyah). Requirement: add the Native-nation states to the roster (with the Revolution-era Haudenosaunee admission branch) + a fixed-EV small-state class (Din√©tah/Lakota always-3-EV) as part of this row's per-state-EV/census work. OPEN (human): full census-sized vs fixed-EV for Sequoyah/Haudenosaunee; EV/bias/era-availability per nation; admit-vs-sovereignty exclusivity (#337); Oklahoma split-out. `4c020c50` POST 1, 3, 4, 66**) |

Reconfirms `Union Loyalist`/`Southern Unionist`/`Secessionist` all STILL absent from `types.ts:62-117`. | 122 | **‚òÖ NEW gap ‚Äî Pardon mechanics entirely unspecified** ‚Äî NEW | No pardon mechanic in the rules; no rule for what happens when an exec pardons a pol with `Controversial` or with a scandal | Per @Bushwa777 (`smallbugs` POST 113): *"Pardon rules not spelled out. What happens if pres pardons someone? Do they lose traits they got like controversial? If not pardoned do they get retired from game?"* ‚Äî no follow-up in the thread. Pardons interact with the Controversial-trait apparatus, the impeachment machinery (#112), the resignation-penalty pipeline (resignation-while-impeached = permanent ‚àí3 future elections / barred from appointment / loses Integrity), and the cabinet-resignation-and-Scandalous-Office-Holder loop (POST 618 ‚Äî a fired pol can be re-appointed next phase). **Author-time decision; no ruling yet.** | Specify pardon mechanics: who can be pardoned (any non-impeached citizen?), what gets undone (Controversial trait? scandal trait? election malus? impeachment-disqualification?), the political-cost side (pardoning a Controversial pol = pardoner gains Controversial? Honest-Gov drop?). Pairs with #112 (impeachment) + the post-`Scandalous Office Holder` re-appointment loop | ingested (**smallbugs (cf82a7d3) ¬ß4b / POST 113**; **‚òÖ completions ¬ßEras POST 174, 177-178 ‚Äî partial AUTHORITATIVE source (NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD): the 3.0.35 "Secessionist Politicians" ruleset establishes the **only concrete pardon hook in the 2022 ref game** ‚Äî seceded politicians are "temporarily unavailable **until pardon**," i.e. a pardon RESTORES a secessionist to the eligible pool. This pins one pardon use-case [Reconstruction re-eligibility]; the broader pardon questions [does a pardon strip Controversial / a scandal trait / an election malus / impeachment-disqualification, and the pardoner's political cost] remain UNSPECIFIED ‚Äî author-time decision, no ruling. Pairs with #121**; **‚òÖ (b62) NEW concrete pardon USE-CASE + an AUTHORITY-SPLIT rule ‚Äî `5500e143` ("How best to handle Trump's arrest in AMPU", Apr 4 2023, proposer Imperator Taco Cat POST 2). The proposal: an arrested pol is **PREVENTED from taking an appointed office UNLESS PARDONED**, and the pardon authority is **role-dependent by CRIME TYPE ‚Äî the PRESIDENT pardons federal crimes, a GOVERNOR pardons state crimes**. This sharpens #122's "who can pardon / what a pardon lifts" with a second concrete use-case (a pardon RELEASES an office-LOCK) beside the b28-established secessionist use-case (a pardon RESTORES a seceded pol to the eligible pool), and adds the **federal-vs-state pardoner split** #122 had not recorded. It is the RELEASE VALVE for #327's office-prohibition flag (which today has NO lift path). ‚òÖ RE-VERIFIED still 0% built: the ONLY `pardon` token in `src/` is a FLAVOR executive action `'Pardon political prisoners.'` (`phaseRunners.ts:3639`) that merely nudges `game.partyPreference` ¬±0.2 and targets NO politician ‚Äî there is NO politician-targeted pardon action for president OR governor, and no governor-pardon path at all. The office-LOCK the pardon would lift is itself unbuilt (office eligibility is gated only by `deathYear`/`retiredYear`/`currentOffice`, `types.ts:1278-1291`; no recoverable ineligibility flag ‚Üí #327). Cross-ref #327 (the office-prohibition flag a pardon releases) / #112 (impeachment-disqualification, the other thing a pardon might lift) / #284 (Reconstruction ex-Confederate lockout, whose lift condition is the same pardon family). `5500e143` POST 2**) |

| 124 | **‚òÖ Cabinet ‚Üí enthusiasm REWORK: IDEOLOGY-COMPOSITION drives enthusiasm; LOBBIES give bonus POINTS** ‚Äî NEW | Cabinet has its own enthusiasm-via-lobby model (gap #31 "cabinet enthusiasm-via-lobbies overwhelms presidential signal," partially patched by #80 ¬±3 cap); ideology-composition not modeled | **‚òÖ Ted's stated rework (`tedchange` POST 1, 4):** (1) Lobby satisfaction now gives **bonus POINTS** to the President + factions w/ matching lobby cards (was enthusiasm). (2) **Ideology composition** drives enthusiasm ‚Äî ‚â•50% of cabinet of an ideology = +enth that ideology; ‚â§20% representation = ‚àíenth. Big-4 / rest-of-cabinet / cabinet-level potentially weighted differently. **3 shifts/half-term cap retained.** | Rewrite the cabinet‚Üíenthusiasm system to separate the POINTS path (lobby-driven) from the ENTHUSIASM path (ideology-composition-driven). Pairs with #31. **RULED IN CONCEPT** (Ted POST 1); specific percentages OPEN. The build's cabinet-enthusiasm logic likely needs a rewrite. **‚òÖ `terror2000` RE-TUNES THE LOBBY‚ÜíENTHUSIASM PATH LIVE (Ted, designer-authoritative):** the old "stacking happiness" rule gave **Mods +18 off one cabinet** (capped +3) ‚Äî Ted/ebrk: "completely broken ‚Ä¶ allowing happiness to stack while not allowing unhappiness to stack" (POST 486-488); Ted reworked it to a **three-state "upset / fine / happy" model** (POST 489): a faction (by lobby cards) that gets **‚â•1** of what it wants = **no impact**; **>1** = **20%/10% chance +1 enthusiasm**; **ZERO** = **20%/10% chance ‚àí1 enthusiasm**; **ONE roll per faction, multiple same-ideology factions STACK; max ¬±3**. This closes the "happiness-stacks-but-unhappiness-doesn't" asymmetry. Note BOTH Era-of-Terror cabinet penalties (this lobby/ideology enthusiasm path + the appointment-fairness ‚àí500 #151 + the cabinet-DIVERSITY penalty) fire together natively in a 2000-start (POST 428, 434) | ingested (**tedchange POST 1-4**; **terror2000 POST 486-491, 428, 434; see #151**; **modernday ‚Äî a 28-seat cabinet confirmed under current rules: ‚â•1 admin + ‚â•1 relevant expertise + not-Incompetent (Easily-Overwhelmed rejects 50%; Senate/House-leaders + SC-justices reject 75%, except Sec-of-State 50%) [POST 396]; lobby/points path fires [2 eggheads in cabinet ‚Üí +100 points, POST 427]; CIA-Director is the modern intel slot, NO DNI office [corroborates #170]; ‚òÖ confirmation THRESHOLD = 50%+1 for cabinet (Nuclear-Option permanently in place for a 2016 start), 60% for SCOTUS ‚Äî Ted-ruled, see NEW #172**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b44) PRIMARY-CHANGELOG ACTUAL NUMBERS ‚Äî `4747b09f` (Ted's actual "Change Log of Doom", topic 5720) POST 19 PINS the percentages `tedchange`/a0f0bf04 logged as "TBD": per lobby/interest ‚Äî exactly ONE wanted Cabinet post = content (no change); ZERO wanted = **20% chance ‚àí1 enthusiasm** (away from Pres's party for any faction holding that card); MORE-THAN-ONE wanted = **20% chance +1**; **does NOT stack** (5 wanted posts = still ONE 20% roll); **one roll per lobby/interest, applied to all factions sharing the card.** Cabinet-LEVEL posts = same model at **10%**; Generals/Admirals/Ambassadors = **5%**. Cabinet="all Secretary posts + AG"; Cabinet-Level="all non-Sec/AG posts EXCEPT Amb/Gen/Adm". This is the version "we've been testing successfully in the 2000 game" ‚Äî matches `terror2000`'s three-state upset/fine/happy 20%/10% model above, confirming that IS the shipped-forum rule. ‚öÝ **USER-CONFIRM #1 (reconciliation):** the primary changelog POST 19 = this **20/10/5% lobby-WANT** model; the older discussion digest `tedchange`/a0f0bf04 ¬ß5 described a DIFFERENT **50%/20% ideology-COMPOSITION** model (‚â•50% of cabinet of an ideology ‚Üí +enth; ‚â§20% ‚Üí ‚àíenth). The 50%/20% composition model is **NOT in the written changelog** ‚Äî it was a separate/later floated proposal whose final status is unknown. **Changelog is AUTHORITATIVE for the build: implement the 20/10/5% lobby-want model, NOT the 50%/20% composition model.** ‚öÝ **Appointments-2.3 (b44, `5d225e8a` topic 5647, Aug 2024) FLAG ‚Äî DO NOT BUILD AS-SPECCED:** Ted+ebrk judged even this rule **overtuned (~20-pt enthusiasm swings uncapped in the 2000 test; the ¬±3 cap is insufficient) AND too complicated**, and earmarked the entire lobby‚Üíenthusiasm system for its **own future "simplifying rebuild" group discussion** (POST 2-3) ‚Äî so the lobby‚Üíenthusiasm path is designed-but-unbuilt AND design-flagged for replacement; await the rebuild before implementing. Caveat (POST 8-10): the cabinet may be the **only reliable POSITIVE meter lever** in the game, so any nerf needs a replacement positive lever. (The competing matt/Arkansas-Progressive Big-Four-20%/lower-10% TIERED proposal ‚Äî see NEW #275 ‚Äî is MORE elaborate and conflicts with the "simplify" goal.) `4747b09f` POST 19; `5d225e8a` POST 2-3, 8-10**; **‚òÖ (b45) STRONG corroboration of the "cabinet is the ONLY reliable POSITIVE meter lever" caveat ‚Äî from the OTHER side: LEGISLATION‚Üímeters is near-ZERO (`38cedf29` April-2023 POST 28-30). After veto-proof 1958 majorities, a single session passed NASA + Dept-of-Transportation + HEW + House-size-cap-repeal + PR&DC statehood (‚Üí52 states) + Medicaid, yet "Medicaid ‚Ä¶ isn't that big a deal in the game either ‚Ä¶ It just garnered 50 points for a few folks and no meters changed" (POST 29). Players game it: "no way would I let anything pass that might help the meters until we get a Democrat in the White House" (POST 30) ‚Äî they STONEWALL marquee bills at near-zero cost precisely BECAUSE bills don't move meters. So if cabinet is the only reliable positive meter lever, marquee legislation being meter-inert is the same hole from the other side. The legislation‚Üímeter WIRING gap proper lives at #12 (updated b45). `38cedf29` POST 28, 29, 30**; **‚òÖ (b48) `3b46b086` (the 1960-playtest ROOT-CAUSE thread) shows this lever reads GARBAGE INPUTS until the boot-stat-init lands: the roster booted on ROOKIE/draft stats ‚Üí "maybe 8 pols with 3 admin and **none with more than 3**" ‚Üí "every cabinet is filled with a bunch of **2's who crater the meters**" (POST 3). Boot-stat-init (NEW **#289**, the 3-tier stat model + draft-class-age back-fill) is the **PRECONDITION** for this cabinet‚Üímeter lever to read sane Admin. Also a structural risk: the authored 1960 bench is LOPSIDED ("Republicans have a far more competent array of cabinet officials; the Democrats ‚Ä¶ will likely continue to plunge the nation into a hole if/when they win in 64", POST 3) ‚Üí whichever weaker party wins triggers a meter DEATH-SPIRAL the cabinet lever can't escape (a per-era bench competence-parity pass, or meter floors, is the residual fix beyond #289). `3b46b086` POST 3**; **‚òÖ (b49) this cabinet‚Üíenthusiasm rework PRESUMES a meter‚Üíenthusiasm step that does not exist ‚Äî NEW **#292** (`6801b9f4`): the build's `game.enthusiasm` is a static authored table mutated only by era events, never derived from meters at end-of-Lingering, so the ideology-composition / lobby-want enthusiasm shifts this row specs have no meter-driven baseline to move from and never propagate to elections. #292 (the meter‚Üíenthusiasm derivation + the per-state-scoring formula) is the structural precondition this row's enthusiasm output feeds into. `6801b9f4` POST 6**; **‚òÖ (b55) THIRD independent witness of the 20/10/5% appointment-enthusiasm model, run LIVE with a worked example + the shipped-shape delta ‚Äî `1892-era-of-progressivism` (26b112e5, GM @Arkansas Progressive, POST 20): the script "CABINET APPOINTMENT ENTHUSIASM CALCULATOR" rolls **once per post-TIER a lobby/interest group holds influence in** ‚Äî **Cabinet 20% / Cabinet-Level 10% / Ambassador-Gen-Adm 5%** (d100, "rolled NN, need <==20") ‚Üí on trigger **‚àí1 enthusiasm to the FACTION(S) holding that group's card**, and the per-faction enthusiasm then **rolls up into ideology‚Üíparty MOVEMENT** ("TOTAL MOVEMENT: Mod ‚àí2 Blue, Cons ‚àí2 Blue, ‚Ä¶"). The 1892 run fired six (Private-Education 07‚Üícabinet, Environmentalist 06‚Üí‚àí1 Blue2, Military-Industrial 03, Science 20‚Üí‚àí1 Red1, Protectionist 12‚Üí‚àí1 Blue5+Red5, Transportation 09‚Üí‚àí1 Red3). This MATCHES the `terror2000`/`4747b09f` 20/10/5% lobby-want model already on this row (confirming it IS the shipped-forum rule across a 3rd era), and SHARPENS the build delta: shipped `Enthusiasm` is **per-IDEOLOGY‚Üíparty** (‚àí5..+5, `types.ts:1415-1418`), built once at scenario start (`scenario1856.ts:18` `buildEnthusiasm`) and **moved ONLY by era-event effects** (`enthusiasm:[{ideology,party,delta}]`, e.g. `eraEvents1856.ts:20`) ‚Äî there is **NO per-FACTION enthusiasm value, NO interest-group-card‚Üífaction binding for enthusiasm, and NO appointment-tier roll** anywhere in the engine (verified `phaseRunners.ts` 2.3.x; grep `enthusiasm`/`faction.*enthusiasm`). So the calculator's per-faction-enthusiasm LAYER (the thing #292 also wants) + the **appointment-driven enthusiasm SOURCE** are both absent ‚Äî the build has a coarser per-ideology, event-only shape. Implement the per-faction enthusiasm layer + the appointment-tier 20/10/5% roll alongside the lobby-want model (await the #275 simplifying rebuild flagged above). Cross-ref #292 (meter‚Üíenthusiasm + per-faction), #51 (enthusiasm engine), #275 (the 2.3 rebuild). `1892-era-of-progressivism` POST 20**) |

| 126 | **‚òÖ Pres implementation 2-step Admin-then-Command rule ‚Äî CANONICAL WORDING** ‚Äî NEW | Pres implementation rolls not modeled as the canonical 5-tier Command-gated model; cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3 captured this in concept ("HYBRID ADOPTED") but with fuzzy wording | **‚òÖ Ted POST 163 publishes the CANONICAL 2-step rule:** **Step 1 ‚Äî Pres rolls Admin for implementation, same as cabinet.** **Step 2 (Blunder check) ‚Äî Pres COMMAND scales the blunder roll:** Cmd 5 + relevant expertise = avoid blunder; Cmd 4 + expertise = 50% avoid; Cmd 3 + expertise = +1 to blunder; Cmd 2 + expertise = 50% chance +1; Cmd 1 + expertise = normal; no Cmd / no expertise = ‚àí2 to blunder (unless Efficient on impl team); **Incompetent = ‚àí3**; **Easily Overwhelmed skips step 2 entirely.** | **Implement the 2-step rule as the canonical Pres implementation flow.** Pairs with cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3 (this thread's POST 163 is the AUTHORITATIVE WORDING; it SUPERSEDES the cf82a7d3 placeholder). | ingested (**tedchange POST 159-164; supersedes cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b44) PRIMARY-CHANGELOG CONFIRMATION ‚Äî `4747b09f` POST 24 #1-8 is the same 2-step rule written into the rules doc itself (the `tedchange` numbers came from the discussion thread; here is the doc text). Ted explicitly REVERSES his earlier "Presidents use Command on implementation" stance. Identical 5-tier table (Cmd5 avoid / Cmd4 50%-avoid / Cmd3 +1 / Cmd2 50%-+1 / Cmd1 normal / no-Cmd-or-expertise ‚àí2 unless someone Efficient on the impl team / Incompetent ‚àí3 / Easily-Overwhelmed skips Step 2). Adds: a "‚àíN to blunder roll" is BAD, "+N"/"avoid" is GOOD; Efficient Pres ‚Üí only they roll (Pres picks if cabinet also Efficient); Delegator Pres = 50% the roll is replaced by a random applicable cabinet member (no longer needs Egghead); Crisis-Manager now applies to EVENTS with ‚â•10% chance of improving the crisis meter; all "Crisis Admin"‚Üí"Crisis Manager" merged. **Net canonical design: ADMIN gates whether implementation SUCCEEDS; COMMAND only gates the SEVERITY of a blunder when implementation FAILS.** `4747b09f` POST 24**; **‚òÖ (b45) NUANCE ‚Äî a botched MAJOR (Admin-gated) presidential action can force RESIGNATION, not merely a meter/point penalty: `9acfc425` May-2023 POST 19 ("Ted Insanity" 1789) ‚Äî President St. Clair + his 27-yo SecState Albert Gallatin BOTCHED the first major presidential action (an early Native-American removal) and BOTH RESIGNED, handing the presidency to VP Thomas Paine. So the blunder outcome at the severe end is office-loss (here a double resignation), tying the implementation-failure model to the succession chain (#61). This whole sequence is GM-adjudicated ‚Äî no shipped per-turn "major action that can be botched" mechanic (cross-ref #126's own unbuilt status + #23 Executive-Actions library). `9acfc425` POST 19-23**) |

Brackets: 1972-present = 1% (25-55) / 3% (55-75) / 5% (75+) / 20% (90+); 1900-1972 = 2% (25-55) / 10% (55-75) / 25% (75+); pre-1900 = 5% (25-50), 10% (51-60), 25% (61-74), 40% (75+). Frail = +5% & rolled first; Hale = not rolled until the 75 bracket; auto-retire at 100. Plus the immediate-succession cascade (Senators‚ÜêGovernors; Governors‚Üêcontrolling faction; Whips promote ‚Üí Maj-Ldr‚ÜíSpeaker; President fills cabinet w/ re-confirmation; SecState fills ambassadors; SecWar fills officers; VP/line‚ÜíPresident; SCOTUS seats stay vacant until the next nomination phase) + per-era 10-cause cause-of-death tables. This is the AUTHORITATIVE source for the death/retirement schedule #130/#143 codify. rulebook ¬ßN POST 17**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b44) PRIMARY-CHANGELOG FULL ERA CHARTS ‚Äî `4747b09f` POST 21-22 publish the era-keyed numeric tables (these are the actual rules-doc edits; `tedchange`/a0f0bf04 only described the trend). **RETIREMENT (POST 21), `+20% if NOT holding an elected/appointed office`, by age band 60-65 / 66-70 / 71-75 / 76-80 / 81+:** 1772-1820 = 20/25/30/40/80%; 1820-1868 = 15/20/25/35/75%; 1868-1916 = 10/15/20/30/70%; 1916-1972 = 5/10/15/25/65%; 1972-2100 = 0/5/10/20/60%. **DEATH (POST 22), by age band 25-40 / 41-60 / 61-80 / 81+:** 1772-1820 = 5/25/30/60%; 1820-1868 = 4/20/25/50%; 1868-1916 = 3/15/20/40%; 1916-1972 = 2/10/15/35%; 1972-2100 = 1/5/10/30%. (Note these era bands/brackets differ slightly from the `rulebook ¬ßN` 3-band table above ‚Äî both are designer sources; the `4747b09f` 5-era √ó age-band charts are the LATER changelog-doc version.) **Roll order:** check RETIREMENTS first; if the **5%-of-faction cap** isn't hit after rolling all eligible, THEN check deaths (shared cap); death rolls **Frail pols first with their % DOUBLED, then eldest‚Üíyoungest; MAX ONE death per faction.** Cabinet/Gen/Adm/Amb/cabinet-level **announce retirement at END of current 2-yr term** (not immediately ‚Äî avoids mid-term confirmation loops); Pres+VP CAN age-retire but stay in office and simply **don't run for re-election**. ‚öÝ **USER-CONFIRM #2 (reconciliation):** the changelog says **Hale = 0% death** (POST 22), later carved with ONE exception ‚Äî **retired ex-Presidents roll the death chart NO MATTER WHAT** (POST 26) incl. The older discussion digest `tedchange`/a0f0bf04 (and this row's Designed-intent column above) says **Hale = 1/2 (half) the chance of death** ‚Äî materially different (immortal-except-ex-Pres vs merely halved). **Changelog is AUTHORITATIVE: Hale = 0% death EXCEPT retired ex-Presidents roll normally.** This INVALIDATES the "Hale = 1/2 chance of death" wording in this row's title/Designed-intent. `4747b09f` POST 21, 22, 26, 28. **‚òÖ b57 ‚Äî shipped retirement is AGE-ONLY; vcczar's "already have most with retirement" is FORUM-not-build (`326c33dd`):** the Disgraced-trait thread's load-bearing ruling (POST 2: "Already have most of this with retirement, but extending it to the cabinet sounds like a good idea") treats disgrace-on-scandal as already served by the retirement/removal system ‚Äî but `runPhase_2_4_1_Deaths` (`phaseRunners.ts:2341-2380`) computes retirement SOLELY from `retireRateFor(p.age)*cfg.retireMult` (mortality brackets), with NO scandal/unpopularity/impeachment input; `markPoliticianRetired` (`:2333-2336`) just sets `retiredYear` + vacates office. ### Designer-backlog & meta-doc deltas (NEW ‚Äî batch 28: `to-do` + `fixes` + `completions` + `vic3`)

> **Batch 28 is a DISCUSSION/CATALOG batch (4 designer meta-docs, no era playtest).**
> Digest shorthands added here: **`to-do`** = the ab93f871 "AMPU To-Do List" digest
> (the designers' authoritative pre-build dev backlog, Dec-2021‚ÜíJul-2022, tier-1
> vcczar+Ted ‚Äî **design-of-record that PREDATES the build**, so its "decisions"
> are *intent to reconcile against shipped reality*, not shipped fact); **`fixes`**
> = the a58b90f4 "Suggested Fixes from Playtests" digest (vcczar's structured
> fix-queue, ~June‚ÜíSept 2022, designer-authoritative ACCEPT/REJECT/DEFER
> dispositions ‚Äî the **ORIGIN** of many later-ingested rulings); **`completions`**
> = the 64f60bbd "To-Do List: Completions List" digest (the designers' *done-half*
> of the backlog, Aug-Sept 2022 ‚Äî what the **2022 reference/spreadsheet game**
> considers DONE; a 2022 "completion" is NOT grounds to close a gap because our
> "Built" = present in **our `src/`**, a different universe; tags **CONFIRMED-SHIPPED**
> / **DESIGNER-DONE-UNVERIFIED** / **NOT-IN-OUR-BUILD**); **`vic3`** = the 76d41a08
> "What AMPU Can Borrow from Victoria 3" digest (aspirational, mostly designer-tagged
> "AMPU 2" parking-lot). These four threads also deliver **provenance upgrades** and
> **Completions-List reconciliations** annotated in-place on the existing rows above
> (see each row's Source cell, tagged `to-do`/`fixes`/`completions`).

| 189 | **‚òÖ Down-ballot election trait stack (Gov/Sen/Rep) ‚Äî distinct from the presidential modifier stack (#103)** ‚Äî NEW | `calcStateVote`+`applyTraitElectionBonus` already apply *some* traits+meters to gov/sen/house (`fixes` verifies `phaseRunners.ts:3685,3707`), but only the ~5 presidential traits; the full down-ballot table is unbuilt | Designers intend a **full trait table for non-presidential races**: Likeable/Unlikable ¬±1, Charismatic/Uncharismatic ¬±1, Leadership +1, Orator/Debater 50%+1, Propagandist ¬±1, Puritan ideology-match ¬±1, Not-Obscure 50%+1, Lowbrow/Egghead, plus **FL +1 / PL +3 in primaries** ‚Äî "in play, never written into the rules." #103 covers presidential-only; this extends it to Gov/Sen/Rep | Add a down-ballot trait-modifier table to `calcStateVote` separate from the presidential stack (#103). Tier-1 (Cal+Ted+vcczar). Pairs with #103/#190 | ingested (**to-do POSTs 977, 979, 982, 991**; **fixes ¬ßA POST 6 ‚Äî partially shipped: build already applies traits+meters to gov/sen/house, but lobby/expertise-industry still gaps; 2nd source**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b58) AUTHORITATIVE DESIGNER CHANGELOG ‚Äî the down-ballot trait model was SIMPLIFIED + already coded into elections (`97aa3f6f` "State of the Rules" POST 5/6, ebrk85, "changes made in development/coding of the elections"), scope explicitly **Gov/Sen/Rep primaries + general elections, NOT pre-primaries.** NEW canonical rule: **50% chance +1** per positive trait ‚Äî `celebrity`, `Military Leader` (now INDEPENDENT of celebrity, was "+1 only if BOTH celebrity AND Military Leader"), `integrity` (**‚àí1 if the candidate ALSO has `controversial`**), `charisma` (‚àí1 if also `uncharismatic`), `likable` (‚àí1 if also `unlikable`). ‚òÖ TWO structural shifts: (1) **opponent-conditional rows are GONE** ‚Äî integrity/charisma/likable now fire on the candidate's OWN trait pair (self-integrity ‚Üí +1; self-controversial ‚Üí ‚àí1); "It no longer matters if your opponent has the opposite trait" (POST 5); (2) the old "highest-Gov-ability +1 unless tied" rule is REPLACED by a per-candidate **20% √ó (Gov OR Legis ability) chance of +1, Gov/Sen ONLY (not House)** ‚Äî captured on #292 as a new scoring INPUT. ‚òÖ SHIPPED `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16-39`) is structurally DIFFERENT: it adds DETERMINISTIC fixed point-magnitudes (`TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS={SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` `types.ts:721-725`) whenever a trait is held ‚Äî no 50% roll ‚Äî and it KEEPS the opponent-conditional bump (`bumpedMagnitude` when an opponent has a listed trait, `electionEffects.ts:29-32`; e.g. Integrity SMALL‚ÜíMEDIUM vs a Scandalous/Controversial/Corrupt opponent `types.ts:748-758`) ‚Äî the exact thing the ruling removes. FIX = convert the fixed magnitudes ‚Üí **50% chance ¬±1** (seeded `chance`, not the existing deterministic add), DROP the opponent-conditional bumps, scope to primaries+generals (the `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` table is already context-keyed `presGeneral/presPrimary/house/senatePre17/governor/internalParty` `types.ts:740+`). Trait-name mapping: charisma‚Üí`Charismatic`, likable‚Üí`Likable`, integrity‚Üí`Integrity`, controversial‚Üí`Controversial`, celebrity‚Üí`Celebrity` (all already in the table); Military-Leader‚Üía command/leadership trait (confirm spelling). This SHARPENS the down-ballot trait stack with canonical numbers and supersedes the deterministic-band model for these contexts. `97aa3f6f` POST 5, 6**; **‚òÖ (b59) NEW down-ballot trait rule to add ‚Äî REWORKED-CONTROVERSIAL ideology-SPLIT election math (`b5e78cbd` POST 1, ties #4 + the #292 scoring inputs): a `Controversial` that FLIPS SIGN by context keyed to the ELECTORATE's LW/RW lean vs. the pol's lean ‚Äî **+1 in a same-ideology-zone primary** (electorate Left-leaning AND pol is LW-Controversial, and vice-versa for RW), **‚àí2 in the general** (lacks broad cross-ideology support), **‚àí2 in a hostile primary** (electorate ideology opposes the pol's lean). AOC worked example: "+1 in primaries in same-ideology zones, but ‚àí2 in the general." SHIPPED reality: `Controversial` bands (`types.ts:830-843`) are UNIFORMLY NEGATIVE (presGeneral ‚àíMEDIUM, presPrimary/house/senatePre17/governor/internalParty ‚àíSMALL) with NO electorate-ideology-conditional row and NO +1 same-ideology primary; `TraitElectionRule` `{trait,context,magnitude,era?,opponentConditional?}` (`types.ts:727-736`) has NO electorate-ideology field. This is the sign-flipping case of the b58 election-trait-scoring rework and needs the #4 era/ideology matrix + a per-pol LW/RW-lean signal the build lacks; the `Obscure` gate the rework references is a real field (`types.ts:104`). 0% built. **‚òÖ RECORD-FIX corrections (preventive ‚Äî verified none of these false claims currently live in the log): (1) `Controversial` AND `Scandalous` BOTH already SHIP as distinct traits (`types.ts:103`/`:101`) with distinct election-band rows (`:816-843`) ‚Äî the thread's "split/rename Controversial‚ÜíScandalous" ask is MOOT (already done); only the ideology-split MATH remains unbuilt. (2) "Controversial = opposite of Integrity" is FALSE in the build ‚Äî in `TRAIT_CONFLICTS` (`types.ts:658-692`) Integrity's ONLY conflict is `Corrupt` (`:663-664`); `Controversial` is in NO conflict pair and freely coexists with `Integrity`. The real relationship is an electoral opponent-conditional (`types.ts:749/753/757`, `:818-840`), NOT a mutual-exclusion; "controversial-and-honest" (Ron Paul/Bernie/Kucinich) is already legal. Useful for any trait-glossary/UI so the design conversation and code agree.** `b5e78cbd` POST 1, 3**; **‚òÖ (`pol-additions-2024`, source `4b5d4ca7`) ‚Äî the ‚òÖ 182-column intended DATA MODEL exposes the election-scoring as an authored PER-POL POINT LADDER: an `Election Bonus/Penalty` column + a **10-tier ladder `20 / 10 / 7 / 5 / 3 / ‚àí3 / ‚àí5 / ‚àí7 / ‚àí10 / ‚àí20 pts`** (`vstyle_clean.csv` cols 168-178) ‚Äî i.e. the down-ballot trait/attribute modifiers this row tracks are meant to resolve to discrete per-pol point magnitudes on this fixed ladder, not ad-hoc ¬±1s. This is the DATA form behind the scoring model (shipped `TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS={SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` `types.ts:721-725` is a 3-tier coarser version). Reconcile the authored 10-tier ladder against the shipped 3-band model when wiring per-pol election bonuses. See digest `playtest-digests/pol-additions-2024.md`**) |

| 191 | **‚òÖ Seat-level "deviant bias" model for House districts (per-seat party bias overrides ONLY the party component of state bias)** ‚Äî NEW | No per-seat/district bias model; #20/#21 are state-level; the `dem1820` focus-Rep abstraction (#55) has no per-seat partisan lean | A specific House seat can carry a **deviant party bias** that **overrides only the party component** of the state bias (ideology/religion biases still apply) to model gerrymander / safe-seat dynamics; each seat carries a vote weight. vcczar ruled the seat bias stays **static** ("Fuck no" to floating within a range) | Add a per-seat `deviantPartyBias` that supersedes the party term of state bias for that district, with static (non-floating) values + per-seat vote weights. Tier-1 (vcczar ruling). Relates #20/#21/#55 + the `completions` gerrymander rework (see #20 annotation) | ingested (**to-do POSTs 994-1003, 1008-1012**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-33 ‚Äî #191 is now FULLY AUTHORED (`housepoll` aed35c6e POST 17-19 supplies the concrete per-seat deviant-bias ENGINE this row only gestured at): (1) **minority-party-seat guarantee** ‚Äî any state reaching **5+ Focus Reps** must have a minority-party seat if none exists, started at **Bias +2** for the minority party (the "Kevin McCarthy / ‚â•20% Red Reps in CA" rule). (2) **new-seat bias roll** = **25% +1 toward the state majority party** (Gov+Sen+Rep, tie‚Üírandom) / **25% tossup** / **50% to the party holding the most era-keyed growth lobbies**, where the growth-lobby set is **era-gated** ‚Äî *pre-Progressivism:* Big Agriculture, Big Oil & Gas, Transportation, Isolationist; *Progressivism onward:* Public Housing, Labor Unions, Technology, Welfare. (3) **seat GAIN** ‚Äî existing districts each re-roll independently: 50% no change / 25% tossup / 25% ‚Üí most-growth-lobby party. (4) **seat LOSS** ‚Äî existing districts re-roll: 50% no change / 25% tossup / 25% ‚Üí the party with the FEWEST growth lobbies (shrinking states trend nostalgic/conservative; growing states trend liberal). This is the explicit per-seat population-realignment engine; pairs with #20 (census)/#34/#55/#219. The bias still stays STATIC (this row's "Fuck no to floating" ruling holds). housepoll POST 7, 17, 18, 19**; **‚òÖ batch 35 ‚Äî DESIGN-ORIGIN PREDATING `housepoll`: `electiondisc` (5a5d988f, Apr 2022) is where the "Kevin McCarthy problem" was first raised ‚Äî a strong minority-party pol in a heavily-leaning state can never win (a great red draftee stuck in deep-blue CA; East-TN/"State of Franklin" voided by the state's +2 D lean, POST 15/21/24). The leading proposal there = `+2/+2/+1` per-seat lean (OrangeP47 POST 18, "really like this" Ted POST 26); alts = a progressive `‚àí2/‚àí3` swing ladder (POST 24) and a random ‚àí1 if a party sweeps all 3 (POST 29). Consensus direction: per-seat lean stratification so big-state minorities get a SWING seat ‚Äî explicitly NOT a guaranteed minority seat (removes the election aspect, POST 25/27/28) and NOT more seats (breaks the ~100-seat balance, Ted POST 26). Also floated: an optional SETUP TOGGLE to disable historical lean at game start so states develop emergently (Willthescout7 POST 24) ‚Äî ties #247 (the "remove historical bias" option) + a new setup-time switch. `housepoll` later AUTHORED the concrete engine; this thread is the unratified design seed. electiondisc POST 15, 16, 18, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30**; **‚òÖ (b39) `8f12ac05` (US House Votes, May 2022) corroborates #191 TWICE: (1) per-seat party-preference VARIABILITY (add variability to large states' individual seat lean to simulate the minority party's share) ‚Äî ShortKing POST 3, vcczar "Yep, we've already fixed that issue" POST 8 (the per-seat deviant-bias work was already in progress May 2022); (2) ‚òÖ **the "McCarthy Rule"** (named for Kevin McCarthy, POST 21) = guarantee the minority party 1 of a big state's named seats with a swing "slightly biased to one party based on historical splits" ‚Äî the minority-party-seat guarantee housepoll later authored (the "‚â•20% Red Reps in CA" / +2 minority bias). POST 21 also notes a **by-year state-demographics MAP already exists** ("research‚Ä¶ pretty much done, just have to pull out the results"). NB this thread's GUARANTEE-a-minority-seat sits in tension with electiondisc's "don't guarantee, just make it swingy" ‚Äî an unresolved #191 sub-fork. 8f12ac05 POST 3, 8, 21**; **‚òÖ (b46) `b7acabd2` (House-incumbency discussion, topic 4467) is a 4th source reconfirming per-district lean from the designer (MrPotatoTed POST 1): "Districts" carry their OWN party preference distinct from the state's, done DELIBERATELY to give a minority-party pol a real shot ‚Äî the canonical **"Kevin McCarthy" case** (a Republican in an extremely BLUE state but a RED district); the largest states get "a seat or two with a strong minority-party lean." Districts are an ABSTRACTION (PA has ~30 total Reps, only ~7 named; "PA-1" = one of the 7 most important PA Reps, NOT a literal district) and are distinguished from each other ONLY by these per-seat leans. Literal per-district simulation is explicitly deferred to "AMPU 3" (needs 10,000+ obscure US Reps + failed nominees in the dataset, POST 2-3). The shipped model has only a single state-level `bias` (codebase-verified, `types.ts:1320-1329`), no per-district lean. `b7acabd2` POST 1, 2, 3**; **‚òÖ (b50) `58da1636` FAQ supplies the canonical INTERACTION RULE this per-seat model resolves to (POST 321-322, vcczar-authoritative): **a seat's own bias OVERRIDES the STATE bias in Rep elections ‚Äî use ONLY the seat bias** (a blank seat falls back to state bias; a **"Toss-Up" seat = NO bias at all**). So the designed per-seat deviant-bias is confirmed real AND it SUPERSEDES (not adds to) state bias for that district ‚Äî but the shipped House abstraction has state-level `bias` only (no per-seat field, code-verified, a 5th source after `housepoll`/`electiondisc`/`8f12ac05`/`b7acabd2`). Pairs with #287 (the incumbency +2 that can BEAT a ¬±1 seat bias). `58da1636` POST 321-322**) |
| 250 | **‚òÖ House-seat-COMPETITION model fork ‚Äî how multiple focus-Rep seats in one state compete (per-seat vs free-for-all top-N vs single multi-member pool) + the CPU-keeps-incumbents-in-seat rule + seat-lock-released-on-reapportionment ‚Äî NEW (`electiondisc`; Decision-gated likely; sharpens #219/#55/#191)** | Shipped focus-Reps are flat per-state `representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) with NO seat-competition grouping, NO per-seat race structure, and NO reapportionment-driven seat-lock logic ‚Äî distinct from #219 (how seats are SIZED) and #191 (per-seat partisan LEAN); this row is the COMPETITION STRUCTURE | **The design-origin discussion (Apr 2022, MrPotatoTed-led) of how a state's multiple focus-Rep seats compete; raised + informally dispositioned but NOT ratified by vcczar ‚Äî an OPEN design fork.** Under focus-Reps a state with multiple seats runs them as separate independent races (GA-1 vs GA-2); seats are NOT geographic districts ("Bill Clinton AR-1, Hillary AR-2 despite the same house"; relocation modeled via the pol's alternate-states, POST 5/8/15/16). THREE options on the table: **(A) per-seat (status quo)** ‚Äî separate race per seat, candidate picks GA-1 *or* GA-2, per-seat primaries (**informally PREFERRED/kept** ‚Äî "GA-1 stays in GA-1's lane," Ted POST 5; not ratified); **(B) free-for-all** ‚Äî one pool, no per-seat primaries, top-2 overall take GA's 2 seats (raised, not adopted); **(C) single multi-member pool** ‚Äî every GA candidate competes in ALL GA races at once (OrangeP47 POST 13 says this is what Ted's explanation logically implied but "I don't know how to make that work"). The motivating quirk (POST 1/2): under A, both incumbents can run against each other for GA-1, leaving GA-2 to outsiders ‚Üí **CPU RULE (NEW concrete requirement): CPU keeps incumbents in their current seat by default; a HUMAN may override** ("that's the human's fault," Ted POST 2). Rezi's alt (POST 3): a hard seat-LOCK releasing **only when state rep count changes** (reapportionment) so the pol gets a chance to move ‚Äî sharpens #55's seat-locked incumbency with the unlock trigger; reapportionment edge (POST 5): if GA shrinks to 1 Rep, the displaced GA-2 incumbent becomes eligible to contest GA-1 (so seat count must be dynamic per era and the eligibility shuffle keys off it). Pairs with #219 (focus-rep ladder / how many seats), #55 (seat-locked incumbency), #191 (per-seat deviant lean ‚Äî the "Kevin McCarthy problem" was ALSO raised in THIS thread, POST 15-30, making it the DESIGN-ORIGIN of #191, predating `housepoll`), #47 (per-seat primaries) | Decide the canonical model (A/B/C; leans A, unratified by vcczar). If A: implement per-seat race grouping + per-seat primaries; **CPU rule = incumbents run in their current seat, humans may override**; seat-lock released on reapportionment (dynamic per-era seat count); the reapportionment eligibility shuffle (state‚Üífewer-seats frees the displaced incumbent). Likely a setup/Decision-gated choice. Pairs with #219/#55/#191/#47 | ingested (**`electiondisc` (5a5d988f) POST 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 13, 14, 15, 16; codebase-verified flat `state.representativeIds` (types.ts:1327), `seatClass?:1|2|3` (types.ts:1247), per-state `bias` only ‚Äî no seat-competition or reapportionment-eligibility logic**; **‚òÖ (b39) `8f12ac05` (US House Votes) corroborates the seat-as-prize logic the weighted-vote rule sharpens: a big-state seat (CA-1 worth ~18 votes) becomes a HIGH-VALUE primary target ‚Üí crowded primaries + higher loss/penalty risk + a save-him-for-another-office decision (POST 18, 20); Speaker math reshapes (most named reps ‚âÝ most votes ‚Üí cross-player alliances "real sketchy real quick," Willthescout7 POST 18, 20). 8f12ac05 POST 18, 20**; **‚òÖ (b46) `b7acabd2` (House-incumbency discussion, topic 4467) is the RATIFIED-RULING 2nd source that resolves this row's seat-lock + reapportionment-unlock fork (consensus across POST 6, 8, 11-13, 16; designer + heavy playtesters). **Rule A (district-lock):** once elected, a Rep is "assigned" to the district NUMBER they won and **must re-run in that same seat** ‚Äî no hopping to a friendlier seat (that is "gaming the system," POST 16); a pol may move to a different district ONLY after losing his current seat; outside the lose-a-seat case you should NEVER see two incumbents run against each other. **Rule B (lose-a-seat redistricting exception):** when a state loses a district (census/EV drop), for THAT ONE ELECTION ONLY ‚Äî primary rules temporarily ignored so incumbents can challenge each other (AI agrees), the **ideology rule WAIVED** ("a Moderate can run against a Moderate, but ONLY in this scenario," POST 12), the **highest district number is removed** ("largest n," mirroring IRL, POST 17/18), and the **AI tiebreak when all affected are the same faction = seniority ‚Üí random** (POST 6). ‚òÖ Rule-B status caveat: it "isn't in 2.95," "went in for the CPU logic" only, and still needs writing into the official rules (POST 13-15) ‚Äî a resolved design decision that may be CPU-only / unwritten. Precondition: census/EV reapportionment, which the build lacks (#34). Shipped model = flat `representativeIds`, every seat re-contested from scratch each cycle, no district identity, no reapportionment trigger (codebase-verified `types.ts:1327`). `b7acabd2` POST 6, 8, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18**) |
