| 246 | **‚òÖ N1 ‚Äî STRONG never-nominated marquee figures are full-strength `CURATED_ROWS`-class, NOT sub-floor: do they need an electoral cap distinct from `ROWS`(full) / `ERA_FIGURES`(sub-floor)? ‚Äî NEW (`strongnevernom`; for tech-lead)** | The sub-floor balance rule (CLAUDE.md + `seedDataset.mjs:239-240`) ‚Äî failed / never-nominated figures get electoral stats floored (legislative ‚â§ 1, modest command) so they rarely win ‚Äî is **wired ONLY to `ERA_ROWS`/`ERA_FIGURES`** (never-served era notables), verified at `seedDataset.mjs:242` (`ERA_ROWS` carries the sub-floor comment). So the sub-floor rule does **not** apply to them ‚Äî they are CURATED_ROWS-class (marquee, full-strength) overrides, the SAME tier as `histpres` (#240). vcczar's stated DESIGN INTENT is to model "never won" through **electability / PV / regional-likability-ideology factors**, NOT stat suppression (he flags Rockefeller / Seward / Calhoun / Jesse Jackson as "may have a hard time getting elected" via region/ideology, POST anchors in the digest). **Open Q for tech-lead:** when ingested into `ROWS` at full strength, is there ANY guard preventing a never-nominee like Ted Kennedy from WINNING the presidency in-sim ‚Äî or is "strong but historically never nominated" purely flavor with no electoral cap (won-never modeled by PV/electability, per intent)? If a cap IS wanted, it would be a THIRD tier distinct from both `ROWS` (full) and `ERA_FIGURES` (sub-floor). Flag any wiring assumption to the contrary. Pairs with #240 (the roster) + #120 (dataset balance) + the sub-floor rule (CLAUDE.md / `seedDataset.mjs:239-242`) + the PV/electability cluster (#18/#214) | **HUMAN/tech-lead CALL:** decide whether strong-never-nominees ingest as plain full-strength `ROWS` (won-never = emergent from PV/electability, vcczar's intent) or need a distinct electoral cap (a third tier between full `ROWS` and sub-floor `ERA_FIGURES`). | 241 | **‚òÖ VARIABLE presidential TERM LENGTH (6-yr / 2-yr presidency) vs the hardcoded `year % 4` / `year % 2` election cadence ‚Äî the cadence must become DATA (a term-length field driving the phase scheduler), not arithmetic ‚Äî NEW (`ampuelections`; a 2nd, independent reason to replace the year-predicates beyond #92's era-advance)** | **The presidential/election cadence is hardcoded 4-/2-yr arithmetic, NOT a variable:** `isPresidentialYear`/`isDraftYear` = `year % 4 === 0`, `isElectionYear` = `year % 2 === 0` (`phases.ts:49-58`); `shouldRunPhase` keys off these + a 2-yr-per-turn rollover. **There is NO presidential-term-length field anywhere in `src/`** (`grep termLength|presidentialTerm|termYears` = ZERO); `ConstitutionalArticles.termLimits` (`types.ts:1396`) is a binary `'two_terms'\|'no_limits'` = a term-COUNT, not term LENGTH; the 4-year length is baked into a LABEL STRING ("Elected President (4-year term)", `constitutionalConvention.ts:19`) | **A real shipped-cadence-coupling concern vcczar flagged in his OWN voice (`ampuelections` 92f0659b POST 1, re-quoted POST 6).** He added a **"6-year term presidency Amendment (may delete)"** and a **"2-year term presidency Amendment (may delete)"** at @themiddlepolitical's request, then warned: *"Anthony might recommend against it as it might upset how the election cycles are programmed. Same with the two-year terms."* A 6-yr or variable term would BREAK every `year % 4`/`% 2` predicate (and the 2-yr rollover). So the term length **cannot be a mere content card** ‚Äî it requires a **variable election-cadence model** (term length as game-state data driving the phase scheduler) in place of the year arithmetic. This **piggybacks on #92** (which already wants the year predicates replaced for era-advance) ‚Äî term-length is the SECOND, independent reason the hardcoded cadence must go; it also touches the election cluster #18/#184/#185 (which assume a fixed 4-yr presidential cycle). The "(may delete)" tag + the programmer caveat = an UNRESOLVED designer/programmer tension, not a settled rule. `GM‚áíApp` | Replace the `year % 4`/`year % 2` predicates with a **variable election-cadence model**: a per-office term-length field (default 4-yr pres / 2-yr House / 6-yr Senate-class) read by the phase scheduler, so a passed 6-yr/2-yr-presidency Amendment reschedules the presidential cycle deterministically. Generalize alongside #92's era-advance predicate-replacement. **HUMAN-CALL:** is the 6-/2-yr term a feature to BUILD (variable cadence) or to DROP (vcczar tagged both "may delete")? Pairs with #92/#18/#184/#185/#242 | ingested (**ampuelections (92f0659b) ¬ß2 / POST 1, 6; codebase-verified phases.ts:49-58 (`year%4`/`year%2`), no term-length field in `src/`, `termLimits` is term-count (types.ts:1396), 4-yr in a CC label (constitutionalConvention.ts:19)**; **‚òÖ (b38) the batch-38 prereq finding (NEW #258 ‚Äî content availability keyed to game-STATE, not the calendar) is broad corroboration that the cadence/scheduling layer should be data/state-driven rather than year-arithmetic; #258 is the content-primitive face, this row is the election-cadence face, both replacing `year % N`. civilrights POST 1; agriculture POST 1**) |

Design principle (POST 1): biggest PV bonuses go to office-AGNOSTIC traits (Master-Kingmaker/Leadership/Iron-Fist/Charisma), narrow office-conditional traits sit in the +5 tier. It also enumerates the in-game ¬±1 trait/identity ELECTORAL knobs the reweight must price: home-state +2, home-region +1, Experience +1, debate-win +1, preferred/least-preferred ideology ¬±1, Puritan ‚àí1 (fires for both), "enthusiastic base" up to +2, domestic/economic crisis ‚àí2 (POST 31) ‚Äî home-state/region & debates are the high-value knobs, far above raw skills. | 215 | **Non-linear per-ability PV curve (first-point-worth-more; Command ~25/level with decreasing returns; Admin/Military low-floor thresholds) replaces the flat `skill √ó officeWeight √ó 4` ‚Äî NEW (`revampPV`; `GM‚áíApp`)** | `pv.ts` weights skills **flat**: `skill √ó officeWeight` then `√ó4` (`pv.ts:70-73`) ‚Äî every point worth the same ‚Äî and **`command √ó 10` flat per level** (`pv.ts:74`) | **The skill/command half of the PV revamp.** The thread's designers (Cal P32/P40, jnewt P35/P36) ruled the per-point value is **non-linear**: the **1st point of an ability is worth more** than later points (Command **25** then +5 each after; Legislative 10 then +3). **Two exceptions** invert the curve: **Admin** and **Military** ‚Äî their 1st point is near-worthless (a 1-Admin/1-Military pol is "absolute trash"), so for Admin the **+10 should start at the 2nd point** (the cabinet-eligibility threshold). **Command is the single highest-value attribute** (universally #1, +25/level vs the shipped flat √ó10) ‚Äî which ties directly into the perennial, **UNRESOLVED** "restrict who's BORN with Command" fight (Ted P12: only real-life nominees should boot with 1 Command, to stop nobodies windfalling +25; OrangeP47 P45-50 "vehemently oppose" ‚Äî restricting Command **breaks modern-start scenarios** [too few Command pols to field a presidential field] **and the 3rd-party-run rules** [which key off Command]). This is a designer-authoritative office-prestige table the shipped `OFFICE_PRESTIGE` (`pv.ts:7-31`) badly under-shoots: Senator 5 (spec 20), Rep 2 (spec 10), Gov 6 (spec 15), VP 12 (spec 15), Speaker 12 (spec 25), Cabinet 8-12 (spec 5). Treat `revampPV` POST 33 as the LATER word, but this confirms the office tier was already being raised dramatically in May 2022 (legislators/reps grossly under-weighted in the shipped pv.ts vs design). Also corroborates the command-as-eligibility GATE as a balance datum: the shipped primary gate requires command‚â•2, and a 0-skill 2-command trait-stack pol (James Dean) clears it and rides traits to the top ‚Äî the command floor is too easy to satisfy for an otherwise-empty pol (primary2020 POST 22). primary2020 POST 22, 112**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-33 ‚Äî #215 is now UNBLOCKED on direction (the `groupthinkpv` gate, c6f6dcc7). (a) Ability curve RULED: **Command √ó 15/level ¬∑ Legislative/Governing/Admin √ó 10/level ¬∑ Military/Judicial √ó 5/level** (POST 28, 49); topic expertise = +5 each. (Shipped `pv.ts:74` is `command √ó 10` flat and `pv.ts:70-73` is uniform `skill √ó officeWeight √ó 4` ‚Äî the design re-weights Command up to √ó15 and drops per-office skill weighting for fixed per-ability multipliers.) (b) ‚òÖ OFFICE-PV ruling = **YES, but scoped** ‚Äî holding an office RAISES PV (losing it lowers it), ordering Pres > VP > leaders (Speaker/Maj/Min/Whips) > Senator/upper-cabinet > Gov/cabinet > Rep > military; **the PURPOSE is the post-1772 ACTIVE DRAFT + persuasion/steal AI**, so the CPU drafts/steals/promotes sitting office-holders over identical-stat "unemployed randos" (vcczar conceded "maybe just for active draft," POST 41-42). This is NOT a vote input (see the #214 PV-not-in-elections reframe). Office-PV is ALREADY shipped via `OFFICE_PRESTIGE` (`pv.ts:7-31`) but at far smaller magnitudes than proposed (MPT POST 28 = Pres +1000 ‚Ä¶ Rep +50; vcczar "I'd have HALF the points, those are too high," POST 31) ‚Äî the relative ordering matches code; the magnitudes do not. (c) NEW sub-requirement ‚Äî **partial office-legacy decay** ("lose SOME PV on leaving office, not all," POST 3/6/8/15): Excel-infeasible then (needed a "previous offices" tracker), TRIVIAL in code (office history persists) ‚Üí shipped PV recomputes from CURRENT office only = full loss/no legacy carry today (corroborated by `calsleaderboard`'s "office loss = PV collapse, no legacy" finding). (d) TUNE ‚Äî age band designed 45/75 (POST 30) vs shipped 30/70 (`pv.ts:85-86`); minor. ‚òÖ HUMAN-CALL (carried to Open questions): the office-bonus MAGNITUDES (+1000‚Ä¶+50, or "half") are flatly INCOMPATIBLE with the 0‚Äì100 #220 display scale ‚Äî someone must reconcile office magnitudes ‚Üî display scale; also Easily-Overwhelmed's tier value (flagged omitted, POST 29/40). groupthinkpv POST 3, 6, 8, 15, 28-31, 41-42, 49**; **‚òÖ BATCH-36 corroboration ‚Äî vcczar states the Command re-weight as DONE: *"I made Command worth more points"* (`failednoms` 55c18fde POST 14), the playtest-side confirmation of this row's Command ‚âà25/level (vs the shipped flat `command √ó 10`, `pv.ts:74`) and the `groupthinkpv` Command √ó15 ruling. failednoms POST 14**) |

Designer pushback (Ted/Cal): several overlap efficient/egghead/easily-overwhelmed ‚Äî vcczar kept them, "slightly different" ‚Üí **flag overlap risk**. This is the implementation/lingering depth #179 points at, plus #182 (action budget) + #20 (gov actions). Pairs with #179/#182/#20/#214 (these traits also need PV values) | Build a president/cabinet implementation sub-engine: a post-legislation success‚ÜíBlunder pass, modified by the expertise-gated genre traits + Delegator/Micromanager + Crisis-{Manager,Admin,Gov} (incl. the Crisis-Admin 10/20% Lingering crisis-pull on top of #179's cabinet rolls) + Passive/Predictable + Passive+Pliable PUPPET + Magician. De-dup against efficient/egghead/easily-overwhelmed first (overlap risk). The community VOTED to **KEEP the focus-rep abstraction; modeling every real ~435 seat was REJECTED** (perf/tedium/authoring cost; >10k historical Reps would need a pol-generator) ‚Äî explicitly parked as **"AMPU 2"** (POST 12-14; matthewyoung123 "VOTE 106 over 435!!", POST 7/15). DESIGNER DISPOSITION: vcczar did NOT do every-seat; he ADOPTED + immediately BUILT an EXPANDED apportionment-driven ladder (the "3.0.16 Determining US Reps" rule, POST 19-20) across every start date (POSTs 27-41), adding only ~3 net reps. The CANONICAL rule: **(A) US Reps a state has = historical EVs ‚àí 2** for that year, recomputed at a **census election = first election after a decade** (decade year skips, N+2 midterm applies); ahistorical drift carries forward; early-join = +3EV/+1Rep, late-join = erase EVs. **(B) US Reps ‚Üí Focus Reps via an EV-derived 1-10 ladder** (‚â§5‚Üí1 ¬∑ 6-10‚Üí2 ¬∑ 11-15‚Üí3 ¬∑ 16-20‚Üí4 ¬∑ 21-25‚Üí5 ¬∑ 26-30‚Üí6 ¬∑ 31-35‚Üí7 ¬∑ 36-40‚Üí8 ¬∑ 41-45‚Üí9 ¬∑ ‚â•46‚Üí10 ‚Äî SUPERSEDES the flat 3/2/1; "Small state" = 1 focus rep, "Large" = 4+). **(C) ‚òÖ vote power = `floor(stateUSReps / focusReps)`, odd REMAINDER ‚Üí the highest-Legislative rep gets the extra vote(s)** (randomized if tied) ‚Äî EXACTLY the #219 rule, confirming this thread is its origin spec (POST 19/20/23). No new "every-seat" gap; every-435 stays AMPU-2 parking. groupthinkhouse=housepoll POST 1, 7, 12-15, 19-23, 27-41**; **‚òÖ batch 35 ‚Äî `electiondisc` (5a5d988f, Apr 2022, PREDATES housepoll) corroborates the focus-Rep model KEPT (1-3 Reps/state scaled by population, NOT 435 seats; seats are NOT geographic districts ‚Äî geography abstracted via the pol's alternate-states, POST 1/5/8/15/16) and adds an explicit House-SIZE balance target: ~1-3 Reps/state averaging ~2 ‚Üí a ~100-seat House at 50 states (Ted POST 26) ‚Äî the balance constant that BLOCKS the "give big states 5 seats" fix and forces #191's per-seat-lean approach. See NEW #250 for the seat-COMPETITION fork raised in the same thread. electiondisc POST 1, 5, 8, 16, 26**; **‚òÖ (b39) `8f12ac05` (AMPU: US House Votes, May 2022) is the explicit **DESIGN-ORIGIN** of this weighted-vote rule (predates both the summer2021 statement and the housepoll resolution). vcczar POST 1: named reps STAY (3/2/1 focus-Rep abstraction unchanged), but each rep's VOTING POWER = the state's real delegation size split among its named reps (CA 53 / 3 ‚Üí 18/18/17). This is a **REVERT of an old rule** vcczar had removed only because "it was a pain in the ass to playtest on a forum" (a forum-tedium removal, NOT a design rejection ‚Äî the browser build removes that objection); Ted's load-bearing AGREE (POST 7): weighting makes the House mechanically DISTINCT from the Senate ("or else the House is just the same as the Senate"). ‚òÖ‚òÖ **REMAINDER-DIRECTION DISCREPANCY TO RECONCILE (pick ONE canonical formulation):** THIS thread drops the off-vote from the **LOWEST-Legislative** rep (round up then subtract ‚Üí 18/18/17); #219/`housepoll` (POST 19/20) add the remainder to the **HIGHEST-Legislative** rep (floor then add). Same ranking-by-legislative, but the off-vote can land on a different rep. Downstream effects confirmed: vote-swaying scales with the swayed rep's WEIGHTED votes (a CA rep worth 18 ‚â´ a VT rep worth 1, POST 4); Speaker math = most-named-reps ‚âÝ most-votes (POST 18, 20); big-state seats become high-value primary targets (POST 18, 20). 8f12ac05 POST 1, 4, 7, 18, 20**; **‚òÖ (b40) `expansionism` (4e76e6c3 POST 1, 4) surfaces a NEW apportionment-cascade trigger: **state-FISSION** (split TX/CA into two seat-bearing states) requires recomputing reps/EV/senators across the resulting states (POST 4's CA-split ‚Üí southern-Nevada‚ÜíArizona question is the worked example) ‚Äî broken out as NEW #269 (structural state-map mutations); it cascades into this row's focus-rep + vote-weight machinery. expansionism POST 1, 4**) |
| 220 | **‚òÖ PV DISPLAY-scale is UNRESOLVED design ‚Äî raw ("pink") vs 0-100/0-200 vs 100-normalized; the build must pick what (if anything) humans see ‚Äî NEW (`revampPV` + `summer2021`)** | `pv.ts` exposes the **raw** `computePV` number (`pvCache`); there is no display-scaling/normalization layer, and the raw value is `Math.max(0,‚Ä¶)`-clamped (negatives lost) | **The presentation/normalization side of the PV revamp, left UNDECIDED.** `revampPV` debated 0-100 / 0-200 / %-of-Napoleon / %-of-best / raw "pink" presentations at length (¬ß6 D7, P66-91): the designers **leaned raw ("pink") for HUMAN display** (scaled views bunch pols too close ‚Äî Santos 22 vs Biden 28), with a normalized scale acceptable **only for a hidden/AI value**; Ted's one hard demand was **"no negative numbers"** ‚Üí fix via a **flat `+X` floor offset** (NOT the current hard `Math.max(0,‚Ä¶)` clamp, which destroys ordering among bad pols the CPU still needs). **No final vote was taken.** `summer2021` POST 200-205 pulls the opposite direction ‚Äî a recalibration so the **top pol = 100** on a normalized scale (founding pols getting a larger *draft* value). So the build faces a live fork: raw-for-humans + offset-not-clamp (revampPV lean) vs 100-normalized (summer2021). Sharpens the PV-display side of #18/#192 (which assume raw PV). Pairs with #214 (the underlying values) / #192 / #18 | Decide the PV display layer: (a) replace the `Math.max(0,‚Ä¶)` clamp with a constant floor-offset so sub-zero pols keep ordering for the CPU; (b) pick human presentation ‚Äî raw ("pink", revampPV lean) vs 0-100-normalized (summer2021) ‚Äî normalized acceptable only if PV is hidden/AI-only. UNRESOLVED in both sources; build is free to calibrate. Pairs with #214/#192/#18 | ingested (**revampPV ¬ß6 D6/D7, ¬ß2 / POST 16, 18, 20, 57, 66-91; summer2021 POST 200-205 (top pol = 100, larger founding draft value); codebase-verified pv.ts:88 clamp, pvCache raw**; **‚òÖ BATCH-33 ‚Äî #220 direction RULED by `groupthinkpv` (c6f6dcc7): the display scale = **0‚Äì100 with 50 = the AVERAGE politician** ("weak below 50, great above" ‚Äî Madden / Romance-of-the-Three-Kingdoms ratings, POST 39, 44). Normalization method given by jnewt (POST 43): assign trait/ability values ‚Üí sum the "average" pol ‚Üí solve `a¬∑x = 50` for the scale constant `a` ‚Üí multiply all weights by `a` and offset the title. This LEANS toward the `summer2021` "100-normalized" fork over the `revampPV` "raw pink" lean ‚Äî but cap-vs-centered is still SOFT (jnewt: "it bothers me when I see PV over 100," POST 45; unresolved whether 100 is a hard cap or just the average-centered title). ‚òÖ UNRECONCILED (human-call): the 0‚Äì100 scale is flatly incompatible with #215's office magnitudes (+1000‚Ä¶+50) ‚Äî the office-magnitude ‚Üî display-scale reconciliation is the one numeric thing left open across the whole PV epic. groupthinkpv POST 39, 43-45**; **‚òÖ BATCH-34 trait-EFFECT data point (`strongnevernom` 326eee56 POST 12) ‚Äî a durable definition feeding the PV/trait-EFFECTS epic: **Predictable** = the politician is so consistent in behavior/demands/rhetoric that the **opposition can strategize against them and STONEWALL** ‚Äî concretely HURTS passing legislation when Congress is opposed (cited for Sanders, Bayard, Kefauver, Taft). A legislative-effect (not a flat PV number) for the EFFECTS layer of the PV epic (groupthinkpv/#214). strongnevernom POST 12**; **‚òÖ BATCH-36 ‚Äî vcczar confirms the 0-100 scale is DONE in the master sheet: *"recalculated the political values‚Ä¶ to create a 0-100 scale (although someone can go beyond 100 and below 0)"* (`failednoms` 55c18fde POST 14) with concrete anchors **Clay 98 / TR 100** ‚Äî corroborating the `groupthinkpv` "0-100, 50=average, can exceed" ruling over the `revampPV` raw-pink lean, and confirming the can-exceed-100/below-0 behavior (so a hard cap is OUT; it's average-centered). failednoms POST 14**) |

| 222 | **Committee size scales with state count (3 ‚Üí 11 at 50 states) ‚Äî NEW (`ampuUpdates`, `GM‚áíApp`)** | Committee composition is not state-count-scaled in the build | **A state-count-scaled committee-size rule (`ampuUpdates` POST 236).** Committees = **Chair + Majority + Ranking Minority (3)** baseline; **+1 per party at 20 states**, then **+1 per party per +10 states** ‚Üí **11 members at 50 states**. Affects the committee/legislative-ladder system (the Legis ladder 1=committee/2=chair/3=whip/4=leader/5=Speaker is confirmed canonical in `summer2021` POST 233-235). Author-side design data, not yet in code. Pairs with #110 (legislative loop / committee system) | Scale committee size with the active state count: 3 baseline, +1/party at 20 states, +1/party per +10 thereafter (11 at 50). Pairs with #110 | ingested (**ampuUpdates (970616d5) ¬ß4 N3 / POST 236; Legis ladder canonical per summer2021 POST 233-235**; **‚òÖ CORROBORATED-AUTHORITATIVE (batch 31, `rulebook` ¬ßK POST 12): committee SIZE scales with the union ‚Äî 1788 base = chair + 1 same-party + 1 ranking other-party; at 20 states add +1 member PER PARTY; per +10 states add +1 per party (so 50 states ‚áí 11-member committees); chair breaks ties; majority-flip reshuffle (Ranking‚Üí3rd member, Chair‚ÜíRanking, a random former-majority member removed if odd). Confirms the 3‚Üí11 scaling from the canonical rulebook (matches ampuUpdates). Also pins committee structure: 4 standard committees (Domestic / Foreign-Military / Economics / Judicial) + optional Special; chair = Rep/Senator w/ Legislative ‚â•2 + prior member of that committee; all committee positions earn 50 pts. rulebook ¬ßK POST 12**) |

the shipped `Era` enum is 4 values (`types.ts:1337`) so there is no 13-era boundary to score at, and `HalfTermSummary` is per-turn only (`types.ts:1762`) ‚Äî no per-era policy snapshot archive (codebase-verified) | **The win/score model from #68/#102 is now CONTENT-COMPLETE as a dataset (vcczar, tier-1, sole author of the finished lists).** ~**10 benchmarks per era √ó 13 eras √ó 2 parties ‚âà 260** authored, party-specific state PREDICATES, evaluated **at END OF ERA** with **active-or-issued semantics** ‚Äî a benchmark must be **active at era end** (lingering policies/states) **OR "issued" during the era** (one-shot actions that don't linger) (POST 1). vcczar's design emphasis: this "makes winning the election right before an era change really important." **DUAL PARTISAN (Red/Blue) MIRRORED sets** encode each party's per-era ideological win-state: Red rewards pro-British-trade/US-Bank/high-tariffs/low-income-tax/strict-immigration/pro-business/pro-gun/military-buildup/RW-justices/keeping specific states red; Blue rewards pro-labor/civil-rights/popular-vote/internationalism/welfare(min-wage/Social-Security/TVA/healthcare)/LW-justices/environment + REPRESENTATION milestones (black/woman president, woman Speaker, black-woman-in-Congress, LGBT officeholder) (POST 1, 2). **Predicate classes observed:** policy-active checks, action-issued checks, per-nation relations thresholds (UK/France/Russia/China), war-state flags (French-Rev/Napoleonic/Cold-War/War-on-Terror), counts ("3 states"/"two chairmen"), identity/office records, and **era-relative COMPARATIVES** ("tariff higher/lower than the previous era"; "income-tax bracket higher/lower than previous era"). **‚òÖ SUB-REQUIREMENT:** the comparative class forces a **per-era SNAPSHOT ARCHIVE** of key economic levers (tariff, income-tax bracket, etc.) captured at each era boundary ‚Äî the scorer cannot read only current state. **Score/penalty WEIGHTS are TBD** ("yet to determine how much these score/penalize"); the **OLD benchmark point system is RETAINED, "only slightly amended"** (POST 3) ‚Äî this dataset SUPPLEMENTS the existing scoring, does not replace it. Benchmarks are a CONSUMER of the #221 content-primitive surface (named Legis-Props/Pres-Actions/policies ‚Äî US Bank, TVA, Fugitive Slave Act, Civil Rights Act 1964 ‚Äî must be addressable objects). DATA-FIDELITY FLAG: the CSV export FLATTENED per-era grouping (13 headers then a flat benchmark run) ‚Üí exact era‚Üîbenchmark assignment is NOT 1:1 recoverable from this export; re-source the original sheet before implementation (counts/timeline/model are reliable). Pairs with #68/#102 (the scoring pipeline this is the content+engine for ‚Äî do NOT duplicate them), #206/#92 (the 13-era boundaries it scores at), #221 (the predicate surface) | (a) Author the ~260-predicate Red/Blue benchmark dataset as era-keyed, party-keyed records (re-source per-era grouping from the original sheet); (b) add an END-OF-ERA scoring pass that evaluates each predicate with active-or-issued semantics and feeds the #68 point-banking / #102 dual-score (weights TBD; keep the retained old point system); (c) add a per-era SNAPSHOT ARCHIVE of key economic levers (tariff/income-tax-bracket/‚Ä¶) so "vs previous era" comparatives can be evaluated; (d) ensure the named-content surface (#221) is addressable for predicate evaluation. Requires the 13-era band model (#206/#92) to exist first. This is fundamentally a UI relabel, but it couples to every site that parses/renders `year` (turn header, election prompts, age calc). DISTINCT from #216 (the trait/term-taxonomy remap) ‚Äî different thread strand, different surface. (1) ‚òÖ `a938ac91` ("Should Some Gov Actions Be Scripted Events", Oct 2022, vcczar) makes the **Jim-Crow-on-installation-of-a-friendly-governor** pattern the textbook #258 archetype: OrangeP47's load-bearing event formation (POST 4) = *"if {state} has {gov - trad/rw pop} then {do Jim Crow}"*, fired **per state on installation of a governor matching an ideology predicate** (Traditionalist/RW-Populist), NOT a global tick ‚Äî "make the event happen upon the installation of the governor 'friendly' to the idea." This needs a NEW **governor-identity/ideology/trait `Predicate` clause** the shipped union (`types.ts:~1487`) cannot express; the existing `eraGraph.ts evalPredicate` interpreter is the reuse target. The predicate IS the agency-preserver (a hostile governor never triggers it ‚Äî "as long as who the governor is still matters," Ted POST 7). Dual-track design (POST 1): each significant gov action exists as BOTH a scripted auto-fire backstop AND a manual early/override Gov-Action, authored case-by-case (OrangeP47 POST 4), not a blanket switch. Variant (POST 5): a scripted event can present to the sitting governor as sign/veto-with-penalty ‚Üí needs a `'governor'` value on `EraEvent.decider` (`types.ts:1473`, currently `'president'|'congress'|'cabinet'|'cc-president'|'auto'`). ‚òÖ Card-trigger-WINDOW risk (POST 14): realignment reshuffles lobby cards (Reformist ‚Üí term-limits gov action), so a card-conditioned content gate can STARVE ‚Äî a faction loses the gating card before it ever holds the relevant office; any card-conditioned #258 gate must account for this churn. (2) ‚òÖ `9f2ab25f` (Environment genre) writes multiple gates INLINE: **Amazon‚ÜíBrazilian statehood** (POST 1, the clearest hard-gate); **Canada‚ÜíGulf pipeline ‚Üí "Canada is a US state OR a trade partner"** (POST 2 ‚Äî needs a NEW **diplomatic/trade-relationship predicate class**, same family as the b39 Diplomacy per-nation-state target); **NPS‚ÜíWilson-era, NFS‚ÜíTeddy-era** (POST 7, era-window gates); **Great-Plains-park‚Üífuture depopulation** (POST 7). No explicit `Preq:` field ‚Üí reconfirms the prereq field + territory/era/diplomatic-relation predicate classes. (3) ‚òÖ era-gated legislation UNDER-ENFORCED (live ruling): `243dc711` (Sep-Oct 2023, 1868 run) POST 24-26 ‚Äî a Senator successfully reinstated Reconstruction in SC **~50 yrs after the war ended**; GM ruling (Bushwa777): "Well after we move to Progressive era it's no longer an allowed law" ‚Äî but the gate did NOT catch it ‚Üí Reconstruction-era laws should be **predicate-DISABLED once the era advances to Progressive**, an era-window availability gate that is not (or not fully) enforced today. a938ac91 POST 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 14; 9f2ab25f POST 1, 2, 7; 243dc711 POST 24, 25, 26**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b47) THE PUREST WORKED PREDICATE EXAMPLE IN THE CORPUS ‚Äî the biggest single corroboration of this row. `de64de1a` (AMPU Currency, vcczar, near-ratified POST 1-2) authors the entire Currency tree **organized BY PREREQUISITE** ‚Äî every group is a precondition, every line a gated policy ‚Äî making it the canonical data model #258 must support. It demands these predicate CLASSES the shipped era-event-only `Predicate` union (`types.ts:1487`) cannot express, recorded explicitly here: **(a) active-INSTITUTION / active-policy** (`Bank of the US` active, `US Mint` active, `Bank of North America active & NOT privatized`, `Federal Reserve active`, `Gold Standard active`, `Bimetalism active`, `Crypto Currency Advocated`, `No US Currency active`) ‚Äî incl. the **`not privatized` qualifier** (a policy can be repealed/privatized, a state the build has no concept of); **(b) economic-meter BAND** (`Panic or worse`, `Depression or worse`) ‚Äî shipped `NationalMeters.economic` (`types.ts:1401`) is a RAW number with `meterAtMost` but NO named bands/thresholds (corroborated by Banking's `Recession-or-worse` read, b42 #258); **(c) active-war / era** (`Major War`, `Rev War active`, `Reconstruction is active`) ‚Äî shipped `warActive` is HARDWIRED to RevWar only (`eraGraph.ts:31`), no generic major-war predicate, and **Reconstruction-active has NO representation at all**; **(d) program-STATE** (income-tax-active, IRS-exists, Soc-Sec-exists, sea-colony-exists ‚Äî from `1bf19872` Taxation, all 0% expressible); **(e) per-state SLAVERY FLAG** (slavery-enabled-in-state ‚Äî from `5d7c29ee` Slavery, the secession-eligibility input ‚Äî see NEW #288); **(f) policy-CHAIN gate** (sub-36¬∞30‚Ä≤ pop-sovereignty unlocks ONLY after Missouri-Compromise-permanent passes, `5d7c29ee` POST 2; Currency's literal `Many preqs` placeholder for Bretton-Woods‚ÜíNixon-Shock, `de64de1a` 3l); **(g) sequential / segment-BUILT** ("prior segment built" ‚Äî National-Road extensions, HSR-NYC‚ÜíBoston "like the national road is handled," turnpike‚ÜíMcAdam‚Üímodern-road‚Üí1916-act‚ÜíHighway-Act, `d62ea397` Infrastructure POST 1, 2, 4 ‚Äî the headline pattern there). Currency also exhibits **monotonic / one-way transitions** (vcczar POST 5 ruling: once the US Dollar exists, the `No US Currency active`‚ÜíSpanish-Dollar+State-Scrip default is PERMANENTLY unreachable ‚Äî a `not` over an active-institution predicate once (a) lands) and an L-vs-P **enacting-office** dimension (Specie-Circular / Bretton-Woods / Nixon-Shock are Presidential, the rest Legislative ‚Äî ties #66 enacting-office tag). The four batch-47 policy-genre threads (Currency / Slavery / Taxation / Infrastructure) all write these gates INLINE with no `Preq:` field ‚Üí reconfirms the field + interpreter extension. de64de1a POST 1, 2, 5; 5d7c29ee POST 1, 2; 1bf19872 POST 1; d62ea397 POST 1, 2, 4**; **‚òÖ (b48) `391df03b` (Credit/Debt, vcczar ‚Äî the "smallest subtype," prereq-STRUCTURED like Currency) is the 2nd canonical worked exemplar and adds TWO predicate classes to this set: ‚òÖ **(a) diplomatic-RECOGNITION** ("Dutch Recognize US" gates Secure-Dutch-Loan, ¬ß3d) ‚Äî distinct from the shipped `diplomacyAtLeast` (a relationship *value*, not a recognition *state*); ships only as a bespoke one-off (`dutch_recognition` event ‚Üí the single hardcoded `loansEnabled` graph flag), needs a generic recognition-of-nation-X predicate; ‚òÖ **(b) policy-active / program-STATE** ("Preq: Funding Act" gates Emergency-Foreign-Loan, ¬ß3e ‚Äî a prior enacted policy becomes the gate; same class Currency demanded for Bank/Mint/Fed) + "National Debt Exists" (¬ß3c). ‚òÖ‚òÖ **NEW concrete leaf the corpus most needs: a DEBT predicate.** ‚òÖ CORRECTION to prior batch hypotheses (codebase-verified 2026-06-28): `game.nationalDebt` **DOES ship** (`types.ts:1573`, seeded $0/1772 & $32M/1856, displayed, passively paid down `phaseRunners.ts:3376`) ‚Äî but it is **NOT a `MeterKey`** (`NationalMeters` `types.ts:1399-1407` = revenue/economic/military/domestic/honest/quality/planet, no debt) and the `Predicate` union (`types.ts:1487-1504`) has **NO debt leaf** ‚Üí nothing can gate on the debt at all; needs a `debtExists`/`debtAtLeast`/`debtAtMost` leaf (or fold debt into the meter/band machinery). The `revenue` meter already ships the exact named bands the laws want (Bankruptcy‚Ä¶Deficit‚Ä¶Balanced‚Ä¶Surplus, `Meter.tsx:14`) but they are display-only strings, not queryable thresholds (same as Currency's Panic/Depression-band ask). Credit/Debt + Currency = the two canonical prereq-tree exemplars; this nails the diplomacy/institution/program-state predicate family. 391df03b POST 1 (¬ß3c/3d/3e), 10**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b49) `5261c8ff` (Bailouts, vcczar ‚Äî the 3rd prereq-STRUCTURED tree, sibling of Currency/Credit-Debt) is the STRONGEST single source yet for the predicate-class set, exercising three classes INLINE: ‚òÖ **economic-meter BAND** ("Economic Meter at *Stagnant* or worse", "at *Panic* or worse") ‚Äî ‚òÖ RECONCILE: the author's band vocabulary ("Stagnant"/"Panic") does NOT match the shipped 11-band economic DISPLAY scale (`Depression‚Ä¶Severe Recession‚Ä¶Recession‚Ä¶Slowdown‚Ä¶Sluggish‚Ä¶Stable‚Ä¶Growth‚Ä¶Expansion‚Ä¶Boom‚Ä¶Strong Boom‚Ä¶Roaring`, `Meter.tsx:15`), and shipped `economic` is a RAW `number` (`types.ts:1401`) with only `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` numeric leaves ‚Äî so "meter at <band> or worse" must COMPILE to a numeric `meterAtMost` threshold AND the author bands must be mapped onto the 11-band scale (Stagnant‚âàSlowdown/Sluggish? Panic‚âàRecession/Severe-Recession? ‚Äî OPEN); ‚òÖ **policy-active / policy-NOT-active** (three instances: "Ban Too Big to Fail" NOT active gates Corp/Wall-St bailouts; "any pandemic package" active gates Expand-Stimulus; "Garn-St-Germaine Act" active gates the S&L-Crisis event ‚Äî requires tracking which policy nodes are in force, which the build cannot do); ‚òÖ **EVENT-active** ("Requires any pandemic" gates the 3 Pandemic-Relief packages ‚Äî predicates on a live scripted-event/condition, NOT a passed bill; no pandemic state ships). ‚òÖ **CONTENT‚ÜîEVENT interplay** (new observation): two scripted-event (`S`) nodes are EMBEDDED in the tree as siblings of legislation ‚Äî Subprime-Mortgage-Crisis (`S-Ter`) and Savings-&-Loans-Crisis (`S-Neo`, itself gated by Garn-St-Germaine being active = a policy‚Üíevent chain) ‚Äî events flip flags/meters that then gate downstream player policy responses (the same coupling the engine must model). Also an **ideology-gated** (`L-Ide`) content axis (the income-tier worker bailouts gate on player ideology, not era). ‚òÖ `faf2d8b3` (Drugs) adds a **program-STATE** predicate need ‚Äî "Prohibition is in force" / "War on Drugs instituted" / "legal in state X" gate the modifier-verb rows (Increase-Punishments-for-Violating-Prohibition, Expand-War-on-Drugs) and the 21st-Amendment repeal (gated on the 18th passed) ‚Äî none expressible by the shipped `Predicate` tree. This is the strongest demand yet for a **policy-active / repealed-by predicate relationship** (one law's active-status conditions a whole genre's availability) ‚Äî no shipped `Predicate` variant expresses "law X is active/passed/repealed" or "agency Y exists" (FBI/CIA/DNI/NSC era-window + exists gates also implied inline). `067233f3` (Guns) adds TWO predicate needs: ‚òÖ a genuinely-NEW **cross-state policy-active linkage** ‚Äî STATE RECIPROCITY (POST 3, "all states honor each other's carry laws") needs a "policy X active in state Y" predicate SPANNING states; the shipped `Predicate` tree has only `stateAdmitted`, no cross-state policy-active relationship anywhere; AND ‚òÖ a **program-STATE "shield in force"** gate ‚Äî the manufacturer-liability-shield ‚Üî open-to-lawsuits opposed pair (PLCAA-analog, POST 4) means the counter-law's availability is gated on the shield being active (same policy-active-flag class as the Privacy Amendment). This is the first call for a per-state-keyed boolean/year FLAG predicate (distinct from the global game-state conditions this row already covers). Pairs with #238 (the gender attribute + the federal gate the per-state flag overrides) / #34. `92202e9a` POST 3, 6, 7. **‚òÖ b57 SHARP ‚Äî the predicate machinery EXISTS but is wired in the 1772 graph ONLY (`2bb66197`):** the serializable `Predicate` tree (`types.ts:1487-1504`), its interpreter `evalPredicate` (`engine/eraGraph.ts:12`), and an optional `GraphNode.precondition` that selection gates on (`eraEvents1772.ts:25`, gated at `eraGraph.ts:114`) are all REAL and shipped ‚Äî BUT predicate-gating is deployed in the 1772 era graph ONLY: `eraEvents1856.ts`, `data/anytimeEvents.ts`, and `data/anytimeNationalEvents.ts` carry **zero** `precondition`/`Predicate` usage (grep `-c` = 0). The future speed-run is the strongest author-voiced evidence: the GM names **two** events firing with no prereq ‚Äî "Half the animals that existed in 2020 are extinct" fires nonsensically/repeatedly ("**second test**", POST 1) and "Apply Bill of Rights & Voting Suffrage to Citizens in Space" fires before any enabling "citizens in space" state exists ("there's no prereqs", POST 3). Delta = extend predicate gating to ALL event content (1856 + both anytime pools + any future band) and make it an **authoring requirement**, not just an available primitive. The repeat-firing argues for a once-only/`eventCompleted` guard, not merely a year gate. [`2bb66197` POST 1, 3]**; **‚òÖ (b58) TWO more b58 witnesses confirm predicate-gating is 1772-GRAPH-ONLY and must extend to NON-event content. (1) `df11a769` (Trump-2.0 catalog): ~20 proposed EXEC ACTIONS are precondition-gated ("requires Reciprocal Tariff Act," "requires Jan-6 era-evo," "requires natural disaster," "requires high unrest + protest event," "populist president"; POST 17/23) ‚Äî but `precondition` is authored ONLY in `eraEvents1772.ts`; `eraEvents1856.ts` gates by hardcoded `if(year>=1856 && year<=1858)` blocks with ZERO preconditions (`:6/30/55‚Ä¶`), and executive/governor actions have **NO predicate hook at all** (the exec stub `phaseRunners.ts:3632-3646` is a 4-item `pick()`). So the gate must generalize to modern era events AND to pres/gov ACTIONS, not just events. (2) `1ae348bb`: the investigation system's "Era of Populism and later" gate is NOT EXPRESSIBLE ‚Äî Era enum is 4 values, no Populism/future band (`types.ts:1337`; "Populism" exists only as an ideology card `:325`), so a Populism-era availability gate cannot be written without a new era-band or year threshold (cross-ref #206). Both reinforce that the excellent `Predicate`/`evalPredicate` engine is deployed on 1772 events only. `df11a769` POST 17, 23; `1ae348bb` POST 1**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b61) A PLAYER-AUTHORED NOTATION for this exact model + a NEW sub-primitive ‚Äî `8f7ae0b9` ("Era of the Future additions", vcczar + Arkansas Progressive). AP's authoring DSL (POST 3) maps 1:1 onto the shipped `Predicate`/`evalPredicate`/`ERA_GRAPH_1772` model: **`A -> B`** = the `eventCompleted` edge (B fires from A); **`(other requirement)`** combinable with **AND/OR/NOT** = the meter/interest/`not` predicates; **green text = a bill node, red text = a president-action node** (the two content-primitive types). Concrete in-thread predicates exercise the whole vocabulary: a compound AND-gate `(Federal Reserve AND US on Cryptocurrency standard) -> ‚Ä¶`; a meter-gated node (Great Climate Migration requires planet-health 1-2); an OR-fork (Blue Scare from Communist-Party-Pres OR Socialist-Party-Pres); a deep multi-clause `(Anti-Russian Bloc) AND (Russia NOT fragmented) AND (Russia had been Communist)`; and a CROSS-CHAIN require (Turkey-Superpower requires Russia-collapse). Confirms #258 exactly ‚Äî but AP's notation is for the FUTURE era, which **has no graph at all** (0% built; `Era` enum lacks `future`), while 1856 gates by hardcoded `if(year>=)` (grep `-c` precondition = 0), so the model is STILL 1772-graph-only. ‚òÖ‚òÖ NEW SUB-PRIMITIVE (folded here, not its own row ‚Äî it's a shape on this same content-primitive machinery): the **event-BLOCKS-event / one-to-many NEGATIVE edge** ‚Äî POST 3 "Climate Control being invented BLOCKS the following events:" then lists a disaster cluster (10.0 CA quake / continent-sized New-Orleans hurricane / Great Climate Migration / AZ+TX at 135¬∞F). This is a **blocker-side, one-to-many suppression declaration** that the current `Predicate` type CANNOT express first-class ‚Äî the only emulation is hand-adding `{ not: { eventCompleted: 'climate_control' } }` to the precondition of **each** blocked node individually (grep `blocks|blockedBy|excludes|mutex|cancels|preempts` in `eraEvents1772.ts` ‚Üí none). REQUIREMENT: a blocker-side `suppresses: [templateId‚Ä¶]` (or `mutex`/exclusion-group) primitive on `GraphNode`. ‚òÖ Also supplies a large FUTURE-CONTENT CORPUS to author node-by-node (motivated by the era's AI content predating ChatGPT/Midjourney): generative-AI events, data-center energy crisis, internet enshittification, 3D‚Üí4D printing, usable fusion, peak-oil‚Üíalt-energy, Climate-Control + its blocked cluster, cancer-cure chain, alien-life debate, **China Fragments (Wu/Shu/Wei, requires a Far-Future Chinese Clone War)** and **Russia Fragments (E/W Russia + United Siberia)** geopolitics chains, and **ideology-takeover war-chains** (a party controlling the Presidency unlocks alliance + war-of-expansion chains terminating in a NAMED WAR node). It is the same shape as the whole row (availability gated on a prior enacted law being in force, not on the calendar), and it lands on a Pres ACTION (reinforcing the b58 finding that pres/gov actions have NO predicate hook ‚Äî the exec stub `phaseRunners.ts:3632-3646` is a hardcoded 4-item `pick()`, and the `Predicate`/`evalPredicate` engine is deployed 1772-events-only). No "Alien & Sedition Acts active" flag ships (grep `alien|sedition` in `src/` ‚âà 0 mechanics ‚Äî only a trait comment `types.ts:1041` naming Adams' Sedition Acts as flavor + "alienating" in `eraEvents1856.ts:15`). Grading note ("big and easy to grade, RW Pop") = an ideology-scored choice, consistent with the exec-action-content pattern (#169). Confirms the OPEN question this row already carries ‚Äî a persistent "which laws are in force" registry is needed to gate downstream content (the A&S-active flag is one entry). `c8d3fd84` POST 2**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (`era-events` dataset, `b41ac9b1`) THE AUTHORITATIVE FLAT DATA for this predicate class ‚Äî the ground-truth `Requires` corpus. Of 2,475 scripted events, **1,664 (67%) carry a `Requires` precondition** (analysis.md ¬ßPrecondition-coverage), and analysis.md's MODEL-RECONCILIATION maps `Requires` ‚Üí the shipped `precondition: Predicate` (`types.ts:1487`) directly. The corpus exercises the exact predicate classes this row already lists, on real data: **prior-event-occurred** (Gasp√©e-Affair ‚Üí Committees-of-Correspondence; Bay-of-Pigs-blundered ‚Üí Cuban-Missile-Crisis ‚Äî the crisis gated ONLY to the blundered path), **mutually-exclusive world-state forks** (the Louisiana pair ‚Äî "France HAS regained Louisiana from Spain" vs "‚Ä¶has NOT," each gating a different purchase event, both also requiring "Napoleon is not a US politician"), **trait/eligibility** (Watergate fires for ANY `Controversial` incumbent), **active-war/era** (Conciliatory-Resolution requires "Rev War occurred + not ended"), **world-nation state** (Franco took control + Spain not Communist). ‚òÖ THE CHOSEN PATH (the reconcile-with-prior-ingests deliverable): the sheet is the AUTHORITATIVE FLAT BASE (identity + `Requires` DATA per event) but it is a FLAT file ‚Äî one `Requires` string per row ‚Äî and it LACKS the event-CHAIN DSL STRUCTURE the forum threads supply. `8f7ae0b9` (b61) is the STRUCTURE: multi-edge `A -> B` / AND / OR / NOT graphs + the one-to-many **blocker edge** ("Climate-Control BLOCKS [disaster cluster]") the flat single-`Requires` string cannot express. So: **the sheet populates #258's predicate DATA; `8f7ae0b9`/the forum digests supply #258's chain TOPOLOGY + the blocker/mutex primitive** ‚Äî coexist, do not subsume. Re-confirms the row's headline finding (predicate-gating is real but wired 1772-graph-ONLY): loading this corpus for 1856 + both anytime pools + a future band is exactly the interpreter-extension this row requires. | 260 | **‚òÖ Event‚ÜíPOPULATION/DEMOGRAPHIC-shift effect + multi-turn SPATIAL-SPREAD event chains (per-state infestation/effect state, spread over turns, a population/demographics mutation that feeds apportionment/lean) ‚Äî NEW (`agriculture`)** | **0% built.** `EraEvent` is a single year-windowed FIRE-ONCE shape (no multi-turn spread, no per-state propagation, no expiry); `state.bias` is a static scalar and the only dynamic population lever is the #247 ¬±1 region `stateBias` anytime-event effect (clamped, un-time-boxed, `phaseRunners.ts:2608`); no event‚Üípopulation/demographic mutation exists (codebase-verified) | **Scripted events that mutate geography and population over MULTIPLE TURNS, beyond fire-once `EraEvent` (POST 5/6).** ‚òÖ **BOLL WEEVIL** = a geographic, multi-turn SPREAD mechanic: comes from Mexico ‚Üí infests TX cotton (income‚Üì, dissatisfaction‚Üë), then **spreads to neighboring cotton states every 4 yrs** on a dated propagation table (1896 TX ‚Üí 1900 AR/LA ‚Üí 1904 MS/TN ‚Üí 1908 AL/GA ‚Üí 1912 FL/SC ‚Üí 1916 NC/VA), with **variant spread-rates** (a slow/never variant ‚Äî "Great Migration never happens"; a catastrophic/fast variant ‚Äî neighbors in 2 yrs not 4); the spread DRIVES the **Great Migration** (South‚ÜíNorth/West **population shift**); a **counter-event** (~1950 DDT) appears and then **EXPIRES** (stops working by 1960 as the weevil develops resistance) ‚Äî a time-boxed counter with an expiry; secondary effect = crop diversification (peanuts). ‚òÖ **POTATO BLIGHT ‚Üí immigration¬±**: Ireland‚ÜíUS famine event whose magnitude **modulates immigration inflow** (worse famine ‚áí more immigration; milder ‚áí less). So the model needs **(a) per-state infestation/effect STATE**, **(b) spread propagation over turns** (with variant rates + geography), and **(c) an event effect that mutates state POPULATION/DEMOGRAPHICS** (which feeds apportionment/EV + lean). DISTINCT from #247 (event-driven *lean*; this is population/demographic MAGNITUDE + geography) and from #201/the Shaw/John-Brown one-shot "demographic-removal" events (fire-once, no spread). Per-era density tracks real eventfulness (Nuclear-Age 304 / Ideologies 283 / Gilded 252 / Progressivism 238 at the top; Independence 71 at the trough ‚Äî historian ¬ß1). ‚òÖ GENRE-COVERAGE SKEW (a concrete imbalance to track): scripted-event genres are **Flavor 1,348 ¬∑ Foreign-Mil 621 ¬∑ Domestic 428 ¬∑ Economics 71 ¬∑ Judicial 7** ‚Äî **Judicial (7) and Economics (71) are conspicuously thin** given how much U.S. politics turns on the Court and the economy; major turning points (Marbury/Dred-Scott/Lochner/Brown/Roe; every panic/depression) are filed Domestic/Flavor rather than getting their own genre. This is the per-GENRE coverage counterpart to the row's per-ideology (RW-non-repeal) and per-primitive (thin Pres/Gov-Action) skews ‚Äî the authoring backlog under-weights judicial/economic scripted events (and the forum SC-case threads `964b8857`/`07fa6116` are effectively the WHOLE judicial future, since the sheet has 7). See the digest `## ‚òÖ The coverage delta`. `era-events` analysis.md ¬ßCoverage-vs-shipped + ¬ßType-distribution; historian ¬ß1**) |
| 263 | **‚òÖ Game-SETTINGS / start-of-game OPTIONS system ‚Äî a togglable-options model that GATES engine rules at new-game time; the umbrella the scattered single toggles lack ‚Äî NEW (`f735601c` "AMPU Rules Update", DESIGN-ORIGIN, Aug 2021)** | **No settings/options framework exists.** There is no new-game options screen and no per-toggle engine plumbing; individual toggles live only as separate scattered gap rows (#171 era-keyed draft-restriction, #238 gender/suffrage-eligibility, #24/#115 curated what-if pool, #4 draft-ideology profile) with no shared on/off channel; the two shipped scenarios are fixed hand-authored data files (codebase-verified) | **vcczar, for the first time in the corpus, explicitly DESIGNS a settings menu (`f735601c` POST 1-3, 11): start-of-game toggles that gate engine rules.** The canonical floated list: **(1) randomize politician start dates** (you never know when a pol arrives in a draft ‚Äî vs the fixed `draftYear`=nearest-mult-of-4 to birthYear+25; NEW, no existing gap); **(2) allow politicians to move to ANY state** (removes the home-state relocation restriction; NEW); **(3) turn OFF auto-assigning rookies a random trait** (NEW, cross-ref the rookie-trait cluster); **(4) women/minorities draftable BEFORE suffrage** (‚Üí #238 + #24/#115); **(5) factions divided by party vs draft-ANYONE-regardless-of-party** (‚Üí #171/#4); **(6) leave "what-if" candidates IN** (‚Üí #24/#115); **(7) ‚òÖ turn OFF ability requirements = "unrealistic mode"** (disables ability prereqs for office eligibility ‚Üí a NEW toggle on #182, vcczar's ONLY concession after refusing to weaken the eligibility model ‚Äî see #182). vcczar's governing frame (POST 11): **"no more overhauls"** ‚Äî fundamental mechanic changes are closed; he will accept changes ONLY as game settings, and wants the game **‚â•50% realistic**, so the settings model is the sanctioned channel for ahistorical play. DISTINCT from #115 (the scenario-boot PROCEDURE ‚Äî the deterministic setup pipeline; this is the player-facing OPTIONS layer that parameterizes it) and from each individual toggle gap it would subsume. OPEN (carried from the digest): should each toggle stay folded in its rule-gap, or live under this umbrella? The thread argues umbrella (one sanctioned channel, ‚â•50% realism floor). Pairs with #115 (boot procedure) / #171 / #238 / #24 / #115 / #182 / #4 | Add a start-of-game **options/settings system**: a new-game options screen + per-toggle engine plumbing, each toggle gating a specific engine rule. Resolve umbrella-vs-per-gap ownership (human call; thread leans umbrella). The novel entry mechanic is NEW #272; the pool toggle is on #115. d474f718 POST 1**; **‚òÖ (b52) `7d91c4c7` (Alternate Drafting Poll, Aug 2021) adds TWO modes to this umbrella, both 0% built: (a) a **PARTIES-ONLY mode** ‚Äî "modes in which there are no factions, only parties for a two player game" (POST 1), collapsing the 10-faction structure to bare parties; (b) an **explicitly UNRESTRICTED-ideology mode** ‚Äî "an option to allow play without restrictions to ideologies" (POST 1), the player-selectable opposite of the restricted matrix (#4/#171). (1) the **draft-ideology-restriction toggle** (`0af55af3` POST 6-7): default-ON ‚áí historical party shift, OFF ‚áí ahistorical play ‚Äî a user-facing settings switch (see #171/#306; 0% built, grep `draftRestriction`=0). | 264 | **‚òÖ COUP / INSURRECTION PROCEDURE subsystem ‚Äî TWO coup types (opposition-led Standard event-triggered + incumbent-President Populist/Jan-6 choice), the "Following the Coup" consequence package (install loyalists + TREASON TRIALS ‚Üí supermajority + +3 buff), the countercoup reinstate-actual-winner ruling, and the Lingering‚Üícoup‚ÜíLegislation turn-loop hook ‚Äî the SURVIVE-AND-CONSOLIDATE path #88 does NOT own ‚Äî NEW (`4c14847b` Jan-6 thread, DESIGN-ORIGIN, Nov 2022)** | **Almost certainly 0% built (tech-lead to verify).** #88 owns only the coup LOSS-ROLL / end-condition (DomStab/EconStab/MilPrep-gated ‚Üí game-over). The coup PROCEDURE ‚Äî choice-to-launch, treason trials, install-loyalists, +3 buff, countercoup, and the turn-loop hook ‚Äî has NO home; `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` (`types.ts:1635`) is event-driven with no coup path; the meters ship (DomStab/MilPrep per #88/#92) but the coup-GATE logic and the survive-consolidate machinery do not | **The canonical design source for the whole coup subsystem (the `rule 3.0` coup rules; co-authored by Willthescout7). TWO coup types: (A) STANDARD COUP** = opposition-LEADER choice, EVENT-TRIGGERED only (Authoritarian-overthrow scripted event / "Newburgh Conspiracy is Real" / the hardened "Attempted Coup" general event ‚Äî gen-event hardened this thread to Economy ‚â§ Great Recession AND MilPrep ‚â§ Slightly-Unprepared AND DomStab ‚â§ Violent-Unrest, plus the opposing leader has Controversial/Leadership); base gate = DomStab in the WORST position + MilPrep < slightly-prepared + trait gate. **(B) POPULIST COUP** = incumbent-PRESIDENT choice (the Jan-6 analogue: an incumbent who LOST re-election launching to stay in power); gate = DomStab worse-than-neutral + MilPrep worse-than-slightly-prepared + President-trait gate. **‚òÖ "FOLLOWING THE COUP" consequence package (designed EXTREME, applies to both types when the incumbent survives):** (1) install loyalists = the President APPOINTS the winner of EACH federal election that cycle (House + Senate); (2) **TREASON TRIALS** for every opposition member NOT up for reelection (Senate, military, SCOTUS) ‚Üí losers REMOVED from the game + President appoints replacements ‚Üí **supermajority in all branches** (fold the treason-trial mechanic INTO this gap ‚Äî it is a coup-driven impeachment/removal; cross-ref the broken-impeachment design holes DH-29..DH-35 for plumbing reuse); (3) **+3 in ALL federal elections** for same-party members for the President's tenure OR ~10 yrs, with opposition challengers who run-and-lose REMOVED; (4) domestic-stability + foreign-relations meter penalties. **‚òÖ COUNTERCOUP ruling:** in a Populist Coup the opposition leader launches a countercoup; if it succeeds, **the ACTUAL winner of the Presidential election is reinstated as President ‚Äî NOT the opposition leader who launched it** ‚Äî and that winner joins the treason/impeachment rolls (worked example: 2020 Trump coup ‚Üí Pelosi countercoup ‚Üí BIDEN reinstated). **Turn-loop hook:** coups resolve AFTER the Lingering phase, BEFORE Legislation (so a treason trial runs the same turn). **CPU odds rise across eras but CAP LOW (~1‚Äì5% early ‚Üí ~25% by 2020 if conditions met), tied to Domestic Stability.** DISTINCT from #88 (the loss-roll/end-condition ‚Äî coups are effectively rare/dead there: MilPrep trivially maxes, no Populist-eligible President in any playtest; the one near-miss, Benedict Arnold, declined). Cross-ref #221 (event-triggered choice payload), #258/#92 (meter+trait game-state predicates + era-scaled odds), DH-29..DH-35 (impeachment plumbing the treason trial reuses). Pairs with #88/#221/#258/#92/DH-29 | Build the coup PROCEDURE subsystem (verify NONE ships): (a) two launch paths ‚Äî Standard (opposition-leader, event-gated) + Populist (incumbent-President choice) with their meter+trait gates; (b) the "Following the Coup" package ‚Äî install-loyalists appoint, treason trials (remove+replace ‚Üí supermajority; ‚òÖ tech-lead b39: impeachment is itself UNBUILT ‚Äî `impeach` absent from `src/`, so there is NO plumbing to reuse ‚Äî build/co-build it; DH-29..DH-35 are design holes), +3 federal-election buff for tenure/~10yr with lose-and-removed, DomStab+foreign-relations penalties; (c) the countercoup ‚Üí reinstate-the-ACTUAL-Presidential-winner rule; (d) the Lingering‚Üícoup‚ÜíLegislation turn-loop hook; (e) CPU odds curve (~1‚Äì5%‚Üí~25%, capped, DomStab-tied). ‚òÖ OPEN PRODUCT QUESTION (record): build the full two-type subsystem vs SCRAP it for a single Era-of-Populism scripted event ‚Äî coups are vanishingly rare (no Populist-eligible President ever; vcczar floated the scripted-event replacement in POST 1). Also decide buff duration (tenure vs ~10yr) + the "party leader" identity rule for the countercoup. | 266 | **‚òÖ "METER-AT-FLOOR = NO DETERRENT" systemic balance flaw ‚Äî once DomStab / party-preference / any score hits MINIMUM, further penalties are toothless, so the incumbent has nothing to lose ‚Üí reckless play (the root cause of the ignore-losing-treaty exploit, but GAME-WIDE not treaty-specific) ‚Äî NEW (batch 40, `foreign-affairs/treaty`; cross-ref #88/#188)** | **Shipped behavior (the flaw IS the build): penalties clamp at the meter floor with no further consequence.** `NationalMeters` are bounded scales; a penalty against an already-floored meter is absorbed by the clamp and registers nothing. #88 owns the meter-driven game-over/coup end-conditions (fire when meters SIT at the floor), but there is **no mechanism that makes a penalty-against-an-already-floored-meter still bite** ‚Äî no spill-over to other scores, no acceleration of the loss countdown (codebase-verified pattern: meters clamp; #88's end-conditions are threshold rolls, not a penalty-spill model) | **Largo833's systemic reframing (`foreign-affairs/treaty` POST 16, Ted-concurred POST 1): the ignore-losing-treaty exploit is *"partly just a symptom"* of a deeper flaw ‚Äî once Domestic Stability / party-preference reach their WORST value, further penalties do nothing, so the incumbent can act recklessly with nothing left to lose** (in-universe: a nation in "open rebellion" at DomStab 1 wouldn't think "at least it can't get any worse"). This will recur ANYWHERE a penalty targets an already-floored meter ‚Äî it is a meters/game-over-subsystem flaw, NOT a war/treaty bug. **Two floated fixes (design spitballs, no tier-1 commitment in-thread):** (a) **SPILL-OVER** ‚Äî when a score would drop but is already at minimum, the penalty spills into OTHER scores (so there's always something left to lose); (b) **SHORTEN THE GAME-OVER COUNTDOWN** ‚Äî a floored meter that would drop has a % chance to shorten the loss-window (Largo's example: **DomStab=1 ‚Üí 50% chance the game-over countdown drops 8yr‚Üí6yr** ‚Äî a direct modifier on #88's meter-driven clock; the "8yr‚Üí6yr" figure implies a multi-year loss-countdown exists once a meter floors). DISTINCT from #88 (the end-condition rolls themselves) and #188 (the nuclear/floored-meter loss path) ‚Äî this is the "make floor-penalties still matter" upstream pressure those rely on. Cross-ref the loss-debuff package on #45 (which dumps penalties onto already-floored party-pref) ‚Äî this flaw is exactly why that package was toothless in the Butler playtest. Pairs with #88/#188/#45 | Add a "floor-penalty still bites" mechanism to the meters/game-over subsystem: (a) SPILL-OVER (a penalty against an at-minimum score redistributes into other scores), and/or (b) a floored-meter % chance to SHORTEN the #88 game-over countdown (DomStab=1 ‚Üí 50% 8yr‚Üí6yr). Game-WIDE, not treaty-specific. ‚òÖ OPEN (human/tech-lead): is this one gap or a sub-row of #88; pick spill-over vs countdown-shorten (no tier-1 designer chose in-thread). Pairs with #88/#188/#45 | ingested (**foreign-affairs/treaty (a2312dd2) POST 1, 16 (Largo833 systemic flaw + two floated fixes; Ted-concurred the symptom framing); cross-ref #88 (meter-driven game-over clock ‚Äî the countdown-shorten fix is a direct modifier on it) / #188 (floored-meter loss path) / #45 (the loss-debuff package this flaw made toothless); codebase-verified meters clamp at floor with no spill/countdown-accel mechanism**) |
| 267 | **‚òÖ TREATY subsystem ‚Äî auto-PROPOSED win/loss treaty + Senate CONFIRM-OR-RENEW-THE-WAR vote + war-length UNPREDICTABILITY + SecState/ambassador negotiation; "automatic" = auto-proposed, NOT un-rejectable ‚Äî NEW (batch 40, `foreign-affairs/treaty`; cross-ref #45 [combat] / #178 [treaty‚Üíterritory] / #26 [SecState])** | **0% as a distinct subsystem.** The shipped war code (`revolutionaryWar.ts`, 1772-scoped) has the combat/War-Score side (#45) but **no treaty-confirmation machinery**: no auto-proposal on win/loss, no Senate up/down treaty vote, no rejection‚Üíbacklash‚Üíwar-renew path, no White-Peace-before-resolution option. The KB's only prior "treaties are AUTOMATIC" note (folded on #45/#26 from `completions`) is the BARE claim this thread fleshes out; `summer2021`/#178 show a Treaty-of-Paris AUTO-PROPOSED post-RevWar (territory-granting) but with treaty-failure rules UNDOCUMENTED (codebase-verified no treaty-vote path) | **The core deliverable of the batch-40 war/treaty thread ‚Äî the war-END side, distinct from #45's combat side. Ted's merged tier-1 direction (POST 15, restated 17):** (1) **AUTO-PROPOSAL** ‚Äî on a war win or loss the treaty is auto-proposed in the NEXT legislative session; **White Peace is proposable by the Sec of State only BEFORE winning/losing, NOT after**. (2) **SENATE CONFIRM-OR-RENEW** ‚Äî the Senate votes to confirm; **failing to confirm causes backlash against the Senate Majority Leader AND can RENEW the war**, and **anti-treaty (NAY) senators are penalized** (10centjimmy POST 17, Ted-endorsed). This **walks back option-3's "treaties can't be rejected"** ‚Üí reconcile: *automatic = auto-PROPOSED, not un-rejectable*. (3) **WAR-LENGTH UNPREDICTABILITY** ‚Äî replace today's readable per-phase chart (you can see if a loss is possible this 2-yr period) with **guaranteed first two battles, then a 50% chance of more battles** (or roll a d6 for battle count); resolve via **tier-scaled net-score thresholds** (Skirmish +1 / Minor +3 / Major +5 net ‚Üí win treaty; opposite ‚àí1/‚àí3/‚àí5 ‚Üí losing treaty; else White Peace), with a **~4-yr (2-phase) cushion before any penalties, escalating at ~6 yr** (Arkansas-Progressive's per-phase lingering-penalty ladder, POST 7; playtest baseline = 6 wars: RevWar ~8yr / NW-Indian 12yr / Barbary 6yr / War-of-1800 4yr / Canada 4yr / Brazil ongoing ‚Äî "pretty close to historical"). (4) **SecState + ambassador NEGOTIATION** (POST 6, folded with #26): cumulative SecState+ambassador admin adjusts the victories-required (<5 raises, >7 lowers; set once / re-set on ambassador change); 5%/phase loss-threshold roll + 33%/phase white-peace prompt, trait-weighted (Geostrategic/Egghead vs Lowbrow/Disharmonious); Integrity gates refusing white peace. (5) **Successor-eats-predecessor's-treaty attribution** (ties #45's instigator-attribution rule) ‚Äî refinement: a successor who *chooses* to fight on / snatch a stalemate keeps that agency rather than auto-eating the predecessor's loss-penalty (the live Butler‚ÜíBlue case, POST 10/16). **‚òÖ DESIGN-EVOLUTION reconcile (tier-1 call needed):** this thread's **tier-scaled net-score thresholds** (Apr 2022) appear SUPERSEDED by the LATER `rethinkwar` (Sept 2022) **|raw-score|√ó10%-per-phase roll** already folded on #45 ‚Äî chronology suggests the %-roll replaced the thresholds; flag for a tier-1 call (likely the thresholds are the earlier statement of the same war-end intent). **The ROOT exploit this subsystem fixes** = ignore-the-losing-treaty (penalty-free at meter floor, #266); whether that exploit is a SHIPPED-code defect is a tech-lead verification (the treaty system may not be in `src/`) ‚Äî recorded as a (b40) note on #45, only becomes a DH row if confirmed shipped. DISTINCT from #45 (combat/War-Score) and #178 (treaty‚ÜíTERRITORY acquisition ‚Äî that path should plug INTO this confirmation flow). Pennsylvania** (unhistorical) and expected it to NEGATE the fugitive-slave law; vcczar's ruling: apply a consequence ONLY where the source spreadsheet specifies one, else the verdict is COSMETIC (Prigg carries no such spec today), changeable after the 1st release. (POST 2, 14); Luzon‚ÜîQuezon intermotion (POST 3); authored before either partner state was finished (cross-state deps up front). | 285 | **‚òÖ CC 3/5-REFUSAL APPORTIONMENT + per-delegate PERSUADE roll ‚Äî refusing the 3/5 compromise should DEPLETE Southern EV/Reps (DESIGNED-BUT-UNBUILT; corrects the b44 "CC slave-plank rewrites EV" claim); and CC ratification uses a governor-bias `chance(0.5)` PROXY instead of the forum's per-delegate ~25% persuade roll ‚Äî NEW (b45, `38cedf29`; alt-states digest code-verified the correction)** | **Two deltas, both code-verified.** (1) **The slave-compromise choice has NO apportionment consequence in code:** post-ratification EVs are set UNIFORMLY ‚Äî `s.electoralVotes = Math.max(3, (s.ccDelegateSlots ?? 2) + 1)` for EVERY state (`constitutionalConvention.ts:210`) and **never reads `slaveCompromise`** (`slaveCompromise` IS a real CC vote ‚Äî `three_fifths`/`full`/`none`, `constitutionalConvention.ts:34-39`, default `three_fifths` ‚Äî but it does not multiply EV). (2) **Ratification is decided by a governor-BIAS PROXY:** `govApproves = gov.partyId === 'RED' \|\| chance(0.5)` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:189`), `ratified = approve >= 9` (`:192`, ‚úî threshold matches) ‚Äî NOT a per-delegate persuade roll | **‚òÖ CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION: the b44 tech-lead/brief said "CC slave-compromise plank rewrites EV." CODE-VERIFIED imprecise ‚Äî `constitutionalConvention.ts:210` sets EVs UNIFORMLY from `ccDelegateSlots+1` and NEVER reads `slaveCompromise`; the only shipped in-engine EV rewrite is the colony‚Üírepublic transition. So "refusing the 3/5 compromise depletes Southern EVs/Reps" is DESIGNED-BUT-UNBUILT, not shipped.** Forum evidence (`38cedf29` April-2023 POST 26-27, 1786 CC): the North "went way too hard," REFUSED the 3/5 compromise, and "the South has almost no political power as their electoral votes and number of Reps in the House have been depleted" ‚Üí the slaveCompromise choice SHOULD drive Southern EV/Rep apportionment (refusing 3/5 ‚áí slaves not counted ‚áí fewer Southern EVs/seats). Same thread confirms the per-delegate persuade roll: ratification was clutched when "a random Georgia delegate succeeded on a 25% chance to convince his Governor to sign" (POST 26) ‚Üí a per-delegate/per-governor ~25% persuade roll, NOT the shipped `chance(0.5)` bias-proxy. (`9acfc425` POST 3 corroborates the persuade flow ‚Äî "Lambert persuades 4 states to flip a plank" ‚Äî also GM-adjudicated, no automated convention sub-action.) Pairs with #280 (the apportionment-rewrite surface), #61 (foreign-born eligibility plank, also CC) | (a) Make post-ratification EV/Rep apportionment **read `slaveCompromise`**: refusing 3/5 ‚áí South loses EVs/seats (ties the colony‚Üírepublic rewrite at `:210`). OPEN: proportional to enslaved population or a flat penalty (forum says "depleted," not the formula). (b) Replace the governor bias-PROXY (`:189` `chance(0.5)`) with a **per-delegate/per-governor ~25% persuade roll** (low-prob clutch outcomes like the Georgia delegate); KEEP the ‚â•9-governor threshold (`:192`, correct). Add a "persuade N states to flip a plank" convention sub-action (forum-only today). Term limits are NOT hard-coded (3rd terms reachable). Blaine won by 1 point in deep red Maine" ‚Äî held only "by luck"; "it was his incumbency that saved him. Without it the Dems would have flipped it." So enthusiasm + party-pref + a CONTINUOUS state-lean term DO ship, but: **MISSING the incumbent +1, media-card +1, and president's-party-midterm ‚àí1 terms** (zero grep matches for `incumbentBonus`/`midterm`/`mediaCard`/`presParty` in `src/`); the state-lean is a graduated `state.bias*5`, NOT the designed FLAT +5-on-party-match; the die is non-deterministic `Math.random()` (a CLAUDE.md determinism bug, should use `rng.ts`). ‚òÖ **Two ENTHUSIASM gaps:** (1) the enthusiasm term is applied UNCONDITIONALLY ‚Äî there is NO `State.preferredIdeology` field to gate it (`State` `types.ts:1318` has only `bias`; grep `preferredIdeology`/`likedIdeology`/`favoredIdeology` = zero in `src/`); and (2) there is NO +1 ideology-match bonus (neither the 75%-designed nor guaranteed-as-shipped-on-forum variant) ‚Äî again because no per-state ideology-preference data exists. ‚òÖ‚òÖ **Biggest: the meter‚Üíenthusiasm DERIVATION does not exist anywhere** ‚Äî `game.enthusiasm` is a STATIC authored table (`buildEnthusiasm()` per scenario, e.g. `scenario1856.ts:18-31`) only ever MUTATED by era-event effects (`applyEffect` `phaseRunners.ts:3229-3232`); grep `refreshEnthusiasm`/`computeEnthusiasm`/`enthusiasmFromMeters` = none; nothing derives enthusiasm from meters and nothing runs "at end of Lingering." Meters and enthusiasm are DECOUPLED in the build. ‚òÖ **Era-gated meter activation is INVERTED:** `quality` + `planet` are permanent `NationalMeters` fields seeded LIVE from turn one (1856 `quality 0/planet 4`; 1772 `quality -1/planet 5`) and rendered unconditionally (`MetersPage.tsx:5`) ‚Äî no unlock mechanism ‚Äî whereas the design unlocks Quality-of-Life + Planet's-Health only in a later era. (Build has 7 meters; the design's 8th "Party Pref" meter is the separate `game.partyPreference` scalar.) | **The canonical per-candidate election score, stated + WORKED by the GMs (`6801b9f4`, Sept-2022 rules debate against a 1916/v2.8 playtest).** ‚òÖ Formula (POST 1, corrected POST 2): `score = ideological-enthusiasm-from-meters + party-pref + incumbent(+1) + media-card(+1) + state-bias(+5 for the candidate whose PARTY matches the state lean) + president's-party-midterm-penalty(‚àí1) + random die roll`; highest total takes the seat. Worked 1916 Alabama-Governor race: Aldrich (Moderate, Red) = +3 party-pref + die 3 ‚Üí 6; Gov. Henderson (LW-Pop, Blue, incumbent) = incumbent +1, media-card +1, +2 meters (LW-Pop), +5 AL state bias (Blue), ‚àí1 midterm, die 4 ‚Üí 13; **Henderson wins 13-6.** ‚òÖ THREE high-value designer rulings: **(a) the enthusiasm-from-meters bonus applies ONLY if that ideology is ALSO the STATE's preferred ideology** (Rezi POST 5: "I don't give ideological enthusiasm bonuses unless it's also the state ideology ‚Ä¶ makes 0 sense for a LW Populist to get a boost in Alabama"; Ted POST 6: "Yeah, I do it Rezi's way. I think that's how V said to do it forever ago") ‚Äî national enthusiasm does NOT transfer to a state that doesn't like that ideology; this requires a **per-state preferred-ideology field** (the precondition for both this gate AND the +1 below). **(b) a SEPARATE, party-AGNOSTIC +1 (designed at 75% chance) for the candidate's ideology == the state's preferred ideology** (Ark POST 7; Ted POST 8 ‚Äî the "Joe Manchin" case: a Blue Conservative in WV gets the +1 ideology-match but NOT the Conservative-enthusiasm bonus). ‚òÖ But Ted CONCEDES the 75% term never shipped that way (POST 9: "I did probably forget to set up the 75% columns ‚Ä¶ this was when the state bias matches were guaranteed") ‚Äî design = 75%, forum-shipped = guaranteed; the browser build has NEITHER. **(c) enthusiasm is computed from the meters at the END of the Lingering phase, not at election time** (Ted POST 6) ‚Äî a once-per-turn snapshot the election step reads. **(d) Quality-of-Life + Planet's-Health meters are ERA-GATED UNLOCKS** (Tyler POST 3, unchallenged: "Quality and Life and Planet Health wouldn't be active ‚Ä¶ They unlock later on") ‚Äî not all 8 meters are live from start. **(e) the entire election scoring model is a designer-acknowledged REWORK target** (Ted POST 10: "I'd like to really rework how elections work in general") ‚Äî treat as OPEN, not settled canon. ‚òÖ This is the per-state SCORING half upstream of #12/#124 (the bill/cabinet‚Üímeter levers feed meters that SHOULD feed enthusiasm ‚Äî but the derivation is missing, so those levers cannot reach elections) and the SCORING-composition complement of #51 (the enthusiasm-RESHUFFLE algorithm) + #18 (the State-of-Meters‚Üíbonus 2-layer MAP ‚Äî note #18's "universal per-ideology meter modifier applied to ALL candidates of that ideology" is in tension with this thread's "enthusiasm-only-if-state-ideology" gate; reconcile the two readings) + #287 (the +2 HOUSE-incumbency term, a distinct office-specific bonus vs this formula's general incumbent +1). The IN-GAME development path DOES ship (`careerTrack`/`careerTrackYears` `types.ts:1279-1280`, `addSkillPoint` every 4 career-yrs, `CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS = 20` `types.ts:236`) ‚Äî only the pre-game back-fill is missing | **The load-bearing root-cause revelation of the 1960 playtest (`3b46b086` POST 5-9).** The data model has, **per politician**, THREE stat tiers: **historic/peak** ("take place at peak year or after ‚Äî each politician has a peak year", POST 5), **midway** ("start after draft year but before peak year", POST 6), and **rookie/draft-start** (the floor). The 1960 boot "used the stats Cal started the playtest with ‚Ä¶ midway and peak stats were NOT added" so "everyone is starting with rookie stats" (POST 6-7) ‚Üí the roster booted at rookie stats: "maybe 8 pols with 3 admin and **none with more than 3**", so "every cabinet is filled with a bunch of 2's who crater the meters" (POST 3) ‚Äî the ADMIN-SCARCITY CRATER. The fix: at boot, BACK-FILL pre-game draft classes by **career-AGE** ‚Äî Vols21's sheet rule (POST 9): **draft classes ‚âà16 career-yrs & prior = MAXED/peak ("follows the career path logic"), 8-15 yrs = MIDWAY, <8 yrs = rookie**; apply ONLY to pre-game drafts (in-game drafts develop via the shipped career track, POST 7-8). ‚öÝ RECONCILE: the sheet's 16-yr "maxed" path vs the code's `CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS = 20` cap ‚Äî which governs the boot back-fill is OPEN. This is the **data-model PRECONDITION** for #124 (cabinet‚Üímeters), #12 (legislation‚Üímeters), and #179 (lingering-roll engine) to read sane Admin/competence ‚Äî until it lands, every meter computation runs on rookie stats and spirals. DISTINCT from #186 (the boot PROCEDURE that consumes this model ‚Äî incumbents/offices/fatigue/delegate-seeds) and #86/#115 (boot data/shape); this row is specifically the multi-tier stat DATA MODEL + the age-based stat-init formula. (consensus leaning NO, POST 317 ‚Äî ties #292's era-gated-meter finding); **(5)** proposer-selection ties (sort by Legis then party; equal-Legis tie-break unspecified ‚Äî PV is not in rules); **(6)** conversion order / Pliable‚ÜíPuritan (rules: ascending faction size, ties random, POST 318); **(7)** Gov-vs-Rep eligibility for a dual-eligible pol (CPU defaults to Gov ‚Äî ties the CPU-init cluster); **(8)** election candidate-selection should weight ¬±1 modifiers + traits (Provincial/Military-post-war) but doesn't (ties #20 gov-candidate + #287/#292); **(9)** ideology-shift CPU rules don't protect Integrity/Puritan pols (gain Flipflopper/Two-Faced too often ‚Äî ties #127); **(10)** early-ConCon (~1780-82) institution-gating gap (post-ratification Pres/Senate/SC elections expected but many rules "don't apply until Federalism"); **(11)** initial SC Chief-Justice selection unspecified (tends to seat an all-one-party court); **(12)** pre-party Pres elections pick by most-kingmakers, alternate-candidate rules rarely fire. Record as the boot-procedure punch-list this stat-model row + #186/#115 + #20 consume. `58da1636` POST 315-318**; **‚òÖ‚òÖ (b61) AUTHORITATIVE CPU-RULES CHANGELOG `b83f7fd0` (Ted) SHARPENS the CPU CAREER-TRACK ASSIGNMENT + early-REMOVAL procedures this row's `careerTrack` build-column touches (the CPU-decision side; the stat-tier model above is the data side). ‚òÖ (A) The 5-STEP ASSIGNMENT (POST 1, ratified POST 5): **(1) NEW ‚â•3-skill KEEP-OFF gate** ‚Äî any track-eligible pol with **‚â•3 in any skill has a 50% chance to be kept OFF the track** (so a Hamilton can serve in a cabinet instead of being stuck on-track); **(2) first-pass fill 75% each, IN ORDER** Backroom‚ÜíLegislative‚ÜíGoverning‚ÜíAdmin‚ÜíMilitary‚ÜíJudicial (a failed 75% leaves the slot temporarily vacant); **(3) Private track = highest-PV remaining rookie**; **(4) retry the failed 75% slots, same order**; **(5) force-fill unfillable slots** by randomly picking a rookie who must still pass the chart's 50% roll. Per-slot selection: **Backroom** = a pol from the state where the faction has the MOST pols **UNLESS** it already has a kingmaker (active-or-not) **under 60** there (then next-most-populated state without one) ‚Äî this is the "don't stack kingmakers in one state" rule; **Legis/Gov/Admin/Mil/Judicial** = highest-skill rookie in that skill; **Private** = highest-PV. **3 TIE-BREAKS in order:** (i) favor a pol whose state is NOT yet a US State; (ii) favor the pol who is NOT also top-skilled in another field (dual-skilled pol takes the other slot); (iii) random. ‚òÖ (B) EARLY REMOVAL (before 20 yrs, POST 1): **+5% every 4 years** (5%@4yr / 10%@8yr / 15%@12yr‚Ä¶), **per individual pol**, with a **hard gate: won't remove a pol whose home state has NOT reached territory STATUS**; backfill an UNQUALIFIED rookie only if no qualified one exists. ‚òÖ SHIPPED = ~0% of this: `runPhase_2_1_2_CareerTracks` Pass 1 (`phaseRunners.ts:422-452`) is the ENTIRE CPU logic ‚Äî for each track-less CPU pol **age<50** assign `bestAvailableTrack` = the pol's HIGHEST skill below 5 whose track isn't full (`:392-399`, ties by `SKILLS` order); cap `CAREER_TRACK_CAP=5`/faction/track (`types.ts:237`); a pol finishing 20 yrs re-tracks if age<60. **Absent:** the ‚â•3-skill 50% keep-off gate, the ordered 75%-per-slot fill/retry, Private=highest-PV, the force-fill 50% roll, Backroom-kingmaker-state selection, and all 3 tie-breaks (no PV/state/kingmaker awareness ‚Äî raw skill only). **Early removal does NOT exist** ‚Äî the CPU removes a pol ONLY at `careerTrackYears >= CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS`(=20, `:427`; `types.ts:236`); no +5%/4yr ladder, **no territory-status gate**. `CAREER_ODDS` (`types.ts:229-234`) governs threshold GAINS, not removal. NEW sub-items to build: the **‚â•3-skill keep-off gate** and the **territory-status early-removal gate**. Cross-ref #24 (the per-individual-pol roll clarification from `26b112e5`, re-ratified here). `b83f7fd0` POST 1, 5**) |

Corroborates this row's lobby‚Üîindustry mechanic + the canonical-9-industry reconcile; pairs with the modern-lobby keying NEW #321. `abilities-expertise-traits-reference` ¬ß5 / POST 17-20. **‚òÖ b57 DIRECTION CONFLICT (`0ff5e582`):** the CPU-gov-action thread wants industry-expansion to be the "help party overall" move BECAUSE improving an industry **feeds POINTS to the matching lobby card** (Agriculture-expansion ‚Üí Big-Ag; Finance ‚Üí Wall Street; POST 2/3/4) ‚Äî i.e. the desired arrow is **industry-expansion ‚áí lobby-card-points**. The shipped `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` coupling runs the **REVERSE arrow**: held lobby cards bump the matching state industry +1 in the 2.1.8 alignment-drift pass (`phaseRunners.ts:1638-1656`). Same lobby‚Üîindustry table, OPPOSITE causal direction; the gov-action use case (improve-industry-as-help ‚Üí lobby points) is UNBUILT. Tracked as the industry sub-gap of the #20 gov-action overhaul. [`0ff5e582` POST 2, 3, 4]**) |

Pairs with #4 (ideology profile), #290 (the generator revamp this sizing feeds), #281/#280 (alt-states that modulate the count), the dataset pipeline (`scripts/`).
