Strengthened **#116/#92/#41/#18/#51/#56/#107** with `ideo1928` tags; **DH-53** corroborated (per-bill effect-sign bug, the earthquake-aid +budget bug). **‚òÖ GA-vs-designer caveat: 10centjimmy is a GA, so the career-track-preplacement, inaugural-cabinet-holdover, and EconStab-cascade interpretations are GA-level rulings, NOT designer-authoritative ‚Äî confirm with V/Ted.** **DH-36 nuance: the thread did NOT die of burnout per se ‚Äî it slowed and faded mid-1936-term as the GA + players juggled RL and factions dropped to CPU; no in-game ending, never reaches a WWII/later era.** **Provenance: batches 1-18, ~23 digests; the era-band model now confirmed across 1772/1800/1820/1856/1948/2000/1928 starts ‚Äî the interwar/New-Deal economic engine is now documented from a native 1928 campaign.** _Resolved/clarified by batch 31 (a 5-thread RULES/FEEDBACK/TEST/DATA RECONCILE batch, NO playtest ‚Äî the canonical RULEBOOK + the appointments deep-dive are PRIMARY sources that SETTLE many open items):_ **‚òÖ‚òÖ The canonical 15-era TRIGGER-YEAR TABLE is logged (#92/#206)** ‚Äî the authoritative band-label calendar (Independence 1774 ‚Üí Federalism 1788 ‚Üí Republicanism 1800 ‚Üí Democracy 1820 ‚Üí Manifest-Destiny 1840 ‚Üí Nationalism 1856 ‚Üí Gilded 1868 ‚Üí Progressivism 1892 ‚Üí Normalcy 1916 ‚Üí Ideologies 1928 ‚Üí Nuclear-Age 1948 ‚Üí Neocons 1972 ‚Üí Terror 2000 ‚Üí Populism 2012 ‚Üí Future = after the current real-world cycle), reconciled three-way (15 designer LABELS vs #92 game-state TRANSITION-PREDICATE vs the shipped 4-enum). **‚òÖ #199 SUPERSEDED** ‚Äî the rejected-nominee fallout is Ted's FINAL 33%-Pres‚ÜíControversial / 33%-SML‚ÜíControversial / 34%-nobody + already-Controversial‚Üílifetime-Cabinet/Cabinet-Level-ban (Ambassador still allowed), REPLACING the prior Controversial‚ÜíIncompetent cascade (human-canonical flag set). **‚òÖ #183 RESOLVED-DIRECTION** ‚Äî the endorsement/withdraw-MOMENTUM spec IS specced (the `primary2020` May-2022 origin: 60/75/50% withdraw + endorsement splits + Puritan/Disharmonious spite + doubled rates; delegate transfers visible), the app implements NONE ‚Üí specced-but-unbuilt. **‚òÖ #182 CONFIRMED** (rulebook ¬ßY pins Command = the per-half-term executive-action budget ‚Äî now authoritative, not a single-thread inference). **‚òÖ #172 + DH-76 SHARPENED** with the full confirmation control structure: 60% cabinet/SCOTUS default + nuclear-option‚Üímajority + party-wide block (5 reasons) + filibuster + invoke-cloture (SML Manipulation + Legislative-advantage) + the auto-confirm GATE (‚â•3 admin ‚àß ¬¨Controversial ‚àß ¬¨IronFist-SML ‚àß ¬¨Top-Four) + Top-Four-always-vote (State/Def/Treasury/War; differs from the cross-party-cap Top-Four that includes AG) + admin-1 50/50-flip + trait‚Üívote=75%. **Corroborated/provenance-upgraded:** #179/#134 (rulebook lingering algorithm + the 10%/2yr-from-age-10 tax/tariff decay ‚Üí crisis-at-20 numbers + officer‚Üímeter map), #50/#51 (rulebook ¬ßI canonical card-assignment algorithm ‚Äî plurality + compatibility/ban tables + 5-pol floor + cascade), #211/#115 (playtestdraft: baseline = within-party FIXED round-robin not snake/PV + CPU autodraft = D10‚ÜíNth-highest-PV + preference-list automation), #214/#215 (primary2020 = the May-2022 ORIGIN of the trait‚ÜíPV revamp; the James-Dean 0-skill-2-command-8-trait OP exhibit + the POST-112 office‚ÜíPV table), #31/DH-23 (apptdeepdive states the root cause: lobby-first selection + admin-1 50/50 flip), #112 (apptdeepdive = ORIGIN of the ¬±1-ideology gate + Pliable/Passive‚ÜíIron-Fist-SML nomination substitution 50/75/100%), #25/#49/#31 (apptdeepdive 16-yr cumulative tenure + ambassador rotation + +50/expertise + senior-military-confirm + lobby-enthusiasm scoring ‚Äî merged, not re-created), #130 (rulebook per-era death-bracket table), #222 (rulebook committee-size 3‚Üí11 scaling), #133 (apptdeepdive CC-mode appointment structure). **‚òÖ POST-143 trait CLOSURE (designed‚Üíbuilt, `bestmoments`):** most proposed traits SHIPPED ‚Äî `Cosmopolitan`/`Provincial`/`Magician`/`Numberfudger`/`Crisis-Manager`/`Crisis-Admin`/`Crisis-Gov` all in `types.ts` (vcczar's own "the 3 crisis traits are the same thing" objection was OVERRIDDEN ‚Äî all three shipped) ‚Äî and **`Lackey` was the ONE proposed trait NOT adopted** (no match in `types.ts`); a reconciliation, NOT a gap (decide drop-or-build for Lackey). **8 NEW rows #228-#235** (third-party/spoiler vote-drain #228 `GM‚áíApp`; CPU appointee acceptance-odds matrix #229 `GM‚áíApp`; competitive-map/swing-state cap #230; modern-Moderate over-performance balance datum #231; starting-draft 2-ideology restriction #232; CC-President appointment rule #233; Fog-of-War hidden ability/trait #234 PARKING-LOT/AMPU-2; D6 vetting roll #235 PARKING-LOT) + the deep-dive's N2/N3 merged into #25/#31/#49 (no re-create). **NO new bugs** (DH-78 unused ‚Äî these are primary RULES sources recording intent, not shipped-behavior defects). **‚òÖ The rulebook EXPLICITLY does NOT settle the Election math** (primaries/conventions/general/governor/congressional, #13/#47/#111/#183/#184/#185 stay owned by the playthrough digests) NOR the Special Rules / Implementation rules / external charts (Career-Track / Cabinet / Gov-Actions / War) ‚Äî author-withheld (rulebook POST 33). **Provenance: batches 1-31; the canonical rulebook (~24 systems) + the appointments-confirmation design-origin are now the authoritative spine for the per-turn loop 2.1‚Üí2.8, with the election system still owned by the playtests.** Still open:

  gov action that would govern this is discussed but may not exist. Pin a stated rule.

**‚òÖ CORPUS-COVERAGE GAP (batch 30, `presidents` record).** The "Playtest Presidents"

  the record (~100 in-game years, 27+ presidents)** with a **persistent Whig major party

- **1868-Bushwa** ‚Äî Grant‚ÜíWashburn‚ÜíCannon‚ÜíDepew (Red Cloud = 1st Native VP); roster ‚âÝ
  ingested `gilded`/`gild1868`; "all 1-term Red" balance signal (POST 46-66).
- **1960-start** ‚Äî Bricker def. JFK/Reagan; president ‚âÝ ingested `redbutton` 1960 run ‚Üí
  a distinct 1960 thread; defunct, CPU-revived as "1962" (POST 6, 9, 11).
- **2016-start** ‚Äî Warner; the run that **drove the 2-Admin cabinet-minimum rule** (POST 44-45).
- **2024-start** ‚Äî Haley‚ÜíInslee (Haley = 1st female president); ‚âÝ ingested `trump2024`
  (setup-only) (POST 30, 35-37).
- **Three "old-forum" 1788 runs** + **an "old" 2000 run** (Bush-unlimited-terms‚ÜíBiden) ‚Äî
  pre-current-rules; likely unrecoverable but named in the record (POST 5, 12, 18, 21, 24).
- Balance topline (positive): the designer's cross-playtest read is **healthy party
  alternation** ‚Äî never 3 same-party terms, never one-party-forever (POST 9-10); sub-signals
  (trait/command over-weighting "failing-upward" winners, weak early-era incumbency, frequent
  House-decided elections) feed #18/#184/#192/#214.

## Sources

Sources are now split into **playtest threads** (continuous narrative threads
of an actual game-in-progress ‚Äî the primary source for mechanics in play) and
**discussion threads** (community-curated fix-catalogs / rule-clarification
threads ‚Äî secondary, but the primary source for **dataset accuracy**, small
mechanical bugs, and designer rulings on rules ambiguity).

### Playtest threads (ingested digests)

- **Ingested digests:**
  - [`f4c7c2c4-ampu-1800-playtest-continued-1868`](playtest-digests/f4c7c2c4-ampu-1800-playtest-continued-1868.md)
    (`gilded`) ‚Äî 343 posts / 5 chunks. Gilded-Age (1868‚Äì1872) **multiplayer**
    playtest, 8 human factions + 2 CPU per party; continuation of a longer
    1800-start campaign. Establishes the multiplayer, multi-era, Gilded-Age,
    and convention / legislation / governor gap rows. GM: @ebrk85.
  - [`bf590684-1868-the-gilded-age-playtest`](playtest-digests/bf590684-1868-the-gilded-age-playtest.md)
    (`gild1868`, batch 14) ‚Äî **6318 posts / 77 chunks, the largest thread in the
    KB**. The first dedicated **native-1868 Gilded-Age multiplayer** campaign
    (RED=Republicans, BLUE=Democrats), 1868‚Üí~1886 over ~9 cycles; GM @Bushwa777
    (first-timer) + co-GM @ebrk85. The native Gilded spec ‚Äî source of NEW
    #147‚Äì#150 (tariff-as-%-rate, 20-yr Reconstruction timer, civil-service
    merit-vs-spoils, the "1872 Rule") + DH-63 (currency-exclusivity bug);
    corroborates the Gilded cluster (#3/#5/#6/#21/#41/#57). **3rd GM-burnout
    death** in the KB (after `new1772`, `dem1820`).

    **federalism era**: faction roster + nickname table, era-event spine,

    Ironclad-vs-10% choice DEADLOCKED even with humans ‚Üí states drifted back with

    4 plan types + a "plan-adopted-by-Congress-OR-President" prerequisite)**; a
    **‚òÖ "Union wins too easily" war-balance critique (NEW #155**; multiplier
    1.0‚Üí0.5 + per-theater scoring adopted live). **Corroborates the
    Reconstruction/war/secession cluster (#56/#57/#58/#59) across a 5th campaign.**
    Source of NEW #155‚Äì#157 + DH-64 (the `Southern Unionist` dataset-labeling bug).

    multiplayer campaign**, GA'd by **@10centjimmy** (a GA, NOT the designer ‚Äî so

    @MrPotatoTed). Opens at the Roaring-Twenties peak (Hoover + Republicans [RED]
    dominating Al Smith's Democrats [BLUE]), runs ~1928‚Üí~1936 (Hoover‚ÜíFDR; reaches
    the 1936 conventions with FDR President), then fades mid-term. **Hinge polarity
    (PRE-realignment): RED=Republicans, BLUE=Democrats; the modern BLUE-economic-left
    is CREATED in play by the New Deal (a player renames his faction "New Deal
    Democrats").** Engine would run on `modern`/`progressive` tuning ‚Äî UNBUILT.
    **‚òÖ Headline = the ECONOMIC ENGINE: the Great Depression is a SCRIPTED ERA EVENT
    with VARIABLE timing** (fired late, ~1930-31 vs real Oct-1929) carrying a

    ‚àí3) + an **EconStab cascade** (2 random industries ‚àí1 nationwide ‚Üí EV reflow;
    meter-gating in crisis) + crisis-GATED legislation; the GA flags the era's static
    party-modifiers as too strong AND the Depression as too weak (decoupled). The
    1932 Hoover-v-Roosevelt general exercises the #18 2-layer meter‚Üíelection scorer
    live; diplomacy (#107) corroborated with a 7-nation 1928 roster (no Israel); the
    "Republicanism vs Fascism vs Communism"/WWII framing is FLAVOR, not a war-engine
    trigger. **NEW: #160** (Depression-as-scripted-event + EconStab cascade + static-
    modifier decoupling), **#161** (1928 = 6th era-band start), **#162** (interwar
    diplomacy roster + flavor-not-war), **#163** (career-track pre-placement house

    Corroborates **#116/#92/#41/#18/#51/#56/#107** + **DH-53**. **‚òÖ #92 era-band model
    now SIX-START-CONFIRMED (1772/1800/1820/1856/1948/2000 + 1928).**

    confirms BUG-1** (LA-Purchase dropped at an 1800 start). **Corroborates the

### Discussion threads (community-curated catalogs; the dataset-accuracy source)

A *separate* class of source from the playtest threads. Discussion threads are
**not** game-in-progress narratives ‚Äî they are running community-curated lists
of small fixes (dataset bugs, mechanical typos, designer-ruling clarifications)
that the build inherits as fix-it tickets, not as gameplay-validation. **Do not
confuse with playtest threads** ‚Äî there are no GA rulings on a running game
here; there are designer rulings on policy/dataset Qs.

- [`cf82a7d3-a-thread-for-small-low-priority-changes-and-errors`](playtest-digests/cf82a7d3-a-thread-for-small-low-priority-changes-and-errors.md)
  (`smallbugs`) ‚Äî **804 posts / 10 chunks** (single-pass reduce ‚Äî a 3-year+
  community catalog of polish-tier fixes). Started Apr 2023 by @Imperator Taco
  Cat with the explicit charter "not‚Ä¶ major changes to game rules or systems
  but fixing small errors"; ongoing through May 2026. The **primary source for
  dataset accuracy and small mechanical bugs.** Each entry is a one-line
  hand-off to the build, typically acknowledged within 1-2 reply posts by
  @MrPotatoTed, @vcczar, or @Arkansas Progressive and either "fixed inline" in
  the master sheet or deferred to "when Anthony codes that rule." **Headline
  contributions:** ~50 named-pol fixes, ~30 mechanical fixes, ~20 dataset
  additions (gap-log row **#120** umbrella) ‚Äî all going via
  `scripts/seedDataset.mjs` `CURATED_ROWS` overrides; one NEW dataset gap
  (**#121 ‚Äî Secessionist trait missing**); one NEW rules gap (**#122 ‚Äî Pardon
  mechanics unspecified**); one late-modern content-fixes bundle (**#123**).
  **Designer-ruling distillate (¬ß5 of digest):** Sept-2024 Ted batch of 8
  proposed rule changes (Pres-uses-Command-for-blunder hybrid adopted; Iron-Fist
  PL no-block / Disharmonious-or-Independent only-challenger gate adopted; etc.);
  **POST 734-735 is the channel through which the #38 relocation-cap-4 change
  was sent to Anthony for the build** (12-30-25, "Approved by vczar"). Strongly
  **corroborates DH-27 (trait-conflict slips in boot data, 3rd thread), DH-51
  (modern-pol overpowered recency bias, 3rd thread), DH-53 (bill‚Üímeter

- [`a0f0bf04-discussion-teds-change-log-of-doom`](playtest-digests/a0f0bf04-discussion-teds-change-log-of-doom.md)
  (`tedchange`) ‚Äî **408 posts / 7 chunks** (single-pass reduce ‚Äî a
  designer-led decision-log). **‚òÖ The AUTHORITATIVE channel for current rule
  wording in the entire forum corpus.** Started Sept 18 2024 by @MrPotatoTed
  as the FEEDBACK channel for his parallel "Change Log of Doom" thread, which
  enumerated the rule changes Ted was considering. The thread runs a
  **section-by-section systematic rules-doc cleanup pass from phase 2.1 ‚Üí
  2.6** between Sept 2024 and Mar 2025, then **resumes Oct 2025** on a
  Lingering script clarification. Each section gets a Ted draft + 5-15
  pushback/refinement posts + a Ted final ruling + write to rules doc.
  **Critical context ‚Äî Anthony coded ALONGSIDE this thread**, meaning rulings
  here are the LATEST shipped-design-intent the programmer was targeting.
  **Headline contributions:** ~25 NEW gap rows (#124-#142) and ~10 RESOLUTIONS
  of OPEN questions / SUPERSESSIONS of prior digest interpretations. **The
  canonical 2-step Pres implementation Admin-then-Command rule (POST 163)** is
  the authoritative wording of cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3. **‚òÖ Key NOT-touched items:**
  the relocation-cap-4 ruling, the amendments-not-SCOTUS-challengeable rule,
  the Civil-Rights-Act-not-repealable decision ‚Äî those are 12a's (cf82a7d3)
  domain. The two discussion threads have **complementary scope**: 12a =
  playtest-bug catalog; 12b = section-by-section systematic rewrite.
  **SUPERSESSIONS** (Ted's rulings override prior GA reads): Kingmaker vs
  Master Kingmaker bonus scope (was "either-or" per Matt's reading; now
  Kingmaker = state-only / Master = every-state-incl-own, POST 316); 3+3
  random-traits + random-alt-states draft count (was 5/5 in earlier reads,
  now 3/3, POST 50); the Hale=1/2-death + ex-Pres-only-rolls-death rules
  close the John-Adams-immortality loop (POST 143, 396). **Open items at
  thread end:** Mil-Prep meter level 4 fix (independently re-discovered but
  NOT closed), Universal Election Modifier proposal, Crisis-trait
  consolidation, faithless-elector wording rework, term-limit Gov-action
  era-gating. **NO conflict with batch-9 review-gate Senate cloture ruling**
  (60% not discussed in this thread).

- [`2d3ffb3e-suggested-fixes-fall-2022`](playtest-digests/2d3ffb3e-suggested-fixes-fall-2022.md)

  confirmation-inflation fix** (883-895, AGREED-not-implemented), the CPU

  **Strongly corroborates #120, #121, DH-53, DH-64, #124/#31/#80, #135, #153,

- [`fd4c3c4e-a-more-perfect-judiciary-dlc-or-game-mechanic-overhaul-idea`](playtest-digests/fd4c3c4e-a-more-perfect-judiciary-dlc-or-game-mechanic-overhaul-idea.md)
  (`judiciaryidea`, **batch 36** ‚Äî JUDICIARY-OVERHAUL design thread + a design
  debate + ONE shipped partial, NOT a playthrough) ‚Äî **18 posts / 1 chunk** (Apr
  2022). Cal proposes two big systems both PUNTED to AMPU 2/DLC by vcczar+CE2:
  **Judicial Philosophy** (Judicial Ideology [Cons/Swing/Liberal, per-personal-
  ideology base + trait mods] + Judicial Doctrine [per-case aye/nay mods],
  hidden-until-revealed) + **Focus Courts** (4 regional appellate courts +
  Original/Appellate jurisdiction + **Rule-of-Four cert** + affirm/reverse [points
  unchanged by reverse] + **deny-cert-on-Landmark ‚Üí SC loses points**) ‚Üí **ORIGIN
  of NEW #251**. The DESIGN ORIGIN of #218/#249/#25/#52. The ONLY thing SHIPPED
  (forum-era AMPU 1, NOT this build) = a confirmed justice 10% immediate ¬±1
  ideology shift + per-10-yr further shifts + 5% leftward (sharpens #52). Fully
  argues the #52 player-vs-CPU debate (CE2 independent-default w/ a Roe abuse-
  vector vs vcczar keep-player-SC). Shipped court = coin-flip (`phaseRunners.ts:3397`).
  Cites `POST n`.

**Dataset ingest (NOT a forum batch) ‚Äî the authoritative hand-authored roster.**
